# Aspera Orchestrator v4.1.5 Plugins Manuals

## Asset Management

### Dalet Asset Management Plugin

![Dalet Asset Management Plugin icon](icons/DaletAssetManagement.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in provides the ability to interact with Dalet Asset Management System.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.5   | Fixing reference in help file.                      |
| 0.1.4   | Setting SSL flag to true to handle https            |
| 0.1.3   | Corrected usage of resp.body and resp_data          |
| 0.1.2   | Added more payloads                                 |
| 0.1.1   | Changed the category to Asset Management            |
| 0.1.0   | adding payload columns                              |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Payload file**: The file containing the API request to the Dalet Media Server. Click on 'refresh' to update the saved inputs.

- **Saved inputs**: The parameters to be sent in the API request. This is updated after the payload file is specified.

- **Soap wsdl path**: The URL for the wsdl file used to make the Dalet API calls.

There are three inputs available in this plugin: payload file, saved inputs, and soap wsdl path. All three inputs are required. Only the payload file and soap wsdl path may be specified at runtime.

##### Outputs description

- **response**: The processed response from the request

##### Operating Instructions

This plugin allows for API calls to be made to a Dalet Media Server.\
The API calls need to be created in the actions_config folder. API calls should be specified in the Payload file, then clicking 'refresh' should populate the saved inputs field. The response from the server is processed in an array format.\
\
This plugin is compatible with Dalet version 4.0 or greater.\

### ShowMgr

![ShowMgr icon](icons/ShowMgrOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-ins is used to interact with ShowMgr (using API calls)

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.1   | Changed the category to asset mgmt.                 |
| 0.1.0   | added service url                                   |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

- "This action plug-ins is used to interact with ShowMgr (using API calls)"\

  ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: Comments associated to this template or configuration.

- **Subscription Key**: The Access key used to access the API calls

- **Service URL**: The Service url of the service we are trying to reach to

- **API Operation**: The list of operations currently supported by the plugin. (Get Orders List, Get Orders Info, Put Orders Status, Get and Update Trigger)

- **Order Class**: The Class of the Order we are trying to access

- **Requesting Order Status**: The Order status of the Orders we are looking for.

- **Updating Order Status**: The Order status of the job we want it to be updated with.

- **Order Id**: Order Id of the job we want information about

- **Keep Trigger Ongoing?**: This is for the one API operation where we can keep looking for the orders at a given frequency

  Details:\
  'Get Orders List' - To get the Orders list matching the given Order Class and Order Status.\
  'Get Order Info' - Given an Order Id this operation will retrieve the information about the order.\
  'Update Order Status' - Given the Order id it will update the job with the collected status information.\
  'ShowMgr Trigger Operation' - This operation combines the Get Order and Update in to 1 step and has a trigger associated with it to support constant polling.\

<!-- -->

- Service URL And Subscription Key are the only mandatory parameter required for this plugin.\
  Every Operation Requires some input while not the Other.\
  Get Orders List - No Mandatory input. Order Class and Status can be provided to narrow down the result\
  Get Order Info - Order Id must be provided to collect the information\
  Update Order Status - Order Id and the status to be updated with must be provided\
  ShowMgr Trigger Operation - Updating Order Status must be provided. Also, Order Status and Class if provided will narrow down the results better.\

##### Outputs description

- This action executes an Subler transformation action on a remote node, via SSH.\
- **Order_Info**: Hash of the information about the order id given
- **Orders_list**: List of Orders matching the given criteria
- **Order_Id**: Order Id of the Order that was taken in to execution
- **File Location**: File Location of the Order id provided
- **Job Status**: Job status of the Order Id provided
- **Execution Report**: Response received as is from the API call
- **Error**: If the request failed, this parameter will hold the reason for the failure.
- **Order Status**: Status of the Order Id Provided.

##### Operating Instructions

Make sure that you have a valid subscription key to reach the ShowMgr API methods.

### Sony Ci

![Sony Ci icon](icons/SonyCi.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin enables management of media assets within Sony Ci cloud platform through upload, download, and metadata operations.

| Version | Comment                                                         |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.1   | Fix issues when storing certain string characters (Like colons) |
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8             |
| 0.1.0   | add operations upload, get metadata, set metadata               |
| 0.0.2   | add Content-Length header                                       |
| 0.0.1   | initial development                                             |

### How It Works

- Integrates with Sony Ci cloud platform to manage media assets through upload, download, and metadata operations.
- Connects to Sony Ci using API credentials (username, password, client id, and client secret) for secure authentication.
- Supports four main operations: UPLOAD (create assets), DOWNLOAD (retrieve assets), GET METADATA (read asset metadata), and SET METADATA (update asset metadata).
- Generates Aspera transfer specifications for high-speed file transfers in conjunction with Node API Transfer plugin.
- Handles workspace and folder management within Sony Ci for organized asset storage.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`Sony Ci Media Asset Management`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the Sony Ci authentication:`
    - **`Username`**`: Your Sony Ci account username.`
    - **`Password`**`: Your Sony Ci account password.`
    - **`Client id`**`: Sony API client id for authentication.`
    - **`Client secret`**`: Sony API client secret for authentication.`
3.  `Set up the operation parameters:`
    - **`Operation`**`: Choose UPLOAD, DOWNLOAD, GET METADATA, or SET METADATA.`
    - **`Workspace id`**`: Target workspace (for UPLOAD operations).`
    - **`Folder id`**`: Target folder within workspace (for UPLOAD operations).`
    - **`Asset id`**`: Asset identifier (for DOWNLOAD, GET/SET METADATA operations).`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter           | Example Value                                 |
|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| **Username**        | user@company.com                              |
| **Password**        | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*                              |
| **Client id**       | sony-api-client-123                           |
| **Client secret**   | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*                              |
| **Operation**       | UPLOAD                                        |
| **Workspace id**    | ws-12345-abcde                                |
| **Folder id**       | folder-67890-fghij                            |
| **Source filepath** | /path/to/video.mp4                            |
| **Source filesize** | 1073741824                                    |
| **Asset id**        | asset-98765-klmno (for non-upload operations) |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | The comments of the Sony Ci Transcoder template. |
| **Username** | The Sony Ci account username for authentication. |
| **Password** | The Sony Ci account password for authentication. |
| **Client id** | The Sony API client id used for authentication. |
| **Client secret** | The Sony API client secret used for authentication. |
| **Operation** | The operation to execute: UPLOAD, DOWNLOAD, GET METADATA, or SET METADATA. |
| **Workspace id** | The target workspace id for UPLOAD operations. Optional - defaults to user's personal workspace if not specified. |
| **Folder id** | The target folder id for UPLOAD operations. Optional - defaults to workspace root folder if not specified. |
| **Source filesize** | The filesize in bytes of the source file to upload for UPLOAD operations. Optional - defaults to 1 byte. |
| **Source filepath** | The filepath of the source file to upload for UPLOAD operations. Can be filename only if in docroot. |
| **Asset id** | The asset id for DOWNLOAD, GET METADATA and SET METADATA operations. |
| **Download folder** | The download folder path for DOWNLOAD operations. Must be relative to docroot. |
| **Add metadata** | Metadata name-value pairs to add for SET METADATA operations. |

### Inputs Description

**Authentication parameters (available for all operations):**

- **Username**: The Sony Ci account username. Overrides configuration value.
- **Password**: The Sony Ci account password. Overrides configuration value.
- **Client id**: The Sony API client id used for authentication. Overrides configuration value.
- **Client_secret**: The Sony API client secret used for authentication. Overrides configuration value.

**For UPLOAD operations:**

- **Workspace id**: The target workspace id for asset creation.
- **Folder id**: The target folder id within the workspace.
- **Filesize**: The filesize in bytes of the source file to upload.
- **Filepath**: The filepath of the source file to upload.

**For DOWNLOAD, GET METADATA and SET METADATA operations:**

- **Asset_id**: The asset id for DOWNLOAD, GET METADATA and SET METADATA operations.
- **Parameters**: The metadata name-value pairs for SET METADATA operations. Format: { 'name1' =\> \['value1'\], 'name2' =\> \['value2'\] }

### Outputs Description

**For UPLOAD operations:**

- **asset_id**: The created asset id.
- **result_transfer_spec**: The upload transfer specification.

**For DOWNLOAD operations:**

- **result_transfer_spec**: The download transfer specification.

**For GET METADATA and SET METADATA operations:**

- **metadata**: The array of collected or set name-value pairs.

### Supported Operations

- UPLOAD - Create an asset in Sony Ci and get Aspera transfer specification
- DOWNLOAD - Get Aspera transfer specification to download an asset from Sony Ci
- GET METADATA - Return metadata name-value pairs for a given asset
- SET METADATA - Set metadata name-value pairs for a given asset

### Operating Instructions

**Prerequisites:**

- The api.cimediacloud.com endpoint must be reachable on port TCP 443 from your Orchestrator system.
- The Sony Ci workspace must be Aspera enabled for the plugin to work.
- This plugin must be used in conjunction with the Node API Transfer plugin for actual file transfers.

**Upload Configuration:**

- **UPLOAD Operation:** Creates an asset in Sony Ci and returns an Aspera transfer specification for upload.
- Specify Sony Ci credentials (username, password, client id, client secret).
- Configure workspace id (optional - defaults to personal workspace), folder id (optional - defaults to root), source filesize (optional - defaults to 1 byte), and filepath.
- Source filepath must be relative to the docroot of the system user linked to the node API user. If in docroot, use filename only.
- Use the returned transfer spec with Node API Transfer plugin by selecting 'Submit Transfer Using Payload'.
- Set activity_logging to true in aspera.conf \<server\> section for completion.
- Files with the same name create new assets instead of overwriting existing ones.

**Other Operations:**

- **DOWNLOAD Operation:** Returns Aspera transfer specification for downloading assets from Sony Ci. Requires the Node API Transfer plugin to perform the actual download using the returned transfer specifications.
- Download folder must be specified relative to docroot of system user linked to node user.
- **GET METADATA Operation:** Returns metadata name-value pairs for specified asset.
- **SET METADATA Operation:** Sets metadata name-value pairs for specified asset. Metadata names must exist or error occurs.
- Metadata can be entered directly using the 'Add Metadata' interface by specifying Name and Value fields, or programmatically using the format: { 'name1' =\> \['value1'\], 'name2' =\> \['value2'\] }. For example: { 'test' =\> \['Test-val2'\] }.

### Dependencies

- Sony Ci workspace must be Aspera-enabled for transfer operations.
- Node API Transfer plugin is required for actual file upload/download operations.
- The aspweb user must have read permissions for source files and write permissions for download folders.
- Network connectivity to api.cimediacloud.com on port TCP 443 is required.

### Troubleshooting

- Authentication failures: Verify Sony Ci credentials (username, password, client id, client secret) are correct and active.
- Upload failures: Ensure workspace is Aspera-enabled and source file path is correct relative to docroot.
- Download failures: Check that asset id exists and download folder path is correct relative to docroot.
- Metadata errors: Verify metadata names exist for the asset before attempting SET METADATA operations.
- Transfer spec issues: Ensure Node API Transfer plugin is properly configured with 'Submit Transfer Using Payload' option.
- File size mismatches: Transfers complete even if the specified file size is inaccurate, but avoid setting it to 0 bytes.
- Network connectivity: Test connectivity to api.cimediacloud.com on port 443 if operations fail.

### The Platform

![The Platform icon](icons/ThePlatform.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to create, read, and update thePlatform objects.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.2 | verify ssl false |
| 0.3.1 | Adding help file |
| 0.3.0 | Adding support for Program and Server Objects |
| 0.2.0 | token storage, support for all form types, more configuration options |
| 0.1.8 | Changing the category |
| 0.1.7 | Added styling to help text. |
| 0.1.6 | Added logic to return failed status and propagate exceptions from thePlatform when API calls go wrong. |
| 0.1.5 | Removing code to explicitly encoding query values. RestClient is doing it just before the call |
| 0.1.4 | Added new output for total result count |
| 0.1.3 | Improvements requested by Roger 4-11-2014: Make fields run-time variables, fix for count and pretty which are always true, added new output |
| 0.1.2 | Improvements requested by Roger 4-10-2014 |
| 0.1.1 | Improvements and bug fixes 4-9-2014 |
| 0.1.0 | Added columns needed for media obj create and media obj update |
| 0.0.1 | Initial Release |

#### Description

##### Saved Parameters Description

**Comments**: The comments to identify the plugin template

##### Authentication Parameters Start

\

**Account Name**: Name of the account which contains the data that you are querying. The value can be found on the top right hand side when you are logged in to ThePlatform GUI. Usually Development, Staging and Production accounts are setup

**ThePlatform User Name**: User name used to log into ThePlatform GUI

**ThePlatform Password**: Password used to log into ThePlatform GUI

##### Authentication Parameters End

\

**Object**: Supported Objects to query are Media, MediaFile, Program, Server. Depending on the Object chosen the respective API call is made to ThePlatform

**Schema**: Choosen the schema that supports the latest API calls

**Form Type**: Choosen the format of the response. We recommend JSON

**Store Token**: If checked, Orchestrator will store the API authentication token until it expires instead of generating one every API call. Tokens could be limited and this option could be used to use tokens as needed.

**ThePlatform URL**: The URL to which the API call is made to. Parameterized for special cases. Default URL is https://data.media.theplatform.com/media/data/ which is valid for Media, MediaFile and Server Objects. The URL is usually appended by the object_id. Use http://data.entertainment.tv.theplatform.com/entertainment/data/Program for Program Object

**Authentication URL**: The URL to which the API call is made to for obtaining the token. Defaulted to https://identity.auth.theplatform.com/idm/web/Authentication/

**Account URL**: URL of the account under which the Media Object is stored. Used for Media Object creation, update and delete

**Media Asset**: Name of the Media Asset being created.

**Source URL**: URL for the source file used for creation of new media file within a media object

**Media ID URL**: URL for the media object which needs to be updated with a new media file

**Fields**: Every ThePlatform object contains many fields, including custom fields. Check the fields needed in the response. Not chosing any will result in a response that includes

**Count**: If checked, the API call requests for a count field in the response. Useful for cases like search when only a count is needed.

**Entries**: If checked, the API call requests for all entries. Unchecking will result in the API call being submitted with entries='false'. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.

**Owned**: If checked, the API call will include /owned. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.

**Types**: If checked, the API call is submitted with types='variable'. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.

**Valid feed**: If checked, the API call is submitted with validFeed='true'. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.

**Feed**: If checked, the API call will include /feed. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.

**Pretty**: If checked, the API call requests for a formatted response adhering to the Format chosen above.

**Sort By**: Click 'Add Sort By' to build out the API call so that the response contains resulted sorted either in descending or ascending order of the fields chosen

**Query By**: Click 'Add Query By' to build out the API call so that the response includes only the data that matches with the query parameters and their values.

**Range**: Provide a range to limit or expand the number of entries. For example, enter range as 0 to 4 to obtain the top five results. Range is calculated after the Sort and Query operations.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the thePlatform action template.

- All required parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory for thePlatform API call:**\

- **Account Name**: Name of the account which contains the data that you are querying. The value can be found on the top right hand side when you are logged in to ThePlatform GUI. Usually Development, Staging and Production accounts are setup
- **ThePlatform User Name**: User name used to log into ThePlatform GUI
- **ThePlatform Password**: Password used to log into ThePlatform GUI
- **Object**: Supported Objects to query are Media, MediaFile, Program, Server. Depending on the Object chosen the respective API call is made to ThePlatform
- **Schema**: Choosen the schema that supports the latest API calls
- **Form Type**: Choosen the format of the response. We recommend JSON
- **Operation**: Supports Search operation for all Objects. Creation and Deletion of Media Objects. Creation of Media File Objects inside a Media Object (essentially updating a Media Object).
  \
  **For Media Object creation below inputs are needed:**\
- **Account URL**: URL of the account under which the Media Object is stored. Used for Media Object creation, update and delete
- **Media Asset**: Name of the Media Asset being created.
  \
  **For Media File Object creation below inputs are needed:**\
- **Account URL**: URL of the account under which the Media Object is stored. Used for Media Object creation, update and delete
- **Source URL**: URL for the source file used for creation of new media file within a media object
- **Media ID URL**: URL for the media object which needs to be updated with a new media file

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Store Token**: If checked, Orchestrator will store the API authentication token until it expires instead of generating one every API call. Tokens could be limited and this option could be used to use tokens as needed.
- **ThePlatform URL**: The URL to which the API call is made to. Parameterized for special cases. Default URL is https://data.media.theplatform.com/media/data/ which is valid for Media, MediaFile and Server Objects. The URL is usually appended by the object_id. Use http://data.entertainment.tv.theplatform.com/entertainment/data/Program for Program Object
- **Authentication URL**: The URL to which the API call is made to for obtaining the token. Defaulted to https://identity.auth.theplatform.com/idm/web/Authentication/
- **Fields**: Every ThePlatform object contains many fields, including custom fields. Check the fields needed in the response. Not chosing any will result in a response that includes
- **Count**: If checked, the API call requests for a count field in the response. Useful for cases like search when only a count is needed.
- **Entries**: If checked, the API call requests for all entries. Unchecking will result in the API call being submitted with entries='false'. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.
- **Owned**: If checked, the API call will include /owned. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.
- **Types**: If checked, the API call is submitted with types='variable'. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.
- **Valid feed**: If checked, the API call is submitted with validFeed='true'. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.
- **Feed**: If checked, the API call will include /feed. Check ThePlatform documentation for more information.
- **Pretty**: If checked, the API call requests for a formatted response adhering to the Format chosen above.
- **Sort By**: Click 'Add Sort By' to build out the API call so that the response contains resulted sorted either in descending or ascending order of the fields chosen
- **Query By**: Click 'Add Query By' to build out the API call so that the response includes only the data that matches with the query parameters and their values.
- **Range**: Provide a range to limit or expand the number of entries. For example, enter range as 0 to 4 to obtain the top five results. Range is calculated after the Sort and Query operations.
  \
  **In addition, every Query By value chosen becomes a optional runtime input**\

##### Outputs description

- **QueryResults**: Result of the Search operation. Depending on the Type chosen it is either going to be a Hash or a String.
- **ThePlatform_Token**: A String value representing the API token used to query thePlatform.
- **Is_Token_Released**: Result of the API call to release the API token. It is either true or false.
- **Create_result**: Result of the Create Media Object API call. Depending on the Type chosen it is either going to be a Hash or a String.
- **Media_Id_Url**: Media ID URL obtained as a result of the Media Object creation
- **Delete_result**: Result of the Delete Media Object API call. Depending on the Type chosen it is either going to be a Hash or a String.
- **Update_result**: Result of the Create New Media File object API call (to modify a Media Object). Depending on the Type chosen it is either going to be a Hash or a String.
- **Total_result_count**: Total count of the objects returned.

##### Operating Instructions

This action plug-in is used make API calls to ThePlatform (also known as MPX). The operations supported are:\

- Search for Media Objects, Program Objects, MediaFile Objects, Server Objects
- Create New Media Object
- Create New File Object
- Delete Media Object

\
The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\

### Examples given below

\
\

##### An example of Search

\
a) Provide the Authentication Parameters.\
b) Select Object to Search, Form Type and Schema. Check ThePlatform API documentation for latest schema listings.\
c) Select Operation as Search and click to store the token for optimal use of tokens\
d) Check the Fields desired in the response.\
e) Check the Count, Entries, Feed, Valid Feed, Owned and Pretty falgs if desired.\
f) Add fields to sort the response by.\
g) Add fields to Query by and supply the values. Limit the range or expand it depending on the need.\

##### An example of Create Media Object

\
a) Provide the Authentication Parameters.\
b) Select Object as Media, Form Type and Schema. Check ThePlatform API documentation for latest schema listings.\
c) Select Operation as CreateNewMediaObject and click to store the token for optimal use of tokens\
d) Supply the account url and asset name.\
e) Check Pretty flag to format response.\

##### An example of Create Media File Object

\
a) Provide the Authentication Parameters.\
b) Select Object as MediaFile, Form Type and Schema. Check ThePlatform API documentation for latest schema listings.\
c) Select Operation as CreateNewFileObject and click to store the token for optimal use of tokens\
d) Supply the account url, media id url and soure url.\
e) Check Pretty flag to format response.\
\

### XytechMediaPulse

![XytechMediaPulse icon](icons/XytechMediaPulse.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in provides the ability to query the MediaPulse API and return MediaOrders and corresponding source file(s).

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.3   | Changed the category to asset mgmt.                 |
| 0.0.2   | Removed the check_once input parameter.             |
| 0.0.1   | Plugin release.                                     |

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the XytechMediaPulse action template.

- **Name**: Name of the action step.
- **Comments**: Comments associated to this template or configuration.
- **End point**: The end point URL for the API.
- **Payload file**: The payload file of the operation that is to be performed.

\
**Plugin currently supports Query and Retrieve operations.**\
End point and Payload file are Mandatory inputs. On selecting the payload file, the template shows the\
Mandatory attributes are loaded from the payload file residing in the plugin payload directory.\
Inputs **Database name and its UserId and Password** are mandatory for any operation on the MediaPulse API.\
**Query operation** is achieved by performing query on **date_added** column. The Minimum and Maximum values for date added with return array of all the MediaOrder that were added between that duration.\
**Retrieve operation** is achieved by querying on the database with MediaOrder Id. The plugin code returns the array of all the source files associated to that MediaOrder.

##### Outputs description

- **Status**: The response status can be 'true' is the call is successful and 'false' if it fails.
- **Row_count**: If the API call is successful, row count returns the number of rows that are returned by the API.
- **Response_message**: Message is NULL for successful API calls. For failed calls, Message displays the message sent by API, which typically contains the error that caused the failure.
- **Response_code**: Response code of the API. E.g. 200 for success.
- **MO_list**: Shows array of MO in case of Query request OR array of File(s) in case of Retrieve request.

##### Operating Instructions

In order to perform the API calls, provide all the mandatory inputs.\
Select the payload file that needs to be performed and click refresh link.\
Provide all the mandatory attributes for the operation selected.\
Response is sent back as XML document and is converted to a hash.This hash is stored in the plugin output.\
\

## File Operations

### Archive manager

![Archive manager icon](icons/ArchiveManager.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in can be used to manage a file archive, by deleting or compressing aging entries.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.0 | 2015-04-22 ADM - Added do not delete flag |
| 0.2.5 | 2015-03-02 ML - New icon |
| 0.2.4 | Modified inputs spec to have manifest_dir accept dynamic parameters |
| 0.2.3 | Modified inputs spec to have archive_root and kept_duration accept dynamic parameters |
| 0.2.2 | Added help |
| 0.2.1 | Layout change for kept duration |
| 0.2.0 | Added option to use mtime instead of ctime |
| 0.1.1 | Fixed optional use of runtime archive root |
| 0.1.0 | Made optional the recursive search in the file pattern |
| 0.0.3 | Added support for non-zip compression format |
| 0.0.2 | Added management of the manifest files |
| 0.0.1 | Created based on Trim Archive custom ruby code |

#### How It Works

- This action manages file purging or archiving from a local folder on the orchestrator server.
- The folder can be on a local disk or on a network-mounted storage.
- If no archive path is specified, files older than the specified age will be deleted.
- This action is not a trigger, the archiving is performed only once. To make the archiving periodic, you should link an Archive manager step with a Scheduled Trigger step.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  This action manages file purging or archiving from a local folder on the orchestrator server.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The base path for the files to archive. Supports GLOB syntax.
    - : The pattern used to select archive source files.
    - : Allows you to specify if the selection has to descend into subfolders.
    - : Allows to specify after how long a file is moved to the archive.
    - : If set, uses mtime instead of ctime to determine the keep duration.
    - : If set, the archiving result is computed but no action is taken on the file systems for the real files.
    - : If set, moves all matching files to an archive.
    - : The name to use for the archive file (if specified). Default is the name of the directory if the entity being compressed is a directory or archive otherwise. Note: '.zip' will be appended as a suffix.
    - : The format for the archive file (if selected). Optional - default is 'zip'
    - : Optional - If specified, manifest files of the deletions will be created in the provided directory. By default no manifest file is generated.
    - : Optional - If a manifest directory is specified, it allows to specify the manifest retention period. 0 for unlimited.

#### Example Configuration

This is a minimum setup configuration for the plugin.

| Parameter                                                 | Value     |
|-----------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| Archive root                                              | /home     |
| Purge file regexp pattern                                 | .\*.log\$ |
| Pattern matching descends in sub-directories              | Selected  |
| Make a compressed copy of the files (zip) before deleting | Selected  |
| Keep duration                                             | 5 seconds |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Archive root** | The base path for the files to archive. Supports GLOB syntax. |
| **Purge file regexp pattern** | The pattern used to select archive source files. |
| **Pattern matching descends in sub-directories** | Allows you to specify if the selection has to descend into subfolders. |
| **Keep duration** | Allows to specify after how long a file is moved to the archive. |
| **Use mtime** | If set, uses mtime instead of ctime to determine the keep duration. |
| **Do not delete files** | If set, the archiving result is computed but no action is taken on the file systems for the real files. |
| **Make a compressed copy of the files (zip) before deleting** | If set, moves all matching files to an archive. |
| **Compressed copy archive name** | The name to use for the archive file (if specified). Default is the name of the directory if the entity being compressed is a directory or archive otherwise. Note: '.zip' will be appended as a suffix. |
| **Compression format** | The format for the archive file (if selected). Optional - default is 'zip' |
| **Purge manifest directory** | Optional - If specified, manifest files of the deletions will be created in the provided directory. By default no manifest file is generated. |
| **Manifest retention duration** | Optional - If a manifest directory is specified, it allows to specify the manifest retention period. 0 for unlimited. |

#### Inputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Archive_root (mandatory)* | The base path for the files to archive. Supports GLOB syntax. |
| *Compressed_name* | The name to use for the archive file (if specified). Default is the name of the directory if the entity being compressed is a directory or archive otherwise. |
| *Kept_duration (mandatory)* | Allows you to specify after how long a file is moved to the archive. |
| *Kept_duration_unit* | Defines the time unit for Kept duration, such as seconds, minutes, or hours. |
| *Manifest_directory* | Allows specification of the directory to place the manifest |
| *Purge_file_pattern* | Allows to specify if the selection has to descend into sub folders. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter      | Description                                 |
|----------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Files_purged   | The list of purged (archived) files.        |
| Purge_manifest | The full path of the manifest file, if any. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

- Configure the Archive root path to specify the directory containing files to manage.
- Define the Purge file regexp pattern to match the files you want to archive or delete.
- Set the Keep duration to specify how long files should remain before archiving/deletion.
- Optionally enable 'Make a compressed copy' to create a zip archive before deletion, or leave it disabled to simply delete files.
- For periodic archiving, combine this action with a Scheduled Trigger in your workflow.
- Use 'Do not delete files' option to perform a dry run and verify which files would be affected without making actual changes.
- Ensure that the **aspweb** user has the necessary permissions to access and modify files in the specified directory.

### Aspera Node API

![Aspera Node API icon](icons/AsperaNodeApi.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to perform file system operations such as search, rename, and delete. It also supports retrieving node information (e.g., token, SSH user, and port) and checking node availability through status queries (ping).

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.3 | Returns failure if rename operation is unsuccessful |
| 0.1.2 | Support the form hostname:port in node address |
| 0.1.1 | fix search criteria analysis |
| 0.1.0 | fixed reading mapped inputs for server address |
| 0.0.9 | Added styling to help text. |
| 0.0.8 | RemoteNode Dependency |
| 0.0.7 | Uses double-entered password |
| 0.0.6 | Adding help file. Hiding polling frequency field from the view, as it is not used in the plugin yet. |
| 0.0.5 | Defaults port to 9092 is use_SSL is checked |
| 0.0.4 | Fixes delete operation |
| 0.0.3 | exception handling of RemoteNode.find_by_name() |
| 0.0.2 | Moved to file operation category |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release. Supports ping, info, search, rename, delete |

### How It Works

- Connects to a remote Aspera Node using the Aspera Node API over HTTPS (port 9092) or HTTP (port 9091, if enabled).
- Supports operations including Ping, Info, Search, Rename, and Delete on the remote node.
- Authenticates with a configured Node API username and password.
- Allows advanced search and filtering options for file operations.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **AsperaNodeApi** plugin.
2.  Fill out these fields with appropriate values:
    - **Name**: A label for the action step.
    - **Comments**: Optional notes or description.
    - **Aspera node**: Select a remote node or enter IP/domain manually.
    - **Aspera node IP address or hostname**: Remote node IP or domain name.
    - **Node api port**: Port for Node API (default 9092 for HTTPS).
    - **Node user name**: Node API username.
    - **Node password**: Password for Node API access.
    - **Use ssl?**: Check to use HTTPS (default 9092), otherwise HTTP on port 9091 if enabled.
    - **Operation**: Choose from Ping, Info, Search, Rename, Delete.
    - **Source**: For Search - a folder path; for Rename - one or more comma-separated file paths.
    - **Target**: For Rename - target filename or regex pattern.
    - **Overwrite during rename**: Enable to overwrite existing files during rename.
    - **Search Parameter - Filter file type**: Enter one or more of 'file', 'directory', 'symbolic link' as comma-separated values. If provided, the result set will include only those file types.
    - **Search Parameter - Filter basename**: Enter one or more values comma-separated. If provided, the result set will include only files that match the file base name.
    - **Search Parameter - Max count**: Default is 1000000. Total number of files to return after a search operation.
    - **Search Parameter - Minimum size**: Enter an integer value (bytes) to search for files whose size is more than the value provided.
    - **Search Parameter - Filter mtime min**: Filter for files modified after a specific time, in the format '2011-01-31T12:59:59Z' (T or space in the middle; Z optional at the end).
    - **Search Parameter - Filter mtime max**: Filter for files modified before a specific time, in the format '2011-01-31T12:59:59Z' (T or space in the middle; Z optional at the end).

### Example Configuration

| Field | Value |
|----|----|
| Name | `New AsperaNodeApi Step` |
| Comments | Retrieve info from remote Aspera node |
| Aspera node | `-Select Node-` |
| Aspera node IP address | `aspera-node.example.com` |
| Node api port | `9092` |
| Node user name | `node_watchfolder` |
| Node password | `<password>` |
| Operation | `Info` |
| Use ssl? | Checked |
| Source | (e.g., `/tmp/` for Search, or comma-separated paths for Rename) |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Label identifying this action step. |
| **Comments** | Optional descriptive notes. |
| **Aspera node** | Remote node selected or specified. |
| **Aspera node IP address** | IP or domain for the remote node. |
| **Node api port** | Port for Node API; 9092 default for HTTPS. |
| **Node user name** | Node API username, distinct from OS user. |
| **Node password** | Password for Node API user access. |
| **Use ssl?** | Enables HTTPS on the Node API endpoint. |
| **Operation** | API operation to perform (Ping, Info, etc.). |
| **Source** | Path(s) for Search or Rename operations. |
| **Target** | Rename destination or regex pattern. |
| **Overwrite during rename** | Overwrite files on rename if checked. |
| **Search Parameters** | Filters limiting search results by type, size, and date. |

### Inputs Description

- **Aspera_Node_Server_Address**: Remote node name, IP address, domain, or address:port.
- **node_user_name**: Node API user name.
- **node_password**: Node API password.
- **filter_basename**: Filters files matching these base names.
- **filter_file_type**: Filters by file type (file, directory, symbolic link).
- **filter_mtime_min**: Minimum file modification date/time.
- **filter_mtime_max**: Maximum file modification date/time.
- **max_count**: Max number of files to retrieve.
- **use_ssl**: Use HTTPS (default 9092) if checked, otherwise HTTP on port 9091.

### Outputs Description

|  |  |
|----|----|
| Output | Description |
| **NODE_API_RESULTS** | Results as a hash/map: info, search results, rename/delete status. |
| **Step_information** | Execution metadata including operation, completion status, and timing. |

### Supported Actions

Ping, Info, Search, Rename, and Delete.

### Dependencies

Remote nodes must run Aspera High Speed Transfer Server or Endpoint (Linux/Windows) with the asperanoded service and valid license.

### Node API Usage Overview

This action can:

1.  Ping: Check if the remote node responds to Node API requests.
2.  Info: Retrieve primary information about the remote node.
3.  Search: Detect files or folders matching patterns under watch directories. Supports globbing and multiple patterns (comma-separated).
4.  Rename: Rename source files to specified targets or using regex patterns.
5.  Delete: Delete specified files.

Globbing pattern examples:

- `*.txt`: Matches all files with a .txt extension.
- `foo*/\*.txt`: Matches files under directories starting with "foo".
- `foo/*/bar/*.txt`: Matches files under foo/bar subdirectories.
- `fo?`: Matches "foo" or "fo1", but not "foo1".
- `fo[1-9]`: Matches "fo1", "fo2", etc., but not "foo1".

Note: All paths are relative to the transfer user’s docroot defined in `/opt/aspera/etc/aspera.conf` on the remote server.

Example excerpt from `aspera.conf`:

      <user>
        <name>xfer</name>
        <file_system>
          <access>
            <paths>
              <path>
                <absolute>/home/xfer/data</absolute>
              </path>
            </paths>
          </access>
        </file_system>
      </user>

To enable both HTTP (port 9091) and HTTPS (port 9092), ensure `aspera.conf` contains:

      <server>
        <server_name>example.aspera.com</server_name>
        <http_port>9091</http_port>
        <https_port>9092</https_port>
        <enable_http>true</enable_http>
        <enable_https>true</enable_https>
      </server>

Restart the `asperanoded` service as root after changes:

      service asperanoded restart

For more detailed API documentation, see the <a href="https://developer.ibm.com/api/view/aspera-prod:ibm-aspera:title-IBM_Aspera#Overview" target="_blank">Aspera Developer Network</a>.

### Path conversion

![Path conversion icon](icons/ConvertPath.png){.plugin-icon}

Converts an absolute file path from one mount point to another based on configured input and output mappings. Supports one-to-one and multi-mount point conversions.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.2 | 2015-02-17 ML - New icon |
| 0.1.1 | Adds file path breakdown in outputs, changed error status to error |
| 0.1.0 | Adds option to run on an array of file path |
| 0.0.4 | Fixed problem with multiple network paths |
| 0.0.3 | Adds display name |
| 0.0.2 | support for multiple mount points |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

### How It Works

- This plugin converts an absolute file path from one file system to another.
- If the same file is exposed via two different mount points, the plugin infers the corresponding path from the second mount point.
- If no match is found in the input mount points, the original path is returned.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **ConvertPath** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - **Name**: The label to identify this ConvertPath step.
    - **input_mount_point** \*: The absolute path of the mount point to be matched for substitution.
    - **output_mount_point** \*: The absolute path of the target mount point used in the substitution.
    - **file_path** \*: The absolute file path to convert.

### Example Configuration

**Example 1:** Two hosts mount the same shared storage using different names:

- Host1 mounts it as `/mnt/external`
- Host2 mounts it as `d:\`

If a file `file1.dat` exists at `/mnt/external/file1.dat` on Host1, set:

- **input_mount_point**: `/mnt/external`
- **output_mount_point**: `d:\`

The plugin converts the path to: `d:\file1.dat`

**Example 2:** Hosts use multiple mount mappings:

- Host1: `/mnt/external` (storage1), `/mnt/ext_storage` (storage2)
- Host2: `/ext/storage1` (storage1), `/ext/storage2` (storage2)

Configure:

- **input_mount_point**: `["/mnt/external", "/mnt/ext_storage"]`
- **output_mount_point**: `["/ext/storage1", "/ext/storage2"]`

If file path is `/mnt/ext_storage/file1.dat`, output will be `/ext/storage2/file1.dat`

If no input mount point matches, the output is the original `file_path`.

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved ConvertPath configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Some comments about this saved ConvertPath configured instance. |
| **input_mount_point** | The absolute path of the mount point to be matched for the substitution. |
| **output_mount_point** | The absolute path of the target mount point to use in the substitution. |
| **file_path** | The absolute file path to convert. |

### Inputs Description

Inputs depend on the ConvertPath configuration. All values are mandatory and can be provided in the template or at runtime.

### Outputs Description

| Output               | Description                       |
|----------------------|-----------------------------------|
| **output_file_path** | The converted absolute file path. |

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

None

### Operating Instructions

This plugin is useful when the same storage is mounted in a different way across hosts. It helps to infer the correct path on the target system based on mappings.

- Use it when transferring files between systems with different mount point names.
- Simplifies cross-host file references in shared storage setups.

### Dependency checker

![Dependency checker icon](icons/DependencyChecker.png){.plugin-icon}

The DependencyChecker plug-in monitors the presence of specific files in a defined location and allows workflows to pause until those files are detected. This ensures that dependent data is available before proceeding to the next step.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.1.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.6 | Allow to cancel step |
| 0.0.5 | Fixes stability period |
| 0.0.4 | Added Pending files output |
| 0.0.3 | Fixes problem with stability detection when file is not available at first check |
| 0.0.2 | fix UI labels, add widtget for list of files, fix no-stability check option |
| 0.0.1 | Initial implementation |

### How It Works

- Checks for the presence of one or more files.
- If all specified files are found and stable, the step completes successfully.
- If not found within the specified timeout period, the step fails.
- If timeout is `0`, it waits indefinitely.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **DependencyChecker** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - **Name**: A label for the step (e.g., `Wait for Files`).
    - **Dependency Paths** \*: Enter one file name per field. Use the **Add Dependency Paths** button to add more. (e.g., `test_dependency.txt`, `test_dependency2.txt`)
    - **Polling Frequency**: How often (in seconds) to check for files (e.g., `5`).
    - **Stability Period**: Time (in seconds) files must remain unchanged (e.g., `10`).
    - **Base Path** (optional): Directory path prefix for files (e.g., `/tmp`).
    - **Detect Partial Files**: If checked, considers a file found as soon as it appears.
    - **Timeout**: Total time (in seconds) to wait before failing (e.g., `60`). Use `0` to wait forever.
3.  Click **Update** to save the step.

### Example Configuration

| Field                | Value                     |
|----------------------|---------------------------|
| Base Path            | `/tmp` (if any)           |
| Dependency Paths     | `test_dependency2.txt`    |
| Polling Frequency    | `10`(default)             |
| Stability Period     | `10`(default)             |
| Timeout              | `0` (waits indefinitely ) |
| Detect Partial Files | Unchecked                 |

**Behavior:** The plugin will monitor `/tmp/test_dependency2.txt` and complete the step once it appears and remains stable for 10 seconds.

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Label to identify this DependencyChecker step |
| **Dependency Paths** | List of file names to monitor (comma-separated) |
| **Polling Frequency** | Time interval (in seconds) to check for files |
| **Stability Period** | Duration a file must remain unchanged to be valid |
| **Base Path** | Optional base directory path for dependency files |
| **Detect Partial Files** | Checks file availability immediately if enabled |
| **Timeout** | Maximum wait time before failure (0 = wait forever) |

### Inputs Description

Inputs are derived from the step configuration. Most important input:

- **dependency_file_paths**: Full file paths being monitored (e.g., `/tmp/test_dependency2.txt`)

### Outputs Description

| Output                    | Description                       |
|---------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| **Dependency file paths** | Array of successfully found files |

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

None

### Operating Instructions

This plugin is ideal for workflows that must pause and wait until required input files are available. Common scenarios include:

- Waiting for files uploaded by external systems
- Triggering jobs only after prior steps generate required output files

### Troubleshooting

- Ensure the file names and base path are correct.
- Verify that files appear in the expected location.
- Adjust stability period if files change too frequently.
- Set an appropriate timeout value to avoid indefinite waiting.

### Diva archive

![Diva archive icon](icons/DivaArchive.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to archive or restore files to/from the DIVArchive system. It implements the DIVA REST WEB services API 1.0, 2.1 and 2.2

| Version | Comment                                                   |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.4.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8       |
| 0.3.2   | Adding support for dynamic parameters in Object Category. |
| 0.3.1   | Set default priority to -1                                |
| 0.3.0   | Add support for basic auth                                |
| 0.2.1   | Add support for Diva version 2.2                          |
| 0.2.0   | Add Diva version                                          |
| 0.1.0   | Add hash output                                           |
| 0.0.9   | Add support for getObjectInfo operation                   |
| 0.0.8   | Run time support for polling frequency.                   |
| 0.0.7   | Added styling to parameter help text.                     |
| 0.0.6   | Adding Remote Node dependency                             |
| 0.0.5   | Runtime parameter option to server_address and port       |
| 0.0.4   | 2015-04-29 CT - Allow remote node in server address input |
| 0.0.3   | 2015-02-17 ML - New icon                                  |
| 0.0.2   | added handling of avid mobid                              |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                           |

#### Description

##### Saved Parameters Description

**Name**: The name used to identify a saved DivaArchive configured instance.

**Comments**: Some comments about this saved DivaArchive configured instance.

**Diva Node**: The Diva node name from defined Remote Nodes.

**Diva server IP address**: The Diva node IP address or domain name.

**Diva WEB service port**: To change the default Diva WEB service port.

**Basic auth login**: HTTP basic authentication login. Only for the 2.2 API.

**Basic auth password**: HTTP basic authentication password. Only for the 2.2 API.

**Diva version**: The Diva WEB services API version.

**Operation**: The operation to execute.

**Object name**: Name of the object.

**Object category**: Category of the object.

**Source/Dest**: Source for an archive or destination for a restore (e.g. disk, video server, browsing server). It must be defined in DIVA configuration.

**Media name**: Designate a Group of Tapes or an Array of Disks defined in the DIVA configuration where the instance has to be created. (e.g. STORAGE-ON-DISK). Only for the archive operation.

**File path root**: Root folder for the files to archive/restore. Required for the archive operation, optional for restore

**Filename list**: List of filenames to archive (string with filenames separated by commas if several of them - see examples below). Only for the archive operation.

**Quality of service**: Quality of service (value between 0 and 6, see below).

**Priority**: Level of priority for the operation. (value between 0 and 100, 0 is the lowest priority and 100 the highest, default -1).

**Options**: Additional options for archive/restore.

**Polling frequency**: Interval in seconds to search for matching files in the watch directory.

\
partialRestore specific parameters

**Instance ID**: The ID of a non-spanned Tape Instance, or -1 = DIVA_ANY_INSTANCE.

**Format**: Value of the video format. See below for the values.

**Parameters**: List of source file name, destination file name, offset

**offset**: byte begin, byte end, time code begin, time code end (between 00:00:00:00 and 99:99:99:99), type of offset (0=OFFSET_FORMAT_BYTE, 1=OFFSET_FORMAT_TIMECODE). Use format 0 for offset type 0.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the DivaArchive action template.

Changes in this list of inputs will override any values set in the action template.\
\

- **server address**: Remote node name or remote server IP address or remote server domain name
- **Basic auth login**: HTTP basic authentication login. Only for the 2.2 API.
- **Basic auth password**: HTTP basic authentication password. Only for the 2.2 API.
- **object name**: Name of the object to archive/restore
- **object category**: Category of the object. Optional for the restore, delete and getObjectInfo operations.
- **source / dest**: Source for an archive or destination for a restore. It must be defined in DIVA configuration.
- **media name**: Designate a Group of Tapes or an Array of Disks defined in the DIVA configuration where the instance has to be created. Only for the archive operation.
- **filepath root**: Root folder for the files to archive/restore. Required for the archive operation, optional for restore.
- **filename list**: List of files to archive expressed as a string with filenames separated by commas such as JTM-140803-195.mxf, IMI_SONY test.MXF or 'JTM-140803-195.mxf', "IMI_SONY test.MXF" Only for the archive operation.
- **format**: Value of the video format. Only for the partial restore operation.
- **partial restore parameters**: List of source file name, destination file name, offset expressed as a string with content such as \[\["IMI_SONY test.MXF", "\_IMI_SONY test.MXF", "", "", "00:00:00:00", "99:99:99:99", "1"\], \["JTM-140803-195.mxf", "\_JTM-140803-195.mxf", "", "", "00:00:00:00", "99:99:99:99", "1"\]\]. Use double-quotes, not single-quotes.

\

##### Outputs description

- **XML_result**: The Diva output in XML format
- **HASH_result**: The Diva output in Ruby hash format
- **Diva_status_message**: The Diva status message
- **Diva_request_status_message**: The Diva request status message. It comes from getRequestInfo() and is checked to make sure the request is successful.
- **Avid mob id**: AVID MOB ID if returned by the getRequestInfo operation within the additionalInfo section.

##### Operating Instructions

Specify the Diva WEB services API version. The supported versions are 1.0 for DIVArchive 6.5, 2.1 for DIVArchive 7.1 and 2.2 for for DIVArchive 7.5.\

Only the object name is required for the delete and getObjectInfo operations.

Basic authentication is required for all operations in the 2.2 version.

The available values for the quality of service are:\
0 DIVA_QOS_DEFAULT Restoring is performed according to the default Quality Of Service (currently: direct and cache for restore operations).\
1 DIVA_QOS_CACHE_ONLY Use Cache Restore only.\
2 DIVA_QOS_DIRECT_ONLY Use Direct Restore only.\
3 DIVA_QOS_DIRECT_AND_CACHE Use Direct Restore if available or Cache Restore if Direct Restore is not available.\
4 DIVA_QOS_CACHE_AND_DIRECT Use Cache Restore if available or Direct Restore if Cache Restore is not available.\
5 DIVA_QOS_NEARLINE_ONLY Use Nearline only.\
6 DIVA_QOS_NEARLINE_AND_DIRECT Use Nearline if available or Direct if Nearline is not available.\

The available values for the video format are:\
0 DIVA_FORMAT_BYTES Raw byte partial restore\
1 DIVA_FORMAT_BYTES_HEADER Deprecated feature\
2 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_GXF GXF video format of PDR video servers\
3 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_SEA Seachange video format\
4 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_AVI_MATROX Matrox-specific AVI format (+ WAV files). Restrictions apply, see documentation.\
5 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_MPEG2_TS MPEG2 Transport Stream.\
6 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_MXF MXF video format\
7 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_PINNACLE Pinnacle video format\
8 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_OMNEON Omneon video format\
9 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_LEITCH Leitch video format\
10 DIVA_FORMAT_VIDEO_QUANTEL Quantel QCP video format\
11 DIVA_FORMAT_AUTODETECT Automatic detection of input format\
12 DIVA_FORMAT_FOLDER_BASED Fully restore the specified folders\
13 DIVA_FORMAT_DPX DPX video format

If the request is aborted (Diva_request_status_message = DIVA_ABORTED), the reason could be:\
0 DIVA_AR_NONE Request not aborted\
1 DIVA_AR_DRIVE Drive troubles\
2 DIVA_AR_TAPE Tape troubles\
3 DIVA_AR_ACTOR Actor troubles\
4 DIVA_AR_DISK Disk troubles\
5 DIVA_AR_DISK_FULL Disk is full\
6 DIVA_AR_SOURCE_DEST Source/destination troubles\
7 DIVA_AR_RESOURCES Resources attribution troubles\
8 DIVA_AR_LIBRARY Library troubles\
9 DIVA_AR_PARAMETERS Wrong request parameters\
10 DIVA_AR_UNKNOWN Unknown code\
11 DIVA_AR_INTERNAL Internal DIVArchive WS error\

### Exiftool xmp tagger

![Exiftool xmp tagger icon](icons/ExiftoolXmpTagger.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in can be used to retrieve metadate information about an image, video or audio file using the exiftool application. Also supported is the ability to insert metadata tag in image file (but not videos).

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.1.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.4 | 2016-04-11 JG Adding dependency for execution node |
| 0.0.3 | 2015-02-16 ML - New icon |
| 0.0.2 | 2014-12-15 ML - Disable auto-complete and accept tags as hash, make options optional |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved ExiftoolXmpTagger configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved ExiftoolXmpTagger configured instance.

- **Media file path**: The source file for the analysis operation.

- **Tagging operation**: The plugins can execute either to read or to write tags. In the case of write, 2 different operations are supported. One modifies the source file by setting the metadata as instructure. The other leaves the source file untouched, but create a copy of it in whihc the metadata is set as instructed.

- **Tagged media file path**: (only in mode Insert and copy) The full path to the output file consisting of a copy of the source file with metadata tags set as instructed.

- **Tags to insert**: (not in mode Read) The set of tags to be set. For each tag, a pair of field is provided, the first identifies the tag, the second is the value to set that tag to. Dynamic attributes are supported, so a value can be an input variable (or a combination).

- **Tags to read**: (only in mode Read) The set of tags to extract as named, singled-out outputs.

- **Execution Node**: The node where the analysis operation must be executed (i.e. the node where the ExifTool application is installed).

- **Execution Login**: The user account used to execute the ExifTool analysis operation. If not specified the default user accound for the node will be used.

- **Execution Password**: The password used to log in into the remote node. If not specified the default password for the node will be used.

- **Path to the mediainfo binary**: The path to locate the exiftool binary in the remote node. Not needed if the binary folder for ExifTool is in the sytem path for the account use to remote connect on the specified server.

- **Any optional parameters to pass to the ExifTool process**: Any optional parameters supported by the exiftool binary. Those options will be passed on the command line as they are.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the ExiftoolXmpTagger action template.

- **Source file path**: All the variables listed in Source file path field are mandatory and must be of type string.
- **Options**: All the variables listed in the optional parameters to pass to the exiftool process are mandatory and must be of type string.
- **Dynamic variables**: All the variables listed in the 'tags to insert' or 'tags to read sections'

\

##### Outputs description

- **Tagged_media_file**: The full path to the file being tagged. Points to the copied file in the case of Insert and copy and to the source file otherwise.
- **Metadata**: (only in mode Read) A hash containing all the metadata tags found for that file with their values. For example: { 'Epidose Title' =\> 'Morning light', 'Duration' =\> '2435s', 'Season' =\> '3' }
- **Tags read**: (only in mode Read) The specified singled-out tags, with their value returned as a string

##### Operating Instructions

This action executes the specified metadata tagging operation on a remote node where ExifTool is available. The connection to the remote server is done over SSH, so make sure that the target server has SSH enabled to it. If the server is a Windows machine, OpenSSH can be installed.

It is typically a good idea to provide the explicit path to the exiftool binary to avoid configuration issues. On a Unix-flavored machine, running '**which exiftool**' should provide the path information.

Important Note: this action plugins does not do any file movement. Hence, for it to run a file analysis, the specified file has to be accessible. Either the file is transferred to that server or it must be accessible from that server either on some type of network share.

Testing has been done against an install of ExifTool v9.08 and it is to be noted that tag insertion is only supported for images and documents at this point. For video files, only read is supported. Also, the insertion of tags only works for standard tags or for tags that are declared in the .ExifTool_config file, which is located in the executing user home directory. If that file does not exist, one can be created using the sample file provided with the plugin.

### FFProbe Information

![FFProbe Information icon](icons/FfprobeInfo.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to submit a file to FFProbe to grab media properties and technical Metadata

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.3   | Correcting the plugin_name in metadata.yml          |
| 0.0.2   | -\> Adding Remote Node Dependency                   |
| 0.0.1   | -\> Initial release                                 |

#### How It Works

- Executes **FFProbe** (FFmpeg's multimedia stream analysis tool) on a remote node via SSH.
- Analyzes audio and video files to extract **metadata, codec information, stream details, and technical properties**.
- Connects to a configured **remote node** via SSH to execute FFProbe commands.
- Generates a detailed **report file** with FFProbe output on the remote node.
- Returns both the **report file path** and **report content** as outputs.
- Supports custom **FFProbe command options** for flexible analysis configuration.
- Requires **FFProbe to be installed** on the remote node.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  This action allows to execute an FFProbe task on a remote node.
2.  Create a new action step using the **FfprobeInfo** plugin.
3.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved FFProbe Info configured instance.
    - : Comments associated to this template or configuration.
    - : The remote node to execute the FFProbe operation on. Select from the list of already configured Remote Nodes.
    - : The path of the ffprobe executable on the remote node (e.g., /usr/bin/ffprobe or /opt/ffmpeg/bin/ffprobe).
    - : The path of the input file to analyze with FFProbe on the remote node.
    - : The path on the remote node where the report file with the FFProbe output will be saved.
    - : Any FFProbe options or parameters. This string will be added to the FFProbe command BEFORE the input file path (e.g., -show_format -show_streams -print_format json).

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the FfprobeInfo plugin to analyze a video file and output JSON format.

| Parameter           | Value                                         |
|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Name                | Analyze Video File                            |
| Comments            | Extract metadata from video files             |
| Execution node      | MediaServer01                                 |
| FFProbe Binary path | /usr/bin/ffprobe                              |
| Input file path     | /media/incoming/video.mp4                     |
| Report file path    | /media/reports/video_info.json                |
| Command options     | -show_format -show_streams -print_format json |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved FFProbe Info configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments associated to this template or configuration. |
| **Execution node** | The remote node to execute the FFProbe operation on. Select from the list of already configured Remote Nodes. |
| **FFProbe Binary path** | The path of the ffprobe executable on the remote node (e.g., /usr/bin/ffprobe or /opt/ffmpeg/bin/ffprobe). |
| **Input file path** | The path of the input file to analyze with FFProbe on the remote node. |
| **Report file path** | The path on the remote node where the report file with the FFProbe output will be saved. |
| **Command options** | Any FFProbe options or parameters. This string will be added to the FFProbe command BEFORE the input file path (e.g., -show_format -show_streams -print_format json). |

#### Inputs Description

The run time inputs of this action depend on how the configuration is done. Variables exposed in the command line will be exposed as mandatory inputs.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Execution_node* | The remote node identifier (optional if configured in template). Must be a configured Remote Node with SSH access. |
| *FFProbe_Binary_path* | The full path to the ffprobe executable on the remote node (optional if configured in template). |
| *Input_file_path* | The full path to the input media file on the remote node (optional if configured in template). |
| *Report_file_path* | The path where the FFProbe report will be saved on the remote node (optional if configured in template). |
| *Command_options* | FFProbe command line options and parameters (optional if configured in template). |
| *Dynamic variables* | Any variables used in the command options will be exposed as mandatory runtime inputs. |

#### Outputs Description

This action executes an FFProbe analysis action on a remote node, via SSH.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Report_File_Path | The path of the output file on local Orchestrator server, with the output of FFProbe command |
| Report | A report containing the complete output of the FFProbe command execution |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

FFProbe needs to be installed on the remote node. FFProbe is part of the FFmpeg project and can be installed via package managers or compiled from source.

#### Operating Instructions

This action plug-in provides the ability to submit a command to FFProbe tool. FFProbe command will be executed on a remote node via an SSH connection.

**Command Line Construction\**

- The FFProbe command line is constructed in the following order:
- `<FFProbe Binary path> <Command options> <Input file path>`
- **Example:**\
  `/usr/bin/ffprobe -show_format -show_streams -print_format json /media/input/video.mp4`
- The command is executed via SSH on the remote node specified in the configuration.

**Common FFProbe Options\**

- **Output Format Options:**
  - `-print_format json` - Output in JSON format (recommended for parsing)
  - `-print_format xml` - Output in XML format
  - `-print_format csv` - Output in CSV format
  - `-print_format flat` - Output in flat key=value format
- **Information Display Options:**
  - `-show_format` - Show container format information (duration, bitrate, size)
  - `-show_streams` - Show stream information (codec, resolution, framerate)
  - `-show_chapters` - Show chapter information
  - `-show_packets` - Show packet-level information
  - `-show_frames` - Show frame-level information
- **Stream Selection:**
  - `-select_streams v:0` - Select first video stream only
  - `-select_streams a:0` - Select first audio stream only
  - `-select_streams v` - Select all video streams
  - `-select_streams a` - Select all audio streams
- **Verbosity Control:**
  - `-v quiet` - Suppress FFProbe logging output
  - `-v error` - Show only errors
  - `-v info` - Show informational messages (default)
- **Common Combinations:**
  - `-show_format -show_streams -print_format json` - Complete media info in JSON
  - `-show_format -show_streams -v quiet -print_format json` - Clean JSON output
  - `-select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=width,height,codec_name -print_format json` - Video dimensions and codec only

**Common Use Cases\**

- **Media file validation:** Verify codec compatibility, resolution, and format before processing.
- **Quality control:** Extract technical metadata to ensure media meets quality standards.
- **Asset management:** Catalog media files with detailed technical properties.
- **Transcoding preparation:** Analyze source files to determine optimal transcoding parameters.
- **Compliance checking:** Verify media meets technical specifications for broadcast or delivery.
- **Debugging:** Troubleshoot playback or processing issues by examining stream details.
- **Metadata extraction:** Extract embedded metadata, timecodes, and technical information.

**Best Practices\**

- Use **JSON output format** (`-print_format json`) for easier parsing and processing in downstream steps.
- Add **-v quiet** to suppress FFProbe logging and get clean output.
- Specify **absolute paths** for FFProbe binary, input files, and report files.
- Ensure the **report directory exists** on the remote node before execution.
- Use **-select_streams** to limit analysis to specific streams and improve performance.
- Test FFProbe commands **manually via SSH** before configuring in Orchestrator.
- Configure **appropriate timeouts** for large media files that take longer to analyze.
- Store report files with **descriptive names** (include timestamp or file ID).
- Implement **error handling** for missing files, permission issues, or FFProbe failures.
- For sensitive data, ensure **secure SSH connections** and proper file permissions.

**Troubleshooting\**

- **FFProbe not found:** Verify FFProbe is installed and the binary path is correct. Run `which ffprobe` on the remote node.
- **Permission denied:** Ensure SSH user has execute permissions on the FFProbe binary and read permissions on the input file.
- **Input file not found:** Verify the file path is correct as seen from the SSH session on the remote node.
- **Report write failure:** Ensure the report directory exists and the SSH user has write permissions.
- **SSH connection failure:** Verify Remote Node configuration and SSH connectivity.
- **Invalid command options:** Test the FFProbe command manually via SSH before configuring it in Orchestrator.
- **Empty or corrupted report:** Check FFProbe version compatibility and verify the input file is a valid media file.
- **Timeout errors:** Increase timeout settings for large media files that take longer to analyze.

### File archiving operation

![File archiving operation icon](icons/FileArchivingOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides the ability to execute file archiving operations on both Orchestrator locally-mounted file systems and remote Linux systems via SSH.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.4.0 | Removing Windows support code, Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.1 | Plugin includes remote node dependency on workflow export |
| 0.3.0 | Allow plugin to perform operations on remote nodes. |
| 0.2.4 | Fix compatibility issue on OS_untar with ruby 2.3 and command line options with multiple |
| 0.2.3 | Remove duplicate permissions and cmd line options |
| 0.2.2 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.2.1 | Bug fix for os_untar - Only fail when the error code is explicitly non-zero. Exposing more outputs which are useful for debugging errors/failures |
| 0.2.0 | Adding new field to allow customers to add flags to OS commands |
| 0.1.1 | Fixing issue where OS operations failures did not translate into step errors |
| 0.1.0 | Adding new field to support CHMOD and CHOWN on extracted files |
| 0.0.4 | 2015/05/25 - PM -\> Bug fix in inputs_spec, adding unzip to when statement |
| 0.0.3 | 2015/03/04 - JG -\> Enhancement to all functionalities and fixed bugs. |
| 0.0.2 | 2015/01/20 - JG -\> Fixed layouts and exception scenarios. |

### How It Works

- Provides comprehensive file archiving operations including tar, untar, tgz, untgz, zip, and unzip with both Ruby library and OS-based command line implementations.
- Supports both local execution and remote operations on Linux systems via SSH connections with configurable node selection options.
- Handles single files, directories, and arrays with glob pattern support for flexible source selection and recursive directory processing.
- Includes base directory functionality for simplified path management and target directory creation with configurable failure handling.
- Offers multiple node configuration methods including global remote nodes, runtime selection, and manual advanced parameter specification.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`File Archiving Operation`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the execution environment:`
    - **`Use remote node?`**`: Check to enable remote execution (Linux only).`
    - **`Remote node`**`: Select from global nodes, runtime, or manual configuration.`
    - **`File operation`**`: Choose archiving operation (tar, untar, zip, unzip, tgz, untgz).`
3.  `Set up file paths:`
    - **`Sources`**`: Absolute paths to files or directories to archive.`
    - **`Target`**`: Absolute path for the archive destination.`
    - **`Base`**`: Optional base directory for path simplification.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                  | Example Value        |
|----------------------------|----------------------|
| **Use remote node?**       | unchecked            |
| **File operation**         | tar                  |
| **Sources**                | /home/user/documents |
| **Target**                 | /backup/archive.tar  |
| **Base**                   | /home/user           |
| **Is target a directory?** | unchecked            |
| **Is base a directory?**   | checked              |
| **Fail on incomplete?**    | checked              |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Use remote node?** | Check this box to show and use options for picking a remote node to execute on. Note that remote operation is currently only supported on remote Linux machines. |
| **Remote node** | A remote node is required if *Use remote node?* is checked. This is the remote node where the operations will be performed. The remote node can be selected in three ways: from the global remote nodes, at runtime, or by manually writing the details of the remote node in the advanced parameters. If runtime is selected, the plugin will use the runtime node regardless of whether the advanced parameters are set. |
| **IP Address** | Advanced - The IP address of the remote node. |
| **Port** | Advanced - The port to be used to connect to the remote node through SSH. By default, port 22 is used. |
| **Node user** | Advanced - The username associated with the remote node. |
| **Node password** | Advanced - The password associated with the remote node. |
| **File operation** | The operation that will be executed. The full set of options are: tar, untar, OS tar, OS untar, tgz, untgz, OS tgz, OS untgz, zip, and unzip. However, note that some operations may be unavailable if you are using a Windows machine or using the remote node functionality. |
| **Sources** | Source absolute directory or file paths. |
| **Target** | Target absolute directory path. |
| **Is target a directory?** | Check this box if path entered for *Target* is a directory that already exists. If it does not already exist, it will be created. Only used when executing OS tar or OS tgz on a remote node. |
| **Base** | The base directory. See the explanations in the operating instructions. |
| **Is base a directory?** | Check this box if path entered for *Base* is a directory that already exists. Only used when executing OS tar or OS tgz on a remote node. |
| **Fail on incomplete?** | Return a failure if the operation does not reach completion. |

### Inputs Description

Changes in this list of inputs will override any values set in the action template.

- **source**: Source absolute paths.
- **base**: The base directory.
- **operation**: The operation to execute.
- **target**: Target directory path.

### Outputs Description

- **Failed_list**: The files or directories unsuccessfully handled as an array.
- **File_list**: The files or directories successfully handled as an array.

### Operating Instructions

- This plugin is primarily used for archiving operations. It provides archiving operations using both Ruby libraries and standard command line utilities (OS-based).
- The plugin supports both file-based and directory-based operations. If a directory is specified, all included files and subfolders will be archived.
- Example: Setting Sources to `/root/foo/` and Target to `/root/bar/` will create the directory `/root/bar/foo/`.
- For any of the operations, either the source or the base directory should be provided.
