- Sources is usually an array type that can accommodate a single path or an array of paths. However, when using tgz or zip operations, source is a string.
- Example: `Sources = /root/foo/abc.txt` or `Sources = ["/root/foo/abc.txt"]`
- Glob expressions are supported within Sources. Example: `Sources = /root/foo/*.txt` to process all files under /root/foo/ with a txt extension.
- Put a '/' at the end of the Target directory path. Otherwise, if the last directory name does not exist, it will be used as the file name to be created.
- Example: Setting Sources to `/root/foo/abc.txt` and Target to `/root/bar` when the bar directory does not exist will create a file `/root/bar` with the content of `/root/foo/abc.txt`. If instead Target is set to `/root/bar/` and the bar directory does not exist, the first source file's directory path will be considered as target path.
- The tgz operation doesn't work on multiple elements. It takes only a single source folder or file to archive. Use OS tgz if more than one folder needs to be processed.
- Base is the "base directory" that the operation should refer to. When executing: `tar -cvf /root/result.tar /root/foo/bar.txt /root/bar/foo.txt`, the result is the archiving of both files. Instead of mentioning root twice, you could store `/root` as base and provide the rest under source files.

### Dependencies

- For remote operations, SSH access must be available on the target Linux system with appropriate user credentials.
- The aspweb user must have proper filesystem permissions for all specified source and target paths when executing locally.
- Required archiving utilities (tar, zip, etc.) must be installed on the execution system (local or remote).

### File generator

![File generator icon](icons/FileGenerator.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in is used to create a file using dynamic content provided from parameters and/or the output from previous steps in the workflow. Although it can generate any text file, it is primarily used to generate XML files to be used by other steps or other applications. The user defines the template to be used, the variable content required and variable names, and maps the variable inputs.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.5.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.4.0 | body template to longtext |
| 0.3.0 | Adds support for append mode |
| 0.2.2 | Adds support for implied types in target_file_path |
| 0.2.1 | Adds styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.2.0 | Adds support for changing status within file generation erb |
| 0.1.9 | New icon |
| 0.1.8 | Avoid crash when target_file_path is blank |
| 0.1.7 | Fixed bug introduced in 0.1.6 due to wrong variable name |
| 0.1.6 | Added file body path as an input if neither path nor body is configured |
| 0.1.5 | Moved to File Operations category |
| 0.1.4 | Adds help file |
| 0.1.3 | Makes template_name a payload |
| 0.1.2 | Adds location of generated file as output |
| 0.1.1 | fixes an edge case for method_missing |
| 0.1.0 | adds option to remove extra line feed added by ERB |
| 0.0.4 | adds auto creation of target folder |
| 0.0.3 | enables rich text edit, bug fix in method_missing |
| 0.0.2 | adds target file path as optional input |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

### How It Works

- Generates files dynamically using a template written with ERB (Embedded Ruby).
- Templates can include variables such as `<%=filename%>` and embedded Ruby logic (`<% %>`).
- Both required and optional inputs can be declared. If required inputs aren't mapped to any value, then the workflow will fail.
- Target file paths can be defined directly or constructed dynamically using variables.
- Generated content can be appended to existing files if desired.
- File content can optionally be returned as workflow output for downstream steps.
- Linefeed suppression allows cleaner output by avoiding blank lines from embedded Ruby logic.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`FileGenerator`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Provide the following parameters:`
    - **`File body template`**`: Define the ERB template for the file. Supports variables and Ruby logic.`
    - **`Required inputs`**`: Declare inputs that must be provided for rendering.`
    - **`Optional inputs`**`: Declare optional variables that may be used in the template.`
    - **`Target file path`**`: Path for the generated file (can use variables).`
    - **`Append to existing?`**`: If enabled, appends content to an existing file instead of overwriting it.`
    - **`Return file content?`**`: Makes file contents available as workflow outputs.`
    - **`Suppress marked linefeeds`**`: Prevents blank lines caused by embedded Ruby.`

### Example Usage

**Example using embedded Ruby code**

If an input `recipients` contains *bob@ac.com; bill@ac.com; doug@ac.com*, the following template:

    <destinations>
      <% destinations = recipients.split(/[ ; ]+/) %>
      <% destinations.each do |destination| %>
        <to><%= destination %></to>
      <% end %>
    </destinations>

Will be rendered as:

    <destinations>
      <to>bob@ac.com</to>
      <to>bill@ac.com</to>
      <to>doug@ac.com</to>
    </destinations>

#### *Same example with suppression marked linefeeds*

    <destinations>
    <% destinations = recipients.split(/[ ; ]+/) %>\
    <% destinations.each do |destination| %>\
      <to><%= destination %></to>
    <% end %>\
    </destinations>

Will be rendered without extra blank lines:

    <destinations>
      <to>bob@ac.com</to>
      <to>bill@ac.com</to>
      <to>doug@ac.com</to>
    </destinations>

**Behavior:** With this configuration, the workflow generates the file using the provided template, required/optional inputs, and target file path. Outputs are available if enabled.

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                     | Value                                 |
|-------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| **File body template**        | XML template with embedded Ruby loops |
| **Required inputs**           | recipients                            |
| **Optional inputs**           | None                                  |
| **Target file path**          | /tmp/output.xml                       |
| **Append to existing?**       | No                                    |
| **Return file content?**      | Yes                                   |
| **Suppress marked linefeeds** | Yes                                   |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **File body template** | The ERB template text used to generate the file. |
| **Required inputs** | Declared mandatory variables for rendering. |
| **Optional inputs** | Declared optional variables that may or may not be used in the template. |
| **Target file path** | Path where the generated file is created. |
| **Append to existing?** | If enabled, appends content to the existing file. |
| **Return file content?** | If enabled, exposes generated content as an output. |
| **Suppress marked linefeeds** | Prevents blank lines caused by embedded Ruby. |

### Inputs Description

- **Target file path:** Requested if the 'Target file path' parameter is left blank.
- **Declared inputs:** All required and optional inputs referenced in the template or file path.

### Outputs Description

- **File path:** The full path of the generated file.
- **File content:** The generated content (only if 'Return file content?' is checked).

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

No external dependencies. Templates may use standard Ruby logic supported by ERB.

### Troubleshooting

- Ensure all required inputs are defined; missing inputs will cause the workflow to fail.
- Verify that the target file path is valid and writable.
- Check ERB syntax in the template—unmatched tags will cause rendering errors.
- Use suppression linefeeds if strict formatting is required.

### File information

![File information icon](icons/FileInfo.png){.plugin-icon}

Extracts multiple information about a local file, given its full path.

| Version | Comment                                                        |
|---------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8            |
| 0.1.1   | Fix compatibility issue with array mode (when set to nil)      |
| 0.1.0   | Accept an array of files                                       |
| 0.0.6   | Help file fix.                                                 |
| 0.0.5   | Output dates in unix format.                                   |
| 0.0.4   | Fixed file size and added file size in Bytes.                  |
| 0.0.3   | added file size in KB as integer to the outputs                |
| 0.0.2   | added custom icon, and file type (folder, symlink), fixes help |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                                |

#### How It Works

- Retrieves detailed information about a local file or folder, given its full path.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **FileInfo** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name of this template or configuration.
    - : Comments associated with this template or configuration.
    - : The full path of the file to retrieve information for.
    - : By default, the action supports non-existent paths and computes available path information, setting all other values to -1. When this option is enabled, the action will fail if the specified file does not exist.
    - : When enabled, allows the action to receive an array of file paths instead of a single file path. Each file path in the array will be processed independently, and the outputs will contain a corresponding list of results.

#### Example Configuration

**NOTE:** In this example, we are retrieving information for a single file path. When using array mode, replace this field with a list of paths.

| Parameter                   | Value                                     |
|-----------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| File path                   | /home/product/test                        |
| Fail if file does not exist | Selected                                  |
| Accept array of files       | Not selected (example uses a single file) |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name of this template or configuration. |
| **Comments** | Comments associated with this template or configuration. |
| **File path** | The full path of the file to retrieve information for. |
| **Fail if file does not exist** | By default, the action supports non-existent paths and computes available path information, setting all other values to -1. When this option is enabled, the action will fail if the specified file does not exist. |
| **Accept array of files** | When enabled, allows the action to receive an array of file paths instead of a single file path. Each file path in the array will be processed independently, and the outputs will contain a corresponding list of results. |

#### Inputs Description

| Parameter   | Description                                            |
|-------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| *File path* | The full path of the file to retrieve information for. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| file_name | The file or folder name. |
| naked_name | The file name without its extension, if any. |
| file_size | The file size as a string. |
| file_size_in_bytes | The file size in Bytes as a number. |
| file_size_in_KB | The file size in KBytes as a number. |
| extension | The file extension. |
| parent_name | The name of the parent folder. |
| parent | The full path of the parent folder. |
| symlink? | Returns true if symlinks are supported by the OS and the specified path points to a symlink (not an actual file). |
| atime | The last access time, if the file exists. -1 otherwise. |
| mtime | The last modification time, if the file exists. -1 otherwise. |
| ctime | The change time, if the file exists. -1 otherwise. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

This action takes a full path as input and extracts detailed information about the file or folder. When array mode is enabled, provide a list of file paths and the action will return a corresponding list of results.

### ISM and M3U8 File Parser

![ISM and M3U8 File Parser icon](icons/IsmM3u8Parser.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides ability to parse an ISM file and a M3U8 file

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.4   | Added styling to parameter help text.               |
| 0.0.3   | Added help file.                                    |
| 0.0.2   | Fetches content from the head tag as well.          |
| 0.0.1   | First version                                       |

#### Description

##### Saved Parameters Description

**Name**: The name used to identify a saved IsmM3u8Parser configured instance.

**Comments**: Some comments about this saved IsmM3u8Parser configured instance.

**File Path**: File path of the .ism or .m3u8 file.

**Return output as array?**: By default the .ism file or .m3u8 files are parsed and returned as Hash of file name(s). Checking this checkbox will also return the parsed result in form of an array.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the IsmM3u8Parser action template.

\- File Path is a mandatory input.\
- Return output as array? in optional. Defaults to 'false'.\

##### Outputs description

- **Array_of_ISM_Filenames**: Array of the file name(s) from the .ism file.
- **Hash_of_ISM_Filenames**: Hash of the file name(s) from the .ism file.
- **Hash_of_M3u8_Filenames**: Hash of the file name(s) from the .m3u8 file.
- **Array_of_M3u8_Filenames**: Array of the file name(s) from the .m3u8 file.

##### Operating Instructions

\- Operation of this plugin is simple and straightforward.\
- The plugin strictly supports '.ism' and '.m3u8' file formats only.\
- Provide a '.ism' or '.m3u8' file as input.\
- Plugin returns Hash and Array(in case the 'Return output as array?' is checked.) of the parsed file.\
E.g.\
File Path: /Users/root/m3u8/stsp.m3u8\
Return output as array? False (Unchecked)\
\
Outputs:\
Hash_of_M3u8_Filenames : { "/Users/root/m3u8/stsp.m3u8" =\> { "/Users/root/m3u8/pl.m3u8" =\> \["/Users/root/m3u8/playlist.mp3"\] }}\

### Local file operation

![Local file operation icon](icons/LocalFileOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug‑in executes local file operations on Orchestrator‑mounted file systems. It supports actions such as copy, move, delete, makedir, sym‑link, touch, listing, and archive handling, with options for validation, overwriting, timestamp preservation, and permissions control.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.1.0 | Adds no glob on sources for delete |
| 1.0.3 | Updating help page |
| 1.0.2 | Adding troubleshooting tip |
| 1.0.1 | Fixes chmod and chown for mkdir |
| 1.0.0 | Adds option to fail when listed source files are missing |
| 0.9.2 | Removing terminate execution (not needed anymore) |
| 0.9.1 | Adding chown to touch and fixing it for link. |
| 0.9.0 | Adds option to fail when file listing is empty, hides irrelevant sections in editor |
| 0.8.2 | Adds chmod and chown functionality to symlink and overwrite flag overwrites symlink if it exists |
| 0.8.1 | Adds chmod and chown functionality to list operation also |
| 0.8.0 | Add option to create hard symlink |
| 0.7.3 | Fixes metadata |
| 0.7.2 | Fixes touch option to allow new file creation |
| 0.7.1 | New icon |
| 0.7.0 | Added new field to expose failed list an an array without error description |
| 0.6.4 | Added help file |
| 0.6.3 | Change default block size to 1MB |
| 0.6.2 | Making file permissions runtime |
| 0.6.1 | Making base optional for UnTAR and TAR |
| 0.6.0 | Added two enhancements a) Added logic to allow list operation using base folder (if provided) b) Added logic to CHMOD or CHOWN directories created or files copied |
| 0.5.1 | fixes problem with copy and move due to the overwrite flag |
| 0.5.0 | adds overwrite option for copy and move |
| 0.4.2 | fixes failed list output in mkdir |
| 0.4.1 | adds progress reporting to tar/untar and makes copy/move err when explicitly specified files are not moved |
| 0.4.0 | adds copy by block (to provide progress report) |
| 0.3.5 | removes un-needed dependencies |
| 0.3.4 | adding tar and untar |
| 0.3.3 | fix timestamping |
| 0.3.2 | adds directory listing |
| 0.3.1 | fix symlink typo |
| 0.3.0 | adds touch, symlink and stat operations. Adds option to fail on incomplete operation and failure list as output |
| 0.2.4 | fixes bug when multiple files are processed |
| 0.2.3 | returns list of files |
| 0.2.2 | Supports for payload in sources, target and base |
| 0.2.1 | Uses terminate instead of throw |
| 0.2.0 | Adds an option to preserve or not timestamp when moving or copying files |
| 0.1.1 | Returns failed and successful list as outputs |

### How It Works

- Provides file system actions such as copy, move, delete, makedir, stat, symlink, touch, directory listing, tar, and untar.
- Works on single files, directories, arrays, and supports glob expressions for source selection.
- Options include validating sources, overwriting target files, preserving timestamps, and exposing results/failed files.
- Includes permission configuration (chmod/chown) and supports recursive operations.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`LocalFileOperation`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Set up the minimal required fields:`
    - **`File operation`**`: Choose the action (e.g., copy, symlink).`
    - **`Sources`**`: Absolute path(s) to source file(s) or directories.`
    - **`Target`**`: Absolute path for target location.`
    - **`Overwrite?`**`: Check to allow overwriting files in target.`
    - **`Preserve timestamp?`**`: Enable to try to retain source timestamps.`
    - **`Validate sources?`**`: Enable to error if any source is missing.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                        | Example Value                   |
|----------------------------------|---------------------------------|
| **File operation**               | copy                            |
| **Sources**                      | /opt/aspera/orchestrator/lfo3   |
| **Target**                       | /opt/aspera/orchestrator/lfo2   |
| **Validate sources?**            | checked                         |
| **Overwrite?**                   | checked                         |
| **Preserve timestamp?**          | checked                         |
| **Fail on incomplete?**          | checked                         |
| **Copy block size**              | 10                              |
| **Make the symlink a hardlink?** | checked (for symlink operation) |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **File operation** | The operation to execute: copy, move, delete, makedir, stat, sym-link, touch, dir listing, tar, untar. |
| **Sources** | Source absolute file/directory paths; supports arrays or glob patterns. |
| **Validate sources?** | Errors if specified sources do not exist; required for safer operations. |
| **Disable glob on sources?** | Disables wildcard/glob expansion, using sources literally. |
| **Target** | Target absolute path for copy/move and related operations. |
| **Base** | Base directory prefix for copy/move path transformations. |
| **Copy block size** | Block size for copy/move, in bytes (default 1MB; e.g. 10). |
| **Overwrite?** | Overwrite target files if they exist. |
| **Preserve timestamp?** | Attempts to preserve source timestamps (depends on environment). |
| **Fail on incomplete?** | Marks step as failed if operation not complete. |
| **Fail on empty?** | Fails step if a list/match returns no results. |
| **Expose failed files as an array?** | Exposes failed file paths as output array. |
| **Chmod** | Octal permissions mode for created/copied files (e.g. 755). |
| **Recursive** | Enable recursive handling for directories. |
| **User ID** | User ID for file/directory ownership. |
| **Group ID** | Group ID for file/directory ownership. |
| **Make the symlink a hardlink?** | If enabled and using symlink, will create hardlink instead. |

### Inputs Description

- **source**: Absolute path(s) (array or glob supported) to operate on.
- **target**: Destination absolute path for relevant actions.
- **operation**: Operation type (copy, move, etc.).
- **base**: Base directory prefix; affects destination path in copy/move.
- **preserve_timestamp**: Try to preserve file timestamps (true/false).
- **Chmod**: Permissions mode (e.g. 755) for output.
- **Uid**: User ID for ownership.
- **Guid**: Group ID for ownership.

### Outputs Description

- **Failed_list**: Array of files/dirs that failed to process.
- **File_list**: Array of successfully handled files/dirs.

### Supported Actions

- copy
- move
- delete
- makedir
- stat
- symlink
- touch
- dir listing
- tar
- untar

### Operating Instructions

- The plugin supports both file-based and directory-based operations. If a directory is specified, all included files and subfolders will be copied, moved, or deleted.
- When copying/moving a directory, the destination will include the directory itself unless configured otherwise. Example: `Sources = /root/foo/`, `Target = /root/bar/` will create `/root/bar/foo/`.
- Sources may be a single path or an array of paths.\
  Example: `Sources = /root/foo/titi.txt`\
  or\
  `Sources = ["/root/foo/titi.txt"]`
- Glob patterns are supported in sources (e.g., `Sources = /root/foo/*.txt` to include all text files).
- Target path rules: If the target is missing a trailing slash, and the last directory does not exist, it will be treated as a filename.\
  Example: `Sources = /root/foo/titi.txt` with `Target = /root/bar` (and bar does not exist) → result: `/root/bar` (a file).\
  If instead `Target = /root/bar/` (and 'bar' does not exist) → an error will be displayed.
- The **touch** operation will not create a new file if it does not exist; instead, it updates the timestamps on an existing file.
- The **Base** parameter modifies how paths are replicated. Example:\
  Command: `cp /root/foo/bar.txt /home/`\
  Result: `/home/bar.txt`\
  With Base = `/root`:\
  Same copy gives `/home/foo/bar.txt`.

### Dependencies

- The aspweb user must have proper filesystem permissions for all specified source and target paths.

### Troubleshooting

- In Windows, if the 'move' operation is failing between two mapped drives, check that the source and target paths are configured properly in the plugin. UNC paths are always preferred.
- In Windows, the 'move' operation usually works between mapped drives if the paths are configured with backslashes. Example: "\\10.0.154.185\sharefororch\\
- Sometimes Windows struggles with backslashes in paths. Using double backslashes like "\\share\\folder\\" may resolve issues.
- If 'Preserve timestamp?' does not retain the original time, confirm filesystem and user permission capabilities.
- Hardlinks require both source and target to be on the same filesystem/partition. Verify this if hardlink operations fail.
- Ensure your OS and filesystem support symlinks/hardlinks, as not all filesystems (e.g., FAT32) allow them.

### Local file permission

![Local file permission icon](icons/LocalFilePermission.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to do the chmod and chown file operations after a file has been downloaded.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.1.1 | Updating to allow aspweb user to perform file operations |
| 0.1.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.5 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.0.4 | 2015-02-16 ML - New icon |
| 0.0.3 | Fix type of chmod_get where the plugin errors when there is no value |
| 0.0.2 | Fix type of source_files types to be array, with tolerance for string |
| 0.0.1 | First version of the permissions plugin allowing users to do CHOWN and CHMOD operations on file(s) |

#### How It Works

- This action plug-in performs file permission and ownership operations on the local system.
- It supports both CHMOD and CHOWN operations on files and directories.
- GLOB patterns can be used to target multiple files or directories.
- When a directory is specified, all contained files and subdirectories can be processed recursively.
- Successful and failed operations are reported separately for CHMOD and CHOWN.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **LocalFilePermissions** plug-in.
2.  Complete the following configuration fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved LocalFilePermissions configuration.
    - : Files or directories to apply the operation on. GLOB wildcards are supported. Multiple entries can be separated by commas.
    - : Octal mode value used for CHMOD operations (e.g. 755).
    - : User ID or username used for CHOWN operations.
    - : Group ID or group name used for CHOWN operations.
    - : If enabled, operations are applied recursively to subdirectories and files.

#### Example Configuration

Below is an example configuration for the LocalFilePermissions plug-in.

| Parameter         | Value               |
|-------------------|---------------------|
| File(s)           | /data/output/\*.mp4 |
| Permissions code  | 755                 |
| User UID or name  | aspera              |
| Group GID or name | media               |
| Recursive         | Selected            |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved LocalFilePermissions configuration. |
| **File(s)** | Files or directories to apply the operation on. GLOB wildcards are supported. Multiple entries can be separated by commas. |
| **Permissions code** | Octal mode value used for CHMOD operations (e.g. 755). |
| **User UID or name** | User ID or username used for CHOWN operations. |
| **Group GID or name** | Group ID or group name used for CHOWN operations. |
| **Recursive** | If enabled, operations are applied recursively to subdirectories and files. |

#### Inputs Description

All parameters are available as runtime inputs if they are not specified in the template. This plug-in does not support value overwrites: parameters defined in the template are not exposed as runtime inputs.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| file_list | Files or directories to operate on, if not specified in the template. |
| permissions_code | Octal permissions value for CHMOD operations. |
| user_uid_or_name | User ID or name for CHOWN operations. |
| group_gid_or_name | Group ID or name for CHOWN operations. |
| recursive | Whether the operation should descend recursively. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| CHOWN_File_list | List of files on which the CHOWN operation was successful. |
| CHOWN_Failed_list | List of files on which the CHOWN operation failed. |
| CHMOD_File_list | List of files on which the CHMOD operation was successful. |
| CHMOD_Failed_list | List of files on which the CHMOD operation failed. |

#### Supported Actions

CHMOD, CHOWN

#### Dependencies

Local file system access and sufficient permissions to execute CHMOD and CHOWN commands.

#### Operating Instructions

1.  The plug-in executes CHMOD and/or CHOWN commands on the local file system.
2.  Both files and directories can be specified as input.
3.  When a directory is provided, all contained files and subdirectories are included.
4.  Recursive processing is controlled using the Recursive option.
5.  Results are separated into successful and failed file lists for each operation.

### MD5 checksum

![MD5 checksum icon](icons/Md5Checksum.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in calculates MD5 checksums for specified files and optionally verifies them against previously calculated values. It supports memory-efficient processing with configurable block sizes and dynamic file path inputs for flexible file integrity verification workflows.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.3.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.2.2   | Added styling to parameter help text.               |
| 0.2.1   | 2015-02-16 ML - New icon                            |
| 0.2.0   | Adds blocksize configuration                        |
| 0.1.0   | Adds progress reporting                             |
| 0.0.3   | Adds display name                                   |
| 0.0.2   | fixes template and adds help                        |
| 0.0.1   | first tracked version                               |

### How It Works

- Calculates the MD5 checksum for a specified file.
- Optionally verifies the calculated checksum against a provided value.
- Uses configurable block size for memory-efficient processing of large files.
- Fails the step if verification mode is enabled and checksums don't match.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **Md5Checksum** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - **Filter name**: Label for the step (e.g., `File Checksum Validator`).
    - **Comments**: Notes for the template or configuration.
    - **File path**: Path to the file for MD5 calculation (e.g., `/path/to/example.txt`).\
      If left blank, file path will be required as input.
    - **Block size**: Number of bytes read at a time for calculation (default: `131072`).

### Example Configuration

| Field       | Value                  |
|-------------|------------------------|
| Filter name | `New Md5Checksum Step` |
| File path   | `/path/to/example.txt` |
| Block size  | `131072`               |

**Behavior:** The plugin will calculate the MD5 checksum for `/path/to/example.txt`, reading 131,072 bytes at a time for optimal memory usage.

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | Notes and comments for this step or template configuration. |
| **File path** | Path to the file for which the MD5 checksum needs to be computed. |
| **Block size** | Number of bytes read at a time in memory to compute the calculation. |

### Inputs Description

- **File_path**: Required if 'File path' field is left blank, or when using dynamic parameters (must be string type).
- **Check Against**: Optional. If provided, enables verify mode - the computed checksum will be compared to this value.
- All dynamic variables listed in the 'File path' field are mandatory and must be of type string.

### Outputs Description

| Output               | Description                                         |
|----------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| **MD5_checksum**     | The calculated MD5 checksum for the specified file. |
| **Checked_filepath** | The actual path of the file that was checked.       |
| **Step_information** | Step execution details and metadata.                |

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

None

### Operating Instructions

This action performs the calculation of the MD5 checksum for a specified file.

- Use for file integrity verification and validation workflows.
- Enable verification mode by providing a checksum value in inputs.
- Adjust block size based on available memory and file sizes.

If an MD5 checksum is provided in inputs, the action will compare the calculated checksum against it and exit with 'Failed' status if the two do not match.

### Troubleshooting

- Ensure that the file path is accessible and the file exists.
- Verify that file permissions allow read access.
- Check that dynamic input variables are properly formatted as strings.
- For large files, consider adjusting the block size to optimize performance.

### Media info

![Media info icon](icons/MediaInfo.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin extracts comprehensive technical and tag metadata from video and audio files using the MediaInfo application.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.7.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.6.0 | Add XML output option |
| 0.5.0 | Add options to skip cover data from generic result |
| 0.4.0 | Adding Local execution mode |
| 0.3.5 | Adding Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.3.4 | Fixing Parsing in output |
| 0.3.3 | Improvement to help file to indicate when MediaInfo outputs might be blank |
| 0.3.2 | 2015-02-16 ML - New icon |
| 0.3.1 | fixes migration name for additional sections |
| 0.3.0 | adds option to enter additional sections |
| 0.2.0 | exposing image information if available |
| 0.1.9 | preventing autocomplete of user info by browsers |
| 0.1.8 | exposes execution login and password as runtime params |
| 0.1.7 | fixes bug on removal of additional outputs |
| 0.1.6 | escapes binary path to avoid windows issues with space in binary path |
| 0.1.5 | adds ability to dynamically provide node and binary_path |
| 0.1.3 | strips \r when server is windows, avoids crash on badly formatted output |
| 0.1.2 | fails if no results are returned |
| 0.1.1 | adds support for space and other exotic characters in filename |
| 0.1.0 | adds support for singled out additional outputs |
| 0.0.7 | adds functionality to mask password |
| 0.0.6 | fixes default name in template editor |
| 0.0.5 | exposes detailed mode checkbox |
| 0.0.4 | help file provided |
| 0.0.3 | remove duplicate method process_outputs |
| 0.0.2 | bug fix (missing constant) |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

### How It Works

- Executes MediaInfo analysis operations on remote nodes via SSH to extract comprehensive metadata from media files.
- Supports detailed mode equivalent to the -f (full) verbose option for extensive metadata extraction.
- Extracts metadata organized into General, Video, Audio, and Image sections with optional additional sections.
- Provides output in hash format by default or XML format when specified, with customizable additional data fields.
- Handles embedded cover data with options to ignore problematic outputs that might affect database storage.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`MediaInfo`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the essential parameters:`
    - **`Media file path`**`: Source file path for analysis (relative to remote node).`
    - **`Execution Node`**`: Remote node where MediaInfo application is installed.`
    - **`Path to the mediainfo binary`**`: Full path to MediaInfo executable on remote node.`
    - **`Detailed Mode`**`: Enable for comprehensive metadata extraction (-f option).`
3.  `Optionally configure advanced settings:`
    - **`Execution Login`**`: User account for remote execution (if different from node default).`
    - **`Execution Password`**`: Password for remote login (if different from node default).`
    - **`Additional Sections`**`: Extract other sections beyond standard Audio, Video, General, Image.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                              | Example Value            |
|----------------------------------------|--------------------------|
| **Media file path**                    | /media/videos/sample.mp4 |
| **Detailed Mode**                      | checked                  |
| **Ignore Cover Data output**           | unchecked                |
| **Additional Sections**                | unchecked                |
| **Return xml output instead of hash?** | unchecked                |
| **Execution Node**                     | media-analysis-server    |
| **Execution Login**                    | mediauser                |
| **Path to the mediainfo binary**       | /usr/bin/mediainfo       |
| **Any optional parameters**            | --Output=JSON            |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Media file path** | The source file for the analysis operation. Must be accessible from the remote node. |
| **Detailed Mode** | Equivalent to the -f (full) verbose option for the mediainfo command line. When enabled, most parameter values are returned as arrays instead of strings. |
| **Ignore Cover Data output** | If the media has embedded cover data, it could prevent storage in the database. This option ignores cover data to avoid storage issues. |
| **Additional Sections** | Allows extraction of other sections besides Audio, Video, General and Image if present (e.g., Audio \#1, Audio \#2). |
| **Additional Outputs** | Provides the ability to extract specific data fields from MediaInfo results. Requires name, value type, and field identification using hash_name -\> hash_value syntax. |
| **Return xml output instead of hash?** | Produces XML output instead of the default Hash output format. |
| **Execution Node** | The node where the analysis operation must be executed (where MediaInfo application is installed). |
| **Execution Login** | The user account used to execute the MediaInfo analysis operation. Uses node default if not specified. |
| **Execution Password** | The password for remote node login. Uses node default if not specified. |
| **Path to the mediainfo binary** | The path to locate the MediaInfo binary on the remote node. Not needed if binary folder is in system path. |
| **Any optional parameters to pass to the MediaInfo process** | Optional parameters supported by the mediainfo binary, passed directly to command line. |

### Inputs Description

- **Source file path**: All variables listed in Source file path field are mandatory and must be of type string.
- **Command line options**: All variables listed in the optional parameters to pass to the MediaInfo process are mandatory and must be of type string.
- **Message**: Optional message parameter for the analysis operation.

### Outputs Description

- **General**: A hash containing all the metadata information that MediaInfo classifies as General.\
  For example: *{ 'Writing library' =\> 'VirtualDub build 11636/release', 'Format' =\> 'AVI', 'File size' =\> '5.21 MiB', 'Format/Info' =\> 'Audio Video Interleave', 'Overall bit rate' =\> '798 Kbps', 'Complete name' =\> '/Users/mlusinchi/Movies/annie_iodenthour.avi', 'Duration' =\> '54s 720ms' }*
- **Video**: Same as above for the Video metadata.\
  For example: *{ 'Stream size' =\> '4.30 MiB (83%)', 'Codec ID/Info' =\> 'Intel Indeo Video 5.0 Wavelet', 'Format' =\> 'Indeo 4', 'ID' =\> '0', 'Codec ID' =\> 'IV50', 'Display aspect ratio' =\> '2.35:1', 'Width' =\> '320 pixels', 'Duration' =\> '54s 720ms', 'Height' =\> '136 pixels', 'Bits/(Pixel\*Frame)' =\> '0.606', 'Bit rate' =\> '659 Kbps', 'Frame rate' =\> '25.000 fps' }*
- **Audio**: Same as above for the Audio metadata.\
  For example: *{ 'Compression mode' =\> 'Lossy', 'Stream size' =\> '855 KiB (16%)', 'Format' =\> 'MPEG Audio', 'Mode' =\> 'Joint stereo', 'Mode extension' =\> 'MS Stereo', 'ID' =\> '1', 'Codec ID' =\> '55', 'Channel(s)' =\> '2 channels', 'Alignment' =\> 'Split across interleaves', 'Interleave, duration' =\> '40 ms (1.01 video frame)', 'Format version' =\> 'Version 1', 'Format profile' =\> 'Layer 3', 'Interleave, preload duration' =\> '451 ms', 'Sampling rate' =\> '48.0 KHz', 'Duration' =\> '54s 720ms', 'Bit rate mode' =\> 'Constant', 'Codec ID/Hint' =\> 'MP3', 'Bit rate' =\> '128 Kbps' }*
- **XML output**: The XML output if 'Return xml output instead of hash?' is selected.

**Note:** For each of those hash fields, if several values are provided by mediainfo for the same field, an array is returned instead. This is the case if the **-f** option is used.

For example, in default mode in the Video hash, a pair 'Bit rate'=\>'638 Kbps' whereas in full mode (-f), the pair is 'Bit rate'=\>\['637520', '638 Kbps'\]

### Operating Instructions

- This action executes the specified file analysis operation on a remote node where MediaInfo is available.
- The connection to the remote server is done over SSH, so make sure that the target server has SSH enabled. If the server is a Windows machine, OpenSSH can be installed. Aspera PointToPoint or Enterprise Server will provide SSH with it.
- It is typically a good idea to provide the explicit path to the mediainfo binary to avoid configuration issues. On a UNIX-flavored machine, running '**which mediainfo**' should provide the path information.
- **Important Note:** This action plugin does not do any file movement. Hence, for it to run a file analysis, the specified file has to be accessible. Either the file is transferred to that server or it must be accessible from that server either on some type of network share.
- **Important Note II:** MediaInfo does not explicitly fail when the file is not found. The plugin provides empty information for General, Video, Image. So during development or testing, when you encounter this scenario check the file path (relative to the remote node) and also the permissions to the file.

<!-- -->

- **Note about the use on a Windows node:**
- When the execution node is Windows, a couple of considerations need to be taken into account during the configuration:
- 1/ The mediainfo CLI needs to be installed. It can be downloaded separately from http://mediaarea.net/download/binary/mediainfo/0.7.64/MediaInfo_CLI_0.7.64_Windows_i386.zip (for 32 bit) or http://mediaarea.net/download/binary/mediainfo/0.7.64/MediaInfo_CLI_0.7.64_Windows_x64.zip (for 64 bit).
- 2/ The etc/passwd file needs to be set for the SSH user used by Orchestrator with a valid user root and to use the 'bin/sh' shell (the switch shell has bizarre escape rules that are best avoided). In the case below '/' is used as the doc root for the user Orchestrator.
- If Aspera Point to Point or Enterprise Server is used to provide remote access, the passwd file will be found in C:\Program Files\Aspera\Enterprise Server\etc\passwd
- 3/ When configuring the action, make sure the path to the mediainfo executable is provided. The path should be provided as a UNIX path since it is used through a UNIX Shell. Typically, the mediainfo command line should be installed at c:/mediainfo/mediainfo.exe or C:/Program Files/Mediainfo/mediainfo.exe
- The path can also use the cygwin preferred POSIX equivalent: /cygdrive/c/mediainfo/mediainfo.exe
- File path should also be provided in UNIX format. For example: c:/DATA/in/Violin Margo.mov or /cygdrive/c/DATA/in/Violin Margo.mov

**Example passwd file configuration:**

    Administrator:unused_by_nt/2000/xp:500:513:U-EXPERIEN-F1FAFA\ 

       Administrator,S-1-5-21-1801674531-1202660629-2067101283-500:/home/Administrator:/bin/switch 

    orchestrator:unused_by_nt/2000/xp:1003:513:orchestrator,U-EXPERIEN-F1FAFA\ 

       orchestrator,S-1-5-21-1801674531-1202660629-2067101283-1003:/:/bin/sh

### Dependencies

- MediaInfo application must be installed on the target execution node.
- SSH access must be enabled on the target server for remote execution.
- The aspweb user must have appropriate permissions to access source files when executing locally.
- Network connectivity between Orchestrator and execution node is required for SSH operations.

### Troubleshooting

- Empty metadata results: Verify that the source file path is correct relative to the remote node and that file permissions allow access.
- SSH connection failures: Ensure SSH is enabled on target server and credentials are correct. For Windows, verify OpenSSH installation or Aspera server configuration.
- MediaInfo 'not found' errors: Provide explicit path to mediainfo binary or ensure the binary location is in the system PATH for the execution user.
- Path format issues on Windows: Use UNIX path format (e.g., /cygdrive/c/path) and ensure etc/passwd file is properly configured with /bin/sh shell.
- Cover data storage issues: Enable 'Ignore Cover Data output' option if embedded cover data prevents database storage of results.
- Detailed mode array handling: When using detailed mode, expect array values for fields with multiple entries instead of single string values.

### Mediabin asset management

![Mediabin asset management icon](icons/MediabinAssetManagement.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provide the ability to perform some asset management on a Mediabin server. Supported operations include asset listing and retrieval (with download).

| Version | Comment                                                    |
|---------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8        |
| 0.0.4   | Added Remote Node Dependency                               |
| 0.0.3   | Added help file and corrected the label for Use SSL field. |
| 0.0.2   | exception handling of RemoteNode.find_by_name()            |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                            |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved MediabinAssetManagement configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved MediabinAssetManagement configured instance.

- **Mediabin node**: Select the node where Mediabin server is installed. The select option lists all the remote nodes that are configured on the orchestrator box.
  \
  **Advanced parameters section**\

- **Mediabin address**: The host address of the Mediabin server.

- **Mediabin wsdl url**: The URL for Mediabin API WSDL.

- **Mediabin user**: User name to access the API.

- **Mediabin Password**: Password for the user to use API.

- **re-enter**: Re-enter the password.

- **Use ssl?**: If checked the API call will be performed over HTTPS. Else, it is made on HTTP.
  \

- **Operation**: Two operations are available for current version of the plugin. List assets and Download asset.

- **Mediabin path**: Path on the Mediabin server where the above mentioned operation is to be performed on.

- **Local path**: In case the operation selected is as Download asset, local path must be provided as the destination of the downloaded asset.
  \

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the MediabinAssetManagement action template.

Mediabin wsdl url, Mediabin user, Mediabin password are mandatory fields.\
Either of Mediabin node **OR** Mediabin Address should be provided\
If Operation is selected as 'List assets' Mediabin path is a mandatory field.\
If Operation is selected as 'Download asset' Mediabin path and Local path are mandatory fields.\
\

##### Outputs description

- **Assets**: An array of all Mediabin asset that are present on the Mediabin path.
- **Local_path**: The path of the local directory where the downloaded asset will reside. This is the same as the one given in the input. Will be populated when the asset is downloaded successfully. Asset name is appended in the path.

##### Operating Instructions

In order to operate the plugin, installation of Mediabin server is required.\
Access to the Mediabin WSDL and credentials to consume the API should also be present\
With this version of plugin, Listing of assets and downloading of an asset are the two operations that are supported.\
Select the operation that needs to be performed and provide the required inputs for the selected operation.\
Execution of the plugin is on synchronous mode.\

### Remote file operation

![Remote file operation icon](icons/RemoteFileOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to execute file operations (copy, move, delete, makedir, dir list) on remote nodes using Aspera transfer servers.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.3.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.2.8 | Mask password field. |
| 0.2.7 | Added styling to help text. |
| 0.2.6 | Adding Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.2.5 | Add error description to status details in case of delete error |
| 0.2.4 | Fixed the ordering in move and copy with overwrite |
| 0.2.3 | 2015-02-17 ML - New icon |
| 0.2.2 | added a help file |
| 0.2.1 | exception handling of RemoteNode.find_by_name() |
| 0.2.0 | Adds flag to overwrite on cp or mv |
| 0.1.0 | 0.0.10 is not a well supported version number. Switching to 0.1.0 instead |
| 0.0.10 | fixed regression introduced in 0.0.9 with deletions |
| 0.0.9 | continue deletion even if one fails |
| 0.0.8 | removed timeout |
| 0.0.7 | fix wrong variable name for remote password |
| 0.0.6 | removes need for target when using option 'list' |
| 0.0.5 | adds comments about trailing '/' in editor |
| 0.0.4 | returns target file list as output |
| 0.0.3 | allow payload string definition for source and target |
| 0.0.2 | adds list command |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

### How It Works

- Executes file operations on remote nodes using the Aspera ascmd executable for file browsing and management.
- Supports copy, move, delete, makedir, and directory listing operations via SSH authentication.
- Works with remote nodes running Aspera transfer servers (e.g., point-to-point) and SSH servers.
- Handles both single files and directories with multiple source support.
- Uses relative paths based on remote user's docroot as defined in /opt/aspera/etc/aspera.conf.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`RemoteFileOperation`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the essential parameters:`
    - **`Name`**`: Enter a descriptive name for this step.`
    - **`File operation`**`: Select the operation (copy, move, delete, makedir, dir list).`
    - **`Remote node`**`: Choose the target remote node from the list of configured Remote Nodes.`
    - **`Source`**`: Add source file/directory paths (click 'Add Source' for multiple sources).`
3.  `Set operation-specific options:`
    - **`Target`**`: Specify target directory path (for copy/move operations).`
    - **`Base`**`: Optional base directory for path transformations.`
    - **`Overwrite?`**`: Check to allow overwriting existing files in the target directory.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter          | Example Value              |
|--------------------|----------------------------|
| **Name**           | Remote File Copy Operation |
| **File operation** | copy                       |
| **Remote node**    | aspera-server-01           |
| **Source**         | /data/source_file.txt      |
| **Target**         | /backup/                   |
| **Overwrite?**     | checked                    |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Display name for this remote file operation step. |
| **Comments** | Notes or description for this step. |
| **File operation** | Operation to execute: copy, move, delete, makedir, dir list. |
| **Overwrite?** | Allow overwriting files if already present in the target directory (for copy/move operations). |
| **Remote node** | Target remote node name from the list of defined Remote Nodes. |
| **Source** | Source directory or file paths. Multiple sources can be added by clicking 'Add Source'. |
| **Target** | Target directory path for copy and move operations. |
| **Base** | Base directory for copy/move operations affecting path replication (see Operating Instructions). |

### Inputs Description

Changes to the inputs listed below will override any values set in the action template.

- **source**: Source path for the operation.
- **target**: Target directory path for copy/move operations.
- **base**: Base directory affecting path replication.
- **remote_node_password**: Password for remote node authentication.
- **remote_node_user**: Username for remote node authentication.

### Outputs Description

- **Directory_list**: An array of files or directories processed by the operation. For copy/move operations, returns the target paths.

### Supported Actions

- copy
- move
- delete
- makedir
- dir list

### Dependencies

- The remote node must have the Aspera transfer server installed (e.g., point-to-point).
- The remote node must run the SSH server for authentication.
- The remote user must be an operating system account defined in /opt/aspera/etc/aspera.conf.
- Referenced files/folders need appropriate permissions for the remote user.
- The Aspera ascmd executable must be available for file browsing operations.

### Operating Instructions

#### Aspera Configuration Requirements

This plugin uses the Aspera ascmd executable to browse files on remote nodes with Aspera transfer servers. The remote user must be defined in `/opt/aspera/etc/aspera.conf` with appropriate docroot configuration:

    <user> 

      <name>xfer</name> 

      <file_system> 

          <access> 

              <paths> 

                  <path> 

                      <absolute>/home/xfer/data</absolute> 

                  </path> 

              </paths> 

          </access> 

      </file_system> 

    </user> 

**Path Resolution:** A target directory like "/foo" refers to "/home/xfer/data/foo" on the remote server.

#### File and Directory Operations

- Supports both file-based and directory-based operations. If a directory is specified, all included files and subfolders will be copied/moved/deleted.
- Directory handling example: `Source = /foo/`, `Target = /bar/` will create the directory `/bar/foo/`.
- Source supports single path or array of paths:\
  Example: `Source = /foo/titi.txt` or `Source = ["/foo/titi.txt", "/foo/toto.txt"]`
- Glob expressions (e.g., \*.txt) are **not supported** within Source paths.
- Target path handling: Add '/' at the end of Target directory path. Without it, if the last directory doesn't exist, it will be treated as a filename.\
  Example: `Source = /foo/titi.txt` with `Target = /bar` (bar doesn't exist) → creates file `/bar`\
  With `Target = /bar/` (bar doesn't exist) → creates directory and results in `/bar/titi.txt`

#### Base Directory Functionality

The **Base** parameter controls path replication behavior:

- **Without Base:** `cp /foo1/foo2/titi.txt /bar/` → Result: `/bar/titi.txt`
- **With Base = /foo1/:** `cp /foo1/foo2/titi.txt /bar/ --base /foo1/` → Result: `/bar/foo2/titi.txt`

Base directory allows replicating the directory structure under a specified base path to the target location.

### Troubleshooting

- Verify Aspera transfer server is properly installed and configured on the remote node.
- Ensure SSH connectivity and authentication work between Orchestrator and remote node.
- Check that the remote user account exists in both the operating system and /opt/aspera/etc/aspera.conf.
- Verify file/directory permissions allow the remote user to access specified paths.
- Confirm that paths are relative to the user's docroot as defined in aspera.conf.
- For permission issues, check that the remote user has read access for sources and write access for targets.

### SglFlashnetArchive

![SglFlashnetArchive icon](icons/SglFlashnetArchive.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in proivdes capability to perform rest operation on CatDV server.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.4   | Ajusting name of plugin.                            |
| 0.0.3   | Added styling to parameter help text.               |
| 0.0.2   | Changed the category type to File Operations        |
| 0.0.1   | Plugin release                                      |

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the SglFlashnetArchive action template.

--\>

- **Name**: Name of the action step.
- **Comments**: Comments associated to this template or configuration.
- **Sgl ip address (or hostname)**: The hostname or the IP address of the SGL FalshNet archiving server.
- **Sgl xml api port**: SGL FalshNet archiving server port where the request XML will be printed.
- **Api version**: The API version of the server.
- **Status polling frequency**: The polling frequency in seconds to poll for the response.
- **Data source server**: Datasource server is the datasource on the server from where the context will be accesses / archived.
- **Operator username**: User name of the operator performing the operation.
- **Calling application**: Name of the system that is accessing the API.
- **Operation**: Select the operation that needs to be performed (e.g. Archive, Restore etc.). Click the refresh link next to the dropdown menu. This will load all the attributes that are need to for the selected operation.
- **Mandatory attributes**: These attribute(s) are mandatory for the selected operation.
- **Optional attributes**: These attribute(s) are optional for the selected operation.

Mandatory and Optional attributes are loaded from the payload file residing in the plugin payload directory\
Plugin payload directory also contains attirbuts_mapping file. This is required because certain attributes in the payload, are difficult to interpret as in on the plugin template. E.g. PartialStart.QWD is mapped to FilePartialStart. So on the template user will see File Partial Start while the value of this attribute will go as PartialStart.QWD in the actual payload that is submitted.\
**Sgl ip address (or hostname), Sgl xml api port, Data source server, Operator username, Calling application and Operation are Mandatory inputs.**

##### Outputs description

- **Errors**: Response error(s).
- **Status**: The status of the request that was submitted. Typically it will be Passed / Failed.
- **RequestId**: The request id of the API request that was made. Not returned in case of Search operation.
- **Output_file**: Returned in case of Search operation.It returns array of all files that matched the search criteria.
- **Job_status**: Status of the operation that was requestion.
- **Exit_Code**: The exit code of the operation that was performed.
- **Error_code**: In case error occurs this output field is populated with the error code that is returned.
- **Job_status_info**: Contains hash of the response on the operation performed. Job_status, Error_code, Exit_code are part of Job_status_info.

##### Operating Instructions

In order to perform the API calls, provide all the mandatory inputs.\
Select the operation that needs to be performed and click refresh link.\
Provide all the mandatory attributes for the operation selected.\
Response is sent back as XML document and the string. This JSON string is converted to a hash and is stored in the plugin output.\
\

### Spreadsheet parser

![Spreadsheet parser icon](icons/SpreadsheetParser.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in allows workflows to read and process data from spreadsheet files such as Excel. It extracts the spreadsheet content into structured data that can be used in later steps.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.5 | Fix Input validation would fail with no real reason |
| 0.1.4 | Fix spreadsheet rows missing cells |
| 0.1.3 | Fix for parsing small spreadsheets |
| 0.1.2 | Fixed multisheet parsing |
| 0.1.1 | Fixed bug in the mark_warnings code |
| 0.1.0 | warning marking as \* made optional |
| 0.0.7 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.0.6 | Updating the code for nil fields as per Randy's diff to handle boolean and date fields |
| 0.0.5 | 2015-03-02 ML - New icon |
| 0.0.4 | reads large spreadsheets in chunks with reporting |
| 0.0.3 | modification of xlsx support to fix issues |
| 0.0.2 | fixes validation, corrects typo in GUI and fixes error handling of unknown formats |
| 0.0.1 | first tracked version |

### How It Works

The SpreadsheetParser plug-in reads the specified file, then organizes its contents by rows, columns, or individual cells. You can choose how the data is indexed (by headers, column names, etc.) and whether to include header rows/columns.

### Configuration Instructions

Create a new action step using the **SpreadsheetParser** plug-in.

Fill out the following fields:

**Name**: A label for the step (e.g., `Parse Sheet`).

Comments: Optional notes about this configured instance.

**File Path**: The full path to the spreadsheet file. Dynamic attributes are supported. Optional if provided as input.

**Return First Sheet Only**: If checked, only the first sheet will be parsed.

**Organize Data**: Select how to organize data – by rows, columns, or cells.

**Index By**: Choose how to index data – by headers, header-row column names, header-column row names, or both rows and columns.

**Drop Header Row**: If checked, the top-most row will be excluded from results.

**Drop Header Column**: If checked, the left-most column will be excluded from results.

**Mark Untyped Entries with a Warning?**: Appends `*` to entries without a type if checked.

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify this SpreadsheetParser instance. |
| **Comments** | Optional comments about this instance. |
| **File Path** | Full path to the spreadsheet file. Dynamic attributes are supported. Optional if provided as input. |
| **Return First Sheet Only** | If checked, only the first sheet will be parsed. |
| **Organize Data** | Choose to organize data by rows, columns, or cells. |
| **Index By** | Select how to index data: by headers, header-row column names, header-column row names, or rows and columns. |
| **Drop Header Row** | If checked, removes the first row from results. |
| **Drop Header Column** | If checked, removes the first column from results. |
| **Mark Untyped Entries with a Warning?** | If checked, appends `*` to values with no type. |

### Inputs Description

The list of inputs depends on how the SpreadsheetParser action template is configured.

- **File_path**: All variables listed in the File Path field must be provided as input and must be of type `string`.

### Outputs Description

| Output | Description |
|----|----|
| **First Sheet** | Only available if 'Return First Sheet Only' is checked. Contains data from the first sheet structured as a hash. |
| **Sheet Name** | Name of the first sheet (only if 'Return First Sheet Only' is checked). |
| **Sheets** | All sheet data indexed by sheet name (if 'Return First Sheet Only' is not checked). |

**Note:** The structure of sheet data depends on the selected organization and indexing configuration.

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

None

### Operating Instructions

A parsed spreadsheet is returned as a hash. Its structure depends on the **Organize By** setting:

- **By Rows**: Hash keys are row identifiers; values are nested hashes (columns).
- **By Columns**: Hash keys are column identifiers; values are nested hashes (rows).
- **By Cells**: Flat hash with spreadsheet cell ID as keys (e.g., `A1`, `B3`).

Indexing also depends on the chosen **Index By** option.

### Example Output

Given the following spreadsheet:

    Date        Time    Time cost   Expenses   Total
    11/01/2012  4       1200        450        1650
    11/02/2012  3       900         0          900
    11/03/2012  4.5     1350        550        1900
    TOTAL       11.5    3450        1000       4450

**Organize by rows - index by rows and columns**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        5 => {"A" => "TOTAL", "B" => 11.5, "C" => 3450, "D" => 1000, "E" => 4450 },
        1 => {"A" => "Date", "B" => "Time", "C" => "Time cost", "D" => "Expense", "E" => "Total" },
        2 => {"A" => Thu, 01 Nov 2012, "B" => 4, "C" => 1200, "D" => 450, "E" => 1650 },
        3 => {"A" => Fri, 02 Nov 2012, "B" => 3, "C" => 900, "D" => 0, "E" => 900 },
        4 => {"A" => Sat, 03 Nov 2012, "B" => 4.5, "C" => 1350, "D" => 550, "E" => 1900 }
      }
    }
      

**Organize by rows - index by header-row columns name**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        5 => { "Expense" => 1000, "Time cost" => 3450, "Time" => 11.5, "Date" => "TOTAL", "Total" => 4450 },
        1 => { "Expense" => "Expense", "Time cost" => "Time cost", "Time" => "Time", "Date" => "Date", "Total" => "Total" },
        2 => { "Expense" => 450, "Time cost" => 1200, "Time" => 4, "Date" => Thu, 01 Nov 2012, "Total" => 1650 },
        3 => { "Expense" => 0, "Time cost" => 900, "Time" => 3, "Date" => Fri, 02 Nov 2012, "Total" => 900 },
        4 => { "Expense" => 550, "Time cost" => 1350, "Time" => 4.5, "Date" => Sat, 03 Nov 2012, "Total" => 1900 }
      }
    }
      

**Organize by rows - index by header-column rows name**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        "Date" => {...},
        "Thu, 01 Nov 2012" => {...},
        "Fri, 02 Nov 2012" => {...},
        "Sat, 03 Nov 2012" => {...},
        "TOTAL" => {...}
      }
    }
      

**Organize by rows - index by headers**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        "Thu, 01 Nov 2012" => {...},
        "Fri, 02 Nov 2012" => {...},
        ...
      }
    }
      

**Organize by cells - index by rows and columns**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        "A1" => "Date", "B1" => "Time", "C1" => "Time cost", ...
      }
    }
      

**Organize by columns - index by rows and columns**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        "A" => {1 => "Date", 2 => ..., ...},
        "B" => {...}, ...
      }
    }
      

**Organize by columns - index by header-row columns name**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        "Date" => {...},
        "Time" => {...}, ...
      }
    }
      

**Organize by columns - index by header-column rows name**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        "A" => {...},
        "B" => {...}, ...
      }
    }
      

**Organize by columns - index by headers**

    "Sheets" => {
      "Example" => {
        "Date" => {...},
        "Expense" => {...}, ...
      }
    }
      

### PGP encryption

![PGP encryption icon](icons/SymantecPgp.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in can be used to encyrpt/decrypt programatically a file using the symantec pgp command line tool.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.2 | Added Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.3.1 | Adds support for temporary licenses when in Symantec mode |
| 0.3.0 | New icon |
| 0.2.9 | Prevents auto-complete of user field |
| 0.2.8 | Adds display name |
| 0.2.7 | adds functionality to mask password |
| 0.2.6 | fix inputs_spec |
| 0.2.5 | change default naming in configurator |
| 0.2.4 | returns execution outputs |
| 0.2.3 | outputs edge case fix |
| 0.2.2 | accounted for special case of self extracting in the default extensions |
| 0.2.1 | added quotation to passphrase, tuned error reporting and made option a payload |
| 0.2.0 | added gpg mode |
| 0.1.1 | use of module_ssh_tools |
| 0.1.0 | added pgp_binary_path and options |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

#### How It Works

- This action plug-in allows you to encrypt or decrypt files programmatically.
- It uses the Symantec PGP Command Line tool for secure encryption and decryption operations.
- Simply provide the file path and required parameters to perform the desired action.
- Ideal for environments where data protection and compliance are critical.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **SymantecPgp** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved SymantecPgp configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved SymantecPgp configured instance.
    - : The source file(s) for the PGP operation. Supports multiple files, comma-separated.
    - : The destination file(s) for the PGP operation. Supports multiple files, comma-separated.
    - : The passphrase to use for the PGP operation.
    - : Defines whether PGP should encrypt or decrypt the source file to the destination file.
    - : The default timeout for the remote PGP operation. If not specified, the default engine value will be used.
    - : In this mode, the action operates with behavior and output closely aligned to standard GNU PG (GnuPG) conventions, ensuring interoperability with systems and workflows that rely on traditional GPG.
    - : The node where the PGP operation must be executed.
    - : The user account used to execute the PGP operation. If not specified, the default user account for the node will be used.
    - : The password used to log in to the remote node. If not specified, the default password for the node will be used.
    - : The path to locate the Symantec PGP binary on the remote node. Not needed if Symantec PGP is in the system path.
    - : Any optional parameters supported by the Symantec PGP binary. These options will be passed as they are.

#### Example Configuration

This is a minimum setup configuration for the **SymantecPgp** plugin.

| Parameter        | Value                |
|------------------|----------------------|
| Source file path | /project/path/secret |
| Target file path | /home/backup         |
| Passphrase       | passphrase           |
| PGP Operation    | encrypt              |
| Execution Node   | Configured node      |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved SymantecPgp configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved SymantecPgp configured instance. |
| **Source file path** | The source file(s) for the PGP operation. Supports multiple files, comma-separated. |
| **Target file path** | The destination file(s) for the PGP operation. Supports multiple files, comma-separated. |
| **Passphrase** | The passphrase to use for the PGP operation. |
| **PGP Operation** | Defines whether PGP should encrypt or decrypt the source file to the destination file. |
| **Timeout** | The default timeout for the remote PGP operation. If not specified, the default engine value will be used. |
| **Gpg compatibility mode** | In this mode, the action operates with behavior and output closely aligned to standard GNU PG (GnuPG) conventions, ensuring interoperability with systems and workflows that rely on traditional GPG. |
| **Execution Node** | The node where the PGP operation must be executed. |
| **Execution Login** | The user account used to execute the PGP operation. If not specified, the default user account for the node will be used. |
| **Execution Password** | The password used to log in to the remote node. If not specified, the default password for the node will be used. |
| **Path to the PGP binary** | The path to locate the Symantec PGP binary on the remote node. Not needed if Symantec PGP is in the system path. |
| **Optional parameters** | Any optional parameters supported by the Symantec PGP binary. These options will be passed as they are. |

#### Inputs Description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the SymantecPgp action template. If dynamic attributes are used in the message definition, each attribute becomes a required input.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *source_file_path (mandatory)* | The source file(s) for the PGP operation. Supports multiple files, comma-separated. |
| *passphrase (mandatory)* | The passphrase to use for the PGP operation. |
| *options* | Any optional parameters supported by the Symantec PGP binary. These options will be passed as they are. |
| *target_file_path* | The destination file(s) for the PGP operation. Supports multiple files, comma-separated. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| processed_file_path | The path(s) of the file(s) produced by the PGP operation. If multiple files are produced, they are separated by commas. |
| passphrase | The passphrase to use for the PGP operation. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

This action executes the specified PGP operation on a remote node where Symantec PGP is installed. It enables secure encryption or decryption of a file located on that remote node using the provided passphrase. The process ensures that sensitive data remains protected during transmission and storage. By leveraging Symantec PGP’s command-line capabilities, this action supports automated workflows and compliance with security standards.

### Xml Parsing

![Xml Parsing icon](icons/XmlParsing.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in extracts data from XML documents using XPath expressions. It supports XML input from text strings or file paths, allowing multiple configurable parameters to be exposed as workflow outputs.

| Version | Comment                                                         |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.2   | Fix issues when storing xpaths (Eg://root/node/subnode)         |
| 0.1.1   | Fix issues when storing certain string characters (Like colons) |
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8             |
| 0.0.5   | Added raw to parameters field                                   |
| 0.0.4   | Ensure file is closed if used as input for the xml              |
| 0.0.3   | Supports run time inputs for xml and xml file                   |
| 0.0.2   | Fixed bugs from initial version                                 |
| 0.0.1   | initial version                                                 |

### How It Works

- Parses XML documents using XPath expressions to extract specific data elements.
- Supports both XML input as a text string or from an external file path.
- Allows configuring multiple parameters, each with its own XPath expression, name, and data type.
- Extracts values based on XPath queries and exposes them as workflow outputs.
- Handles XML documents with or without namespaces using appropriate XPath syntax.
- No limit on the number of output parameters that can be configured.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`XML Parsing`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the XML source:`
    - **`Accept XML as String?`**`: Check to enter XML directly in a text box, uncheck to specify a file path.`
    - **`Xml file as string`**`: Enter XML content directly (if checkbox is checked).`
    - **`Xml file path`**`: Specify path to XML file (if checkbox is unchecked).`
3.  `Add parameters to extract data:`
    - **`Add Parameter`**`: Click to add new extraction parameters.`
    - **`Name`**`: Define the output parameter name for the workflow.`
    - **`XPath`**`: Specify the XPath expression to locate the data.`
    - **`Type`**`: Select the expected data type (String, Array, etc.).`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                 | Example Value                         |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| **Accept XML as String?** | unchecked (using file path)           |
| **Xml file path**         | /opt/aspera/orchestrator/xml_test.xml |
| **Parameter 1 - Name**    | empName                               |
| **Parameter 1 - XPath**   | //Employee/name                       |
| **Parameter 1 - Type**    | String/Text                           |
| **Parameter 2 - Name**    | empRole                               |
| **Parameter 2 - XPath**   | //Employee/role                       |
| **Parameter 2 - Type**    | String/Text                           |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Accept XML as String?** | Checkbox to choose input method: checked = enter XML in text box, unchecked = provide file path. |
| **Xml file as string** | Text area to enter XML content directly when 'Accept XML as String?' is checked. |
| **Xml file path** | File path to XML document when 'Accept XML as String?' is unchecked. |
| **Add Parameter** | Button to add new extraction parameters for defining outputs. |
| **Type** | Data type for each parameter: specifies expected format of extracted data (String, Array, etc.). |
| **Name** | Parameter name that will be exposed as an output in the workflow. |
| **XPath** | XPath expression used to parse and extract data from the XML document. |

### Inputs Description

Usually this plugin does not have runtime specified parameters, unless an external file is used for the configuration, in which case the file path can be specified at runtime.

- **Xml file path**: File path to XML document (only when external file configuration is used at runtime).

### Outputs Description

- **Configured Parameters**: All specified parameters will be available as outputs based on configuration.
- **Example: empName**: Employee name extracted using XPath (e.g., 'Alice').
- **Example: empRole**: Employee role extracted using XPath (e.g., 'Developer').
- **Step_information**: Processing details including step status, retrieved parameter count, and execution metadata.

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

- No external dependencies. Uses standard XPath processing capabilities.

### Operating Instructions

#### Overview

This action provides parsed outputs from XML using XPath. There is no limit to the number of outputs and it can be requested by the user at the plugin configuration as Parameters. Each Parameter would need an XPath (the expression used to parse the XML provided), Name (to identify the parsed output), Type (the user should know the type of parsed output expected from the XPath expression).

#### What is XPath?

- XPath is a syntax for defining parts of an XML document. It uses path expressions to navigate in XML documents, and it contains a library of standard functions.

#### Basic Example

    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <Employees>
      <Employee id="3">
        <age>40</age>
        <name>Tom</name>
        <gender>Male</gender>
        <role>Manager</role>
      </Employee>
      <Employee id="4">
        <age>25</age>
        <name>Meghna</name>
        <gender>Female</gender>
        <role>Manager</role>
      </Employee>
    </Employees>

To retrieve the age of all employees in an Array from the above XML, add a Parameter with Type as Array and XPath as:

    //age

To retrieve the details of Employee with an attribute of id = 4 in a String from the above XML, add a Parameter with Type as String and XPath as:

    //Employee[@id = '4']

#### Example with Namespaces

    <feed xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
      <title>Example Feed</title>
      <link href="http://example.org/"/>
      <updated>2003-12-13T18:30:02Z</updated>
      <author>
        <name>John Doe</name>
      </author>
      <id>urn:uuid:60a76c80-d399-11d9-b93C-0003939e0af6</id>
    </feed>

To retrieve the name of the author from the above XML, add a Parameter with Type String and XPath as:

    //xmlns:author/xmlns:name

#### Additional Resources

For more information on XPath:\
[Nokogiri XPath Tutorial](http://www.nokogiri.org/tutorials/searching_a_xml_html_document.html)

There are even tools to evaluate XPath from an XML. Example:\
<http://xmltoolbox.appspot.com/xpath_generator.html>

## File Transfer

### Ascp client

![Ascp client icon](icons/AscpClient.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plugin performs a file transfer upload or download operation to/from a server using Aspera's FASP protocol. In order for it to work, an Aspera client or server product needs to be installed on the Orchestrator server.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.6.1 | Removing legacy support |
| 1.6.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.5.2 | Removing windows support code |
| 1.5.1 | Fixed remote node specification as input |
| 1.5.0 | Added cookie |
| 1.4.1 | Added ascp error cause in status details |
| 1.4.0 | Added option to specify an authentication token |
| 1.3.1 | Set display name with correct capitalization |
| 1.3.0 | disable management port connection option added |
| 1.2.4 | ruby 2.3 converting array to string support |
| 1.2.3 | Support both remote node by name or by id (name has precedence) |
| 1.2.2 | Calculating percent complete and passing it to report_progress. |
| 1.2.1 | Support for a file-list as source for upload operation. |
