| 1.2.0 | Support for a file-list as source. |
| 1.1.9 | Added styling to help text. |
| 1.1.8 | Adding RemoteNode Dependency. |
| 1.1.7 | fixed problem that was preventing passing the host as a parameter |
| 1.1.6 | prevents autocomplete of user in UI |
| 1.1.5 | fixed a missing end to a if-end statement which was causing SyntaxError |
| 1.1.4 | fixed problem when remote node is specified in the template |
| 1.1.3 | fixed run time host specification |
| 1.1.2 | run time host can either be an ip address or a RemoteHost name |
| 1.1.1 | removes password from carried thru input |
| 1.1.0 | only carry thru inputs if explicitly specified. help file added |
| 1.0.1 | returns list of transferred files |
| 1.0.0 | uses Aspera process, instead of popen, to isolate environment variable used for the password |
| 0.1.0 | specifies the management port |
| 0.0.10 | protects against management socket creation failures, change defaults display in UI |
| 0.0.9 | improves file path escaping |
| 0.0.8 | fixes download command line |
| 0.0.7 | fixes issue when non-standard port were configured in the node |
| 0.0.6 | adds functionality to mask password |
| 0.0.5 | adds help file |
| 0.0.4 | fixes escaping, auto converts path on windows install, fix input_spec for ad-hoc nodes |
| 0.0.3 | fixes typo in template configurator and add clarifications |
| 0.0.2 | modification of failure handling |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

### How It Works

- Performs high-speed Aspera FASP uploads and downloads between the local Aspera Orchestrator server and remote Aspera servers.
- Supports single and multiple file transfers with comprehensive source file specification options including arrays and file lists.
- Integrates with various Aspera server types including Enterprise Server, Connect Server, and Point-to-Point configurations.
- Provides flexible authentication methods including traditional password-based and token-based authentication for secure transfers.
- Utilizes ascp command-line functionality with full support for all ascp options and parameters for advanced transfer control.
- Includes management connection capabilities for real-time transfer monitoring and status reporting with configurable port ranges.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`ASCP Client`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the remote Aspera server connection:`
    - **`Aspera server node`**`: Select from defined remote nodes with Aspera Server installed.`
    - **`Remote Node details - Server Address`**`: Provide alternate IP address if needed.`
    - **`Remote Node details - Aspera server user/password`**`: Enter credentials if different from node definition.`
3.  `Set up transfer parameters:`
    - **`Operation`**`: Choose Upload or Download from Orchestrator server's perspective.`
    - **`Source files`**`: Specify files using array format or semicolon-delimited list.`
    - **`Destination path`**`: Set target directory (must be directory for multiple files).`
4.  `Configure transfer optimization:`
    - **`Target Rate`**`: Set maximum transfer rate (supports G/M/K suffixes and percentages).`
    - **`Command line options`**`: Add additional ascp options as needed.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter | Example Value |
|----|----|
| **Aspera server node** | aspera-remote-01 |
| **Remote Node details - Server Address** | 192.168.1.100 |
| **Remote Node details - ssh port** | 22 |
| **Remote Node details - Aspera server user** | aspera_user |
| **Operation** | Upload |
| **Source files** | \['/data/file1.mov', '/data/file2.mov'\] |
| **Destination path** | /remote/uploads/ |
| **Target Rate** | 100M |
| **Use token authentication** | unchecked |
| **Management port base** | 33001 |
| **Management port range** | 100 |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Aspera server node** | The node with which the local Aspera Orchestrator server will transfer. That node needs to have an Aspera Server installed on it (Aspera Enterprise Server, Aspera Connect or Aspera Point-to-Point). |
| **Remote Node details - Server Address** | Alternate remote Aspera Server IP address - will override the node address if provided. |
| **Remote Node details - ssh port** | If the remote Aspera Server is not listening on TCP/22 and the node is not configured with the target SSH port, enter the alternate port here. |
| **Remote Node details - Aspera server user** | The Aspera user account on the remote side used for the file transfer, if different from the one used in the node definition. |
| **Remote Node details - Aspera server password** | The password for the Aspera user account on the remote side used for the file transfer, if different from the one used in the node definition. |
| **Token Authentication - Use token authentication** | If selected, token authentication will be used instead of password authentication. |
| **Token Authentication - Token** | The token to use for authentication when token authentication is enabled. |
| **Cookie** | This is required for a transfer to Faspex to display it as complete within Faspex. |
| **Operation** | This is either Upload or Download from the Aspera Orchestrator server's perspective. |
| **File-List as source** | Check this if you want to use the ASCP File List option: the path of the file should be given as the source. The file-list is a file that contains the list of source filepaths. This is a good option to use when there are a lot of files to be downloaded or uploaded. |
| **Source files** | This input is an array of file names to be transferred. It can be in normal Ruby syntax \['/example/file_1', '/example/file_2'\], semicolon delimited list '/example/file_1;/example/file_2', or if file list option is checked, it should be the path of the file list: example/list_file.txt. |
| **Destination path** | This is typically a directory. If multiple files are transferred, this must be a directory. Only if you are transferring a single file can this be a file name – keep in mind that the next transfer would overwrite the file. |
| **Target Rate** | The target (max) transfer rate. This is in the format used for the –l command-line option. It accepts suffixes 'G/g' for Giga, 'M/m' for Mega, 'K/k' for Kilo, and 'P/p/%' for percentage. Decimals are allowed, e.g. '.1G' for 100 Mbit/s. |
| **Command line options** | Other ascp command line options. For more information, see man ascp(1). |
| **Disable Management Connection?** | This option is provided to avoid management connection in case of any socket issues. |
| **Management port - Management port base** | The starting port number of the range of ports that can be used to open the management socket to monitor the transfer status. |
| **Management port - Management port range** | The range of ports that can be used starting at the base. The management port will be selected at random between base and base+range. |
| **Legacy support - Carry thru all inputs** | If checked, all inputs will also be provided as outputs. |

### Source Files Format Options

- **Normal Ruby syntax:** \['/example/file_1', '/example/file_2', '/example/file_3'\]
- **Semicolon delimited list:** /example/file_1;/example/file_2;/example/file_3
- **File list (when File-List as source is checked):** example/list_file.txt

### Inputs Description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the AscpClient action template as the fields for the paths and options all support dynamic inputs. As well, if values are set in the template, they are not shown when you right-click the step and click Map Inputs. By default, when nothing is set, the following inputs are available:

- **Destination_path**: The path on the remote side.
- **Files**: An array of source files to be transferred - a string value would be accepted if the format matches the ones described above.
- **Operation**: Upload or Download operation type.
- **Remote_host**: The IP address of the remote system.
- **Remote_user**: The Aspera user to be used for the transfer.
- **Remote_user_password**: The password for the Aspera user used for the transfer (only if token authentication not selected).
- **Token**: The token to use for authentication if token authentication is selected.
- **Target_rate**: Target rate for the transfer.
- **Ssh_port**: The SSH port used on the remote system.
- **Command_line_options**: Any other ascp command line options.

**Note:** All the required and optional inputs defined in the template body and target file path will also be requested.

### Outputs Description

- **Session ID**: The Aspera session ID for the transfer. This UUID is a string composed of series of hexadecimal characters (e.g., e1e1a704-9080-4a34-a3fa-33d250d90132).
- **Transferred files**: An array of the local path to the files transferred.
- **Transferred bytes**: The total number of bytes transferred (as an integer).

### Supported Actions

- Upload files from local Orchestrator server to remote Aspera server
- Download files from remote Aspera server to local Orchestrator server
- Single and multiple file transfers
- File list-based transfers for bulk operations
- Transfer monitoring and status reporting

### Operating Instructions

- **Transfer Types:** This action performs an Aspera FASP upload or download between the local Aspera Orchestrator server on which an Aspera client or server is also installed. Single or multiple files can be transferred.
- **Command Line Integration:** This action supports all ascp command line options, providing full flexibility for advanced transfer scenarios and optimization.
- **Debugging Support:** Since this action uses the ascp command line behind the scene, it is very useful to access the Aspera log for debugging. That log is typically located in /var/log/messages on the remote server.
- **Doc Root Enforcement:** Since the transfer is done using Aspera, the doc roots set in place for the Aspera user under which Orchestrator runs will be enforced on the local side. Same applies for the remote user on the remote side.
- **Authentication Options:** Choose between traditional password authentication or token-based authentication based on your security requirements and server configuration.
- **File List Usage:** When dealing with large numbers of files, use the File-List option to specify a file containing the list of source file paths rather than listing all files individually.
- **Management Connection:** The management connection can be disabled if socket issues occur, but this will prevent real-time transfer monitoring capabilities.

### Dependencies

- Aspera client or server software installed on the local Orchestrator server.
- Remote Aspera Server (Enterprise Server, Connect, or Point-to-Point) installed and configured on the target node.
- Network connectivity between local and remote systems on the configured SSH port (default 22).
- Valid user accounts and authentication credentials on both local and remote Aspera servers.
- Proper doc root configurations and file system permissions for both source and destination paths.
- Available management ports in the specified range for transfer monitoring (if management connection is enabled).

### Troubleshooting

- If transfers fail to start, verify that the remote Aspera server is running and accessible on the specified IP address and SSH port.
- For authentication failures, confirm that user credentials are correct and that the user has appropriate permissions on both local and remote systems.
- If file path errors occur, ensure that source files exist and are accessible, and that destination paths have proper write permissions.
- For token authentication issues, verify that the token is valid and that the remote server supports token-based authentication.
- If management connection fails, try disabling the management connection option or check that the specified port range is available and not blocked by firewalls.
- For transfer rate issues, verify that the target rate format is correct (use suffixes G/M/K for Giga/Mega/Kilo or % for percentage).
- Check Aspera logs at /var/log/messages on the remote server for detailed error information and transfer status.
- Ensure that doc root configurations allow access to both source and destination file paths on respective systems.

### Aspera Node API Transfer

![Aspera Node API Transfer icon](icons/AsperaNodeApiTransferOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plugin provides the ability to initiate and monitor server-to-server transfers using Aspera's Node API cluster protocol.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.3.1 | Add retry logic for Node API connection failures and timeouts |
| 1.3.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.2.7 | Allows to cancel a transfer |
| 1.2.6 | Fixed problem with src_base wrongly spelled |
| 1.2.5 | Added completed and failed file list |
| 1.2.4 | Added percent_complete support |
| 1.2.3 | Support the form hostname:port in node address |
| 1.2.2 | Fixes error in computing state in some edge conditions |
| 1.2.1 | Detail reasons for error while pulling status |
| 1.2.0 | Add configurable path separator character |
| 1.1.0 | Add src base option for transfers |
| 1.0.0 | Add option for verbose logging |
| 0.9.5 | Set http proxy on instance level, not global level |
| 0.9.4 | Improved logging |
| 0.9.3 | Verify status when using bytes written |
| 0.9.2 | Added remote user only if username present |
| 0.9.1 | Added remote user in token based auth case |
| 0.9.0 | Added FASP and HTTP Proxy URL |
| 0.8.2 | Use bytes written to check if transfer complete |
| 0.8.1 | Add status details for debug logging |
| 0.8.0 | Fixed rate policy default value and exposed at runtime |
| 0.7.0 | Add encryption at rest support |
| 0.6.3 | Fix issue with remote key path |
| 0.6.2 | Added option to force rate policy at runtime |
| 0.6.1 | Work around for rate policy not exposed at runtime |
| 0.6.0 | Added rate policy to default transfer parameter |
| 0.5.4 | adding backward compatibility to partially completed status |
| 0.5.3 | Catch REST timeouts and force retry on next polling |
| 0.5.2 | Node status change from "partially completed" to partially_completed fixed. |
| 0.5.1 | new output - files transferred |
| 0.5.0 | made response hash output optional |
| 0.4.0 | adding key based authentication |
| 0.3.0 | Changing remote_token column from boolean to type string |
| 0.2.1 | Changing code to read port/username/password from configuration instead of remote node. |
| 0.2.0 | Adding option to monitor a job's progress based on id, adding exception handling in progress calculation and default to verify ssl as false |
| 0.1.2 | Adding realtime info to status details - helpful during long transfers |
| 0.1.1 | Adding polling frequency get method definition |
| 0.1.0 | Adding Remote Token to the json structure |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

### How It Works

- Initiates and monitors FASP transfers between Aspera nodes using the Node API for communication and control.
- Supports multiple operation modes: parameter-based transfers, custom JSON payload transfers, and transfer monitoring.
- Handles various authentication methods including SSH keys, access keys/secrets, and Bearer/Basic tokens.
- Works with different Aspera environments: node-to-node transfers, ATS (Aspera Transfer Service), and Aspera On Cloud.
- Provides comprehensive transfer monitoring with configurable polling frequency and detailed status reporting.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`AsperaNodeApiTransferOperation`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the service node (initiating node) parameters:`
    - **`Name`**`: Enter a descriptive name for the transfer operation.`
    - **`Service node`**`: Select configured node or leave empty for manual configuration.`
    - **`Service node IP`**`: IP address or domain name of the initiating node (if Service Node is not provided).`
    - **`User login`**`: Node API username or access key for authentication.`
    - **`User password`**`: Node API password or secret key for authentication.`
    - **`Use ssl`**`: Check for HTTPS (port 9092), uncheck for HTTP (port 9091).`
3.  `Set up transfer operation and remote host details:`
    - **`Operation`**`: Choose 'submit transfer using parameters', 'submit transfer using payload', or 'monitor transfer'.`
    - **`Direction`**`: Select 'Send' or 'Receive' for transfer direction.`
    - **`Remote host`**`: Hostname or IP address of the remote Aspera server.`
    - **`Auth type`**`: Choose authentication method (password, SSH key, or token).`
    - **`Source`**`: Source file paths (use path separator for multiple files).`
    - **`Destination root`**`: Destination directory path.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter             | Example Value                    |
|-----------------------|----------------------------------|
| **Name**              | Node Transfer Operation          |
| **Service node**      | transfer-node-01                 |
| **Use ssl**           | checked                          |
| **Operation**         | submit transfer using parameters |
| **Direction**         | Send                             |
| **Remote host**       | destination.example.com          |
| **Auth type**         | SSH key                          |
| **Remote user**       | xfer                             |
| **Remote key path**   | /opt/aspera/keys/transfer_key    |
| **Source**            | /data/upload/file1.txt           |
| **Destination root**  | /incoming                        |
| **Target rate Kbps**  | 100000                           |
| **Create dir**        | checked                          |
| **Polling frequency** | 10                               |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Display name for this transfer operation step. |
| **Service node** | Initiating node configured with IP, port, username, and password for Node API access. |
| **Service node IP** | IP address, domain name, or address:port of initiating node (when Service Node not provided). |
| **Service node port** | Node API port on initiating node (when Service Node not provided). |
| **User login** | Node API user login or access key (when Service Node not provided). Use access key if restrictions configured in aspera.conf. |
| **User password** | Node API password or secret key (when Service Node not provided). Use secret key if restrictions configured in aspera.conf. |
| **Use ssl** | Enable HTTPS on port 9092, disable for HTTP on port 9091. |
| **Operation** | Operation type: 'submit transfer using parameters' (individual parameters), 'submit transfer using payload' (custom JSON), or 'monitor transfer'. |
| **Response hash as output?** | Return job status response data. Uncheck for multiple file transfers to avoid large outputs. |
| **Direction** | Transfer direction: Send or Receive. |
| **Remote host** | Remote host name or IP address (address:port format not supported here). |
| **Auth type** | Authentication method: password, SSH key, or token. |
| **Remote access key** | Access key for remote host authentication. |
| **Remote secret key** | Secret key for remote host authentication. |
| **Remote user** | Remote username (default: xfer). |
| **Remote password** | Password for remote user authentication. |
| **Token** | Authentication token for remote host access. Precede with 'Basic' or 'Bearer' based on token type. |
| **Remote key path** | Path to private SSH key file on Orchestrator server. |
| **Remote key passphrase** | Passphrase for SSH key if configured. |
| **Source root id** | ID of source root (only for transfers to Aspera On Cloud). |
| **Destination root id** | ID of destination root (only for transfers to Aspera On Cloud). |
| **Destination root** | Destination directory file path (not applicable for Aspera On Cloud transfers). |
| **Source** | Source file paths separated by path separator character for multiple files. |
| **Path separator** | Character used to separate multiple paths (default: semicolon). |
| **Src Base** | Source base path applied to all sources above. |
| **Target rate Kbps** | Transfer rate in kilobits per second. |
| **Fasp port** | FASP UDP port value (default: 33001). |
| **SSH port** | FASP SSH port value (default: 33001). |
| **Multi session** | Maximum number of simultaneous transfer sessions (default: 1). |
| **Delete source** | Delete source files after successful transfer. |
| **Create dir** | Create destination directory if it does not exist. |
| **Rate policy** | Rate policy for the transfer (e.g., high, fair, low). |
| **EAR operation** | Encryption at rest operation: none, encrypt, decrypt, or --Runtime--. |
| **EAR passphrase** | Passphrase for encryption at rest operations. |
| **Payload** | Custom JSON payload when 'submit transfer using payload' operation is chosen. |
| **FASP Proxy** | FASP Forward Proxy setting (format: dnats://node_user:node_password@proxy_addr:9092/). |
| **HTTP Proxy** | HTTP proxy for Node API server calls (format: https://proxy:port/). |
| **Verbose logging?** | Enable detailed request/response logging to Node API (use only for debugging). |
| **Polling frequency** | Frequency in seconds for polling job status during monitoring. |

### Inputs Description

- **Source**: Required source file paths for transfer operations.
- **Service Node information**: Required node connection details (IP, port, credentials) when not using predefined service node.
- **Remote Host**: Required remote host connection information including authentication details.
- **EAR operation**: Optional encryption at rest operation when EAR operation is set to --Runtime--.
- **Authentication parameters**: Required authentication details based on chosen auth type (password, SSH key, or token).

### Outputs Description

- **Job id**: Unique identifier of the initiated transfer.
- **Job_Status_Response_Received**: Raw response received for job status queries.
- **Transfer_Upload_Response_Received**: Raw response received for transfer upload operations.
- **Transferred file count**: Number of files successfully transferred.
- **Transferred file list**: List of successfully transferred files (destination paths relative to docroot).
- **Failed file list**: List of files that failed to transfer (destination paths relative to docroot).

### Supported Actions

- Submit transfer using parameters
- Submit transfer using payload
- Monitor transfer

### Dependencies

- Aspera Transfer Server or Transfer Endpoint with valid license on both service and remote nodes.
- Node API enabled and accessible on the service node.
- Network connectivity between Orchestrator and service node for API calls.
- FASP connectivity between service node and remote host for file transfers.
- The aspweb user must have proper permissions to read SSH key files when using key-based authentication.

### Operating Instructions

#### Transfer Architecture

- **Service node:** The initiating node where Orchestrator uses Node API to start and monitor transfers.
- **Remote host:** The destination node serving the transfers using FASP protocol for actual file transfer.
- **Protocol flow:** Node API for control, FASP protocol for data transfer between nodes.

#### Custom Payload Examples

##### Node-to-Node Transfer with SSH Key

    {
      "direction":"send",
      "remote_user":"root",
      "remote_host":"testchris5.aspera.cloud",
      "ssh_private_key":"-----BEGIN DSA PRIVATE KEY-----\r\nMIIBugIBAAKBgQ5ypo9iLqM=\r\n-----END DSA PRIVATE KEY-----",
      "destination_root":"/",
      "fasp_port":33001,
      "ssh_port":33001,
      "target_rate_kbps":"",
      "multi_session":1,
      "create_dir":true,
      "delete_source":false,
      "rate_policy":"high",
      "paths":[{ "source": "/home/aspera_files/tutu.txt" }]
    }

##### ATS Upload with Basic Token

    {
      "direction": "send",
      "remote_host": "w.demo.aspera.org",
      "remote_access_key": "ABCDEFGH",
      "token": "Basic XXXXYYYYYZZZZZ=",
      "target_rate_kbps": "",
      "create_dir": false,
      "ssh_port": "33001",
      "fasp_port": "33001",
      "destination_root" : "/",
      "remote_user": "xfer",
      "multi_session": "1",
      "delete_source": "false",
      "paths": [ { "source": "/temp.mpg" } ]
    }

##### Aspera On Cloud Package Upload with Bearer Token

    {
      "direction": "send",
      "remote_host": "testchris5.aspera.cloud",
      "token": "Bearer eJwlkEmzmkAAhO/8DK7EvGHYr",
      "target_rate_kbps": 1000000,
      "create_dir": true,
      "ssh_port": "33001",
      "fasp_port": "33001",
      "delete_source": "false",
      "paths": [{ "source": "/data_to_files/small.xml" }, { "source": "/data_to_files/small.mp4" }],
      "tags": {
        "aspera": {
          "node": {
            "file_id": "72",
            "access_key": "jjgawSlKQfISWpdRnWFOuxArb_QoE4qDX_yXKEmy05k"
          },
          "files": {
            "package_id": "BnfkYxtl8Q",
            "package_operation": "upload"
          }
        }
      }
    }

#### Authentication Methods

- **SSH Key:** Use private key file path and optional passphrase for key-based authentication.
- **Access Key/Secret:** Use access key and secret key pairs for ATS and cloud environments.
- **Token:** Use Bearer or Basic tokens for API-based authentication (precede with token type).
- **Password:** Use username and password for basic authentication.

#### Runtime Configuration

- EAR operation can be specified as runtime input when set to --Runtime-- in template configuration.
- Source paths support multiple files using the configured path separator character.
- Enable verbose logging only for debugging to prevent crowding log files.
- Response hash output should be disabled for large multi-file transfers to prevent oversized outputs.

### Aspera Out-of-Transfer File Validation

![Aspera Out-of-Transfer File Validation icon](icons/AsperaOtfv.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides the ability to retrieve the list of files to validate and update the file status during validation.

| Version | Comment                                                    |
|---------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.1   | Updated the label from Keep ongoing to Continue Monitoring |
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8        |
| 0.0.1   | Initial implementation                                     |

### How It Works

- Provides Out-of-transfer file validation (OTFV) capabilities for Aspera file transfers by interfacing with Node API to manage file validation workflows.
- Supports two primary operations: retrieving lists of files pending validation and updating file status during validation processes.
- Enables continuous monitoring through configurable polling with validator ID management to prevent simultaneous validation conflicts.
- Integrates with High-Speed Transfer Server (HSTS) to validate files during transfer operations with real-time status updates.
- Provides flexible connection options including remote node selection, SSL support, and customizable API credentials for secure validation workflows.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`Aspera OTFV`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the Node API connection:`
    - **`Node ip address (or hostname)`**`: IP address or hostname of the Node API server.`
    - **`Remote node`**`: Select configured remote node or use manual IP address entry.`
    - **`Use remote node?`**`: Check to use remote node instead of manual IP address.`
    - **`Node user`**`: Username for Node API authentication.`
    - **`Node password`**`: Password for Node API authentication.`
3.  `Set up operation parameters:`
    - **`Operation`**`: Choose between 'Retrieve list of files' or 'Update file status'.`
    - **`Validator id`**`: Unique ID to prevent simultaneous validation conflicts.`
    - **`Max result`**`: Maximum number of files to collect per validation request.`
    - **`Continue Monitoring`**`: Enable continuous polling for file validation.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                         | Example Value          |
|-----------------------------------|------------------------|
| **Node ip address (or hostname)** | 192.168.1.100          |
| **Use remote node?**              | unchecked              |
| **Use ssl?**                      | checked                |
| **Node port**                     | 9092                   |
| **Node user**                     | aspera_validator       |
| **Operation**                     | Retrieve list of files |
| **Validator id**                  | validator_001          |
| **Max result**                    | 50                     |
| **Continue Monitoring**           | checked                |
| **Polling frequency**             | 30                     |
| **File status**                   | validating             |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Node ip address (or hostname)** | The IP address (or hostname) of Node API. |
| **Remote node** | The remote node from which to get the IP address (or hostname) of NodeAPI. |
| **Use remote node?** | If selected, 'Remote node' is used instead of 'Node ip address'. |
| **Use ssl?** | If selected, SSL will be used to connect to Node API. |
| **Node port** | The port of Node API. |
| **Node user** | The user of Node API. |
| **Node password** | The password of Node API. |
| **Operation** | The operation to execute. |
| **Validator id** | The unique ID to prevent simultaneous validation of the same file by different validators. |
| **Max result** | The maximum number of collected files for validation by each request. |
| **Continue Monitoring** | If selected, trigger will be executed every time files will be detected for validation. |
| **Polling frequency** | The interval in seconds to check for files to be validated. |
| **File status** | The file status to set. |
| **Session uuid** | The FASP session ID. |
| **File id** | The file ID. |
| **Bytes processed** | The number of bytes processed. |
| **Error code** | The custom error code. |
| **Error descrition** | The custom error description. |

### Inputs Description

#### Mandatory Inputs for 'Retrieve list of files' Operation

- **node_ip_address**: The IP address (or hostname) of Node API if 'Use remote node?' is not selected in configuration.
- **remote_node**: The remote node from which to get the IP address (or hostname) of NodeAPI if 'Use remote node?' is selected in configuration.
- **node_user**: The user of Node API.
- **node_password**: The password of Node API.

#### Mandatory Inputs for 'Update file status' Operation

- **node_ip_address**: The IP address (or hostname) of Node API if 'Use remote node?' is not selected in configuration.
- **remote_node**: The remote node from which to get the IP address (or hostname) of NodeAPI if 'Use remote node?' is selected in configuration.
- **node_user**: The user of Node API.
- **node_password**: The password of Node API.
- **File status**: The file status to set if 'file status' value is '--runtime--'.
- **session_uuid**: The FASP session ID.
- **file_id**: The file ID.

#### Optional Inputs for 'Retrieve list of files' Operation

- **node_port**: The port of Node API (default is '9092' if 'Use ssl?' is selected or '9091' if 'Use ssl?' is not selected).
- **validator_id**: The unique ID to prevent simultaneous validation of the same file by different validators (default is 'orchestrator').
- **max_result**: The maximum number of collected files for validation by each request (default is '20').
- **polling_frequency**: The interval in seconds to check for files to be validated if 'Continue Monitoring' is selected (default is '10').

#### Optional Inputs for 'Update file status' Operation

- **node_port**: The port of Node API (default is '9092' if 'Use ssl?' is selected or '9091' if 'Use ssl?' is not selected).
- **validator_id**: The unique ID to prevent simultaneous validation of the same file by different validators (default is 'orchestrator').
- **bytes_processed**: The number of bytes processed if 'file status' value is 'validating' or '--runtime--' (default is '0').
- **error_code**: The custom error code if 'file status' value is 'error' or '--runtime--' (default is '1').
- **error_decription**: The custom error description if 'file status' value is 'error' or '--runtime--' (default is 'error').

### Outputs Description

- **result_count**: The number of collected files for validation if 'Retrieve list of files' operation is selected.
- **file_transfer_info**: The list of files to be validated if 'Retrieve list of files' operation is selected.

### Operating Instructions

- OTFV (Out-of-transfer file validation) needs HSTS (High-Speed Transfer Server) to be configured for proper operation. See the dedicated topic in the High-Speed Transfer Server admin guide.
- The plugin supports two main operations: 'Retrieve list of files' for getting files pending validation, and 'Update file status' for updating the validation status of specific files.
- When using 'Continue Monitoring' option, the plugin will continuously poll for files at the specified polling frequency interval.
- Validator ID is crucial for preventing conflicts when multiple validators are processing the same files simultaneously.
- SSL connection is recommended for secure communication with the Node API, which uses port 9092 by default (9091 for non-SSL).
- File status values include: 'to_be_validated', 'validating', 'error', and 'complete' depending on the validation workflow state.

### Dependencies

- High-Speed Transfer Server (HSTS) must be properly configured for OTFV functionality.
- Node API service must be accessible and running on the specified server with appropriate credentials.
- Network connectivity between Orchestrator and the Node API server is required.
- The aspweb user must have proper permissions for API communication, as Orchestrator runs as aspweb instead of root.

### Console Smart Transfer

![Console Smart Transfer icon](icons/ConsoleSmartTransferOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plugin provides the ability to initiate Smart Transfer templates in Aspera Console or to list available templates.

| Version | Comment                                                  |
|---------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8      |
| 0.1.5   | Verify SSL set to false on polling                       |
| 0.1.4   | Verify SSL set to false                                  |
| 0.1.3   | Added new status of willretry to the check status method |
| 0.1.2   | Adding timestamp to the status details                   |
| 0.1.1   | Adding submitted status to the check_status check        |
| 0.1.0   | Add Monitor status operation                             |
| 0.0.3   | Fixing source path list from string to Array             |
| 0.0.2   | Fixing password field type as password                   |
| 0.0.1   | Initial Commit                                           |

### How It Works

- Connects to Aspera Console to invoke Smart Transfer templates with configurable transfer parameters.
- Supports two main operations: listing available Smart Transfer templates and submitting transfer requests.
- Provides asynchronous status monitoring with configurable polling frequency for submitted transfers.
- Handles source file specification with base path support for flexible file selection.
- Returns lists of templates, transfer IDs, and error information through structured output variables.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`ConsoleSmartTransferOperation`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the essential Console connection parameters:`
    - **`Name`**`: Enter a descriptive name for the step.`
    - **`Console IP`**`: IP address of the Aspera Console node (e.g., '192.168.1.100').`
    - **`Console port`**`: Console port number (default is 443).`
    - **`Login`**`: Username to access the console service.`
    - **`Password`**`: Password for console authentication.`
3.  `Set up the operation and transfer details:`
    - **`Operation`**`: Choose either 'List the Templates' or 'Submit the Transfer'.`
    - **`Smart Transfer ID`**`: The ID of the template to invoke (required for Submit operation).`
    - **`Transfer Name`**`: Descriptive name for the transfer.`
    - **`Monitor Status`**`: Check to enable asynchronous status monitoring.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter             | Example Value            |
|-----------------------|--------------------------|
| **Name**              | Smart Transfer Operation |
| **Console IP**        | 192.168.1.100            |
| **Console port**      | 443                      |
| **Login**             | console_user             |
| **Password**          | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*         |
| **Transfer Name**     | Data Backup Transfer     |
| **Transfer Comments** | Daily backup operation   |
| **Source Path List**  | /data/backup/\*          |
| **Source Base**       | /data                    |
| **Monitor Status**    | checked                  |
| **Smart Transfer ID** | template_001             |
| **Operation**         | Submit the Transfer      |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Display name for this console smart transfer operation step. |
| **Console IP** | IP address of the Aspera Console node to connect to. |
| **Console port** | Port number for console connection (default: 443). |
| **Login** | Username for accessing the console service. |
| **Password** | Password for console service authentication. |
| **Transfer Name** | Descriptive name to assign to the transfer operation. |
| **Transfer Comments** | Optional comments to associate with the transfer. |
| **Source Path List** | List of source files/paths for the transfer operation. |
| **Source Base** | Common base path to fetch source files from. |
| **Monitor Status** | Enable asynchronous status monitoring for submitted transfers. |
| **Smart Transfer ID** | Unique identifier of the Smart Transfer template to invoke. |
| **Operation** | Operation type: 'List the Templates' to retrieve available templates, or 'Submit the Transfer' to initiate a transfer. |

### Inputs Description

#### Required Inputs:

- **login**: Console service username (required for both operations).
- **password**: Console service password (required for both operations).
- **IP**: Console IP address (required for both operations).
- **smart transfer ID**: Template ID to invoke (required for Submit Transfer operation).

#### Optional Inputs:

- **polling frequency**: Status monitoring frequency interval in seconds (default: 10). Used when Monitor Status option is enabled.

### Outputs Description

- **Smart_Template_List**: Array containing the list of Smart Transfer templates available on the console.
- **Transfer_Ids**: Array containing transfer IDs after initiating transfers (when applicable).
- **Error_Message**: Error details and exception messages when operations fail.

### Supported Actions

- List the Templates
- Submit the Transfer

### Dependencies

- Access to Aspera Console with valid authentication credentials.
- Network connectivity to the specified Console IP address and port.
- Smart Transfer templates must be configured and available on the Console.
- Proper permissions to execute the selected Smart Transfer templates.

### Operating Instructions

#### Important Limitations

**NOTE:** According to the Console guide, it is important to note that if a transfer does not allow the user to select the source items at start, then including a "source" section will result in an error being returned when using this call.

**Reference:** Refer to the Aspera Developer Network documentation for more detailed information about Smart Transfers.

### FASP controller

![FASP controller icon](icons/FaspControl.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to control and modify the parameters of running transfers using Aspera's FASP protocol.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.7.0 | No longer support ruby 1.8, Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.6.1 | Fix for queryParams variables type as array |
| 0.6.0 | Adding new columns for max transfer and to get only failed files. Fixing bug when central address is given at runtime |
| 0.5.5 | Fixing gem name in dependencies. Adding more logging for session monitoring |
| 0.5.4 | fixing WSDL files |
| 0.5.3 | removed soap4r dependency |
| 0.5.2 | Fixed adjust rate to continue when one adjust rate call fails |
| 0.5.1 | Fixed issue when no session found |
| 0.5.0 | Adding option to adjust rate |
| 0.4.0 | Adding option to return all inprogress session ids |
| 0.3.4 | Returning percent_complete from check_status method to be consumed by the activities screen. |
| 0.3.3 | Added mkdir for cached WSDL directory for monitor transfer session and monitor transfer job options. |
| 0.3.2 | Added Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.3.1 | Added help file |
| 0.3.0 | \- ML - 2015/06/05 - Adds support for authenticated central requests thru node API |
| 0.2.1 | \- PM - 2015/02/18 - Typo in the migration leading to column not being created. |
| 0.2.0 | \- PM - 2015/02/18 - Making the plugin asynchronous when keep on going is checked. |
| 0.1.4 | \- PM - 2015/02/18 - Defaulting MaxReturns to 100K for every poll |
| 0.1.3 | Improved exception handling and logging |
| 0.1.2 | Improvements to plugin - option to wait until the transfer is finished |
| 0.1.1 | Removing reference to "fasp_controllers" in WSDL_CACHE path which was causing file not found errors |
| 0.1.0 | Adding session and job monitoring |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release, only supports cancel |

### How It Works

- Controls and monitors active Aspera transfer sessions through Central server API and Node API interfaces.
- Supports multiple control operations: cancel sessions, monitor transfers, find all sessions, and adjust transfer rates.
- Provides real-time session monitoring with configurable polling frequency and wait-until-completion options.
- Works with Enterprise Server, Connect Server, Faspex Server, and Point to Point environments.
- Returns comprehensive session information including file lists, transfer status, and rate details.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`FaspControl`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the basic connection parameters:`
    - **`Name`**`: Enter a descriptive name for the control operation.`
    - **`Command`**`: Choose operation (Cancel session, Monitor transfer session, Monitor transfer job, find all sessions, Adjust_rate set).`
    - **`Controlled Aspera node`**`: Select the remote node to monitor or control.`
    - **`Central address`**`: IP address or hostname of the Aspera Transfer server.`
    - **`Node user`**`: Node API username for authentication.`
    - **`Node password`**`: Node API password for authentication.`
3.  `Set up monitoring and control options:`
    - **`Wait until end`**`: Check to keep step in progress until session/job completion.`
    - **`Polling frequency`**`: Interval in seconds for status polling (default: 5 seconds).`
    - **`Session Ids`**`: Specify session IDs to control (optional if using transfer conditions).`
    - **`Transfer Rate`**`: Set transfer rate in kbps for Adjust rate operations.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                  | Example Value                     |
|----------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| **Name**                   | Transfer Session Monitor          |
| **Comments**               | Monitors active transfer sessions |
| **Command**                | Monitor transfer session          |
| **Wait until end**         | checked                           |
| **Polling frequency**      | 5                                 |
| **Controlled Aspera node** | transfer-server-01                |
| **Central address**        | 192.168.1.100                     |
| **Node user**              | node_api_user                     |
| **Node password**          | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*                  |
| **Transfer Rate**          | 100000                            |
| **Shared Rate?**           | unchecked                         |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Display name for this FaspControl step. |
| **Comments** | Notes or description for the FaspControl template. |
| **Command** | Operation to execute: Cancel session, Monitor transfer session, Monitor transfer job, find all sessions, Adjust_rate set. |
| **Wait until end** | Keep step in progress as long as session and job are in progress. |
| **Polling frequency** | Interval in seconds for polling Central server for session and job status (default: 5 seconds). |
| **Controlled Aspera node** | Remote node to connect to for monitoring or canceling Aspera transfers. |
| **Central address** | IP address or hostname of the Aspera Transfer server (format: lala.com or 10.0.200.202). |
| **Service uri root** | Optional SOAP URI part of WSDL URL to Central server (defaults to expected value). |
| **Node user** | Node API username required for authentication. |
| **Node password** | Node API password required for authentication. |
| **Transfer Rate** | Transfer rate in kbps for Adjust rate operations. |
| **Shared Rate?** | Enable to share the rate across all sessions. |
| **Session Ids** | Specific session IDs to control (use instead of transfer conditions if specified). |

### Inputs Description

All parameters are available as runtime inputs if not specified in the template. This plugin does not support value overwrites: template values cannot be overridden at runtime.

- **Command**: Control operation to execute on transfer sessions.
- **Central_address**: IP address or hostname of Aspera Transfer server.
- **Node_user**: Node API username for authentication.
- **Node_password**: Node API password for authentication.
- **Session_Ids**: Specific session IDs to target for control operations.
- **Transfer_Rate**: Transfer rate in kbps for rate adjustment operations.
- **Polling_frequency**: Status polling interval in seconds.
- **Wait_until_end**: Boolean flag to wait for session/job completion.

### Outputs Description

- **Session_file_list**: Array of files to be transferred in the session.
- **Transferred_file_list**: Array of files successfully transferred in the session.
- **No_of_files_transferred**: Number of files successfully transferred in the session.
- **Growing_file_list**: Array of files currently in progress during Orchestrator polling.
- **Session_status**: Session status obtained via API call: Complete, Failed, Error, or Stopped.
- **Transfer_Rate_Set**: Transfer rate set for each identified session.

### Supported Actions

- Cancel session
- Monitor transfer session
- Monitor transfer job
- Find all sessions
- Adjust_rate set

### Dependencies

- Aspera Enterprise Server, Connect Server, Faspex Server, or Point to Point with valid license.
- Aspera Central API must be enabled with persistent store on the target server.
- Node API must be enabled and accessible on the controlled Aspera node.
- Network connectivity to Central server (port 40001) and Node API ports.

### Operating Instructions

#### Plugin Overview

This plugin enables monitoring and control of Aspera transfer sessions. Sessions can be monitored for file transfer progress or cancelled as needed. Supply session IDs at runtime and choose commands to perform on those sessions.

#### Aspera Central API Setup

Works with Enterprise Server, Connect Server, Faspex Server, and Point to Point as they all use Aspera Central to expose transfer statuses.

**Required Configuration:** Enable reliable API on the Aspera Server with IP address polling:

    <central_server>
        <address>0.0.0.0</address>
        <port>40001</port>
        <persistent_store>enable</persistent_store>
    </central_server>

#### Node API Setup

Remote nodes must run Aspera Enterprise Server, Connect Server, or Point to Point with valid license and asperanoded service.

##### Node API Account Configuration

Node username is a Node API account (not an OS account). Find the associated transfer user:

    # /opt/aspera/bin/asnodeadmin -l
    List of node user(s):
                user       system/transfer user                    acls
    ====================    =======================    ====================
            node_user                       xfer       []

##### Transfer User Configuration

The transfer user docroot is defined in `/opt/aspera/etc/aspera.conf`:

    <user>
        <name>xfer</name>
        <file_system>
            <access>
                <paths>
                    <path>
                        <absolute>/home/xfer/data</absolute>
                    </path>
                </paths>
            </access>
        </file_system>
    </user>

##### Port Configuration

Enable both HTTP and HTTPS for Node API in `/opt/aspera/etc/aspera.conf`:

    <server>
        <server_name>test.sl.dev.asperacloud.net</server_name>
        <http_port>9091</http_port>
        <https_port>9092</https_port>
        <enable_http>true</enable_http>
        <enable_https>true</enable_https>
    </server>

Restart asperanoded service to apply the configuration changes:

    service asperanoded restart

#### Command Operations

- **Cancel session:** Terminates active transfer sessions using provided session IDs.
- **Monitor transfer session:** Tracks session progress and provides file transfer status.
- **Monitor transfer job:** Monitors job-level transfer status and completion.
- **Find all sessions:** Discovers and lists all active transfer sessions.
- **Adjust_rate set:** Modifies transfer rate for specified sessions, with optional rate sharing.

#### Session Management

- Session IDs can be provided at runtime to target specific transfers for control operations.
- The wait until end option keeps the workflow step active until monitored sessions are complete.
- Polling frequency determines how often the plugin checks session status (default: 5 seconds).
- Transfer rate adjustments can be applied to individual sessions or shared across all sessions.

**Reference:** More information about Aspera Node API is available on the <a href="https://developer.asperasoft.com/web/node/index" target="_blank">Aspera Developer Network</a>.

### FASP transfer

![FASP transfer icon](icons/FaspTransfer.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides the ability to initiate server-to-server transfers using Aspera's FASP protocol.

| 2.7.4 | Updating help file with contents from ADN |
| 2.7.3 | Adding a new payload - Generic Fasp Transfers with proxy support. |
| 2.7.2 | Bug fix for change introduced in 2.6.1 - the exclusion pattern input must not be mandatory runtime input. |
| 2.7.1 | Fixed problem when specifing a target rate different than the one configured in faspex |
| 2.7.0 | Exposing faspex relative url (/aspera/faspex by default, it can be /faspex in same cases). Republishing all the wsdl files. |
| 2.6.1 | Exposing exclusion pattern (only one for now) |
| 2.6.0 | Exposing crypt operation and password on all url based payloads |
| 2.5.6 | Fix support for authenticated transfer via node |
| 2.5.5 | Including the Error code and Error message for the job to outputs and Jobstats |
| 2.5.4 | Adding the percentage complete (as one of the parameters for jobStatus) for in progress file transfers |
| 2.5.3 | Adding missing ERB templates for WSDL generation under wsdl directory |
| 2.5.2 | Adding MaxResults to getSessionFileList |
| 2.5.1 | Added payload to support node as a target, fix generation of WSDL (missing erb file) |
| 2.5.0 | Fixes fasp push to url payload when referencing the private key |
| 2.4.0 | Adds support for authenticated request via Node |
| 2.3.4 | New icon |
| 2.3.3 | Fixes stats reporting to avoid reset at every check_status |
| 2.3.2 | Fixes XML escape to only apply to XML field values." |
| 2.3.1 | Escape for XML safety the inputs before expanding them into XML payload to avoid malformed message when &, \< or \> are used |
| 2.3.0 | Fixing issue with private-key use when using URL |
| 2.2.9 | Adding a debug statement to inform sessionID for the jobId. Fixing a bug in the UI which caused the template to default to fasp_generic when payload is present. |
| 2.2.8 | Adds Token authentication methods |
| 2.2.7 | Protects again empty optional values. Added remove empty folder. Added options to faspex transfer. |
| 2.2.6 | Adds pull-down for encryption cipher field |
| 2.2.5 | Make support for old and new Faspex RSS APIs more generic |
| 2.2.4 | Fixed problem with new fasp url produced by Faspex RSS APIs |
| 2.2.3 | Fixed problem when files include & |
| 2.2.2 | Fixed regression introduced in 2.2.0 for correct connect server address in payload |
| 2.2.1 | Fixed faspex url parsing in case of embedded credentials having a '@' in their encoded form |
| 2.2.0 | Added faspex_host variable to all payloads interacting with Faspex |
| 2.1.3 | Fixes bug preventing the md5 checksum from being returned |
| 2.1.2 | Fixes typo in generic payload introduced in 2.1.1 |
| 2.1.1 | Added support for source base in generic payload |
| 2.1.0 | Added Overwrite option to default payload. Default is Diff. |
| 2.0.9 | Make Manifest Policy configurable in fasp generic |
| 2.0.8 | Avoid exiting with Error in case of HTTP timeout when getting the session status info |
| 2.0.7 | Fixes 2.0.6 bug |
| 2.0.6 | Restoring ability to provide central IP at realtime (warning: bug - use localhost instead of specified node) |
| 2.0.5 | Add retry on http timeout during get status |
| 2.0.4 | Use selector for create_path values |
| 2.0.3 | Provide Job ID as an output |
| 2.0.2 | Attempt job submission with and without payload escaping |
| 2.0.1 | Adds some fasp url parsing function to support standalone fasp_url payloads |
| 2.0.0 | Makes asynchronous and adds ability to cancel transfers |
| 1.5.0 | Fixes expansion in pull payloads |
| 1.4.9 | Avoids special characters in generated path |
| 1.4.7 | Default operation to Push, fix remove after transfer default |
| 1.4.6 | Fixed nil when accessing outputs (introduced in 1.4.4) and default values for RemoveAfterTransfer and LocalUserName |
| 1.4.5 | Added RemoveAfterTransfer and Local User Name |
| 1.4.4 | Added Checksum as output param It could be nil |
| 1.4.3 | Escape file paths to avoid xml parsing errors in Central |
| 1.4.2 | Expose the central node as standard input |
| 1.4.1 | Bug fix for encryption at rest |
| 1.4.0 | Auto-migrate modified payload to support encryption from URL and generic, fixes protection tag in payloads |
| 1.3.4 | Clean up double defined methods |
| 1.3.3 | Change all runtime payload referennce to FASP_PAYLOADS_DIR |
| 1.3.2 | Supports fasp URL with encryption of rest specification |
| 1.3.1 | Uses defaulted values in the UI, prevents crash if payload not present |
| 1.3.0 | Adds option to lock-down saved inputs (i.e. not shown as optional) |
| 1.2.0 | Fixes issue with saved inputs |
| 1.1.0 | Splits config and runtime |
| 1.0.2 | Fixes issue in reporting of stats when a session is restarted |
| 1.0.1 | Adds default pull/push payload templates and modified multi-files splitting conditions. |
| 1.0.0 | Uses reliable API instead of legacy call-back based. |
| 0.0.7 | Adds retrieval of faspex account from fasp URL if provided (requires FaspexInboxWatcher \>= 0.4.0) |
| 0.0.6 | Fixes typo in authentication error reporting |
| 0.0.5 | Updates to GUI's saved input features, generated wsdl is based on runtime address |
| 0.0.4 | Exposes central address as possible run-time parameter |
| 0.0.3 | Adds capability to generate faspex token |

### How It Works

- Initiates and monitors high-speed file transfers using Aspera's FASP protocol through remote Aspera nodes with comprehensive transfer control and monitoring capabilities.
- Supports multiple Aspera server types including Enterprise Server, Connect Server, Faspex Server, and Point-to-Point configurations for flexible deployment scenarios.
- Utilizes Aspera Central's SOAP API for transfer management, requiring proper configuration to accept external node connections and reliable query API enablement.
- Provides customizable transfer payloads using embedded Ruby templates or predefined XML configurations for precise transfer parameter control.
- Enables real-time transfer monitoring with detailed statistics, file lists, MD5 checksums, and comprehensive error reporting for failed transfers.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`Fasp Transfer`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the payload settings:`
    - **`Payload File`**`: Select from available template files in the fasp_transfers directory.`
    - **`Payload`**`: Write custom embedded Ruby payload code (overrides file selection).`
    - **`Split separator`**`: Define file separator for file lists (comma by default).`
3.  `Set up transfer parameters through payload configuration:`
    - `Define operation type (Push or Pull) for upload or download transfers.`
    - `Configure remote and local system details including authentication.`
    - `Specify file paths, transfer rates, and security parameters.`
    - `Set retry policies and monitoring preferences.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                       | Example Value                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------|
| **Payload File**                | fasp_pull_default.xml.erb    |
| **Payload**                     | (custom Ruby code if needed) |
| **Split separator**             | ,                            |
| **Operation (in payload)**      | Pull                         |
| **Target Rate (in payload)**    | 45000                        |
| **Remote Address (in payload)** | 10.20.13.102                 |
| **Remote User (in payload)**    | username                     |
| **Local Path (in payload)**     | /opt/localfiles/             |
| **Remote Path (in payload)**    | /home/user/files/            |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Payload File** | The template payload file to be filled in and used. These files are stored in the /opt/aspera/orchestrator/config/orchestrator/actions/fasp_transfers directory. Files added to this directory can be seen in the dropdown after pressing the 'refresh' button next to the selector. |
| **Payload** | A custom payload can be written here. The payload should be written as embedded Ruby. The selected Payload file will be ignored if this field is filled in. |
| **Split separator** | The file separator when a file list as a string is passed to the plugin. |

### Inputs Description

The inputs depend on the configuration of the FaspTransfer action template and the payload being used.

- **payload_variables**: Various runtime inputs based on the embedded Ruby payload configuration (e.g., remote_address, remote_user, local_paths, file_lists).
- **operation**: Transfer operation type: Push (upload) or Pull (download).
- **file_paths**: Source and destination file paths for the transfer operation.
- **authentication**: Remote system authentication credentials (username, password, or SSH keys).
- **transfer_parameters**: Rate control, retry policies, and other transfer-specific settings.

### Outputs Description

- **Job_ID**: The ID of the Fasp Session.
- **Transfer_Stats**: The Fasp Session statistics including transfer rates, duration, and completion status.
- **Transferred_File_list**: The files transferred by this session with their status and details.
- **Transferred_Files_md5**: For each file, the computed MD5 checksum for integrity verification.

##### Job Order Description (Parameters)

<colgroup>
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 33%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td width="15%"><strong>Version</strong></td>
<td>The current version is 2.</td>
<td>string</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td width="15%"><strong>Agent</strong></td>
<td>Identifies the agent responsible for submitting the job. It is recommended that it takes the form of Company.Application, such as Aspera.AsperaConnect.</td>
<td>string</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td width="15%"><strong>NoMgmtPortFiles</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><p>boolean</p>
<p>Possible values: { 'Yes'|'No' }.</p>
<p>Default: No</p>
<p>Used to disable reporting (since 3.8.1). <strong>Note:</strong> If you submit a transfer with this parameter set to Yes, the transfer is not retried.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td width="15%"><strong>Operation</strong></td>
<td>Specifies the transfer operation to perform, i.e. download or upload. When set to Pull, files will be downloaded from the remote location to the local location. When set to Push, files will be uploaded from the local location to the remote location.</td>
<td><p>string.</p>
<p>Possible values: { 'Pull', 'Push' }</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
