<td>Not executed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Asynchronous background fire and wait using custom method</td>
<td>The plugin will start a background job and call Polling command to check the status of the execution</td>
<td>Command to execute</td>
<td>Positive value to enable status checking. It is advised to give longer periods, as each status check is a separate SSH connection</td>
<td>Your status checking command.</td>
<td><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>@stdout</th>
<th>@stderr</th>
<th>@exitcode</th>
<th>@pid</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>In processed outputs: file path on server where std_out was redirected to. In Status checking, the output of the status check command a string is provided</td>
<td>In processed outputs: file path on server where std_err was redirected to. In Status checking, the error of the status check command a string is provided</td>
<td>In processed outputs, exit code the command executed. In status checks, exit code of the status checking command</td>
<td>In processed outputs, PID of the parent shell running the command. In status checks, same PID is provided</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></td>
<td>Executed after running the command. @stdout and @stderr will point to files where outputs were redirected. @pid will be the PID of the parent shell.</td>
<td>Code is executed on result of status checking command</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

#### Environment Variables

- Environment variables listed in configuration become mandatory mapped inputs.
- SSH daemon configuration (/etc/ssh/sshd_config) must allow environment variables for security.
- Add variable names to AcceptEnv in sshd_config or use 'AcceptEnv \*' to allow all variables.
- Variables are set on the remote target before command execution.

#### Code Blocks

Two Ruby code blocks are available for customization:

- **Processed outputs:** Executed on command results and/or last status check results. Can override command outputs.
- **Polling stop condition:** Executed after each poll to determine if polling should stop (return true to stop).

##### Available Variables in Code Blocks

- `@stdout`: Standard output from command execution
- `@stderr`: Standard error from command execution
- `@exitcode`: Exit code from command execution
- `@pid`: Process ID of executed command
- `@status`: Step result (STATUS_COMPLETE, STATUS_FAILED, STATUS_ERROR, STATUS_INPROGRESS)
- `@status_details`: Message shown in Orchestrator work order UI

#### SSH Authentication

##### SSH Key Passphrase

Net::SSH library supports only one passphrase for all keys requiring passphrases. Avoid mixing keys with and without passphrases.

##### Connection Options

Additional connection options can be specified as Ruby hash following [Net::SSH documentation](http://net-ssh.github.io/net-ssh/Net/SSH.html#method-c-start).

- Disable host key verification: `{ verify_host_key: false }`
- For Net::SSH 6.x with older SSH servers: `{ append_all_supported_algorithms: true }`

#### Best Practices

- Use longer polling periods for async operations as each poll creates a new SSH connection.
- For fire-and-forget mode, ensure proper cleanup of background processes on target systems.
- Test custom polling commands and stop conditions thoroughly before production use.
- Consider timeout values appropriate for your command execution requirements.
- Use SSH key authentication for better security and automation compatibility.

### Azure Service Bus Operation

![Azure Service Bus Operation icon](icons/AzureServiceBusOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to perform an action onto an Azure Service Bus queue.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.1   | Initial release.                                    |

#### How It Works

- This action plug-in performs operations on an Azure Service Bus queue or topic.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **AzureServiceBusOperation** plug-in.
2.  This action performs the configured Azure Service Bus operation and emits the appropriate outputs.
3.  Fill out the following configuration fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved AzureServiceBusOperation configuration.
    - : Optional comments about this AzureServiceBusOperation configuration.
    - : The Azure Service Bus namespace.
    - : The Azure Service Bus access key name.
    - : The Azure Service Bus access key.
    - : The Azure Service Bus queue or topic name.
    - : For the Message_get_from_topic operation, enter the Azure Service Bus subscription name.
    - : The proxy URL used to access Azure Service Bus. Leave this field blank if a proxy is not required.
    - : The operation to execute.
    - : For the Message_send operation, enter the message body.

#### Example Configuration

This is a minimal configuration example for the AzureServiceBusOperation plug-in.

| Parameter           | Value                                  |
|---------------------|----------------------------------------|
| Azure namespace     | \<sample_namespace\>                   |
| Azure key name      | \<sample_key_name\>                    |
| Azure key           | \<sample_key\>                         |
| Queue or Topic name | topic1                                 |
| Proxy url           | https://test-source.azure-bus.com/bus1 |
| Operation           | Message_send                           |
| Message body        | \<contents to send\>                   |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved AzureServiceBusOperation configuration. |
| **Comments** | Optional comments about this AzureServiceBusOperation configuration. |
| **Azure namespace** | The Azure Service Bus namespace. |
| **Azure key name** | The Azure Service Bus access key name. |
| **Azure key** | The Azure Service Bus access key. |
| **Queue or Topic name** | The Azure Service Bus queue or topic name. |
| **Subscription_name** | For the Message_get_from_topic operation, enter the Azure Service Bus subscription name. |
| **Proxy url** | The proxy URL used to access Azure Service Bus. Leave this field blank if a proxy is not required. |
| **Operation** | The operation to execute. |
| **Message body** | For the Message_send operation, enter the message body. |

#### Inputs Description

Required inputs depend on how the AzureServiceBusOperation template is configured. If dynamic attributes are used, each attribute becomes a runtime input.

| Parameter                         | Description                            |
|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| *Azure_namespace (mandatory)*     | The Azure Service Bus namespace.       |
| *Azure_key_name (mandatory)*      | The Azure Service Bus access key name. |
| *Azure_key (mandatory)*           | The Azure Service Bus access key.      |
| *Queue or Topic name (mandatory)* | The queue or topic name.               |
| *Proxy_url*                       | The proxy URL (optional).              |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Message_body | For Message_get operations, the retrieved message body. |
| Error_message | The error message returned by Azure Service Bus, if an error occurs. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

1.  This action plug-in performs operations on an Azure Service Bus queue or topic.
2.  Specify the Azure Service Bus namespace, access key name, and access key.
3.  Specify the queue or topic name.
4.  Specify the subscription name only when retrieving messages from a topic.
5.  Specify a proxy URL (e.g., https://myproxy.com:8443) if direct Internet access is not available. Without a proxy, Orchestrator must be able to reach .servicebus.windows.net on port 443.
6.  Enter the operation to execute (Message_get_from_queue, Message_get_from_topic, or Message_send). Only one message can be retrieved using Message_get_from_queue or Message_get_from_topic. Only one message can be sent using Message_send. Enter the message body when using the Message_send operation.
7.  Message_get_from_queue and Message_get_from_topic are get-and-delete operations. If no message is available, the step information will report "no message in the queue" and the Message_body output will be empty.

### CatdvRestRequest

![CatdvRestRequest icon](icons/CatdvRestRequest.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides capability to perform REST operations on CatDV server.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | Added styling to parameter help text.               |
| 0.0.1   | Plugin release                                      |

### How It Works

- Provides RESTful API integration with CatDV servers for performing operations on media content and metadata.
- Supports multiple HTTP operations including GET, PUT, POST, and DELETE for comprehensive media management workflows.
- Handles authentication through session management, automatically obtaining and managing session IDs for secure API access.
- Provides flexible endpoint support for various CatDV resources including catalogs, media, clips, smartfolders, and thumbnails.
- Returns structured JSON responses converted to hash format for easy integration with downstream workflow steps.
- Includes query parameter support for filtering and advanced search operations on CatDV content.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`CatDV REST Request`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure CatDV server connection:`
    - **`Service url`**`: Enter the CatDV server API URL (e.g., http://serverip:port/api/4/).`
    - **`Login`**`: Provide the CatDV username for authentication.`
    - **`Basic Auth Password`**`: Enter the password for the CatDV user account.`
3.  `Set up API operation parameters:`
    - **`Endpoint`**`: Select the CatDV resource endpoint (clips, catalogs, media, etc.).`
    - **`Operation`**`: Choose the HTTP method (get, put, post, delete).`
    - **`Selector`**`: Specify resource ID or identifier for targeted operations.`
4.  `Configure optional parameters:`
    - **`Query parameters`**`: Add filtering or search parameters as needed.`
    - **`Post request body`**`: Provide JSON payload for PUT/POST operations.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter               | Example Value                                 |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| **Service url**         | http://catdvserverone.aspera.com:8080/api/4/  |
| **Login**               | catdv_user                                    |
| **Basic Auth Password** | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*                          |
| **Endpoint**            | clips                                         |
| **Operation**           | get                                           |
| **Selector**            | 12                                            |
| **Query parameters**    | filter=and((clip.userFields\[U23\])eq(12345)) |
| **Post request body**   | (empty for GET operations)                    |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | Comments associated with this template or configuration for documentation purposes. |
| **Service url** | The CatDV server API base URL including protocol, server address, port, and API version path. |
| **Login** | Username for CatDV server authentication and API access authorization. |
| **Basic Auth Password** | Password for the specified CatDV user account for secure authentication. |
| **Endpoint** | The CatDV resource endpoint to target for the API operation (clips, catalogs, media, sourcemedia, thumbnail, smartfolders). |
| **Operation** | The HTTP method to perform on the selected endpoint (get, put, post, delete). |
| **Selector** | Resource identifier, ID, or path selector for targeting specific CatDV objects or collections. |
| **Post request body** | JSON payload content for PUT and POST operations. Required when operation is set to 'put' or 'post'. |
| **Query parameters** | Optional URL query parameters for filtering, searching, or modifying API requests (e.g., filter expressions). |
| **Framewise Separation** | BIF(s) Framewise Separation parameter for specialized CatDV operations (legacy parameter). |

### Inputs Description

**Mandatory Parameters:**

- **Service Url**: CatDV server API base URL (required for server connection).
- **Login**: CatDV username for authentication (required for API access).
- **Basic Auth Password**: CatDV user password (required for authentication).
- **Endpoint**: Target CatDV resource endpoint (required to specify API target).
- **Selector**: Resource identifier or selector (required for targeted operations).
- **Operation**: HTTP method for the API request (required to specify action type).

**Conditional Parameters:**

- **Post request body**: JSON payload content (mandatory only when operation is 'put' or 'post').

**Optional Parameters:**

- **Query parameters**: URL query parameters for filtering and search operations.

### Endpoint Operation Support

| Endpoints | Supported Operations |
|----|----|
| **catalogs, media, sourcemedia, thumbnail** | GET operations only |
| **clips** | GET, PUT, POST operations |
| **smartfolders** | GET, PUT, POST, DELETE operations |
| **session** | GET, POST, DELETE operations (used internally for authentication) |

### Outputs Description

- **response_code**: HTTP response code returned by the CatDV server API indicating the success or failure status of the operation.
- **session_hash**: Response hash received during the session authentication response containing session details and authentication information.
- **session_status**: Status of the authentication session establishment and management process.
- **status**: Overall status of the response (Can be OK, ERROR, AUTH, or MODIFIED) depending on the operation selected and execution result.
- **result_hash**: Response hash received during the operation response containing the actual API response data and results.

### Operating Instructions

- **Plugin Purpose:** This action provides capability for performing RESTful operations like GET, PUT, POST, and DELETE for media content on CatDV servers. This plugin requires access to a CatDV server and valid login credentials for accessing the same.
- **Response Processing:** Responses are returned as JSON strings which are automatically converted to hash format and stored in the plugin outputs for easy access by downstream workflow steps.
- **Authentication Handling:** The plugin automatically handles session authentication by creating a session with the provided credentials and managing the session ID (jsessionid) for subsequent API calls.
- **Example Request Formation:** Using the sample configuration (Service URL: http://catdvserverone.aspera.com:8080/api/4/, Endpoint: clips, Operation: get, Selector: 12, Query parameters: filter=and((clip.userFields\[U23\])eq(12345))), the plugin forms a GET request URL like: http://catdvserverone.aspera.com:8080/api/4/clips;jsessionid=9F441C9E62E68812BEAA64F7B2F7297F?filter=and((clip.userFields\[U23\])eq(12345))
- **Session Management:** The jsessionid is automatically obtained after successful authentication with the credentials provided in the login and password fields, and is appended to subsequent API requests for authorization.
- **API Documentation:** Complete REST API documentation is available at: <a href="http://www.squarebox.com/server7-rest-api-introduction/" target="_blank">http://www.squarebox.com/server7-rest-api-introduction/</a> for detailed endpoint specifications and advanced usage patterns.

### Dependencies

- Active CatDV server installation with REST API enabled and accessible on the specified service URL.
- Valid CatDV user account with appropriate permissions for the intended operations on target endpoints.
- Network connectivity between Orchestrator and the CatDV server including any required firewall configurations.
- CatDV server version compatibility with the API version specified in the service URL path.
- Proper CatDV database and media catalog configuration for the endpoints being accessed.

### Troubleshooting

- If authentication fails, verify the CatDV server URL is accessible and the login credentials are correct. Check that the user account has appropriate permissions for API access.
- For connection timeout issues, confirm the CatDV server is running and the service URL includes the correct protocol, hostname, port, and API path.
- If specific endpoints return 'not found' errors, verify the endpoint name is spelled correctly and that the CatDV server supports the requested API version.
- For operation permission errors, check that the selected operation (GET, PUT, POST, DELETE) is supported for the target endpoint as specified in the endpoint operation support table.
- If POST/PUT operations fail, ensure the 'Post request body' contains valid JSON format and includes all required fields for the target CatDV resource.
- When query parameter filtering doesn't work as expected, verify the filter syntax matches CatDV's query specification and that the referenced fields exist in the target resource schema.
- For session-related errors, check that the CatDV server session management is functioning properly and that there are no session timeout or concurrent session limit issues.

### Corba Operation

![Corba Operation icon](icons/CorbaOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin is used to make a CORBA call to the Arris CMM (Content Management and Metadata server) using the JacORB utility.

| Version | Comment                                                    |
|---------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.1   | Update help docs to specify the Java version compatibility |
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8        |
| 0.0.10  | Removing windows support code                              |
| 0.0.9   | Changed category to integration                            |
| 0.0.8   | Java jar updated to support logger library for logging     |
| 0.0.7   | Increasing plugin timeout to 60 minutes                    |
| 0.0.6   | Updating Corba jar to do random port selection (5 times)   |
| 0.0.5   | Updated Jar with IBM licensing annotation                  |
| 0.0.4   | Fixing the directory path for supporting windows           |
| 0.0.3   | Changed Category to Asset Management                       |
| 0.0.2   | Adding support for aspera java                             |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                            |

### How It Works

- Enables CORBA-based communication with CMM (Content Management and Metadata) servers to notify about package arrivals and trigger distribution workflows.
- Establishes connection to CMM servers using configurable IP addresses, ports, and authentication parameters for reliable communication.
- Supports FTP URL integration for CMM machines to fetch data, enabling seamless content retrieval and processing workflows.
- Provides comprehensive logging capabilities with configurable log file paths for execution tracking and debugging purposes.
- Utilizes CORBA naming services and factory patterns to ensure proper service discovery and object instantiation in distributed environments.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`CORBA Operation`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the CMM server connection:`
    - **`Corba Ip`**`: IP address of the target CMM server.`
    - **`Port`**`: Port number for CMM server communication.`
    - **`Name Service`**`: CORBA naming service identifier (typically 'NameService').`
    - **`Factory Name`**`: Factory service name (commonly 'Default').`
3.  `Set up data access and logging:`
    - **`FTP Url`**`: Complete FTP URL with credentials for CMM data access.`
    - **`Package Name`**`: ADI package name used as servant identifier.`
    - **`Log file path`**`: Destination path for execution logs and output tracking.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter | Example Value |
|----|----|
| **Corba Ip** | 124.142.220.1 |
| **Port** | 5000 |
| **FTP Url** | ftp://username:password@16.231.12.8/test/avc/ADI_1234.xml |
| **Name Service** | NameService |
| **Factory Name** | Default |
| **Package Name** | ADI_1234 |
| **Log file path** | /tmp/log1.log |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Corba Ip** | Provide the IP address of the CMM server for establishing CORBA communication. |
| **Port** | Provide the port number of the CMM server for network connection. |
| **FTP Url** | Provide the complete FTP URL along with username and password for the CMM machine to fetch data from the specified location. |
| **Name Service** | Provide the name service for the CORBA call. Usually set to 'NameService' for standard CORBA implementations. |
| **Factory Name** | Name of the Factory service. The example configuration uses 'Default' as the standard factory name. |
| **Package Name** | Name of the ADI package. This is used as a servant name internally within the CORBA architecture. |
| **Log file path** | Provide a path to the log file where the execution logs will be stored. The logs are appended to the file for continuous tracking. |

### Inputs Description

**Note:** All inputs are mandatory for the CORBA call to be executed successfully.

- **corba_ip**: IP address of the CMM server (mandatory for CORBA connection establishment).
- **port**: Port number for CMM server communication (mandatory for network connection).
- **ftp_url**: Complete FTP URL with authentication credentials (mandatory for data retrieval).
- **name_service**: CORBA naming service identifier (mandatory for service discovery).
- **factory_name**: Factory service name for object instantiation (mandatory for CORBA operations).
- **package_name**: ADI package name used as servant identifier (mandatory for package processing).
- **log_file_path**: Destination path for execution logs and output tracking (mandatory for logging).

### Outputs Description

- **Corba_Call_Validation**: Shows the validation output of the execution, indicating success or failure of the CORBA operation.
- **Raw_Execution_Output**: Shows the raw execution output of the call, providing detailed response information from the CMM server.
- **Log_File_Path**: Shows the path to the log file where the outputs are saved, enabling access to detailed execution logs.

### Operating Instructions

- This plugin is used to make a CORBA call to the CMM (Content Management and Metadata) server to inform about package arrival so that it can be distributed to appropriate destinations.
- The plugin establishes a CORBA connection using the specified IP address and port, then uses the naming service to locate the appropriate factory for processing the package information.
- The FTP URL parameter allows the CMM machine to fetch data from the specified location, enabling automated content retrieval as part of the distribution workflow.
- All execution details are logged to the specified log file path, providing comprehensive tracking of operations for debugging and auditing purposes.
- The package name serves as a unique identifier within the CORBA servant architecture, ensuring proper package handling and distribution routing.

### Dependencies

- Java Runtime Environment (JRE) must be installed and accessible via the system PATH, or Aspera's bundled Java at /opt/aspera/lib/java/jre8/bin/java must be available. The plugin executes a JAR file for CORBA operations.
- **Important:** When performing system security updates (e.g., using dnf update or dnf-automatic), be cautious not to accidentally upgrade Java, as this may break CORBA functionality. Consider locking the Java version using package manager version pinning to prevent unintended upgrades.
- CORBA runtime environment must be properly installed and configured on the Orchestrator server.
- Network connectivity between Orchestrator and the specified CMM server IP address and port.
- Valid authentication credentials for FTP access to the specified data location.
- Proper permissions for writing to the specified log file path location.
- CMM server must be running and accessible with the configured CORBA naming service.

### Troubleshooting

- If connection fails, verify the CMM server IP address and port are correct and the server is accessible from the Orchestrator network.
- Check that the CORBA naming service is running on the CMM server and the Name Service parameter matches the expected service name.
- Validate FTP URL credentials and ensure the specified path exists and is accessible with the provided authentication.
- Verify the log file path directory exists and the Orchestrator process has write permissions to create and append to the log file.
- Ensure the Factory Name matches the expected factory service available on the CMM server for proper object instantiation.
- Check network firewall rules and security policies that might block CORBA communication between Orchestrator and CMM servers.

### Curl Operations

![Curl Operations icon](icons/CurlOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in executes curl command operations on remote nodes via SSH, supporting GET and POST requests with authentication, flexible parameter handling, image downloads, and configurable result storage for comprehensive HTTP operations.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | Adding Remote Node Dependency                       |
| 0.0.1   | Initial release                                     |

### How It Works

- Executes curl command operations on remote nodes via SSH.
- Supports both GET and POST request types with authentication.
- Offers two operation modes: direct command execution or pre-configured templates.
- Can download images and store results in specified file locations.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **CurlOperation** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - **Name**: Label for the step (e.g., `API Data Fetch`).
    - **Comments**: Notes for the template or configuration.
    - **Service URL**: The URL from where the fetch operation is performed.
    - **Remote Node**: The node to SSH into to perform the curl operation.
    - **Login**: Username for authentication (if required).
    - **Password**: Password for authentication (if required).
    - **Request Type**: Select GET or POST.
    - **Operation**: Choose between "Enter command to Execute" or "Execute the precompiled command".
    - **Parameters**: Request parameters or file path containing parameters.
    - **Result Storage Location**: File path where execution results will be stored.

### Example Configuration

| Field                   | Value                             |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Name                    | `API Data Fetch`                  |
| Service URL             | `https://api.example.com/data`    |
| Remote Node             | `Linux Host`                      |
| Request Type            | `POST`                            |
| Operation               | `Execute the precompiled command` |
| Parameters              | `fname=John&lname=Doe`            |
| Result Storage Location | `/tmp/api_results.txt`            |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | Notes and comments for this step or template configuration. |
| **Service URL** | The URL from where the fetch operation is performed. |
| **Remote Node** | The node to SSH into to perform the curl operation. |
| **Login** | Username for authentication (if required). |
| **Password** | Password for authentication (if required). |
| **Request Type** | Supports GET and POST calls. |
| **Detailed Execution Result** | Choose this option if detailed command execution information is needed. |
| **Is Parameters a file path?** | Check if parameters should be fetched from a file. |
| **Parameters** | Request parameters or path to parameter file. |
| **Redirect Automatically** | Automatically follow redirect URLs if the service responds with one. |
| **Download Image?** | Check this option if downloading an image. |
| **Image Location** | System location where the image should be downloaded. |
| **Result Storage Location** | File path where execution results will be stored. |
| **Operation** | Choose execution mode: direct command or pre-configured template. |

### Operation Types

- **Enter command to Execute**: Provides flexibility to run commands as-is without processing.
- **Execute the precompiled command**: Uses pre-built configurations within the plugin.

### Inputs Description

- Required inputs depend on the operation chosen.
- For command entry mode: the command text area is mandatory.
- For pre-configured mode: service_url and result_file_path are mandatory.
- If download image option is chosen, Image Location must be provided.
- All parameters can be set in template or provided as runtime input.

### Outputs Description

| Output | Description |
|----|----|
| **Image Location** | The location where the image is stored. |
| **Execution Message** | The execution output displayed if not redirected to file. |
| **File Path** | The location where execution output is stored. |
| **Error** | Exception details if any errors occur during execution. |

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

None

### Operating Instructions

This plugin provides basic curl functionality with two operation modes.

**NOTE:** In Execute command operation, ensure you pass arguments to store execution results (e.g., `> filename.txt`) to prevent database exceptions with large results.

#### Parameter Format Options

- Append to URL directly
- URL format: `fname=Mark&lname=Bates`
- JSON format: `{"name":"Mark"}`
- File path containing parameters

#### Example 1: Direct Command Execution

**Configuration:**

- Remote Node: Linux host
- Operation: Enter Command to Execute
- Command: `curl -X POST -u "user1:password1" quiet-waters-1228.herokuapp.com/login > /tmp/results.txt`

#### Example 2: Pre-configured with Parameters

**Configuration:**

- Remote Node: Linux host
- Operation: Execute the Preconfigured command
- Request Type: POST
- Service URL: `quiet-waters-1228.herokuapp.com/echo`
- Parameters: `fname=Mark&lname=Bates`
- Result Storage Location: `/usr/bin/output/test.txt`

#### Example 3: Image Download

**Configuration:**

- Remote Node: Linux host
- Operation: Execute the Preconfigured command
- Request Type: GET
- Service URL: `http://quiet-waters-1228.herokuapp.com/assets/image.jpg`
- Download Image: enabled
- Image Location: `/tmp/myimage.png`
- Result Storage Location: `/usr/bin/output/test.txt`

### Troubleshooting

- Ensure remote node is accessible via SSH.
- Verify authentication credentials are correct.
- Check that result storage paths are writable.
- For large responses, always redirect output to files to avoid database limitations.

### Custom progress notifier

![Custom progress notifier icon](icons/CustomProgressNotifier.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides the ability to intercept progress notifications from a step and add custom behavior to it. For example, logging to an external log file or RSS feed.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.1 | Remove explicit require for progress_observer to fix Rails 8 Zeitwerk autoloading |
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.6 | Adding exception handling to check status |
| 0.1.5 | Including 'Percent complete' reporting functionality. |
| 0.1.4 | Adds help file, sorts action type |
| 0.1.3 | Fix non-persistence of instance variable for asynch mode |
| 0.1.2 | New icon |
| 0.1.1 | Reset progress notified and logger to avoid singleton serialization issues |
| 0.1.0 | Adds ability to add I/Os |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release |

### How It Works

- Acts as a pass-through wrapper for existing action templates, allowing the proxied action to execute normally while adding custom progress notification capabilities.
- Enables execution of custom Ruby code every time the wrapped action reports a status change, providing real-time monitoring and logging functionality.
- Supports adding additional required and optional inputs to the proxied action, extending its functionality without modifying the original template.
- Provides access to progress details including status messages, state changes, and completion percentages through predefined variables.
- Offers the option to bypass standard system notifications while maintaining custom notification logic for specialized monitoring requirements.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`Custom Progress Notifier`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Select the action template to wrap:`
    - **`Action plugin`**`: Choose the type of action template to proxy.`
    - **`Action template`**`: Select the specific common template to wrap (private templates cannot be selected).`
3.  `Configure additional inputs and notification behavior:`
    - **`Additional required inputs`**`: Define extra mandatory inputs for custom logic (e.g., log file paths).`
    - **`Additional optional inputs`**`: Define extra optional inputs for enhanced functionality.`
    - **`Report progress code`**`: Write Ruby code to execute on each progress report.`
    - **`By-pass standard notifications?`**`: Check to disable standard system notifications.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter | Example Value |
|----|----|
| **Action plugin** | LocalFileWatcher |
| **Action template** | File Watcher Template |
| **Additional required inputs** | log_file_path |
| **Additional optional inputs** | notification_email |
| **Report progress code** | File.open(@inputs\['log_file_path'\], 'a') { \|f\| f.puts "2026-06-23T15:13:18+02:00: -- --" } |
| **Keep alive code** | (not used in current version) |
| **By-pass standard notifications?** | unchecked |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Action plugin** | Select the type of the action template to be proxied. This determines the category of actions available for wrapping. |
| **Action template** | The action template to be proxied - only common templates can be selected, not private ones. This is the actual action that will execute with custom progress monitoring. |
| **Additional required inputs** | Additional inputs required by the custom progress notifying logic that will be added to the inputs expected by the proxied plugin. One example could be the path to the external log file to log to. |
| **Additional optional inputs** | Same as above, but for optional inputs. These provide extra functionality without being mandatory for execution. |
| **Report progress code** | This block of code will be called every time the proxied action template reports progress. The code can reference inputs using the '@inputs' hash and access progress variables. |
| **Keep alive code** | This block of code would be used whenever the keep alive function is called, but it is not in the current version of the engine. Best is to ignore this for now. |
| **By-pass standard notifications?** | By default, the custom progress notification is called, then the standard system progress notification is called as well. If this is checked, only the custom notification code will be called. |

### Inputs Description

The inputs for the action will be the ones of the chosen proxied action template, augmented with those declared in the 'Additional required inputs' and 'Additional optional inputs' sections.

- **proxied_action_inputs**: All inputs required by the selected action template being proxied.
- **additional_required_inputs**: Custom mandatory inputs defined in the configuration for progress notification logic.
- **additional_optional_inputs**: Custom optional inputs defined in the configuration for enhanced functionality.

### Outputs Description

The outputs for the action will be the ones of the chosen proxied action template.

- **proxied_action_outputs**: All outputs produced by the selected action template being proxied, unchanged from the original action.

### Available Progress Variables

- @status_details: Contains the status message being reported by the proxied action (string)
- @status: Contains the status state change if applicable - nil indicates no changes (string)
- @percent: Contains the percent complete if provided (numerical)
- @inputs: Hash containing all inputs (both from proxied action and additional ones)

### Operating Instructions

- This plugin is a pass-through to the chosen proxied action. The chosen proxied action will still be executed in lieu of this action, but it provides the ability to execute Ruby code every time the status of the executed proxied action reports a status change.
- In the progress reporting code, inputs (combining both the inputs from the proxied action and the additional ones requested by the custom progress notifier wrapper) can be accessed through the **@inputs** hash.
- The status message being reported by the proxied action is available in 3 parts: the status message in **@status_details** (string), the status state change in **@status** (string, nil indicates no changes), and the percent complete in **@percent** (numerical, if provided).
- Use the progress reporting code to implement custom logging, notifications, or monitoring logic that executes alongside the wrapped action without interfering with its normal operation.
- When 'By-pass standard notifications' is enabled, only your custom notification code will execute, giving you complete control over progress reporting behavior.

### Example Output Log

The following shows an example custom log output from a File Watcher being monitored:

    2016-02-26T11:21:19-08:00: In Progress -- File Watcher started --
    2016-02-26T11:21:19-08:00: In Progress -- Scanning "/tmp" for files matching *.txt... --
    2016-02-26T11:21:19-08:00: In Progress -- Identified 9 candidate files at Fri Feb 26 11:21:19 PST 2016 --
    2016-02-26T11:21:22-08:00: In Progress -- Scanning "/tmp" for files matching *.mxf... --
    2016-02-26T11:21:23-08:00: In Progress -- Scanning "/tmp" for files matching *.txt... --
    2016-02-26T11:21:23-08:00: Complete -- Triggered by file "/tmp/la1.txt" --
    2016-02-26T11:21:25-08:00: In Progress -- File Watcher started --
    2016-02-26T11:21:25-08:00: In Progress -- Identified 8 candidate files at Fri Feb 26 11:21:25 PST 2016 --
    2016-02-26T11:21:28-08:00: Complete -- Triggered by file "/tmp/la2.txt" --

### Dependencies

- The selected action template to be proxied must be available and properly configured in the system.
- Ruby runtime must be available for executing custom progress notification code.
- Proper file system permissions for any log files or external resources referenced in the custom code.
- Network access if the custom progress code involves external notifications or API calls.

### Troubleshooting

- If the proxied action fails to execute, verify that the selected action template is valid and all its required inputs are properly configured.
- Check Ruby syntax in the 'Report progress code' block if custom notifications are not working - syntax errors will prevent execution.
- Verify that paths referenced in @inputs hash exist and are accessible if file operations are used in the custom code.
- If progress notifications are not appearing, ensure 'By-pass standard notifications' is unchecked unless you want only custom notifications.
- Test the wrapped action independently first to ensure it works correctly before adding custom progress monitoring.
- Check log file permissions and disk space if custom logging is configured but not producing output.

### Facebook Video Upload Operation

![Facebook Video Upload Operation icon](icons/FacebookUploadOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plugin is used to perform a resumable video upload via Facebook Graph API.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.0   | Adds reference_only option and video_id output.     |
| 0.0.3   | Adding to help page.                                |
| 0.0.2   | Correcting revision history.                        |
| 0.0.1   | Initial release.                                    |

#### How It Works

- Uploads **video files to Facebook** on behalf of a User, Event, Page, or Group.
- Uses the **Facebook Graph API** with an access token for authenticated communication.
- Accepts the video as a **file path** on the Orchestrator server.
- Uses a configurable **temporary folder** to stage video chunks during the upload process.
- Automatically **resets the upload** if the video file size or modification time changes during transfer.
- Supports controlling whether the video is **published** to the feed or uploaded as a reference video.
- Returns the **Upload Session ID and Video ID** upon successful transfer.
- Returns the full **server response** from the publish call for downstream handling.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **FacebookUploadOperation** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved FacebookUploadOperation configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved FacebookUploadOperation configured instance.
    - : User, Event, Page, or Group ID that serves as the endpoint for the video upload.
    - : Token needed to authenticate and communicate with Facebook.
    - : Path of the video file to upload.
    - : Path of the folder where temporary video files are stored during transfer. Defaults to the plugin default folder if not specified.
    - : Enable to publish the video to the feed (default: true). Disable to upload as a reference video only.

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the FacebookUploadOperation plugin uploading a product demo video to a Facebook Page.

| Parameter    | Value                                                     |
|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| Name         | Upload Product Demo Video                                 |
| Comments     | Upload weekly product demo video to company Facebook Page |
| Object id    | 123456789012345                                           |
| Access token | (stored securely)                                         |
| Video file   | /data/videos/product_demo_2025_01.mp4                     |
| Folder       | /tmp/facebook_uploads                                     |
| Published    | Selected (default)                                        |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved FacebookUploadOperation configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved FacebookUploadOperation configured instance. |
| **Object id** | User, Event, Page, or Group ID that serves as the endpoint for the video upload. |
| **Access token** | Token needed to authenticate and communicate with Facebook. |
| **Video file** | Path of the video file to upload. |
| **Folder** | Path of the folder where temporary video files are stored during transfer. Defaults to the plugin default folder if not specified. |
| **Published** | Enable to publish the video to the feed (default: true). Disable to upload as a reference video only. |

#### Inputs Description

Usually this plugin does not have runtime specified parameters. Inputs such as the video file path or object ID may be specified at runtime when these values are generated dynamically within the workflow.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *object_id* | User, Event, Page, or Group ID that serves as the endpoint for the video upload. |
| *access_token* | Token needed to authenticate and communicate with Facebook. |
| *video_file* | Path of the video file to upload. Must be accessible from the Orchestrator server. |
| *folder* | Path of the folder where temporary video files are stored during transfer. Defaults to the plugin default folder if not specified. |

#### Outputs Description

The output includes the Upload Session ID, the Video ID assigned by Facebook, and the full server response from the publish call.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| upload_id | ID of the upload session created by Facebook for this transfer. |
| video_id | ID assigned to the uploaded video by Facebook. |
| response | Full server response returned by Facebook after the publish video call. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

Requires a valid Facebook access token with appropriate permissions for video upload to the target User, Page, Event, or Group.

#### Operating Instructions

This action uploads a video file to Facebook using the Graph API, targeting the specified User, Page, Event, or Group object. The upload is staged via a temporary folder before being published.

**Reference Video Option\**

- When **Published** is disabled, the video is uploaded as a **reference video** rather than being published to the object's feed.
- Reference videos can be used for **rights management and content matching** purposes on Facebook.
- Leave **Published checked** (default) for standard video publishing to a User, Page, Event, or Group.

**Best Practices\**

- Store the **Access token securely** using Orchestrator's credential management rather than plaintext configuration.
- Ensure the access token has the appropriate **Facebook permissions** for video upload to the target object (e.g., `publish_video`).
- Verify the video file path is accessible by the **aspweb** user.
- Use a **dedicated temporary folder** with sufficient disk space to stage video chunks during upload, especially for large files.
- Store the returned **Video ID** output for use in downstream steps, such as setting video metadata or verifying publish status.
- Avoid **modifying the video file** during upload — any change to file size or modification time will reset the upload process.
- Implement **error handling** workflows that check the response output to manage upload failures or Facebook API errors gracefully.
- Refresh the **Access token** before expiry to prevent authentication failures mid-workflow.

**Troubleshooting\**

- **Authentication failure:** Verify the access token is valid, not expired, and has the required Facebook permissions for the target object.
- **Object not found:** Confirm the Object ID is correct and the access token has permission to post to that User, Page, Event, or Group.
- **Video file not found:** Verify the video file path is correct and the file is accessible from the Orchestrator server.
- **Upload reset unexpectedly:** Ensure the video file is not being modified by another process during upload. Any change to file size or modification time will trigger a reset.
- **Temporary folder errors:** Verify the specified folder exists and the Orchestrator process has read/write permissions to it.
- **Empty Video ID output:** Check the response output for Facebook API error details. An absent Video ID typically indicates the upload did not complete successfully.
- **Large file timeouts:** For large video files, ensure the workflow timeout is configured to accommodate longer upload durations.

**Important Notes\**

- The **upload process is automatically reset** if the video file's size or modification time changes during transfer. Ensure the file is stable before the workflow runs.
- The **Object ID** must correspond to a User, Event, Page, or Group that the access token has permission to upload video to.
- The **temporary folder** is used to stage video data during transfer — ensure it has sufficient disk space for the video being uploaded.
- The **Access token** must have appropriate Facebook API permissions granted for video upload to succeed.
- The **Video ID** returned is the only reference to the uploaded video — store it if downstream steps require it.
- The operation is **synchronous** — the workflow waits for the upload and publish call to complete before proceeding.

### Frameio Operation

![Frameio Operation icon](icons/FrameioOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to perform operations on Frame.io.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.1 | Fix threading logic to prevent workers stopping due to memory issues |
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.4 | Fixes the Upload Failed issue. |
| 0.1.3 | Fixes file closing issue. |
| 0.1.2 | Limiting the number of active threads. |
| 0.1.1 | Fix for upload urls data type. |
| 0.1.0 | Allows for SSL to not be used. |
| 0.0.4 | Adding compatibility for Ruby 1.8.7. |
| 0.0.3 | Fix for inputs page crash. |
| 0.0.2 | Additional changes to the initial release. |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release. |

#### How It Works

- This action plug-in enables you to perform various operations on Frame.io.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **FrameioOperation** plug-in.
2.  Complete the following configuration fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved FrameioOperation configuration.
    - : Optional comments for this saved FrameioOperation configuration.
    - : The operation you want to perform on Frame.io. Available options include:
      - \- Retrieve the Account ID of a specific user.
      - \- Retrieve all teams associated with an account.
      - \- Retrieve all projects under a specific team.
      - \- Retrieve details of child assets within a Version Stack or Folder. For a single asset, this returns an empty list.
      - \- Create a new asset, which can be either a file or a folder.
      - \- Upload an asset to Frame.io.
      - \- Create and upload an asset in a single step.
    - : Your Frame.io access token.
    - : Select this option to enable SSL verification.
    - : The unique ID associated with your Frame.io account.
    - : The ID of a specific team in Frame.io.
    - : The ID of a specific asset in Frame.io.
    - : Optional parameter. Acceptable values: 'file' for a file asset or 'folder' for a folder asset.
    - : The name of the asset you want to create.
    - : Optional parameter. If the asset is a file, specify its MIME type (e.g., video/mp4 or video/quicktime).
    - : Optional parameter. If the asset is a file, specify its size in bytes. It is recommended to provide the file path instead of manually entering the size. If the file path is specified, you do not need to enter the file size.
    - : An array of upload URLs obtained after creating an asset (e.g., \[Url_1, Url_2, ...\]). All URLs should be in string format.
    - : The local path of the file you want to upload or create as an asset. When creating an asset, this is optional but recommended over manually entering the file size. If the file path is specified, you do not need to enter the file size. Example: /home/123.mov.
    - : The content type (e.g., video/quicktime).

#### Example Configuration

Below is a minimal configuration example for the FrameioOperation plug-in.

| Parameter   | Value              |
|-------------|--------------------|
| Operation   | Get Account Id     |
| Verify SSL? | Selected           |
| Token       | \<Frame.io token\> |

#### Inputs Description

Required inputs depend on how the FrameioOperation template is configured. If dynamic attributes are used, each attribute becomes a runtime input.

| Error_Message | Any error message generated while performing the step. |
| Result        | The response retrieved while performing the step.      |

Additional outputs are generated for each configured extraction. Output names are capitalized versions of the extracted fields (e.g., 'pubDate' -\> 'Pubdate').

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

1.  Use this action plug-in to execute the available operations on Frame.io v2.
2.  Available operations are:
    - \- Retrieve the Account ID of a specific user.
    - \- Retrieve all teams associated with an account.
    - \- Retrieve all projects under a specific team.
    - \- Retrieve details of child assets within a Version Stack or Folder. For a single asset, this returns an empty list.
    - \- Create a new asset, which can be either a file or a folder.
    - \- Upload an asset to Frame.io.
    - \- Create and upload an asset in a single step.

### Google Cloud Storage Operation

![Google Cloud Storage Operation icon](icons/GoogleCloudstorageOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to perform an action on the Google Cloud Storage service.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.1   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.0   | Remove hardcoded aspera directory reference         |
| 0.0.2   | Update path to Ruby / SSL cert file                 |
| 0.0.1   | Initial release.                                    |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved GoogleCloudstorageOperation configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved GoogleCloudstorageOperation configured instance.

- **Operation**: The operation to perform.

- **Credentials filepath**: The file path on Orchestrator of the Google service account JSON key file.

- **Project id**: The project id.

- **Bucket name**: The storage bucket name.

- **Local filepath**: The local filepath. For upload and download operations.

- **Remote filepath**: The remote filepath. For upload and download operations.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the GoogleCloudstorageOperation action template.

- **Operation**: The operation to perform (only visible in input if -- select operation -- is left in the plugin configuration).
- **Credentials_filepath**: The file path on Orchestrator of the Google service account JSON key file.
- **Project_id**: The project id.
- **Bucket_name**: The bucket name.
- **Local_filepath**: The local filepath.
- **Remote_filepath**: The remote filepath.

\

##### Outputs description

- **Error_message**: The error message in case of failure.

##### Operating Instructions

This action plugin is used to perform an action on the Google Cloud Storage service.

#### Before you can use the plugin, you need to perform these operations:\
On Orchestrator, install the google-cloud-storage gem by adding this line in your Gemfile with your google-cloud-storage gem version:\
gem 'google-cloud-storage', '1.21.0'\
Run the following command:\
bundle install\
\
Log in the Google Cloud console at https://console.cloud.google.com/\
Create a project.\
In Billing, enable billing for the project and create a billing account.\
In APIs & Services, under Credentials, create a service account JSON key.\
Keep the json key credential file and copy it somewhere on the Orchestrator machine.

Upload/download operations:\
To upload or download a file, specify the full local file path on Orchestrator with the filename (e.g. /tmp/file1.txt) and the remote filepath on Google Cloud Storage with the filename for the selected bucket (e.g. folder1/file1.txt).\
If the source filepath (local for upload and remote for download) is not existing, an error is returned.\
If the remote file is already existing in an upload operation, it will be overwritten by the local file.\
If you specify a remote folder that is not existing in an upload operation, it will be created on the bucket. For example if you specify folder2/file2.txt and folder2 is not existing in the bucket, it will be created and file2.txt will be put in this folder.\
If the local file is already existing in a download operation, it will be overwritten by the remote file.\
If you specify a local folder that is not existing in a download operation, an error is retuned.\

### Google Pubsub Operation

![Google Pubsub Operation icon](icons/GooglePubsubOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to perform an action on the Google Pub/Sub service.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.1   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.0   | Remove hardcoded aspera directory reference         |
| 0.0.2   | Update path to ruby / SSL certs                     |
| 0.0.1   | Initial release.                                    |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved GooglePubsubOperation configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved GooglePubsubOperation configured instance.

- **Operation**: The operation to perform.

- **Credentials filepath**: The file path on Orchestrator of the Google service account JSON key file.

- **Project id**: The project id.

- **Topic name**: The topic name.

- **Subscription name**: The subscription name.

- **Message body**: The message content for the publish operation.

- **Max number of messages**: The maximum number of messages to be retrieved. For the read messages operation.

- **Keep ongoing?**: If checked, the containing workflow will be cloned after each trigger event, ensuring that the workflow execute everytime messages are returned. For the read messages operation.

- **Polling frequency**: By default, the Pub/Sub service is probed every 5 seconds for new messages. That polling frequency can be changed to any number of seconds. For the read messages operation.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the GooglePubsubOperation action template.

Changes in this list of inputs will override any values set in the action template.\
\

- **Operation**: The operation to perform (only visible in input if -- select operation -- is left in the plugin configuration).
- **Credentials_filepath**: The file path on Orchestrator of the Google service account JSON key file.
- **Project_id**: The project id.
- **Topic_name**: The topic name.
- **Subscription_name**: The subscription name.
- **Message_body**: The message content for the publish operation.
- **Max_number_of_messages**: The maximum number of messages to be retrieved for the read messages operation (optional).

\

##### Outputs description

- **Messages**: The messages array received from the Pub/Sub service for the read messages operation.
- **Error_message**: The error message in case of failure.

##### Operating Instructions

This action plugin is used to perform an action on the Google Pub/Sub service.

#### Before you can use the plugin, you need to perform these operations:\
On Orchestrator, install the google-cloud-pubsub gem by adding this line in your Gemfile with your google-cloud-pubsub gem version:\
gem 'google-cloud-pubsub', '0.39.1'\
Run the following command:\
bundle install\
\
Log in the Google Cloud console at https://console.cloud.google.com/\
Create a project.\
In Billing, enable billing for the project and create a billing account.\
In APIs & Services, under Credentials, create a service account JSON key.\
Keep the json key credential file and copy it somewhere on the Orchestrator machine.

Regarding Google Pub/Sub:\
Any message published to a topic without an existing subscription will be lost.\
Published messages to a topic are delivered to all subscribers.\
By default, subscriptions retain unacknowledged messages for up to 7 days from the moment they are published.\
By default, subscriptions expire after 31 days of inactivity (if no read messages activity).\
Read messages are immediately acknowledged and deleted.\
If you read concurrently messages from 2 subscribers (via 2 workorders) on the same topic, each subscriber will get a message published to this topic.\
The max number of returned messages on a read messages operation operation is by default 1.\

### Ibm mq integration

![Ibm mq integration icon](icons/IbmMqIntegration.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin is used to send/receive MQ messages to/from an IBM MQ system.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.5.3 | Updated the label from Keep ongoing to Continue Monitoring |
| 0.5.2 | Updating to be compatible with Orchestrator 4.1, which isn't shipped with a Java App and Tomcat server |
| 0.5.1 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.5.0 | Remove hardcoded aspera directory reference |
| 0.4.0 | Added SSL support |
| 0.3.2 | Better handling of exceptions |
| 0.3.1 | added tomcat part to the help file |
| 0.3.0 | add user and password support |
| 0.2.0 | add message Id to response |
| 0.1.4 | accept json changed to xml |
| 0.1.3 | Help file update |
| 0.1.2 | Removing log_file_path code since we are introducing logging at MQ level |
| 0.1.1 | Added response code to the checks |
| 0.1.0 | Fixing layouts and added execution node |
| 0.0.1 | Initial implementation |

#### How It Works

- Integrates with an **IBM MQ server** to send (put) and receive (get) messages via configured queues and channels.
- Supports **Put Message** operations to publish a payload to a specified MQ queue.
- Supports **Get Message** operations to retrieve messages from a specified MQ queue.
- Optionally supports **SSL/TLS encryption and authentication** using a configurable cipher and Java trust store.
- Can target an MQ server via a configured **remote Execution Node** or directly via **IP address and port**.
- Uses a **Python-based MQ Bridge service** that wraps the IBM MQ Java SDK JAR file to communicate with IBM MQ servers.
- For Get operations, supports a **Keep Trigger Ongoing** option to continuously listen for new incoming messages.
- Returns the **received message content, Message ID, and JSON-formatted message** for Get operations, and a delivery confirmation for Put operations.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **IbmMqIntegration** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved IbmMqIntegration configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved IbmMqIntegration configured instance.
    - : Remote node on which the IBM MQ server is installed. Used when a remote node is configured for MQ.
    - : IP address of the MQ server. Used when no remote Execution Node is configured.
    - : Port the MQ server listens on. Default is 1414.
    - : Check this box to enable SSL/TLS encryption and authentication.
    - : SSL/TLS cipher suite to use (e.g., TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256). Must use the Oracle JRE TLS\_ prefix.
    - : Filename of the Java application SSL/TLS trust store (e.g., user.jks). Must be placed in the Orchestrator JRE security directory.
    - : Password for the trust store file (optional).
    - : The operation to perform. Supported values: Put Message, Get Message.
    - : Name of the MQ channel to connect through.
    - : Name of the MQ queue to send to or receive from.
    - : Name of the MQ Queue Manager.
    - : Username for authenticating with the MQ Queue Manager.
    - : Password for authenticating with the MQ Queue Manager.
    - : Path to the XML file containing the message payload to send. Used for Put Message operations.
    - : When enabled for Get Message operations, the plugin continues triggering on new incoming messages.

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the IbmMqIntegration plugin sending an order payload to an IBM MQ queue.

| Parameter                      | Value                                   |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Name                           | Send Order Message to MQ                |
| Comments                       | Put order payload onto the Orders queue |
| Execution Node                 | (blank - using IP address instead)      |
| MQ IP Address                  | 192.168.1.50                            |
| MQ Port                        | 1414                                    |
| Use SSL?                       | Not checked                             |
| MQ Operation Type              | Put Message                             |
| MQ Channel Name                | ORDER.CHANNEL                           |
| MQ Queue Name                  | ORDER.QUEUE                             |
| MQ Queue Manager Name          | QM_ORDERS                               |
| MQ User Name                   | mquser                                  |
| MQ Password                    | (stored securely)                       |
| MQ Put Request (XML file path) | /path/to/order_payload.xml              |
| Keep Trigger Ongoing           | Not checked                             |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved IbmMqIntegration configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved IbmMqIntegration configured instance. |
| **Execution Node** | Remote node on which the IBM MQ server is installed. Used when a remote node is configured for MQ. |
| **MQ IP Address** | IP address of the MQ server. Used when no remote Execution Node is configured. |
| **MQ Port** | Port the MQ server listens on. Default is 1414. |
| **Use SSL?** | Check this box to enable SSL/TLS encryption and authentication. |
| **Cipher** | SSL/TLS cipher suite to use (e.g., TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256). Must use the Oracle JRE TLS\_ prefix. |
| **Trust store filename** | Filename of the Java application SSL/TLS trust store (e.g., user.jks). Must be placed in the Orchestrator JRE security directory. |
| **Trust store password** | Password for the trust store file (optional). |
| **MQ Operation Type** | The operation to perform. Supported values: Put Message, Get Message. |
| **MQ Channel Name** | Name of the MQ channel to connect through. |
| **MQ Queue Name** | Name of the MQ queue to send to or receive from. |
| **MQ Queue Manager Name** | Name of the MQ Queue Manager. |
| **MQ User Name** | Username for authenticating with the MQ Queue Manager. |
| **MQ Password** | Password for authenticating with the MQ Queue Manager. |
| **MQ Put Request (XML file path)** | Path to the XML file containing the message payload to send. Used for Put Message operations. |
| **Keep Trigger Ongoing** | When enabled for Get Message operations, the plugin continues triggering on new incoming messages. |

#### Inputs Description

Runtime inputs can override configured values. Queue Manager Name, Queue Name, Channel Name, Body (file path), and IP address are the core required parameters for sending a message.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Queue Manager Name* | Name of the MQ Queue Manager. Required for all operations. |
| *Queue Name* | Name of the MQ queue to send to or receive from. Required for all operations. |
| *Channel Name* | Name of the MQ channel. Required for all operations. |
| *Body* | Path to the XML file containing the message payload. Required for Put Message operations. |
| *Ip* | IP address of the MQ server. Required when no Execution Node is configured. |
| *MQ_cipher* | SSL/TLS cipher suite. Required when SSL is enabled. |
| *MQ_trust_store* | Trust store filename. Required when SSL is enabled. |
| *MQ_trust_store_password* | Trust store password. Required when SSL is enabled and the trust store is password-protected. |

#### Outputs Description

For Put operations, the output confirms message delivery. For Get operations, the output includes the message content, its ID, and a JSON-formatted version where applicable.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Message_Received | Content of the received message for a Get operation. For a Put operation, returns the value 'Message Delivered'. |
| Message_ID | ID of the received message. Available for Get operations only. |
| Message_Received_JSON | Content of the received message formatted as JSON, if applicable. Available for Get operations only. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

Requires Python 3, Flask library, Java Runtime Environment, IBM MQ client libraries, and the MQ Bridge service running on port 8080. The MQ server must be accessible from the Orchestrator host.

#### Operating Instructions

This action integrates with an IBM MQ server to put or get messages via a configured queue and channel. A Python-based MQ Bridge service wraps the IBM MQ Java SDK JAR file to handle communication with IBM MQ.

**IBM MQ Server Setup\**

- **All commands below are run as the `mqm` user on the MQ server.**
- Include MQ binaries in the path: `./opt/mqm/bin/setmqenv -s`
- **Queue Manager commands:**
  - Create a Queue Manager: `crtmqm queue_manager_name`
  - Start a Queue Manager: `strmqm queue_manager_name`
  - Open the command line for a Queue Manager: `runmqsc queue_manager_name`
- **Queue creation** (run inside `runmqsc`): `define qlocal (Queue_Name) usage (xmitq)`
- **Channel creation and listener** (run inside `runmqsc`):
  - Define a channel: `define channel (Channel_Name) chltype (SVRCONN) trptype (TCP)`
  - Start the listener: `start listener(system.default.listener.tcp)`

**MQ Bridge Service Setup\**

- The plugin requires a **Python-based MQ Bridge service** that wraps the IBM MQ Java SDK JAR file to communicate with IBM MQ servers.
- The MQ Bridge service is located at: `/opt/aspera/orchestrator/script/ibm_mq_plugin_scripts/mq_bridge.py`
- **Prerequisites:**
  - Python 3 must be installed on the system
  - Flask Python library: `pip3 install flask`
  - Java Runtime Environment (JRE) to run the IBM MQ JAR file
  - IBM MQ client libraries installed at `/opt/mqm/bin`
- **Starting the MQ Bridge:**
  - Navigate to the scripts directory: `cd /opt/aspera/orchestrator/script/ibm_mq_plugin_scripts`
  - Start the bridge: `./start_mq_bridge.sh`
  - The script will automatically install Flask if not present and start the service in the background
- **Stopping the MQ Bridge:** `./stop_mq_bridge.sh`
- The MQ Bridge listens on **port 8080 by default**. Ensure no other service is using this port before starting.
- Service logs are located at: `/opt/aspera/orchestrator/log/mq_debug.log`
- Health check endpoint: `curl http://localhost:8080/health`
- MQ API message and reason codes reference: [IBM MQ API Codes](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.4.x?topic=reference-messages-reason-codes)

**SSL/TLS Configuration\**

- The cipher must use the **Oracle JRE `TLS_` prefix** (e.g., `TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256`), not the IBM `SSL_` prefix.
- The cipher must **match the cipher defined on the MQ server side**.
- The trust store file must be placed in: `/lib/java/jre/lib/security/`
- Supported trust store formats: **JKS or PKCS**. Specify the filename including its extension in the Trust store filename field.
- The trust store file must contain the **full chain CA certificate** from the MQ server side.
- Trust store files can be created with tools such as **keytool**, **ikeycmd** (bundled with Java), or **runmqckm** (bundled with IBM MQ).
- The **trust store password is optional**. Enter only if one was defined when the trust store was created.

**Best Practices\**

- Store the **MQ Password and trust store password securely** using Orchestrator's credential management rather than plaintext configuration.
- Use an **Execution Node** when the MQ server is configured as a managed remote node in Orchestrator, and use IP address only for direct connectivity.
- Verify the **MQ Bridge service is running** on port 8080 before executing any MQ workflow steps.
- Check **`mq_debug.log`** first when troubleshooting — it contains detailed Python bridge and MQ SDK error output.
- Use **Keep Trigger Ongoing** judiciously — continuous message listening can hold workflow resources; ensure appropriate timeout and termination logic is in place.
- For SSL environments, **test the cipher and trust store configuration manually** before integrating into a workflow to isolate connectivity issues.
- Implement **error handling** workflows to manage scenarios where no message is available on the queue during a Get operation.
- Use **Message_Received_JSON** output for downstream JSON parsing when the expected message format is JSON.
- Ensure the **XML payload file path is accessible** from the Orchestrator server when using Put Message operations.

**Troubleshooting\**

- **MQ Bridge not running:** Ensure the MQ Bridge service has been started using `./start_mq_bridge.sh` and is listening on port 8080. Check for port conflicts.
- **Flask not installed:** Install Flask using `pip3 install flask` or let the start script install it automatically.
- **Connection refused:** Verify the MQ IP address and port are correct and reachable from the Orchestrator server. Confirm the MQ listener is running.
- **Authentication failure:** Check the MQ User Name and Password. Ensure the user has the required permissions on the Queue Manager.
- **Queue or channel not found:** Verify the Queue Name, Channel Name, and Queue Manager Name are correctly specified and exist on the MQ server.
- **SSL handshake failure:** Confirm the cipher uses the Oracle `TLS_` prefix and matches the MQ server cipher. Verify the trust store file is in the correct directory and contains the MQ server CA certificate.
- **Trust store errors:** Ensure the trust store file format is JKS or PKCS and the password (if set) matches the one provided in the configuration.
- **Empty Message_Received output:** For Get operations, confirm a message is available on the queue. Check `mq_debug.log` for API error codes.
- **File not found errors:** Verify the XML payload file path is correct and accessible by the aspweb user.
- **MQ API message and reason codes:** Refer to the [IBM MQ API code references](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.4.x?topic=reference-messages-reason-codes) for detailed diagnostics.
- **Port 8080 conflict:** If another service is using port 8080, stop it before starting the MQ Bridge, or reconfigure the bridge to use a different port in `mq_bridge.py`.

**Important Notes\**

- The **MQ Bridge service must be running** on the Orchestrator host before any MQ workflow step is executed.
- Use either **Execution Node or MQ IP Address** to specify the MQ server location — not both simultaneously.
- The **default MQ port is 1414**. Only change this if your MQ server is configured on a different port.
- SSL cipher values must use the **Oracle JRE `TLS_` prefix** — IBM `SSL_` prefixed values are not supported.
- The trust store file must be placed in the **designated Orchestrator JRE security directory** before the workflow runs.
- **Keep Trigger Ongoing** is only applicable to Get Message operations and will cause the plugin to continuously trigger on new messages until stopped.
- The **MQ Put Request Body field now accepts a file path** to an XML file containing the payload, not the payload itself.
- For IBM MQ server setup and advanced configuration, refer to the [IBM MQ Knowledge Center](https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/ibm-mq/9.4.x).

### Ibm mq mft transfer

![Ibm mq mft transfer icon](icons/IbmMqMftTransfer.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin is used to send a file via IBM MQ MFT.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.5.2 | Updating to be compatible with Orchestrator 4.1, which isn't shipped with a Java App and Tomcat server |
| 0.3.1 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.0 | Remove hardcoded aspera directory reference |
| 0.2.0 | Added SSL support |
| 0.1.2 | help file adding tomcat part |
| 0.1.1 | status part addition |
| 0.1.0 | status part addition |
| 0.0.1 | Initial implementation |

#### How It Works

- Integrates with **IBM MQ Managed File Transfer (MFT)** to submit file transfer requests via a configured MQ queue and MFT agent.
- Accepts the transfer specification as an **XML payload file path** defining source, destination, agent, and transfer options.
- Submits the transfer by placing the XML message onto the **MFT agent's configured queue**, which the agent monitors for incoming transfer requests.
- Monitors transfer status by reading from a **configured subscription queue** — transfer submission and status monitoring are performed in a single plugin call.
- Supports **SSL/TLS encryption and authentication** using a configurable cipher and Java trust store.
- Can target an MQ server via a configured **remote Execution Node** or directly via **IP address and port**.
- Uses a **Python-based MQ Bridge service** that wraps the IBM MQ Java SDK JAR file to communicate with IBM MQ servers.
- Returns the **Message ID** of the submitted transfer for downstream tracking and correlation.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **IbmMqMftTransfer** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved IbmMqMftTransfer configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved IbmMqMftTransfer configured instance.
    - : Node on which the IBM MQ server is installed. Used when a remote node is configured for MQ.
    - : IP address of the MQ server. Used when no remote Execution Node is configured.
    - : Port the MQ server listens on. Default is 1414.
    - : Check this box to enable SSL/TLS encryption and authentication.
    - : SSL/TLS cipher suite to use (e.g., TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256). Must use the Oracle JRE TLS\_ prefix.
    - : Filename of the Java application SSL/TLS trust store (e.g., user.jks). Must be placed in the Orchestrator JRE security directory.
    - : Password for the trust store file (optional).
    - : The operation to perform. Currently supported: Submit Transfer.
    - : Name of the MQ channel to connect through.
    - : Name of the MQ queue configured with the MFT agent to receive transfer messages.
    - : Name of the MQ Queue Manager.
    - : Path to the XML file containing the transfer specification. Must be valid XML conforming to the MFT transfer request schema.
    - : Name of the MFT agent to listen to for transfer status monitoring.
    - : Name of the subscription queue used to read transfer status updates.
    - : Name of the MQ subscription configured for transfer status monitoring.
    - : Message ID of the submitted transfer, used to retrieve the status of that specific transfer.
    - : Username for authenticating with the MQ Queue Manager.
    - : Password for authenticating with the MQ Queue Manager.

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the IbmMqMftTransfer plugin submitting a file transfer request via IBM MQ MFT.

| Parameter | Value |
|----|----|
| Name | Transfer Archive File via MFT |
| Comments | Submit MFT transfer request to move file to archive directory |
| Execution Node | (blank - using IP address instead) |
| MQ IP Address | 192.168.1.50 |
| MQ Port | 1414 |
| Use SSL? | Not checked |
| MQ Operation Type | Submit Transfer |
| MQ Channel Name | SYSTEM.DEF.SVRCONN |
| MQ Queue Name | SYSTEM.FTE.COMMAND.ASPERA_AGENT |
| MQ Queue Manager Name | Aspera_Demo |
| MQ Agent Name | ASPERA_AGENT |
| MQ Subscription Queue Name | TRANSFER.STATUS.QUEUE |
| MQ Subscription Name | TRANSFER.STATUS.SUB |
| MQ User Name | mquser |
| MQ Password | (stored securely) |
| MQ Put Request (XML file path) | /path/to/mft_transfer_spec.xml |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
