- Check the checkboxes for file size validation and MD5 checksum validation. The MD5 checksum check is not case-sensitive.
- Check if the ADI.XML needs to be validated by an ADI.DTD file.
- Check the 'Pass on missing files' checkbox if you want the step to NOT fail when assets are missing.
- Use the Add ADI Validations widget to add a set of validations.
- To add a validation, click the Add ADI Validations widget.
- In the fields that appear, first select the appropriate asset class. The asset classes for a CableLabs ADI v1.1 XML can only be: Package, Title, Movie, Preview, Poster, Still-Image, TrickAsset, Encrypted Asset.
- Not all ADI.XML files contain all asset classes, so choose wisely what to validate.
- Next, in the Field Name text box, enter the field that needs to be validated in the chosen asset class. Familiarity with ADI.XML is needed to know the fields.
- Next, in the Field Value text box, enter a value that you want to use to validate the data.
- This value by default is a glob expression '\*'. The value supports wildcards like \* and ?, globbing like val\*ue, and regular expressions like /.+\[A-Za-z\]/.

### Aurora file verification

![Aurora file verification icon](icons/AuroraFileVerification.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-ins enables the submission of a file verification request to a Tektronix Aurora Server

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.3.1 | Fixed error if ReportFolder is missing |
| 1.3.0 | Updating default port. |
| 1.2.9 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 1.2.8 | Returning percent_complete from check_status method to be consumed by the activities screen |
| 1.2.7 | Fixing the endpoint URL once the WSDL is loaded via the network |
| 1.2.6 | WSDL file for Windows and Linux will be fetched via the network |
| 1.2.5 | Adding RemoteNode Dependency. |
| 1.2.4 | WSDL file on Windows does not work. Accessing from Network. Report generation support broken in 1.2.0, adding it back |
| 1.2.3 | Template list failed when only 1 template present. Now shows error information instead of a blind rescue in template list method |
| 1.2.2 | Adding begin rescue in check status to catch unexpected connection errors |
| 1.2.1 | Fixed bugs after making the plugin asynchronous |
| 1.2.0 | Making the plugin asynchronous |
| 1.1.5 | Expose :queue_on_top as runtime variable |
| 1.1.4 | Made Report Folder mandatory for versions 5.2 and above |
| 1.1.3 | Add a check on server_address for VAR_NODE_OR_ADDRESS to be a required input |
| 1.1.2 | 2015-02-17 ML - New icon |
| 1.1.1 | Prevents autocomplete of user field |
| 1.1.0 | Automatically move wsdl construction files |
| 1.0.0 | Modified to use SharedState (requires Orchestrator 2.0) |
| 0.0.4 | Change made in the migration file to reference new plugin load directory |
| 0.0.3 | Adds functionality to mask password |
| 0.0.2 | Catch no status error |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved AuroraFileVerification configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved AuroraFileVerification configured instance.

- **Aurora Server Node**: The node where the analysis operation must be executed (i.e. the node where the Aurora Server is installed).

- **Server Address**: Can be used to provide a path that overwrite the address of the node. This is useful in the case where the web service can only be accessed through https as the web service address generated from the Node is http by default.

- **Aurora Service Port**: The port to which the connection should be made. By default this is port 1002.

- **Aurora User**: The user account used to execute the Aurora analysis operation.

- **Aurora Password**: The password for the Aurora user.

- **Input source path**: The source file for the analysis operation. This value can be entered using dynamic attributes

- **Do not alter path?**: Deactivate Unix to Windows conversion of file path. When this is checked, path are kept the way they are entered

- **Test template**: Select from the list of test template configured on that Aurora Server.

- **Queue on top?**: Submit to the top of the Aurora job queue instead of the bottom to ensure this job will be picked up next.

- **Polling frequency**: The progress status polling frequency in seconds (the default is 5 seconds).

- **Return xml report?** : If selected an additional output variable 'XML_report' containing the full report formatted in XML will be provided upon action completion.

- **Return html report ?** : Same but provides an additional output variable 'HTML_report' formatted in HTML.

- **Return html report link?** : If selected, provides an additional output variable 'HTML_report_link' containing the URL that can be used to retrieve the HTML analysis report from the Aurora server

- **Return pdf report link?** : Same but provides an additional output variable 'PDF_report_link' pointing to the analysis report formatted as a PDF document.

- **Report folder**: A folder on the Aurora server into which a sub-folder will be created for each job with all the analysis reports. Dynamic parameters can be used to compose the value of that field.

- **Pass if no errors are found?**: Provides the option to consider successful a job with warnings, but no errors

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the AuroraFileVerification action template.

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the AuroraFileVerification action template.\
\

- **Aurora_node_or_address**: Points to the Aurora Server or service address. Can be either a node ID (i.e. 'Aurora_node'), a node address (i.e. '192.168.56.101') or a service address (i.e. 'http://192.168.56.101:1002'). This input is only requested when neither the node nor the service address is provided in the action template configuration.
- **User**: Same as 'Aurora User' but provided at run-time. Only requested if 'Aurora User' is left blank in the action template configuration.
- **Password**: Password for the Aurora user.
- **File_path**: All the variables listed in 'Input source path' field are mandatory and must be of type string. If the 'Input source path' is left blank, then 'File_path' must be provided at run-time.
- **Report_folder**: All the variables listed 'Report folder' field are mandatory and must be of type string.
- **Queue_on_top**: Same as 'Queue on top?' but provided at run-time.
- **Test_plan**: Provides the ability to select the test plan to use for the analysis at run-time. The named in this variable needs to be configured on the Aurora server where the analysis is to be performed.

\

##### Outputs description

- **Date_completed**: The date returned by Aurora as being the completion date for the job.
- **Error_count**: The number of error found during the analysis
- **Warning_count**: The number of warning found during the analysis
- **Aurora_job_id**: The JOB ID assigned by Aurora (an Integer)
- **Aurora_status**: The analysis status returned by Aurora
- **Template_used**: The name of the test template used for the Analysis
- **Time_to_complete**: The number of seconds it took Aurora to complete the analysis job
- **PDF_report_link**: If requested, a link to the report hosted on the Aurora server, formatted in PDF
- **HTML_report_link**: If requested, a link to the report hosted on the Aurora server, formatted in HTML
- **XML_report**: If requested the full HTML report returned as a text field
- **HTML_report**: If requested the full XML report returned as a text field

##### Operating Instructions

This action executes the specified file analysis operation on a remote node where Aurora is available. The connection to the remote server is done using the Aurora web-service.

Note: Since Tektronix Aurora is only available on Windows platform, the path are converted by default to Windows format, so an input pass of 'c:/in/file.avi' is automatically converted to what Aurora expect which is 'c:\in\file.avi'. However, in some case, it is possible to keep the path from going through that translation step. For example, this is required if the file path is a URL 'ftp://remote_folder/file'

Important Note: this action plugins does not do any file movement. Hence, for it to run a file analysis, the specified file has to be accessible on the Aurora server where the analysis is to be performed. Either the file is transferred to that server or it must be accessible from that server either on some type of network share.

### Baton file correction

![Baton file correction icon](icons/BatonFileCorrection.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-ins enables the submission of a file correction request to an Interra Baton Server

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.5.0 | Added support for Baton v9.x REST API |
| 0.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.2 | Failing when no job id is returned. Reduced logging |
| 0.3.1 | Correcting the plugin_name in metadata.yml. |
| 0.3.0 | Added flag to check if the QC server is different than Correction server. |
| 0.2.1 | Exposes the destination file path as a plugin output |
| 0.2.0 | Baton RPC port and Baton Rest port are different so new column for Rest port. |
| 0.1.0 | Modifications based on testing at client. Only task_id is provided and testplan details need to be fetched. |
| 0.0.1 | Initial version for testing |

#### How It Works

- Integrates with **Baton QC/Correction server** to perform automated file correction operations.
- Submits file correction jobs to a **Baton** server.
- Supports both **Baton version \< 9** (password only) and **version \>= 9** (password + API token).
- Can correct **individual files or entire folders**.
- Monitors correction job progress through **configurable polling**.
- Supports **growing file verification** for files still being written.
- Can **stop on first error** or continue through all corrections.
- Optionally retrieves **XML correction reports** for detailed results.
- Returns job details including **job ID, timestamps, and output file paths**.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **BatonFileCorrection** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved Baton File Correction configured instance.
    - : Comments about this Baton File Correction template.
    - : The remote node name of the server which hosts the Baton File Correction server. Use this OR Baton Server address.
    - : The remote hostname or IP address of the server hosting the Baton server. Use this OR Baton Server Node.
    - : The port number for the Baton server RPC communication.
    - : Select the Baton version: \< 9 (password only) or \>= 9 (password + API Token required).
    - : A user configured on Baton server to make API calls.
    - : Password of the Baton user (required for all versions).
    - : (Version \>= 9 only) The API token for Baton server access. Available in Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin.
    - : Check if the IP address for correction server is different from QC server. Makes 'Correction server IP' mandatory.
    - : IP address of the correction server (required if different from QC server).
    - : The port number of the REST API of Baton correction server (default 6060).
    - : The Task ID from Baton UI for the correction task to execute.
    - : XML QC report input for correction processing.
    - : Test plan XML exported from Baton server.
    - : Output folder where corrected files will be placed.
    - : Format specification for output files.
    - : Interval in seconds to check correction job status (default varies based on configuration).

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the BatonFileCorrection plugin using Baton version 9 with API token.

| Parameter            | Value              |
|----------------------|--------------------|
| Name                 | Correct Media File |
| Baton Server address | baton.example.com  |
| Baton RPC port       | 8080               |
| Baton version        | \>= 9              |
| Baton user           | api_user           |
| Baton password       | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*   |
| Baton API Token      | abc123token456     |
| Baton Task ID        | 12345              |
| Polling Frequency    | 30                 |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved Baton File Correction configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this Baton File Correction template. |
| **Baton Server Node** | The remote node name of the server which hosts the Baton File Correction server. Use this OR Baton Server address. |
| **Baton Server address** | The remote hostname or IP address of the server hosting the Baton server. Use this OR Baton Server Node. |
| **Baton RPC port** | The port number for the Baton server RPC communication. |
| **Baton version** | Select the Baton version: \< 9 (password only) or \>= 9 (password + API Token required). |
| **Baton user** | A user configured on Baton server to make API calls. |
| **Baton password** | Password of the Baton user (required for all versions). |
| **Baton API Token** | (Version \>= 9 only) The API token for Baton server access. Available in Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin. |
| **Is Correction Server address different?** | Check if the IP address for correction server is different from QC server. Makes 'Correction server IP' mandatory. |
| **Correction server IP** | IP address of the correction server (required if different from QC server). |
| **Baton Correction port** | The port number of the REST API of Baton correction server (default 6060). |
| **Baton Task ID** | The Task ID from Baton UI for the correction task to execute. |
| **Baton QC Report as XML** | XML QC report input for correction processing. |
| **Test Plan XML exported from Baton** | Test plan XML exported from Baton server. |
| **Destination Folder** | Output folder where corrected files will be placed. |
| **Output Format** | Format specification for output files. |
| **Polling Frequency** | Interval in seconds to check correction job status (default varies based on configuration). |

#### Inputs Description

All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template. This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Baton_Server_Node (mandatory)* | Remote node name for Baton server (required if Baton Server address not provided). |
| *Baton_Server_address (mandatory)* | Hostname or IP address of Baton server (required if Baton Server Node not provided). |
| *baton_task_id (mandatory)* | Task ID from Baton UI for the correction task to execute. |
| *Baton_user* | Username for Baton API authentication. |
| *Baton_password* | Password for Baton API authentication (required for all versions). |
| *Baton_API_Token* | API token for Baton version \>= 9 (required for v9+). |
| *Baton_RPC_port* | RPC port number (optional, default 8080). |
| *Baton_Correction_port* | REST API port number (optional, default 6060). |
| *Correction_server_IP* | IP address of correction server if different from QC server. |
| *destination_folder* | Output folder for corrected files (optional). |
| *output_format* | Output format specification (optional). |
| *Polling_Frequency* | Status polling interval in seconds (optional). |
| *Folder_Correction* | Boolean flag for folder correction mode (default false). |
| *Verify_growing_file* | Boolean flag to verify growing files (default false). |
| *Return_xml_report* | Boolean flag to retrieve XML report post-correction (default false). |
| *Stop_on_first_error* | Boolean flag to fail step on first error (default false). |
| *Pass_if_no_errors* | Boolean flag to pass with warnings only (default false). |
| *Non_Windows_install* | Boolean flag for non-Windows Baton installation (default false). |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Result | JSON result description returned from the correction server |
| Job_ID | Job ID of the request sent to the Baton correction server |
| Start_time | Timestamp when correction server received the correction request |
| Completion_time | Timestamp when correction server completed the correction task |
| Output_file | Destination path where corrected file is placed |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

Requires access to Baton QC/Correction server with valid credentials and network connectivity.

#### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Baton server to perform file correction jobs.

**Correction Modes\**

- **Single File Correction:**
  - Default mode when 'Folder Correction?' is unchecked
  - Corrects individual file specified by task
  - Best for: Single file workflows
- **Folder Correction:**
  - Enable by checking 'Folder Correction?' flag
  - Processes all files in specified folder
  - Best for: Batch processing multiple files
- **Growing File Verification:**
  - Enable by checking 'Verify growing file?' flag
  - Waits for file to finish writing before correction
  - Best for: Files being transferred or recorded
  - Prevents corruption from incomplete files

**Error Handling Options\**

- **Stop on First Error (Enabled):**
  - Workflow fails immediately when first error detected
  - Remaining corrections not performed
  - Best for: Quality gates requiring zero errors
- **Continue Through Errors (Disabled):**
  - Continues correction even after errors
  - All corrections attempted
  - Final result includes all error details
- **Pass if No Errors Found:**
  - Treats warnings as acceptable
  - Step completes successfully with warnings
  - Fails only on actual errors
  - Best for: Non-critical QC scenarios

**Common Use Cases\**

- **Media file correction:** Automatically correct video/audio files that fail QC checks.
- **Batch processing:** Correct entire folders of files using folder correction mode.
- **Quality gates:** Stop workflows on first correction error for critical content.
- **Archive repair:** Correct archived media files before restoration or reuse.
- **Growing file handling:** Wait for recording/transfer completion before correction.
- **Multi-format correction:** Apply Baton correction tasks to various media formats.
- **Report generation:** Retrieve detailed XML reports for audit and analysis.

**Basic File Correction Example\**

- **Step 1:** Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (e.g., 10.0.103.85 or baton.example.com)
- **Step 2:** Provide the Baton Task ID (obtained from Baton UI for a Baton correction task)
- **Step 3:** For Baton version \>= 9, provide API Token from Baton GUI (SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin)
- **Step 4:** File correction job is submitted and polled until complete
- **Step 5:** Output file path and job results are returned

**Best Practices\**

- For Baton \>= 9, ensure **both password and API token** are provided and valid.
- Set appropriate **polling frequency** - balance between responsiveness and server load (30-60 seconds typical).
- Enable **'Stop on first error'** for quality gates requiring zero defects.
- Use **'Verify growing file'** when correcting files during transfer or recording.
- Enable **'Return xml report'** for detailed error analysis and audit trails.
- Verify **Task ID is not archived** in Baton before submitting jobs.
- Ensure that the configured port is accessible from Orchestrator to Baton.
- Test credentials by **logging into Baton UI** before workflow deployment.
- Monitor **job completion times** to optimize polling frequency.

**Troubleshooting\**

- **Connection timeout:** Verify that the configured port is accessible from Orchestrator to Baton server.
- **Authentication failed:** Verify credentials by logging into Baton UI directly with same username/password.
- **API Token invalid (v9+):** Obtain valid token from Baton GUI: SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin. Both password AND token required.
- **Task ID not found:** Verify Task ID exists in Baton and is not archived. Check Baton UI for active tasks.
- **Job stuck in queue:** Check Baton server load and available correction slots. Verify task configuration.
- **Output file not found:** Check destination folder exists and Baton has write permissions.
- **Growing file timeout:** If 'Verify growing file' enabled, ensure file transfer completes within timeout period.
- **Correction server unreachable:** If using separate correction server, verify IP address and port are correct.
- **XML report not returned:** Verify 'Return xml report' flag is enabled and Baton has report generation enabled.
- **Permission denied:** Ensure Baton user has appropriate permissions for correction operations.

**Important Notes\**

- Either **Baton Server Node OR Baton Server address** must be provided (not both).
- Baton version \>= 9 requires **THREE credentials**: user, password, AND API token.
- Baton version \< 9 requires **TWO credentials**: user and password only.
- This plugin **does not support value overwrites** - template values cannot be overridden at runtime.
- The **Task ID must not be archived** in Baton server.
- 'Folder Correction?' flag changes behavior to **process entire folders** instead of single files.
- 'Stop on first error' causes **immediate failure** - use for strict quality gates.
- 'Pass if no errors' allows **warnings** - only errors cause failure.
- Polling frequency affects **detection speed and server load**.
- Output file path may not be exposed depending on **Baton configuration**.

#### How It Works

- This action plug-in submits a file verification (QC) job to a Baton server using the provided inputs.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **BatonFileVerification** plug-in.
2.  This action submits a QC job to a Baton server and reports the results when verification completes.
3.  Fill out the following configuration fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved BatonFileVerification configuration.
    - : Optional comments about this BatonFileVerification configuration.
    - : Select the Baton version to use (5.\*, 6.\*, 7.\*, or 9.\*). For version 9.\*, authentication uses an API Token instead of a password.
    - : The remote node name of the server hosting the Baton File Verification service.
    - : The remote host name or IP address of the Baton server.
    - : The port number of the Baton server.
    - : Select this option if Baton is installed on a non-Windows system.
    - : The user account configured on the Baton server for API access.
    - : (For versions \< 9) The password associated with the Baton API user account.
    - : (For version 9.\*) The API token for authentication. Available in Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin.
    - : The full UNC path as seen by Baton. The UNC path must be registered in the Baton server.
    - : The name of the test plan as defined on the Baton server.
    - : The version of the specified test plan as defined on the Baton server.
    - : The priority assigned to the QC job submitted to Baton.
    - : How often progress is polled. The default is 5 seconds.
    - : Restrict the QC job to one or more specific checkers configured in Baton.
    - : (For version 9.\* only) Specify the number of cores to use for the verification job. Default is 1.
    - : Enables faster polling. This reduces status detail but improves responsiveness. (Folders are always expedited.)
    - : Select this option if the input source path refers to a folder instead of a file.
    - : Select this option when verifying a file that is actively growing.
    - : Select this option if the XML report should be fetched after verification completes.
    - : Select this option to stop the verification job immediately when the first error is detected.
    - : Select this option to consider the step successful when only warnings are found.

#### Example Configuration

This is a minimal setup configuration for the BatonFileVerification plug-in.

| Parameter | Value |
|----|----|
| Baton Server Address | http://test-server.baton-server.com/example1 |
| Baton port | 8080 |
| Baton user | root |
| Baton password | \<password\> |
| Input source path | /source/path/to/plan |
| Baton Test Plan | plan-contents |
| Baton Test Plan Version | Version 5 |
| Status Polling frequency | 15 |
| Priority | 10 |
| Return XML report as an output? | Selected |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved BatonFileVerification configuration. |
| **Comments** | Optional comments about this BatonFileVerification configuration. |
| **Baton Version** | Select the Baton version to use (5.\*, 6.\*, 7.\*, or 9.\*). For version 9.\*, authentication uses an API Token instead of a password. |
| **Baton Server Node** | The remote node name of the server hosting the Baton File Verification service. |
| **Baton Server Address** | The remote host name or IP address of the Baton server. |
| **Baton Port** | The port number of the Baton server. |
| **Non-Windows Install?** | Select this option if Baton is installed on a non-Windows system. |
| **Baton User** | The user account configured on the Baton server for API access. |
| **Baton Password** | (For versions \< 9) The password associated with the Baton API user account. |
| **API Token** | (For version 9.\*) The API token for authentication. Available in Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin. |
| **Input Source Path** | The full UNC path as seen by Baton. The UNC path must be registered in the Baton server. |
| **Baton Test Plan** | The name of the test plan as defined on the Baton server. |
| **Baton Test Plan Version** | The version of the specified test plan as defined on the Baton server. |
| **Priority** | The priority assigned to the QC job submitted to Baton. |
| **Status Polling Frequency** | How often progress is polled. The default is 5 seconds. |
| **Checkers** | Restrict the QC job to one or more specific checkers configured in Baton. |
| **Needed Cores** | (For version 9.\* only) Specify the number of cores to use for the verification job. Default is 1. |
| **Expedite?** | Enables faster polling. This reduces status detail but improves responsiveness. (Folders are always expedited.) |
| **Folder Verification?** | Select this option if the input source path refers to a folder instead of a file. |
| **Verify Growing File?** | Select this option when verifying a file that is actively growing. |
| **Return XML Report as an Output?** | Select this option if the XML report should be fetched after verification completes. |
| **Stop Verification on First Error?** | Select this option to stop the verification job immediately when the first error is detected. |
| **Pass if No Errors Are Found?** | Select this option to consider the step successful when only warnings are found. |

#### Inputs Description

Required inputs depend on how the BatonFileVerification template is configured. If dynamic attributes are used, each attribute becomes a runtime input.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Baton_server_address (mandatory)* | The remote host name or IP address of the Baton server. |
| *File_name (mandatory)* | The full UNC path as seen by Baton. The UNC path must be registered in Baton. |
| *Test_plan (mandatory)* | The test plan name as defined on the Baton server. |
| *Baton_port* | The port number of the Baton server. |
| *Baton_server_password* | The password of the user configured on the Baton server for API access. |
| *Baton_server_user* | The user configured on the Baton server for API access. |
| *Dynamic_test_plan_parameters* | Key/value parameters required for dynamic test plans on Baton. |
| *Polling_frequency* | How often job progress is polled. Default is 5 seconds. |
| *Priority* | The priority assigned to the job submitted to the Baton server. |
| *Test_plan_version* | The version of the test plan as defined on the Baton server. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Report_PDF_link | A link to the Baton QC report in PDF format. |
| Report_VIDEO_link | A link to the Baton QC report in VDR format. |
| Report_XML_link | A link to the Baton QC report in XML format. |
| Folder? | The result of the QC operation: Success, Warning, Failure, or Aborted. |
| Result | The output files generated as part of the QC operation. |
| Start_time | The start time of the QC operation. |
| Completion_time | The completion time of the QC operation. |
| Task_ID | The ID of the QC job submitted to the Baton server. |
| Errors | The number of errors detected during the QC operation. |
| Warnings | The number of warnings detected during the QC operation. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

1.  This action plug-in submits a file verification (QC) job to a Baton server and monitors its progress.
2.  Specify the Baton server address, port, and authentication details.
3.  Specify the test plan name and version as defined on the Baton server.
4.  Specify the full UNC path of the file or folder to verify.
5.  The source path, test plan, job name, and priority are submitted to the Baton server as part of the QC job request.
6.  The QC job is polled until completion, and results are returned when the step completes.
7.  Ensure the configured port (e.g., 8080) is reachable from Orchestrator to the Baton server.
8.  For Baton versions \< 9: Ensure the login credentials (username and password) are valid by verifying them directly in the Baton UI.
9.  For Baton version 9.\*: Ensure the API Token is valid. The token can be obtained from the Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin.
10. Ensure the path submitted to Baton is a valid absolute UNC path.
11. Ensure the UNC path is registered within the Baton UI.
12. For Baton version 6.\*, dynamic test plan parameters must be valid JSON objects in the "Value" field.

### Baton xml report

![Baton xml report icon](icons/BatonXmlReport.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-ins enables the retrieval of XML Report from Baton

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.3.0   | Added support for Baton v9.x REST API               |
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.1   | initial release                                     |

#### How It Works

- Connects to a **Baton server** to retrieve XML quality control reports for specified task IDs.
- Accepts **Task IDs** passed in as an array.
- Supports connecting to the Baton server via a configured **remote Execution Node** or directly via **host name or IP address**.
- Supports **Baton versions \< 9** using username and password authentication, and **Baton version \>= 9** using an API token.
- Accepts an optional **port number**, defaulting to 8080 if not specified.
- Returns the **QC report content in XML format** for all provided task IDs.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **Baton XML Report** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved Baton XML Report configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved Baton XML Report configured instance.
    - : Remote node name of the server hosting the Baton server.
    - : Remote host name or IP address of the server hosting the Baton server.
    - : Port number of the remote server hosting the Baton server. Defaults to 8080 if not specified.
    - : Select the Baton version (\< 9 or \>= 9). Determines the authentication method used.
    - : Username of a user configured on the Baton server to make API calls.
    - : (For versions \< 9) Password of the user configured on the Baton server.
    - : (For version \>= 9) API token for authentication. Available in the Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin.
    - : The Baton Task IDs for which XML reports are to be retrieved.

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the Baton XML Report plugin retrieving XML QC reports from a Baton server using API token authentication.

| Parameter | Value |
|----|----|
| Name | Get QC Report for Verified Files |
| Comments | Retrieve XML report for task IDs from Baton File Verification step |
| Baton Server Node | (blank - using IP address instead) |
| Baton Server address | 10.0.103.85 |
| Baton port | 8080 |
| Baton version | \>= 9 |
| Baton user | admin |
| API Token | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* |
| Baton Task IDs | (passed at runtime from Baton File Verification plugin) |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved Baton XML Report configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved Baton XML Report configured instance. |
| **Baton Server Node** | Remote node name of the server hosting the Baton server. |
| **Baton Server address** | Remote host name or IP address of the server hosting the Baton server. |
| **Baton port** | Port number of the remote server hosting the Baton server. Defaults to 8080 if not specified. |
| **Baton version** | Select the Baton version (\< 9 or \>= 9). Determines the authentication method used. |
| **Baton user** | Username of a user configured on the Baton server to make API calls. |
| **Baton password** | (For versions \< 9) Password of the user configured on the Baton server. |
| **API Token** | (For version \>= 9) API token for authentication. Available in the Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin. |
| **Baton Task IDs** | The Baton Task IDs for which XML reports are to be retrieved. |

#### Inputs Description

All parameters are available as runtime inputs if not specified in the template. Note that this plugin does not support value overwrites — if a value is set in the template, it will not be available as a runtime input.

**Mandatory Inputs\**

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Baton Server Node or Baton IP Address* | Either a remote Execution Node must be selected or a remote host name/IP address must be provided. One of these is required. |
| *Baton Task IDs* | Array of Task IDs for which XML reports are to be retrieved. |

**Optional Inputs\**

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Name* | A name to identify the configured template instance. |
| *Comments* | Additional information to help identify the template. |
| *Baton API Port* | Port number for the Baton server. Defaults to 8080 if not specified. |

#### Outputs Description

The output is the full QC report content returned by the Baton server in XML format.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Xml_Report | Contents of the QC report in XML format for the provided task IDs. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

Requires a running and accessible Baton server. Port 8080 (or the configured Baton port) must be open from the Orchestrator server to the Baton server.

#### Operating Instructions

This plugin connects to a Baton server and retrieves XML QC reports for the provided Task IDs. Task IDs are typically passed in from the Baton File Verification plugin as part of a larger quality control workflow.

**Authentication by Baton Version\**

- **Baton versions below 9:** Authentication uses a **username and password**. Provide the Baton user and Baton password fields.
- **Baton version 9 and above:** Authentication uses an **API token**. The token can be obtained from the Baton GUI under **SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin**.

**Best Practices\**

- Use the **Baton File Verification plugin output** to pass Task IDs directly into this plugin to ensure accurate report retrieval.
- Specify a **descriptive Name and Comments** in the template to make the step easily identifiable in complex workflows.
- Verify the **Baton version setting** matches your server version. Using the wrong authentication mode will cause login failures.
- If using **API token authentication**, ensure the token isn't expired to prevent mid-workflow authentication failures.
- Implement **error handling** workflows to manage cases where the Baton server is unreachable or task IDs are invalid.

**Troubleshooting\**

- **Connection refused:** Ensure port 8080 (or the configured Baton port) is open and accessible from the Orchestrator server to the Baton server.
- **Authentication failure (version \< 9):** Verify the username and password are valid by logging into the Baton UI directly with the same credentials.
- **Authentication failure (version \>= 9):** Verify the API token is valid. Obtain a fresh token from the Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin.
- **Wrong Baton version selected:** Confirm the Baton version setting matches the actual server version. A mismatch will cause authentication to fail.
- **Empty XML report output:** Confirm the provided Task IDs are valid and that reports are available for those tasks on the Baton server.
- **Server not reachable:** Verify the Baton Server address or Execution Node is correctly specified and the Baton server is running.

**Important Notes\**

- Either a **Baton Server Node or a Baton Server address** must be provided. (Both are not required simultaneously)
- If a value is **specified in the template, it will not be available as a runtime input**. This plugin does not support value overwrites at runtime.
- The **Baton port defaults to 8080** if not specified.
- Task IDs must be provided as an **array**. These are typically passed in directly from the Baton File Verification plugin output.
- Authentication method is determined by the **Baton version** selected: password for versions below 9, API token for version 9 and above.
- The API token for version 9 and above is available in the **Baton GUI under SETUP \> Accounts \> User \> admin**.

### Cerify File Verification

![Cerify File Verification icon](icons/CerifyFileVerification.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-in provides the ability to submit a quality control task to a Cerify Server.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.6 | Removing soap4r dependency |
| 0.1.5 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.1.4 | Adding Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.1.3 | Bug fix - MediaSet creation fails when white space is present in the path due to the escaping library used. |
| 0.1.2 | Bug fix - Job Progress was not indicated in newer versions of Cerify, adding a REST call to fetch that status. |
| 0.1.1 | \- 2015-02-11 - ADM -\> Bug fix: Job name made completely flexible |
| 0.0.8 | \- 2015-02-11 - ADM -\> Bug fix: Job name made dynamic |
| 0.0.7 | \- 2015-02-11 - PM -\> Bug fix: adding a rescue for start_time fetch and check for alerts before performing operations |
| 0.0.6 | \- 2014/12/18 - PM -\> Bug fix: added exception handling in get_job_status and checking for nil |
| 0.0.5 | 2015-02-17 ML - New icon |
| 0.0.4 | exception handling of RemoteNode.find_by_name() |
| 0.0.3 | Removes explicit dependency on xmlsimple |
| 0.0.2 | fields will use default_get instead of field value |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Cerify File Verification template

- **Cerify Server Node**: The remote node name of the server which hosts the Cerify File Verification server

- **Cerify Server IP address (or hostname)**: The remote host name or remote IP of the server which hosts the Cerify server

- **Cerify API port (optional)**: The port number of remote server which hosts the Cerify server

- **Cerify Profiles**: A dropdown of Cerify profiles available on Cerify server

- **Cerify Media Locations**: A dropdown of Cerify Media Locations available on Cerify server

- **Media URL**: The absolute path of the file on the Cerify server. Must be under the Media Location and should be prefixed by either 'file://' or 'ftp://' or 'smb://' followed by the file path

- **Keep Media Set on server (optional)**: Check this option if the Media Set created should remain on Cerify server. If un-checked the media set will be deleted

- **Force Pass on Warning (optional)**: Check this option if the file must be deemed passed, if there is a warning. If un-checked the file will be deemed as failed

- **Job Name (optional)**: The name of the file verification job.

- **Force Job Name (optional)**: Do not add punicity postfix to the name of the file verification job.

- **Priority (optional)**: The priority that should be assigned to the job submitted to Cerify server

- **Status Polling frequency**: How often progress will be reported. Default is 5 seconds

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the CerifyFileVerification action template.

- All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory:**\

- **Cerify Server Node or Cerify IP Address**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.
- **Profile Name**: A Profile Name is mandatory while submitting a job, hence a profile must be selected from dropdown.
- **Media Location**: A Media Location is mandatory while submitting a job, hence a media location must be selected from dropdown.
- **Media URL**: Absolute path to the source file on remote server must be provided. Must be under the Media Location and should be prefixed by either 'file://' or 'ftp://' or 'smb://' followed by the file path

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.
- **Cerify API Port**: The port is optional as a default value (80) is used if not specified.
- **Keep Media Set on server**: If the check box is not checked, the media set created will be deleted.
- **Force Pass on Warning**: If the check box is not checked, the file will be failed even if there is only a warning.
- **Job Name**: If a Job Name is not provided a default name suffixed by timestamp is used, making it unique.
- **Priority**: A low priority is assigned to the job if nothing is provided.
- **Status Polling frequency**: Specify if progress needs to be reported within a time other than default.

##### Outputs description

- **Input_Media_Params**: The media parameters involved in the file verification operation
- **File_Verification_Result**: The output file(s) obtained as a result of the file verification operation
- **File_Stats**: The input file's name, size and path obtained from the Cerify server during the file verification operation
- **Job_Name**: The name of the job triggered by the file verification operation
- **Alerts**: Alerts reported by Cerify server during file verification operation

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Cerify server to do a file verification/QA job.\
\
The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\
The plug-in supports the latest API release. An example of a QC job is given below.\
\

##### An example of a QC workflow below

\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Click the button Get Profiles and Media Locations. This provides dropdowns with profiles and media locations obtained from the provided Cerify server\
c) Select a profile from the dropdown\
d) Select a media location from the dropdown and provide a media URL. The media URL should be under the media location.\
e) Example of a media URL is: 'file://C:/Cerify/./cerify_demo/sample.3gp'. Media URLs should be prefixed by eithe 'file://' or 'ftp://' or 'smb://'\
f) The profile name, media location, media URL are submitted along with job name and priority to Cerify server\
\

### Irt mxf analyser

![Irt mxf analyser icon](icons/IrtMxfAnalyser.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-ins enables the submission of a file verification request to a IRT MXF Analyser server.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.5   | Adding Remote Node Dependency                       |
| 0.0.4   | fixed problem when job name set in config           |
| 0.0.3   | add more outputs                                    |
| 0.0.2   | add more outputs                                    |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved IrtMxfAnalyser configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved IrtMxfAnalyser configured instance.

- **Job Name**: The name to give to the verification job. This is optional: if specified it has to be unique or the verification will fail.

- **File path**: The path to the file to verify. It refers to the path on the MxfAnalyser server. See Operating instructions for more details

- **User Id for the job**: The user id to use for the verification job. This is optional: if not specified orchestrator is used

- **Priority**: The priority of the verification job. This is optional: if not specified normal is used

- **Analysis profile**: The profile to use for the verification job. The available profiles depend on the server configuration.

- **Service node**: The node where Irt Mxf Analyser server is running, if configured as an external node. If the node is not configured use the advanced parameters section to set the address and port
  \
  *Advanced parameters:*

- **Server address**: The ip address of the node where Irt Mxf Analyser server is running. This is optional: if the service node is set this value will be ignored

- **Service port**: The port at which the Irt Mxf Analyser server is listening. This is optional: if not set 8080 is used

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the IrtMxfAnalyser action template.

All template parameters can be set at run time using the corresponding input.\
At runtime *File path, Analysis profile and one between Service node and Server address* are mandatory and have to be specified.

##### Outputs description

- **Traffic light**: High level result of the validation. Possible values: Red, Green
- **Analysis report**: The complete validation report in XML
- **Link to Report page**: Link to the report page in the Mxf Analyser Server

##### Operating Instructions

The plugin operates by creating a verification job on the selected Mxf Analyser server using the given inputs.\
The user has to carefully think about how the MxfAnalyser server accesses the input file: the specified file path refers to the local path to the file on the Mxf Analyser machine: since Mxf Analyser runs as a service, if a network path or a mounted network drive is used the service has to run with an authorized user to access the file. Refer to Mxf Analyser manual for more information.\

### Orion OTT Monitor

![Orion OTT Monitor icon](icons/OrionOttMonitor.png){.plugin-icon}

This is Action plug-ins provides the ability to submit file to Orion OTT Monitor

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.3.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.2.0 | Add monitoring template functionality |
| 0.1.1 | Fixing options functionality and decreasing API calls |
| 0.1.0 | Added profile field for start monitoring api call |
| 0.0.7 | support for 1.5 version |
| 0.0.6 | more logging and rescue added to get pdf api |
| 0.0.5 | Help file fix |
| 0.0.4 | outputs fix to reset outputs to make it work in 2.6.1 or older orchestrator |
| 0.0.3 | fixed some issues and added playback url api call |
| 0.0.2 | help file added |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved Orion Ott Monitor configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved Orion Ott Monitor configured instance.

- **execution_node**: Select the execution node for Orion OTT server

- **server_address**: If no remote node is configured for Orion, use this field to enter the server IP address

- **orion_port**: Port to access the Orion OTT server. Defaulted to 2029

- **orion_username**: Username to access the server. Defaulted to admin

- **orion_password**: Password to access the server. Defaulted to admin

- **VOD Content Name**: Content name for the content to be uploaded

- **VOD Content URL**: Content URL for the content to be monitored

- **Encryption Type**: Choose between CENC and Sample-AES encryption

- **Register Keys**: This for for the DRM keys registering part. If more than 1 key is present, enter them as a comma separated string.

- **Decode Media Segments**: Check this box if you want to decode media segments

- **Monitoring Profile**: Optional parameter for the profile to start monitoring. If not provided, the default is used. The default is determined by Orion OTT based on the format. The profile will not be used if a *Monitoring Template* is also specified.

- **Monitoring Template**: Optional parameter for the template to start monitoring. If not provided, the default is used.

- **Enable Quality?**: While monitoring check this option if you want to enable quality

- **Enable Loudness?**: While monitoring check this option if you want to enable loudness

- **PDF storage Path**: Enter the path to storage the monitoring result PDF file

- **Content Status Polling Frequency**: Polling frequency to follow the status of the content until it is not 'Initializing'. Default Value is 15

- **Content Monitoring Polling Frequency**: Polling Frequency to monitor the status of the job. Default value is 300

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the OrionOttMonitor action template.

**Register Keys**: A Key is a combination of KeyID, Initialization Vector(in case of AES), Content Encryption Key. So separate them using "\|" symbol as mentioned in the Orion OTT API guide

##### Outputs description

- **Minor_Errors_Count**: The number of errors deemed minor.
- **Major_Errors_Count**: The number of errors deemed major.
- **Critical_Errors_Count**: The number of errors deemed critical.
- **Pdf_Report_Path**: The path to the auto-generated PDF report.
- **Content_Id**: The ID of the content that was monitored.
- **HTTP_Response_Code**: The HTTP response code that was returned.
- **Raw_Response_Hash**: A hash with the contents of the response received by the client.
- **Orion_Code_Received**: The error code returned by Orion. 0 indicates success while non zero value indicates an error.
- **Message_Received**: The status message returned by Orion.
- **Content_Register_Error_Message**: Error codes returned when registering content.
- **Key_Register_Error_Message**: Error codes returned when registering keys.
- **Asset_Summary**: A hash with the contents of the response received by the client when getting VOD content summary.
- **Play_Url**: The player URL returned by Orion.

##### Operating Instructions

- **Pulsar Server Node or Pulsar IP Address**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.

- **Job_ID**: The id of the job triggered by the file verification operation

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Pulsar server to do a file verification/QA job.\
\
The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\
The plug-in supports the latest API release. An example of a QC job is given below.\
\

##### An example of a QC workflow below

\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Click the button Get Templats. This provides dropdowns with templates obtained from the provided Pulsar server\
c) Select a template from the dropdown\
d) Example of a source file is //10.0.0.1/Source/Movie.mpg\
e) The template, source are submitted to Pulsar server\
\

### QScan file verification

![QScan file verification icon](icons/QscanFileVerification.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to submit and monitor a file verification job to a QScan server.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.1   | Initial implementation                              |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved QscanFileVerification configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved QscanFileVerification configured instance.

- **Address**: The IP address or hostname of the QScan server.

- **Port**: The port number of the QScan server.

- **User**: The username to login to the QScan server.

- **Password**: The password to login to the QScan server.

- **Template name**: A dropdown of the QScan templates.

- **Template id**: The ID of the selected QScan template.

- **Repository name**: A dropdown of the QScan repositories.

- **Repository id**: The ID of the selected QScan repository.

- **File path**: The path to the source file inside a QScan repository.

- **Job name**: The name of the job.

- **Save pdf report**: If selected, the PDF report is saved.

- **PDF report file path**: The path of the PDF report file.

- **Delete completed job**: If selected, the completed job is deleted.

- **Polling frequency**: The interval in seconds to check for the job status.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the QscanFileVerification action template.

- \
  **These inputs are mandatory:**
- **address**: The IP address or hostname of the QScan server.
- **user**: The username to login to the QScan server.
- **password**: The password to login to the QScan server.
- **template_id**: The ID of the QScan template.
- **repository_id**: The ID of the QScan repository.
- **file_path**: The path to the source file inside a QScan repository.
- **pdf_report_file_path**: The path of the PDF report file if 'save PDF report' is selected in configuration.
  \
  **These inputs are optional:**
- **port**: The port number of the QScan server (default is '8080').
- **job_name**: The name of the job (default is an UUID).
- **polling_frequency**: The interval in seconds to check for the job status (default is '10' sec).

##### Outputs description

- **events**: The QC events found.
- **job_name**: The name of the job.
- **pdf_file_size**: The size of the PDF report file if 'save PDF report' is selected in configuration.

##### Operating Instructions

### Quasar Venera QC

![Quasar Venera QC icon](icons/QuasarVeneraQcOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-ins enables the submission of a file verification request to an Quasar Venera QC server

| Version | Comment                                                |
|---------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8    |
| 0.0.2   | Changing the api to JobDetails to get more information |
| 0.0.1   | Initial Release                                        |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Quasar File Verification template

- **End Point URL**: URL of the Quasar Server.

- **Request Name**: Name of the request. (Availabe option currently is only Create Job)

- **Account**: Account number of the QC Server

- **Api Key**: Api key to access the QC server

- **File Path**: Entire location to the file path

- **Template Name**: Name of the template to be executed for the job

- **Template Type**: Type of the template (1 - General, 2 - Adaptive)

- **Job Type**: Type of the job (0 - General, One frame per file, 4 - Adaptive bit rate, 7 - Package)

- **Access Key**: Access key to access the file on S3

- **Access Key Secret**: secret key to access file in S3

- **User Note**: Notes associated with the job

- **polling frequency**: Frequency at which the status has to be polled

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the QuasarVeneraQcOperation action template.

- All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory:**\

- **end point url**: Enter it along with apibroker. Example -https://apisquasar.veneratech.com/apibroker/

\

##### Outputs description

- **Job_Status_Raw_Response**: Job Status polling response hash
- **Create_Job_Raw_Response**: Job Creation response hash
- **Error_Message**: Any error encountered in the job submission
- **Message_Received**: If no error encountered in the job submission, this field will have the value of the message sent by quasar.
- **Error_Count**: If any error present, this field will have the count of the error messages
- **Warning_Count**: If any warning present, this field will have the count of the warning messages. (On warning, we still complete the step if there are no errors)

##### Operating Instructions

### Vidchecker verification

![Vidchecker verification icon](icons/VidcheckerVerification.png){.plugin-icon}

Executes and monitors quality control verifications on a VidChecker server.

| Version | Comment |

| 0.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.0 | Added alternative call for report generation |
| 0.2.9 | Fixed typo in HTML |
| 0.2.8 | Added report type selector, as there can only be PDF or XML, not PDF_and_XML evidently from the wsdl file |
| 0.2.7 | Fixing Ruby warning and removing soap4r gem reference |
| 0.2.6 | Fixed check status on error to support both Rejected and Reject. Fixed generation of file report. Added report file path to outputs |
| 0.2.5 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.2.4 | Returning percent_complete from check_status method to be consumed by the activities screen. |
| 0.2.3 | Adding Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.2.2 | Fixed check status on error to support both Rejected and Reject |
| 0.2.1 | Switch to file based wsdl file |
| 0.2.0 | New icon |
| 0.1.9 | Added Vidchecker Job ID as output |
| 0.1.8 | Removes explicit dependency on xmlsimple |
| 0.1.7 | Fixes API call for alerts information |
| 0.1.6 | Retrieve alerts information and expose them as output |
| 0.1.5 | Renamed sdk class to avoid conflicts with Vantage SDK |
| 0.1.4 | Added possibility to generate a report file |
| 0.1.3 | Added all task information to outputs |
| 0.1.2 | Removed xml report and added edit screen |
| 0.1.1 | Fixed problem with fail on warning |
| 0.1.0 | Added configurable polling frequency |
| 0.0.3 | Fixed problem with persistence |
| 0.0.2 | Fixed problem during job status check |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release |

#### How It Works

- This action plug-in interacts with a VidChecker server to perform media file verification. The verification operations executed depend on the configuration of the selected VidChecker template.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **VidcheckerVerification** plug-in.
2.  This action performs media file verification using a VidChecker server.
3.  Fill out the following configuration fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved VidcheckerVerification configuration.
    - : Optional comments about this VidcheckerVerification configuration.
    - : The name of the VidChecker template to execute.
    - : The remote node on which the VidChecker server is installed.
    - : The IP address of the VidChecker server.
    - : The port on which the VidChecker server listens for API requests.
    - : When selected, the step returns a Failed status if the verification result contains warnings.
    - : When selected, VidChecker generates a detailed verification report file.
    - : The format of the generated report (for example, XML or PDF).
    - : If report generation is enabled, specifies the directory where the report is stored. This path is local to the VidChecker server. Note: VidChecker does not support specifying a report file name; only the destination directory can be configured.

#### Example Configuration

This is a minimal setup configuration for the VidcheckerVerification plug-in.

| Parameter              | Value                   |
|------------------------|-------------------------|
| Template               | HD_Quality_Check        |
| VidChecker server node | VidChecker_Node_01      |
| VidChecker IP address  | 192.168.10.25           |
| VidChecker API port    | 8080                    |
| Fail on warning        | Selected                |
| Generate report file   | Selected                |
| Report type            | PDF                     |
| Report file path       | /var/vidchecker/reports |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved VidcheckerVerification configuration. |
| **Comments** | Optional comments about this VidcheckerVerification configuration. |
| **Template** | The name of the VidChecker template to execute. |
| **VidChecker server node** | The remote node on which the VidChecker server is installed. |
| **VidChecker IP address** | The IP address of the VidChecker server. |
| **VidChecker API port** | The port on which the VidChecker server listens for API requests. |
| **Fail on warning** | When selected, the step returns a Failed status if the verification result contains warnings. |
| **Generate report file** | When selected, VidChecker generates a detailed verification report file. |
| **Report type** | The format of the generated report (for example, XML or PDF). |
| **Report file path** | If report generation is enabled, specifies the directory where the report is stored. This path is local to the VidChecker server. Note: VidChecker does not support specifying a report file name; only the destination directory can be configured. |

#### Inputs Description

Required inputs depend on how the VidcheckerVerification template is configured. If dynamic attributes are used, each attribute becomes a runtime input.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *File_path (mandatory)* | The full path to the media file to be verified. |
| *Service_node_or_address (mandatory)* | The remote node or IP address of the VidChecker server. |
| *Template_id (mandatory)* | The identifier of the VidChecker template to execute. |
| *Polling_frequency* | The polling interval in seconds used to check verification progress. |
| *Report_file_path* | If report generation is enabled, the directory where the report file is stored. |
| *Server_port* | The port used by the VidChecker server for API requests. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Alerts_count | The total number of alerts detected during verification. |
| Verification_result | The overall verification result. |
| Stream_info | A hash containing detailed stream information. |
| Task_alerts | A list of all alerts generated during verification. |
| Task_info | A hash containing detailed information about the verification tasks and results. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

1.  Configure the action to connect to a VidChecker server and select the VidChecker template to execute. The template defines which verification operations are performed.
2.  Specify the media file to be verified. VidChecker executes all verification tasks defined in the selected template.
3.  After verification completes, the action returns the total alert count along with detailed verification results.
4.  Optionally enable report generation to produce a detailed verification report containing all task results.
5.  When report generation is enabled, select the report type (XML or PDF). The report file is stored locally on the VidChecker server.

### VqbifAnalyzer

![VqbifAnalyzer icon](icons/VqbifAnalyzer.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in proivdes capability to execute Vqbif commands on a remote node.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | Adding Remote Node Dependency                       |
| 0.0.1   | Plugin release                                      |

- This action allows to execute an VQBif analysis on a remote Windows node.\

  ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: Comments associated to this template or configuration.

- **Execution node**: The remote node to execute the HandBrake operation from. List of already configured Remote Nodes.

- **VQBif Binary path**: The path (as seen in the SSH session) of the VQBif executable.

- **Input file path**: The path (as seen in the Windows Terminal) of the input file to VQBif.

- **Input file path two**: The path (as seen in the Windows Terminal) of the optional input file to VQBif.

- **Input file path three**: The path (as seen in the Windows Terminal) of the optional input file to VQBif.

- **Output file path**: The path (as seen in the Windows Terminal) of the output file of VQBif.(Preferred format is '.txt')

- **Review Mode**: The review mode for the JPEG export (Comes with two options, Full Set View Mode and Quick Review Mode)

- **Jpeg Folder Path**: The path where you need to export the JPEGs to.

- **Framewise Separation**: BIF(s) Framewise Separation checked against the specified value

<!-- -->

- \- Input file path and the Execution node, VQBif binary path are mandatory parameters for the execution of this plugin.\
  - Output file path if not provided, will be defaulted to the input file name appending '\_PASS' or '\_FAIL' depending on the analysis results.\
  - Input file path two and Input file path three, are optional inputs that can be given in a single command. At the most 3 inputs are be provded in a single command.\
  - Review Mode is an optional input, which always you to exprot the JPEGs file. Full review mode will export all the JPEGs while Quick review mode will export 5x5x5 JPEGs.\
  - Jpeg folder path is to specify where the JPEGs files to be exported to for either of the review mode selected. If Jpeg folder path is not provided, folder name will be defaulted to input file name follwed by '\_JPG'\
  - framewise Separation is again an optional input. Framewise Separation is checked against the specified value.\

##### Outputs description

- This action executes an VQBif Analysis on a remote node, via SSH.\
- **Output_File_Path**: The path of the output file
- **Transformation_Report**: A report of the VQBif Analysis operation

##### Operating Instructions

This action plug-in provides the ability to perform a Analysis using the VQBif command line utility.\
**NOTE: VQBif Executable for command line execution should be installed on the remote node.**\
The Output file will be stored on the specified path in the remote node.\
Note:1) VQBif requires file to be specified in the windows path format. (Example C:\Documents\sample.txt)\
2) Output files are preferred to be in the '.txt' format. 3) Input path two, Input path three, Review mode and Framwize Separation are optional inputs.\
**Example**\
*CONFIGURATION:*\
  VQBif binary path : "/cygdrive/c/Users/Administrator/Desktop/VideoQ_VQBIF/VQBIF.EXE"\
  Input file path : "C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\VideoQ_VQBIF\M.bif"\
  Output file path : "C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\VideoQ_VQBIF\M_result.txt"\
*OUTPUTS:*\
   OutPut Target File : "C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\VideoQ_VQBIF\M_result.txt"\
\
\
*Short guide from VideoQ (source:http://www.videoq.com/Downloads/VQBIF_ShortGuide.txt):*\
Usage: VQBIF -i in1.BIF \[in2.BIF\] \[in3.BIF\] \[-o out.\*\] \[-j/-q\] \[jpeg_folder\] \[-r Ref_Framewise_Separation_ms\]\
Optional input filenames 2 and 3 are in JPEG quality descending order\
Input BIF files must be of matching durations, frame sizes, etc.\
\[-o out.\*\] option specifies output Report file name and extension\
If \[-o\] is omitted, Report file name defaults to in1.TXT\
\[-j\] or \[-q\] options: JPEG files export to Default Folder or Specified Folderv\
If \[jpeg_folder\] specified, VQBIF creates in it sub-folders in1, in2, etc.\
If \[jpeg_folder\] is omitted, VQBIF creates default folders in1_JPG, in2_JPG, etc.\
\[-j\]: all available JPEGs exported (Full Set View Mode)\
\[-q\]: for each .BIF only 5+5+5 JPEGs exported (Quick Review Mode)\
\[-r\] option: BIF(s) Framewise Separation checked against the specified value\

### Watson speech to text

![Watson speech to text icon](icons/WatsonSpeechToText.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to get speech transcriptions with IBM Watson Speech to Text service.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.0   | added API key support                               |
| 0.0.2   | IBM Bluemix is replaced by IBM Cloud in help file   |
| 0.0.1   | Initial implementation                              |

#### How It Works

- Converts **spoken audio to text** using IBM Watson Speech to Text cloud service.
- Supports multiple **audio formats** (WAV, MP3, FLAC, OGG, etc.) and **languages**.
- Operates in **asynchronous mode**, submitting recognition jobs and polling for completion.
- Supports **keyword spotting** to identify specific words or phrases with confidence thresholds.
- Authenticates using either **API key** or **username/password** credentials.
- Returns detailed **transcription results** including text, confidence scores, timestamps, and keyword matches.
- Configurable **polling frequency** to balance responsiveness and API usage.
- Requires an active **IBM Cloud subscription** to Watson Speech to Text service.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **WatsonSpeechToText** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved Watson Speech to Text configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved Watson Speech to Text configured instance.
    - : The path to the source audio file to transcribe.
    - : The URL of IBM Watson Speech to Text service endpoint.
    - : If selected, the API key is used for authentication instead of username and password.
    - : The API key of IBM Watson Speech to Text service (required if 'Use api key' is selected).
    - : The username of IBM Watson Speech to Text service (required if 'Use api key' is not selected).
    - : The password of IBM Watson Speech to Text service (required if 'Use api key' is not selected).
    - : The identifier of the model to be used for the recognition request (e.g., en-US_BroadbandModel, en-GB_NarrowbandModel).
    - : The list of keywords to spot in the audio. Enter one or more values comma-separated.
    - : The confidence value (0.0 to 1.0) for spotting a keyword. Keywords with confidence above this threshold are reported.
    - : The interval in seconds to check for the job status. Lower values provide faster results but increase API calls.

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the WatsonSpeechToText plugin with keyword spotting enabled.

| Parameter | Value |
|----|----|
| Name | Transcribe Customer Call |
| Comments | Transcribe recorded customer service calls |
| File path | /recordings/call_20240115_001.wav |
| Url | https://api.us-south.speech-to-text.watson.cloud.ibm.com |
| Use api key | Selected |
| Api key | your-api-key-here |
| Model | en-US_BroadbandModel |
| Keywords | refund, complaint, cancel, supervisor |
| Keywords threshold | 0.7 |
| Polling frequency | 5 |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved Watson Speech to Text configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved Watson Speech to Text configured instance. |
| **File path** | The path to the source audio file to transcribe. |
| **Url** | The URL of IBM Watson Speech to Text service endpoint. |
| **Use api key** | If selected, the API key is used for authentication instead of username and password. |
| **Api key** | The API key of IBM Watson Speech to Text service (required if 'Use api key' is selected). |
| **Username** | The username of IBM Watson Speech to Text service (required if 'Use api key' is not selected). |
| **Password** | The password of IBM Watson Speech to Text service (required if 'Use api key' is not selected). |
| **Model** | The identifier of the model to be used for the recognition request (e.g., en-US_BroadbandModel, en-GB_NarrowbandModel). |
| **Keywords** | The list of keywords to spot in the audio. Enter one or more values comma-separated. |
| **Keywords threshold** | The confidence value (0.0 to 1.0) for spotting a keyword. Keywords with confidence above this threshold are reported. |
| **Polling frequency** | The interval in seconds to check for the job status. Lower values provide faster results but increase API calls. |

#### Inputs Description

The required inputs depend on the authentication mode selected. API key or username/password must be provided.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *file_path (mandatory)* | The path to the source audio file. Supported formats include WAV, MP3, FLAC, OGG, OPUS, WebM, and more. The file must be accessible by the Orchestrator. |
| *url (mandatory)* | The URL of IBM Watson Speech to Text service endpoint (e.g., https://api.us-south.speech-to-text.watson.cloud.ibm.com). |
| *api_key (mandatory)* | The API key of IBM Watson Speech to Text service. Required if 'Use api key' is selected in configuration. |
| *username (mandatory)* | The username of IBM Watson Speech to Text service. Required if 'Use api key' is not selected in configuration. |
| *password (mandatory)* | The password of IBM Watson Speech to Text service. Required if 'Use api key' is not selected in configuration. |
| *model* | The identifier of the model to be used for the recognition request. Default is 'en-US_BroadbandModel'. Choose based on audio quality (broadband vs narrowband) and language. |
| *keywords* | Comma-separated list of keywords to spot in the audio (e.g., 'urgent, complaint, refund'). Watson identifies these words with confidence scores. |
| *keywords_threshold* | The confidence value (0.0 to 1.0) for spotting a keyword. Default is '0.5'. Only keywords meeting or exceeding this threshold are reported. |
| *polling_frequency* | The interval in seconds to check for the job status. Default is '10' seconds. Balance between responsiveness and API usage. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| results | The complete job output containing transcribed text, confidence scores, timestamps, word alternatives, and keyword spotting results. Returned as a JSON structure with detailed recognition data. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

Requires an active IBM Cloud subscription to Watson Speech to Text service. You get the credentials (URL, username, password, API key) with an online subscription.

#### Operating Instructions

This plug-in converts spoken audio to text using IBM Watson Speech to Text cloud service. The service operates asynchronously, submitting recognition jobs and polling for completion.

**Authentication Modes\**

- **API Key Authentication (Recommended):**
  - Select 'Use api key' checkbox in configuration
  - Provide the API key in the 'Api key' field
  - Username and password are not required
  - More secure and easier to manage
- **Username/Password Authentication (Legacy):**
  - Leave 'Use api key' checkbox unchecked
  - Provide username in the 'Username' field
  - Provide password in the 'Password' field
  - API key is not required
  - Used for older Watson service instances

**Execution Flow\**

- **Step 1 - Job Submission:** Plugin uploads the audio file and submits a recognition job to Watson Speech to Text service.
- **Step 2 - Asynchronous Processing:** Watson service processes the audio file in the background. Processing time depends on file size and duration.
- **Step 3 - Status Polling:** Plugin polls the job status at the configured polling frequency (default: 10 seconds).
- **Step 4 - Job Completion:** When Watson completes processing, plugin retrieves the full recognition results.
- **Step 5 - Results Return:** Complete transcription results are returned in the 'results' output, including text, confidence scores, timestamps, and keyword matches.

**Supported Audio Formats\**

- **Lossless Formats (Recommended):**
  - WAV (audio/wav) - Uncompressed, best quality
  - FLAC (audio/flac) - Lossless compression
- **Compressed Formats:**
  - MP3 (audio/mp3) - Widely supported, good quality
  - OGG (audio/ogg) - Open format with good compression
  - OPUS (audio/opus) - High-quality speech compression
  - WebM (audio/webm) - Modern web format
- **Telephony Formats:**
  - G.729, mu-law (8 kHz audio) - Use narrowband models
- **Audio Requirements:**
  - Maximum file size: Typically 100 MB (varies by service plan)
  - Sample rate: 8 kHz (narrowband) or 16+ kHz (broadband)
  - Duration: No strict limit, but longer files take more time
  - Channels: Mono preferred, stereo supported

**Setup and Configuration Steps\**

- **Step 1: Subscribe to IBM Watson Speech to Text**
  - Go to IBM Cloud (https://cloud.ibm.com)
  - Create an account or sign in
  - Navigate to the Catalog and search for 'Speech to Text'
  - Create a new Speech to Text service instance
  - Select an appropriate pricing plan
- **Step 2: Get Service Credentials**
  - From the IBM Cloud dashboard, open your Speech to Text service instance
  - Go to 'Manage' → 'Credentials' or 'Service credentials'
  - Create new credentials if needed
  - Copy the API Key and URL (or Username/Password for legacy instances)
- **Step 3: Prepare Audio File**
  - Ensure audio file is in a supported format (WAV, MP3, FLAC, etc.)
  - Verify sample rate matches model type (8 kHz for narrowband, 16+ kHz for broadband)
  - Place the audio file in a location accessible by Orchestrator and note the full file path
- **Step 4: Configure Orchestrator Plugin**
  - Create a new Watson Speech to Text action in your workflow
  - Enter the service URL from IBM Cloud
  - Select 'Use api key' and enter your API key (or use username/password)
  - Choose appropriate model based on audio quality and language
  - Specify the audio file path
  - Configure keywords and threshold if needed
- **Step 5: Test Configuration**
  - Use a short test audio file to verify connectivity
  - Check the 'results' output for transcription accuracy
  - Verify keyword spotting if configured
  - Adjust model, keywords, or threshold as needed

**Common Use Cases\**

- **Call center transcription:** Transcribe customer service calls for quality assurance, compliance, and analytics.
- **Meeting transcription:** Convert recorded meetings and interviews into searchable text documents.
- **Voicemail transcription:** Automatically transcribe voicemail messages for email delivery.
- **Content accessibility:** Create text captions for video and audio content.
