    - : The name of the remote workflow to launch.
    - : The Portable ID of the remote workflow to launch.
    - : Optional custom name for the remote work order. If not specified, it is displayed as '(remote)'.
    - : Select whether the remote execution runs as a Sub-Workflow or as a Workflow Launcher.
    - : The priority assigned to the remote workflow execution.
    - : When selected, a dedicated worker is launched to execute the remote workflow.
    - : The preemptive execution level assigned to the workflow.
    - : The interval at which execution status is retrieved from the remote workflow.
    - : When selected, detailed running status updates are retrieved during execution.
    - : When selected, running status details are propagated to the top-level work order.
    - : Defines variables used to store outputs returned by the remote workflow execution.

#### Example Configuration

This is a minimal setup configuration for the FederatedWorkflow plug-in.

| Parameter | Value |
|----|----|
| Remote Node | Remote_Orchestrator_Node |
| Use SSL? | Selected |
| Remote Web-root | /aspera/orchestrator/ |
| Remote user | orchestrator_admin |
| Remote Password | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* |
| Identify Workflow by Name? | Selected |
| Remote Workflow Name | Media_Processing_Workflow |
| Remote Work Order Name | Federated Media Processing |
| Operation | Workflow Launcher |
| Priority | Normal |
| Launch Dedicated Worker | Selected |
| Preemptive Level | Standard |
| Reporting Frequency | 30 seconds |
| Report Running Status details | Selected |
| Report Running Status details to top level work order | Selected |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved FederatedWorkflow configuration. |
| **Comments** | Optional comments about this FederatedWorkflow configuration. |
| **Remote Node** | The Aspera Orchestrator node on which the remote workflow is executed. |
| **Remote Address** | The IP address or hostname of the remote Orchestrator instance, used when a Remote Node configuration is not defined. |
| **Remote Port** | The port used to access Orchestrator on the remote system when a Remote Node configuration is not defined. |
| **Use SSL?** | When selected, the connection to the remote Orchestrator instance uses SSL. |
| **No web-root?** | When selected, indicates that the remote Orchestrator instance does not have a web-root configured. |
| **Remote Web-root** | The web-root path of the remote Orchestrator instance. This is typically set to '/aspera/orchestrator/'. |
| **Remote user** | The login name of the Orchestrator user on the remote instance. |
| **Remote Password** | The password of the Orchestrator user on the remote instance. |
| **Identify Workflow by Name?** | By default, the workflow is identified by its ID. When selected, the workflow is identified by name instead. |
| **Identify Workflow by Portable ID?** | By default, the workflow is identified by its ID. When selected, the workflow is identified by Portable ID instead. |
| **Remote Workflow ID** | The ID of the remote workflow to launch. |
| **Remote Workflow Name** | The name of the remote workflow to launch. |
| **Remote Workflow Portable ID** | The Portable ID of the remote workflow to launch. |
| **Remote Work Order Name** | Optional custom name for the remote work order. If not specified, it is displayed as '(remote)'. |
| **Operation** | Select whether the remote execution runs as a Sub-Workflow or as a Workflow Launcher. |
| **Priority** | The priority assigned to the remote workflow execution. |
| **Launch Dedicated Worker** | When selected, a dedicated worker is launched to execute the remote workflow. |
| **Preemptive Level** | The preemptive execution level assigned to the workflow. |
| **Reporting Frequency** | The interval at which execution status is retrieved from the remote workflow. |
| **Report Running Status details** | When selected, detailed running status updates are retrieved during execution. |
| **Report Running Status details to top level work order** | When selected, running status details are propagated to the top-level work order. |
| **Add Outputs** | Defines variables used to store outputs returned by the remote workflow execution. |

#### Inputs Description

Required inputs depend on how the FederatedWorkflow template is configured. If dynamic attributes are used, each attribute becomes a runtime input.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Remote_address (mandatory) | The IP address or hostname of the remote Orchestrator instance. |
| Remote_workflow_id (mandatory) | The ID of the workflow to launch on the remote Orchestrator instance. |
| Work_order_parameters (mandatory) | A hash of input parameters passed to the remote workflow when creating the work order. |
| Remote_port | The port used to connect to the remote Orchestrator instance (optional). |
| Web_root | The web-root path of the remote Orchestrator instance (optional). |
| Work_order_name | An optional custom name for the remote work order. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Execution_node | Information about the node on which the remote workflow is executed. |
| Execution_address | The IP address or hostname of the node executing the remote workflow. |
| WorkOrder_ID | The ID of the remote work order created for the workflow execution. |
| WorkOrder_Status | The current execution status of the remote work order. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

1.  This action plug-in initiates a workflow execution on a remote Orchestrator instance and optionally monitors its progress until completion.

### Filter

![Filter icon](icons/Filter.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in allows conditional execution of a step by evaluating a custom Ruby expression at run-time. The result determines whether the step completes successfully or fails.

| Version | Comment                                                 |
|---------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.3.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8     |
| 0.2.8   | Added styling to parameter help text.                   |
| 0.2.7   | 2015-02-16 ML - New icon                                |
| 0.2.6   | improved help file                                      |
| 0.2.5   | allows status details to be set for error also          |
| 0.2.4   | allows status details to be set.                        |
| 0.2.3   | adds help file                                          |
| 0.2.2   | adds payload support in command, creates usable default |
| 0.2.1   | enables use of return inside filter command field       |
| 0.2.0   | adds optional outputs                                   |
| 0.1.0   | first tracked version                                   |

### How It Works

- Executes a Ruby command at run-time to determine whether the step should proceed or fail.
- The command must return a boolean value (`true` or `false`).
- Inputs are accessed via the `inputs` hash; outputs can optionally be set via the `outputs` hash.
- If the result is `true`, the step completes. If `false`, the step fails.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **Filter** plug-in.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - **Name**: A label to identify the filter step.
    - **Command**: Ruby code to evaluate. Must return `true` or `false`.
    - **Required inputs**: Declare mandatory inputs (with data types).
    - **Optional inputs**: Declare optional values used by the command.
    - **Filter outputs**: Optionally declare any values to expose from the command execution.
3.  Click **Update** to save the step.

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved Filter configuration instance. |
| **Command** | The body of the Ruby code to execute. The result of the code must be a boolean. Inputs are accessed through the `inputs` hash. |
| **Required inputs** | Used to declare the mandatory inputs for the command. Inputs must be typed. |
| **Optional inputs** | Used to declare the optional inputs for the command. |
| **Filter outputs** | Optional outputs that can be set using the `outputs` hash during command execution. |

### Inputs Description

Inputs are defined in the **Required inputs** and **Optional inputs** fields of the step.

The inputs will be the ones declared in those sections and can be accessed in the Ruby command using the `inputs` hash.

### Outputs Description

| Output | Description |
|----|----|
| **FilterResult** | The boolean result returned by the execution of the filter command. |
| *Other declared outputs* | Any additional outputs defined in the **Filter outputs** section, with their names and types. |

### Supported Actions

None

### Operating Instructions

This plugin allows conditional branching using custom Ruby code. The command has access to the inputs (both required and optional) via the `inputs` hash and may set outputs via the `outputs` hash.

The command must return a boolean value:

- `true`: Step completes successfully.
- `false`: Step fails with **Failed** status.

### Example Configuration

**Validate that a variable `file_size` (Integer) is not null or zero:**

    (inputs['file_size'] > 0)

**Alternate multi-line version:**

    if inputs['file_size'] > 0
      return true
    else
      return false
    end

**Check that file at path input `file_path` (String) exists and is non-empty:**

    filePath = inputs['file_path']
    return File.exist?(filePath) && (File.size(filePath) > 0)

**Same as above, but also output the file size as `file_size` (Integer):**

    filePath = inputs['file_path']
    if File.exist?(filePath)
      fileSize = File.size(filePath)
      outputs['file_size'] = fileSize
      return (fileSize > 0)
    else
      return false
    end

### Funnel-in

![Funnel-in icon](icons/Funnelin.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin collects and aggregates outputs from parallel executions initiated by Array Fanout, converging multiple parallel processing threads back into a unified workflow.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.1.2 | Handle configurations without funneled inputs present |
| 1.1.1 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.0.5 | Added support for step renaming in fan out (ArrayFanout \>= 1.1.0) |
| 1.0.4 | Modified to support internalized workstep inputs/outputs (required for version 3.1) |
| 1.0.3 | Restores compatibility with pre 1.0.0 version for the structure of the "Hash_by_state_id" output |
| 1.0.2 | Changes to avoid crash when variables are not present on some steps |
| 1.0.1 | Fixes status exit when fail_if_failures is set to true |
| 1.0.0 | Modified to work with Orchestrator 2.6 (requires Fan-out 1.0.0), does not work with pre-2.6 version |
| 0.4.0 | Extends inputs column |
| 0.3.7 | Added styling to help text. |
| 0.3.6 | Fixed logic to fail if failures flag when result statuses are not requested |
| 0.3.5 | Added help file |
| 0.3.4 | Implements recover and removes dependency on output list as a system input |
| 0.3.3 | Adds support for pre-processing in funneled in inputs, remove auto-casting of funneled variables into strings |
| 0.3.2 | Fixed logic to fail if failures flag |
| 0.3.1 | New icon |
| 0.3.0 | Adds fail if failures flag |
| 0.2.0 | Adds hash of statuses as output |
| 0.1.2 | Use widget for funnels variables |
| 0.1.1 | Adds funnel by state_id |
| 0.1.0 | Adds name |
| 0.0.3 | Adds display name |
| 0.0.2 | First versioned code |

### How It Works

- Collects and aggregates outputs from parallel executions created by the Array Fanout step, closing the loop on fanned operations.
- Captures the output from each step that executed after the Array Fanout, organizing results by work step ID.
- Provides status tracking for each parallel execution, indicating whether steps completed, failed, or errored.
- Supports variable type validation and customizable failure handling for robust parallel processing workflows.
- Works exclusively in conjunction with Array Fanout to enable complete parallel processing workflows.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`Funnel-In`**` step to your workflow after the parallel execution section.`
2.  `Configure the essential parameters:`
    - **`Funnel variable`**`: Name of the variable to capture output from each fanned step (e.g., result_data).`
    - **`Type of the funnel variable`**`: Expected data type of the collected variables (e.g., string, hash).`
    - **`Funneled inputs`**`: Check to capture output from steps between Array fanout and Funnel-in.`
    - **`Return statuses`**`: Enable to get execution status information for each parallel step.`
3.  `Optionally configure failure handling:`
    - **`Fail if any failures`**`: Check to fail the Funnel-in step if any parallel execution fails.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                       | Example Value    |
|---------------------------------|------------------|
| **Funnel variable**             | processed_result |
| **Type of the funnel variable** | hash             |
| **Funneled inputs**             | checked          |
| **Return statuses**             | checked          |
| **Fail if any failures**        | unchecked        |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Funnel variable** | The name of the variable to expose to capture the output for each fanned out step. |
| **Type of the funnel variable** | The type of the variable produced by the step for each fanned out step. |
| **Funneled inputs** | Capture the output of all the steps between Array fanout and Funnel-in step. |
| **Return statuses** | Returns an extra output when checked which helps in determining the status of each step. |
| **Fail if any failures** | If any of the steps between Array fanout and Funnel-in step fail, then fails the Funnel-in step also. |

### Inputs Description

- **Funnel variable inputs**: Captures input from the Array fanout step to close the loop on the number of steps that executed after the Array Fanout.

### Outputs Description

- **Hash_by_state_id**: An output, as a Hash, that is a map of the work step id that executed after Array Fanout step, and Funnel variable.
- **Status_by_state_id**: An output, as a Hash, that is a map of the work step id that executed after Array Fanout step, and whether that step completed, failed or error.

### Operating Instructions

- This action works in synergy with the **Array Fanout**. When there are multiple values in an array, these two steps allow executing the same action over each value in the array, in parallel.
- The action included between a Fan out and a Funnel in will be executed as many times as values in the input array.
- **Note: only one step is supported between a fan out and the corresponding funnel in.**
- **ATTENTION: by default, if one of the action step (the step between a fan out and the funnel in) execution fails, all others will stop at the same time.** In order to guarantee that all parallel execution completes, make sure to link the complete, fail and error outputs to a logical OR and then to the corresponding Funnel in step.

### Dependencies

- Requires a corresponding Array Fanout step earlier in the workflow to provide the parallel executions to funnel.
- The step between Array Fanout and Funnel-In must produce outputs compatible with the specified funnel variable type.
- Proper workflow connection is required between the parallel execution step and this funnel-in step.

### Troubleshooting

- Missing outputs: Verify that the funnel variable name matches the output variable from the step between Array Fanout and Funnel-In.
- Type mismatches: Ensure the 'Type of the funnel variable' setting matches the actual data type produced by the parallel steps.
- Incomplete parallel execution: If some parallel steps don't complete, check that complete, fail, and error outputs are properly wired to a logical OR before the Funnel-In step.
- Unexpected failures: If 'Fail if any failures' is enabled, review the status outputs to identify which parallel executions failed and why.
- Empty results: Confirm that the Array Fanout step is properly configured and that the intermediate step is producing the expected outputs.

### Merge point

![Merge point icon](icons/MergePoint.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin can be used to reconcile inputs coming from different branches of logical statements. It provides the ability to define pairs of inputs that get merged. Such steps can be used without inputs to clarify branching in complex workflows.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.5.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.4.1 | Fixes problem when using multi inputs without specifying the number. Assuming 2 by default. |
| 0.4.0 | Add option to consider empty objects (array or hashes) has nil. Fixed escaping problem when using dynamic inputs. |
| 0.3.1 | Fix gatherStructValues() when allPairs is null |
| 0.3.0 | Adds support for multi-inputs |
| 0.2.3 | Adds support for password fields |
| 0.2.2 | New icon |
| 0.2.1 | Fixes bug in the typing of hash Inputs in configurator |
| 0.2.0 | Adds inputs merging behavior |
| 0.1.0 | First tracked version (did nothing) |

### How It Works

- Merges multiple inputs from alternate workflow branches into single consolidated outputs for streamlined workflow reconciliation.
- Supports both merged inputs (primary/alternate pairs) and multi inputs (multiple candidate sources) with configurable priority handling.
- Provides flexible input resolution using dynamic attributes and custom expressions for complex workflow scenarios.
- Handles empty object validation by treating empty arrays and hashes as null objects when configured to do so.
- Enables complex logical operations like (A \|\| B) && (C \|\| D) by serving as an intermediate merge point between logical operators.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`Merge Point`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the basic settings:`
    - **`Consider empty objects as null`**`: Check to treat empty arrays and hashes as null values.`
    - **`Merged Inputs`**`: Define primary/alternate input pairs for simple merge scenarios.`
    - **`Multi Inputs`**`: Define multiple candidate inputs for complex selection logic.`
3.  `Set up input definitions:`
    - `For each merged input, specify the name and data type.`
    - `Configure whether the input is required or optional.`
    - `Use dynamic attributes for custom expressions if needed.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                          | Example Value     |
|------------------------------------|-------------------|
| **Consider empty objects as null** | checked           |
| **Merged Input Name**              | file_path         |
| **Merged Input Type**              | string            |
| **Merged Input Required**          | checked           |
| **Multi Input Name**               | processing_result |
| **Multi Input Type**               | hash              |
| **Multi Input Required**           | unchecked         |
| **Number of Alternates**           | 3                 |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | The comments for the Merge Point template. |
| **Consider empty objects as null** | If checked it instructs the plugin to consider empty arrays and hashes as null objects. |
| **Merged Inputs** | Defines a new Merged input. For each merged input, the output will be the primary input if it is found, the alternate input otherwise. In the case of required merged inputs, if neither is found, the action will exit in failure. Dynamic attributes can be used to define expressions for both the primary and the alternate input source. |
| **Multi Inputs** | Defines a new Multi input. For each multi input, the output will be the first encountered matching input candidate for which a value is present. The number of input alternates that can be provided and the type of those inputs can be specified. If required is selected, at least one alternate input needs to have a value or the execution will be declared failed. |

### Inputs Description

- **Primary inputs**: If a custom expression is not provided for the primary and alternate values, then a runtime input with the name of the merged input will be requested as optional runtime input.
- **Alternate inputs**: Runtime inputs with the name suffixed with '\_alternate' will be requested as optional runtime inputs for each merged input.
- **Dynamic attributes**: If dynamic attributes are used, then they are also requested as optional inputs of type 'string'.
- **Multi input candidates**: For multi inputs, all defined candidate inputs are requested as optional runtime inputs with the specified data types.

**Note:** The merged inputs are typed. By default (unless an expression is defined for the primary or alternate value), the requested variable will be of the same type as the merged input type. If an expression uses dynamic attributes, those variables are of type 'string'.

### Outputs Description

- **Merged outputs**: One output will be returned for each defined merged input. The output will have the type defined in the definition of the merged input and will have the same name.
- **Multi outputs**: One output will be returned for each defined multi input, containing the first matching input candidate that has a value present.

### Operating Instructions

- This plugin allows users to merge multiple inputs into a single output. This is useful when merging alternate branches from a workflow and reconciling some inputs.
- **Example use case:** If there is an optional move step, a logical 'OR' will be required connecting back into the workflow the branch in which the move is done and the one in which it is not done. If a step after the OR needs to know where the file is, it needs to use either the location before the move or the one after the move, depending on which branch was followed.
- A Merge Point can be used for that scenario. In this case, only one merged input would be defined, calling it for example: 'file_path'. The step will then have 2 inputs (both marked optional) 'file_path' and 'file_path_alternate'.
- If 'file_path' is found it will be used, otherwise the value of 'file_path_alternate' will be used. If neither are present and the merged input is marked as required, the execution will fail.
- Another use case would be when you need to do something like (A \|\| B) && (C \|\| D). Since you cannot AND logical operators directly, you need a merge step in the middle that is just there to allow you to link the logical operators, but it does not do anything.
- For multi inputs, the plugin evaluates candidate inputs in the order they are defined and selects the first one that has a value present.
- Dynamic attributes allow for custom expressions to determine input sources, providing flexibility for complex workflow scenarios.

### Dependencies

- No external dependencies required - this plugin operates purely on workflow data and logical operations.
- Proper workflow branch connections must be established for effective input merging from alternate paths.

### Snapshot

![Snapshot icon](icons/SnapshotPlugin.png){.plugin-icon}

Takes a snapshot of the Orchestrator configuration.

| Version | Comment                                                          |
|---------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.2.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8              |
| 0.1.0   | Correcting the plugin_name in metadata.yml                       |
| 0.0.6   | Correcting the plugin_name in metadata.yml                       |
| 0.0.5   | Edited help file                                                 |
| 0.0.4   | Adding icon                                                      |
| 0.0.3   | Moving to System category and fixing glitch in metadata          |
| 0.0.2   | Backward compatibility ensured - added logic to obtain constants |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                                  |

#### How It Works

- Creates a **MySQL dump** of the Orchestrator database configuration.
- Includes all **standard Orchestrator tables** by default.
- Optionally includes **custom database tables** specified by the user.
- Stores the snapshot file in the specified directory or uses the **default archive location**.
- Returns the **full file path** of the created snapshot for use in subsequent workflow steps.
- Enables automated **backup and recovery** of Orchestrator configuration data.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **OrchestratorSnapshot** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify this saved Orchestrator Snapshot configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved Orchestrator Snapshot configured instance to help identify its purpose.
    - : The name of the snapshot file to be created. If not specified, a default name with timestamp will be used.
    - : The directory path where the snapshot file will be stored. If not specified, the default archive directory will be used.
    - : Comma-separated list of any custom database tables that need to be included in the MySQL dump along with the standard Orchestrator tables.
    - : Select this option to include a full database dump in the export.
    - : The directory path where the database dump file will be stored.

#### Example Configuration

This is a minimum setup configuration for the OrchestratorSnapshot plugin.

| Parameter           | Value                                        |
|---------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| Name                | Daily Orchestrator Backup                    |
| Comments            | Daily snapshot of Orchestrator configuration |
| Snapshot name       | orchestrator_daily_backup                    |
| Snapshot directory  | /backups/orchestrator                        |
| Custom tables       | custom_workflows,custom_plugins              |
| Export full db      | Checked                                      |
| Db export directory | /tmp/db_backups                              |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify this saved Orchestrator Snapshot configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved Orchestrator Snapshot configured instance to help identify its purpose. |
| **Snapshot name** | The name of the snapshot file to be created. If not specified, a default name with timestamp will be used. |
| **Snapshot directory** | The directory path where the snapshot file will be stored. If not specified, the default archive directory will be used. |
| **Custom tables** | Comma-separated list of any custom database tables that need to be included in the MySQL dump along with the standard Orchestrator tables. |
| **Export full db** | Select this option to include a full database dump in the export. |
| **Db export directory** | The directory path where the database dump file will be stored. |

#### Inputs Description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the OrchestratorSnapshot action template. All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Name* | The name used to identify this template. It is advisable that a descriptive name is entered for easy identification. |
| *Comments* | Information about the template which will help identify its purpose and usage context. |
| *Snapshot_name* | The name of the snapshot file to be created (e.g., 'orchestrator_backup_2024'). If not provided, a default timestamped name will be used. |
| *Snapshot_directory* | Full path to the directory where the snapshot should be stored (e.g., '/backup/orchestrator'). If not provided, the default archive directory will be used. |
| *Custom_tables* | Comma-separated list of custom table names to include in the MySQL dump (e.g., 'custom_workflows,custom_plugins'). |
| *Export full db* | Select this option to include a full database dump in the export. |
| *Db export directory* | Full path to the directory where the database dump file will be stored. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter     | Description                                     |
|---------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| Snapshot_path | The full file path of the created snapshot file |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to create a backup snapshot of the Orchestrator configuration and database data.

**Example Workflow Using This Plugin:**

A typical backup workflow consists of the following steps:

**Step 1:** Configure a **Scheduled Trigger** that triggers every 24 hours (e.g., at 2:00 AM daily).\
**Step 2:** Configure the **Orchestrator Snapshot** plugin to create a backup of the Orchestrator configuration data.\
**Step 3:** Optionally add a **File Transfer** or **Archive** step to move the snapshot to long-term storage or a remote backup location.\
\
With these steps, an automated periodic backup of Orchestrator can be achieved, ensuring business continuity and disaster recovery capabilities.

**Best Practices\**

- Use descriptive snapshot names that include date/time information for easy identification.
- Schedule snapshots during low-usage periods to minimize performance impact on the database.
- Regularly test snapshot restoration to ensure backups are valid and complete.
- Implement a retention policy to manage disk space (e.g., keep daily snapshots for 7 days, weekly for 30 days).
- Store snapshots in a separate location from the Orchestrator server for disaster recovery purposes.
- Monitor disk space on the backup directory to prevent failures due to insufficient storage.

### Sub-workflow

![Sub-workflow icon](icons/SubWorkflow.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in allows you to embed an entire, separately defined workflow as a single step within another workflow. This enables you to break down complex processes into manageable sub-workflows, making them easier to build, maintain, and reuse.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.5.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.4.4 | Fix possible problem when stopping the subworkflow step and the inner steps are not stopped. |
| 1.4.3 | Fix possible duplication of inputs and outputs definition |
| 1.4.2 | Lists published workflows only |
| 1.4.1 | Removed warning for use of deprecated index method |
| 1.4.0 | Adds possibility to specify priority of sub-workflow execution (default was to use parent priority) |
| 1.3.3 | Handles a pontential race conditions. |
| 1.3.2 | Fixes the use of master_id to represent the ID of the top level work order in all sub-workflows. |
| 1.3.1 | Bumping up the version for the ::Context fix |
| 1.3.0 | Reverts 1.2.7(Fixes the bug that creeps up on save). Fixes what 1.2.7 was supposed to fix. |
| 1.2.9 | Fixes backward compatibility of 1.2.8 with pre 2.7.0 |
| 1.2.8 | Avoids polling right after execute on 2.7.0 |
| 1.2.7 | Fix for output variable not showing up in editor |
| 1.2.6 | Combobox full match and empty value bug fix. |
| 1.2.5 | Combobox full match and empty value bug fix. |
| 1.2.4 | Added Combobox for selecting workflows. |
| 1.2.3 | Added link to open selected workflow in designer. |
| 1.2.2 | Fix for the before_save function. |
| 1.2.1 | Added styling to help text. |
| 1.2.0 | Portable id added to the sub-workflows |
| 1.1.3 | Adds progress reporting during the initialization of the sub-workflow (to avoid timeout) |
| 1.1.2 | Adds inheritance of caller tags to the sub-workflow |
| 1.1.1 | Reducing the default timeout from 0 (infinite) to 300 |
| 1.1.0 | Adds support for push status update from sub-workflow WO to caller step |
| 1.0.0 | Proceed to actual launch of sub-workflow (bypassing prior engine heartbeat requirement) |
| 0.5.0 | Added new field to display work order status vs. last work step status" |
| 0.4.2 | New icon |
| 0.4.1 | Adds sub-workflow work order ID as an output |
| 0.4.0 | There is a requirement from a client to not append data after work order name, going to do this with a new flag |
| 0.3.0 | Fix return status in case of SubWorkflow error |
| 0.2.6 | Truncates the name field automatically to size to avoid problems if WO or step names are long |
| 0.2.5 | Limits to authorized workflows, fixes glitch in name when no workflow is selected |
| 0.2.4 | Adds dependency on sub-workflow definition (for export) |
| 0.2.3 | Fixes issue with update of mappings after creation |
| 0.2.2 | More avoidance of crash in case of ghost outputs |
| 0.2.1 | Avoids crash in case of ghost outputs |
| 0.2.0 | Allow custom names |
| 0.1.9 | Uses work order calculated status details instead of default |
| 0.1.8 | Adds a forced clean-up to support reset and restart |
| 0.1.7 | Changes the icon |
| 0.1.6 | Fixes bug with Failed exit status |
| 0.1.5 | Adds display name |
| 0.1.4 | fixes crash when mapped output is a parameter |
| 0.1.3 | fixes the output returned, considers canceled sub-wf as failed |
| 0.1.2 | added support for multi-tenancy |
| 0.1.1 | adds ability to select sub-workflow to launch at runtime |
| 0.1.0 | first tracked version |

### How It Works

- Executes a published workflow as a sub-step within a parent workflow.
- The parent workflow waits until the sub-workflow completes before proceeding.
- Inputs can be mapped from the parent workflow to the sub-workflow.
- Outputs from the sub-workflow can be passed back to the parent workflow if explicitly named.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **SubWorkflow** plug-in.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - **Name**: A label for this step (e.g., `Run Child Workflow`).
    - **Associated with Workflow** \*: The published workflow to execute. Leave blank to select at run time.
    - **Map Error state to**: Exit status to set if the sub-workflow terminates in error (default: `Failed`).
    - **Use true name**: If checked, the launched work order name will not be appended with the user name or timestamp.
    - **Execution priority**: Overrides the calling work order’s priority. Use numbers (`1` = low, `2` = medium, `3` = high) or negative numbers for preemptive priority (`-100` higher than `-1000`).
    - **End with workorder status**: If checked, the parent work order ends with the sub-workflow’s status instead of the last step’s status.
3.  Map inputs from the parent workflow to the sub-workflow inputs as required.
4.  Optionally map outputs from the sub-workflow back to the parent workflow by naming them.

### Example Configuration

| Field                     | Value                      |
|---------------------------|----------------------------|
| Associated with Workflow  | `Data_Processing_Workflow` |
| Map Error state to        | `Failed` (default)         |
| Use true name             | Unchecked                  |
| Execution priority        | `2` (medium)               |
| End with workorder status | Checked                    |

**Behavior:** This configuration will run the `Data_Processing_Workflow` as a sub-step. If the sub-workflow fails, the step will fail. The parent work order will end with the sub-workflow’s status.

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Label to identify this SubWorkflow step |
| **Associated with Workflow** | The published workflow to execute |
| **Map Error state to** | Exit status to use when the sub-workflow errors |
| **Use true name** | If checked, retains the original work order name without suffixes |
| **Execution priority** | Overrides the parent work order priority |
| **End with workorder status** | Adopts the sub-workflow’s final status for the parent work order |

### Inputs Description

When a workflow is associated, all its inputs will be displayed for mapping. These names can be adjusted if necessary.

### Outputs Description

Outputs are only exposed to the parent workflow if they are explicitly named in the “Output Name” field. By default, no outputs are returned.

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

None

### Operating Instructions

This plug-in executes another workflow as a black box step within a parent workflow. It allows passing inputs to the sub-workflow and retrieving named outputs. The parent workflow will wait until the sub-workflow finishes before proceeding.

### Troubleshooting

- Ensure the associated workflow is published and available.
- Verify that input mappings match the sub-workflow’s expected inputs.
- Check priority settings if execution order is important.
- Enable “End with workorder status” if the parent should adopt the sub-workflow’s result.

### User lookup

![User lookup icon](icons/UserLookup.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to lookup the information about orchestrator users identified by ID, name, login or email address.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | 2015-02-18 ML - New icon                            |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

#### How It Works

- Searches for Orchestrator users based on **ID, login, last name, or email address**.
- Supports **exact matching** or **GLOB pattern matching** with wildcards (\* and ?).
- Can return **single match** (first found) or **all matching users**.
- Option to include or exclude **inactive users** from search results.
- Returns comprehensive user information including **ID, login, email, groups, phone, and timestamps**.
- Action exits as **'Failed'** if no matching users are found.
- Output format varies based on **'Return all matches?'** setting.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **UserLookup** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved User Lookup configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved User Lookup configured instance.
    - : Select the key to be used for the search (Id, lastname, login or email address).
    - : By default users marked active are considered, checking this option extends the search to all users.
    - : (only if the lookup is not done by Id) Allows the use of GLOB wildcard in the user search ('\*' for any combination of characters and '?' for any single character).
    - : (only if the lookup is not done by Id) Returns all matches instead of the first one found.

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the UserLookup plugin searching for a user by email address.

| Parameter                   | Value                            |
|-----------------------------|----------------------------------|
| Name                        | Find User by Email               |
| Comments                    | Search for user by email address |
| Lookup by                   | email address                    |
| Include inactive users?     | Not selected                     |
| Enable glob pattern search? | Selected                         |
| Return all matches?         | Not selected                     |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved User Lookup configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved User Lookup configured instance. |
| **Lookup by** | Select the key to be used for the search (Id, lastname, login or email address). |
| **Include inactive users?** | By default users marked active are considered, checking this option extends the search to all users. |
| **Enable glob pattern search?** | (only if the lookup is not done by Id) Allows the use of GLOB wildcard in the user search ('\*' for any combination of characters and '?' for any single character). |
| **Return all matches?** | (only if the lookup is not done by Id) Returns all matches instead of the first one found. |

#### Inputs Description

The specific input required depends on the 'Lookup by' parameter selected. Only one input is used per lookup operation.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *id* | The Orchestrator user ID provided as a numeric value. Used when 'Lookup by' is set to 'Id'. |
| *login* | The login ID string used to authenticate into Orchestrator. Used when 'Lookup by' is set to 'login'. |
| *last_name* | The last name string to search for. Used when 'Lookup by' is set to 'lastname'. |
| *email* | The email address string to search for. Used when 'Lookup by' is set to 'email address'. |

#### Outputs Description

**Important:**The output format varies depending on whether 'Return all matches?' is enabled. See the Operating Instructions section for detailed examples of single vs. multiple match output formats.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Orchestrator user ID | The unique numerical user ID used internally by Orchestrator to identify the user |
| Orchestrator login | The login ID used to authenticate into Orchestrator |
| Group memberships | An array of the group names the user is member of |
| Email address | The email on file for that user if provided |
| Alternate email addresses | An array of the alternate email addresses on file if provided |
| Phone number | The phone number on file for that user if provided |
| Last name | The last name on file for that user if provided |
| First name | The first name on file for that user if provided |
| User type | The user type (OrchestratorUser vs. AD) |
| Active | A boolean indicating if the user is active or has been deactivated |
| Created | The creation date for the user |
| Last updated | The last time the user record was modified. Note: changing group membership would not be considered an update |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

None

#### Operating Instructions

This plug-in searches for Orchestrator users based on various lookup criteria and returns comprehensive user information.

**Lookup Options\**

- **Lookup by Id:** Searches by unique numerical user ID. Always returns exact match. GLOB patterns and 'Return all matches?' are not applicable.
- **Lookup by login:** Searches by login username. Supports GLOB patterns (e.g., `john*` finds all logins starting with 'john').
- **Lookup by lastname:** Searches by user's last name. Supports GLOB patterns (e.g., `Smith` or `Sm*`).
- **Lookup by email address:** Searches by email address. Supports GLOB patterns (e.g., `*@example.com` finds all users from example.com domain).

**GLOB Pattern Matching\**
When 'Enable glob pattern search?' is selected (not applicable for ID lookup), you can use wildcards in your search:

- **Asterisk (\*)** - Matches any combination of characters (zero or more):
  - `john*` matches 'john', 'johnson', 'johnny'
  - `*smith` matches 'smith', 'blacksmith', 'johnsmith'
  - `*@gmail.com` matches all Gmail addresses
- **Question mark (?)** - Matches any single character:
  - `jo?n` matches 'john', 'joan' but not 'jon'
  - `user??` matches 'user01', 'user99' but not 'user1'
- **Combination patterns:**
  - `j*son` matches 'json', 'jackson', 'jameson'
  - `test_???` matches 'test_001', 'test_abc' but not 'test_1'

**Output Format: Single Match vs. Multiple Matches\**

- **Single Match Mode** (Return all matches? = Not selected):
  - Returns the **first matching user** found
  - Output values are **direct values**
  - Example: `"Orchestrator user ID" => 11`
  - Example: `"Email address" => "user@example.com"`
  - Example: `"Group memberships" => ["Contributor", "Scheduler"]`
- **Multiple Match Mode** (Return all matches? = Selected):
  - Returns **all matching users**
  - `"Orchestrator user ID"` is returned as an **array of IDs**
  - All other outputs are **hashes indexed by user ID**
  - Example: `"Orchestrator user ID" => [11, 9, 3]`
  - Example: `"Email address" => {11 => "user1@example.com", 9 => "user2@example.com", 3 => "user3@example.com"}`
  - Example: `"Group memberships" => {11 => [], 3 => ["Contributor", "Scheduler"], 9 => ["Contributor"]}`

**Multiple Match Output Example\**
When 'Return all matches?' is selected, here is an example of the output structure:

    "Orchestrator user ID" => [11, 9, 3]
    "Phone number" => {11 => "", 3 => "", 9 => ""}
    "Alternate email addresses" => {11 => [], 3 => [], 9 => []}
    "Group memberships" => {11 => [], 3 => ["Contributor", "Scheduler"], 9 => ["Contributor"]}
    "Created" => {11 => Tue, 13 May 2014 00:06:13 UTC +00:00, 3 => Wed, 01 May 2013 00:58:44 UTC +00:00, 9 => Thu, 19 Dec 2013 21:10:02 UTC +00:00}
    "User type" => {11 => "OrchestratorUser", 3 => "OrchestratorUser", 9 => "OrchestratorUser"}
    "First name" => {11 => "baba", 3 => "b", 9 => ""}
    "Orchestrator login" => {11 => "b", 3 => "bob", 9 => "baba"}
    "Active" => {11 => false, 3 => true, 9 => true}
    "Last updated" => {11 => Tue, 13 May 2014 00:06:13 UTC +00:00, 3 => Wed, 01 May 2013 17:42:11 UTC +00:00, 9 => Thu, 24 Jul 2014 21:12:45 UTC +00:00}
    "Last name" => {11 => "", 3 => "ob", 9 => ""}
    "Email address" => {11 => "", 3 => "", 9 => ""}

**Common Use Cases\**

- **Find specific user by ID:** Set 'Lookup by' to 'Id', provide user ID as input. Returns exact user details.
- **Find all users with Gmail:** Set 'Lookup by' to 'email address', enable GLOB patterns, enable 'Return all matches?', search for `*@gmail.com`.
- **Find inactive users:** Enable 'Include inactive users?', use appropriate lookup key to find deactivated accounts.
- **Search by partial name:** Set 'Lookup by' to 'lastname', enable GLOB patterns, search for `Smith*` to find all Smiths, Smithsons, etc.
- **Verify user exists:** Use in workflow with error handling to check if a user account exists before processing.

**Best Practices\**

- Use **ID lookup** when you have the exact user ID for fastest, most reliable results.
- Enable **'Include inactive users?'** only when specifically searching for deactivated accounts to reduce result set.
- Use **GLOB patterns carefully** - overly broad patterns (e.g., `*`) may return too many results.
- When using 'Return all matches?', ensure downstream steps can handle **hash-based outputs** indexed by user ID.
- Implement **error handling** for workflows since the action fails when no users are found.
- Test GLOB patterns with a small dataset first to validate the pattern matches expected users.
- Note that **'Last updated'** timestamp does not change when group memberships are modified.

**Important Notes\**

- This action exits as **'Failed'** if no matching users are found.
- GLOB pattern search and 'Return all matches?' options are **not available** when 'Lookup by' is set to 'Id'.
- When 'Return all matches?' is selected, the 'Orchestrator user ID' output is an array, and all other outputs are hashes indexed on those user IDs.
- Group membership changes do not update the 'Last updated' timestamp.

### Work Order Metadata

![Work Order Metadata icon](icons/WorkOrderMetaData.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides work order information and enables customization of work order labels and tags. It supports dynamic labeling, tag management, master ID override, and recursive label updates for better work order identification and management.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.7.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.6.2 | Add workflow name and id in outputs |
| 0.6.1 | Returns initiated_by value |
| 0.6.0 | Add a way to assign tags rather than append tags |
| 0.5.1 | Add previous step information output |
| 0.5.0 | Adding a flag to recursively change label to the master work order |
| 0.4.0 | Adding option to set the master_id |
| 0.3.0 | Adding a flag to show work_order_id with label |
| 0.2.0 | Adding a flag to ignore step related activity during labeling |
| 0.1.1 | Added exception handling |
| 0.1.0 | Added work orders tags edit field |
| 0.0.5 | Added work orders tags |
| 0.0.4 | Removed the include of module ssh tools. |
| 0.0.3 | Added new outputs like step info, previous step etc to the plugin. |
| 0.0.2 | New icon |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release. |

### How It Works

- Assigns custom labels and tags to work orders for easier identification and reporting.
- Allows dynamic and step-specific labeling using workflow attributes or runtime expressions.
- Supports strict labeling, recursive label updates, and master ID override for flexible workflows.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **WorkOrderMetaData** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - **Name**: Label for the step (e.g., `File Labeler`).
    - **Comments**: Notes for the template or configuration.
    - **Label**: Custom display name for the work order (optional).
    - **Tags**: Comma-separated tags to append to the work order (supports dynamic values).
    - **Master ID**: Optional override for the work order Master ID; may use dynamic expressions.
    - **Append work order id to label?**: If checked, will append the system work order ID to the label.
    - **Strict labeling**: If enabled, ignores step-related updates for labeling.
    - **Change label of top level work order**: If checked, applies label recursively to sub-workflows.
    - **Append to tags?**: If checked (default), appends new tag(s) rather than replacing them.

### Example Configuration

| Field                                | Value                        |
|--------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| Name                                 | `New WorkOrderMetaData Step` |
| Label                                | `label`                      |
| Tags                                 | `tag,tags`                   |
| Master ID                            | (empty)                      |
| Append work order id to label?       | Unchecked                    |
| Strict label and tags                | Unchecked                    |
| Change label of top level work order | Unchecked                    |
| Append to tags?                      | Checked (default)            |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | Notes and comments for this step or template configuration. |
| **Label** | Custom display name for the work order (optional, for dynamic titling). |
| **Tags** | Comma-separated tags for this work order instance. |
| **Master ID** | Override master ID assigned to the work order. |
| **Strict labeling** | Ignores step updates for labeling if enabled. |
| **Append work order id to label?** | Appends the system work order ID to the label. |
| **Change label of top level work order** | Recursively updates title for sub-workflows. |
| **Append to tags?** | Appends new tag(s) if checked, replaces if unchecked. |

### Inputs Description

- **Label**: Optional, lets you customize the work order title.
- Most fields can accept dynamic expressions or runtime attributes.

### Outputs Description

| Output | Description |
|----|----|
| **Work Order Label Name** | Name assigned to the work order instance. |
| **Step Information** | Names and statuses of all workflow steps. |
| **Work Order Status URL** | URL for checking work order status (update credentials as needed). |
| **Longest Step Names** | Step(s) with maximum elapsed execution time. |
| **Smallest Step Names** | Step(s) with the shortest execution time. |
| **Work Order ID** | ID of the executing work order. |
| **Previous Work Step** | Name(s) of the step(s) that triggered this step. |
| **Work Step Count** | Total steps in the work order. |
| **Tags** | Tags assigned to the work order. |
| **Master ID** | Master ID of the work order instance. |
| **Previous Work Step Information** | Details about the previous triggering step(s). |
| **Initiated By** | User ID of the initiator. |
| **Workflow Name** | Name of the workflow. |
| **Workflow ID** | ID of the workflow. |

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

None

### Operating Instructions

This plugin enables custom titling and tagging of work order instances for easier identification and reporting.

- Name workflow instances using file names or job attributes.
- Tag work orders for searching, filtering, and reporting.
- Override master ID, or apply custom labeling recursively to sub-workflows.

### Troubleshooting

- Ensure label and tags are set appropriately and support dynamic assignment.
- Verify master ID override validity.
- Check that the tag and label updates are visible in the workflow reports.
- Use the recursive label option as needed for sub-workflows.

### Workflow launcher

![Workflow launcher icon](icons/WorkflowLauncher.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in is used to launch one workflow from within another. It is similar to, but not the same as, the SubWorkflow plug-in. The main difference is that WorkflowLauncher launches another workflow and then immediately allows the current workflow to proceed. This behavior is asynchronous, whereas in SubWorkflow, the current workflow waits for the launched workflow to complete before continuing. A WorkflowLauncher step is considered Complete once it has successfully launched the configured workflow, while a SubWorkflow step is considered Complete only after the sub-workflow it launched has finished execution.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.6.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.5.0 | Adds possibility to use the portable id with run-time selection |
| 1.4.3 | Fix possible duplication of inputs and outputs definition |
| 1.4.2 | Lists published workflows only |
| 1.4.1 | Removed warning for use of deprecated index method |
| 1.4.0 | Added option to fail the step if the launched workflow fails. |
| 1.3.3 | Fix for help file not opening. |
| 1.3.2 | Backward compatibility for default priority. |
| 1.3.1 | Combobox full match and empty value bug fix. |
| 1.3.0 | Makes master_id configurable |
| 1.2.3 | Adds support for run-time provided priority |
| 1.2.2 | Added Combobox for selecting workflows. |
| 1.2.1 | Returns to synchronocity when possible, removes some minor DB calls when un-necessary |
| 1.2.0 | Added option to wait until launched work order completes |
| 1.1.1 | Added link to open selected workflow in designer. |
| 1.1.0 | Offers up comments field for dynamic inputs |
| 1.0.2 | Adds progress reporting during the initialization of the sub-workflow (to avoid timeout) |
| 1.0.1 | Adding a default step timeout of 300 |
| 1.0.0 | Enable direct launch optimization (avoids engine HB wait) |
| 0.4.0 | Adding support for tagging |
| 0.3.1 | New icon |
| 0.3.0 | Added possibility to specify the user when starting the workflow. |
| 0.2.4 | Supports dynamic attribute for work order name in case of dynamic workflow |
| 0.2.3 | Added the initializing and initialized states to the list of complete status |
| 0.2.2 | Added the create state to the list of complete status |
| 0.2.1 | Fix problem migration in 2.0 |
| 0.2.0 | There is a requirement from a client to not append data after work order name, going to do this with a new flag |
| 0.1.4 | Defaults mapped input name to launched workflow inputs name |
| 0.1.3 | Truncates launched work order name to avoid issues if provided name is too long |
| 0.1.2 | Enforces workflow entitlements in selector |
| 0.1.1 | Adds dependency on sub-workflow definition (for export) |
| 0.1.0 | add work_order_name field |
| 0.0.6 | changes the icon |
| 0.0.5 | adds help file |
| 0.0.4 | adds support for multi-tenancy |
| 0.0.3 | adds runt-time selection of workflow |
| 0.0.2 | adds default name and naming run-time override |

### How It Works

- Launches another workflow from the current workflow.
- By default, it launches the workflow asynchronously — the step completes immediately without waiting for the launched workflow to finish.
- If **Wait for Completion** is checked, the step waits until the launched workflow finishes before completing.
- Returns the launched work order ID so it can be tracked via the UI or API.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`WorkflowLauncher`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the workflow selection and identification:`
    - **`Name`**`: A label for the step (e.g., Launch Sub-Workflow).`
    - **`Associated with Workflow (ID)`**` `<span style="color: red;">`*`</span>`: Select the published workflow to launch. This cannot be changed after saving.`
    - **`Comments`**`: Notes about this step configuration.`
    - **`Work Order Description`**`: Description for the launched work order.`
    - **`Work Order Comments`**`: Additional notes stored with the work order.`
3.  `Set up execution parameters:`
    - **`Running as User`**`: User account to run the workflow (default: system).`
    - **`Execution Priority`**`: Priority for the launched workflow (default: 2 = medium; 1 = low; 3 = high; negative values for pre-emptive priorities).`
    - **`Master ID`**` (optional): Work orders launched with the same Master ID are grouped together.`
    - **`Use True Name`**`: If checked, uses the work order description exactly as entered (without timestamp).`
    - **`Use Portable ID`**`: Allows specifying a portable workflow ID instead of a numeric ID (runtime selection only).`
4.  `Configure completion and monitoring behavior:`
    - **`Fail if Launched Work Order Fails`**`: Marks this step as failed if the launched workflow fails.`
    - **`Wait for Completion`**`: Waits until the launched workflow completes. `*`(Mandatory if you want this step to return a success/failure status of the launched workflow.)`*
    - **`Polling Frequency`**`: Interval (in seconds) to check the launched workflow's status (default: 1).`
    - **`Run with Dedicated Execution Resource`**`: Launches the workflow in a dedicated worker thread, which processes only steps from that work order.`
5.  `Map inputs to the launched workflow:`
    - **`Sub-workflow inputs mapping`**`: These will match the inputs required by the workflow associated in the drop-down box above.`
    - **`Saved inputs`**`: Provides the ability to save in the template the values for some inputs requested by the workflow specified.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                         | Example Value            |
|-----------------------------------|--------------------------|
| **Name**                          | Launch Exit Status Check |
| **Associated with Workflow (ID)** | exitstatus (5)           |
| **Running as User**               | system (default)         |
| **Execution Priority**            | 2 (default)              |
| **Wait for Completion**           | checked                  |
| **Polling Frequency**             | 1 (default)              |

**Behavior:** This configuration launches the workflow `exitstatus`, waits for it to finish, and then returns the final status.

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Label to identify this WorkflowLauncher step. |
| **Comments** | Notes about this step configuration. |
| **Associated with Workflow (ID)** | Selected published workflow to launch. This cannot be changed after saving. |
| **Work Order Description** | Description for the launched work order. |
| **Work Order Comments** | Additional notes stored with the work order. |
| **Running as User** | User account to run the workflow (default: system). |
| **Execution Priority** | Priority level for the launched work order (default: 2 = medium; 1 = low; 3 = high; negative values for pre-emptive priorities). |
| **Master ID** | Optional - Work orders launched with the same Master ID are grouped together. |
| **Use True Name** | If checked, uses the work order description exactly as entered (without timestamp). |
| **Use Portable ID** | Allows specifying a portable workflow ID instead of a numeric ID (runtime selection only). |
| **Fail if Launched Work Order Fails** | Marks this step as failed if the launched workflow fails. |
| **Wait for Completion** | Waits for the launched workflow to finish before completing this step. |
| **Polling Frequency** | Time interval (in seconds) to check launched workflow status (default: 1). |
| **Run with Dedicated Execution Resource** | Launches the workflow in a dedicated worker thread, which processes only steps from that work order. |

### Inputs Description

Changes in this list of inputs will override any values set in the action template.

- **workflow_id**: ID of the workflow to launch.
- **workflow_name**: Description for the launched work order.
- **workflow_params**: Hash of the input parameters for the launched workflow.
- **running_as_user**: User account for execution (default: system).

### Outputs Description

- **work_order_id**: ID assigned to the launched workflow (can be used to track execution).
- **status**: Status of the launched workflow at step completion (usually 'In Progress' unless Wait for Completion is checked).

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

- The workflow to be launched must be published and available in the Orchestrator system.
- User account specified in 'Running as User' must have appropriate permissions to execute the target workflow.

### Operating Instructions

#### Overview

This action launches another workflow from the current workflow. The behavior depends on whether you want synchronous or asynchronous execution.

#### Execution Modes

- **Asynchronous (default)**: If **Wait for Completion** is unchecked, the step completes immediately after launching the workflow. The launched workflow continues running independently. This is useful when you want to trigger a workflow without waiting for its completion.
- **Synchronous**: If **Wait for Completion** is checked, the step polls the launched workflow until it completes, then returns its status. This is mandatory if you want this step to return a success/failure status of the launched workflow.

#### Tracking Launched Workflows

The launched workflow's ID (work_order_id) can be tracked in multiple ways:

##### Via UI

`http://orchestrator_url/work_orders/<work_order_id>`

##### Via API

`http://orchestrator_url/api/work_order_status/<work_order_id>?login=admin&password=aspera`

The result will be presented in an XML file detailing step-by-step status for that work order.

#### Priority Levels

- **1**: Low priority
- **2**: Medium priority (default)
- **3**: High priority
- **Negative values**: Pre-emptive priorities - these work orders will be processed before all others

#### Work Order Grouping

Use the **Master ID** parameter to group related work orders together. All work orders launched with the same Master ID will be associated, making it easier to track and manage related workflows.

#### Dedicated Execution Resources

When **Run with Dedicated Execution Resource** is enabled, the launched workflow executes in a dedicated worker thread. This thread processes only steps from that specific work order, ensuring isolated execution and preventing interference from other workflows. This is useful for workflows that require guaranteed resources or isolation.

#### Important Notes

- Use **Wait for Completion** when the parent workflow needs to know the outcome of the launched workflow before proceeding.
- Set appropriate **Execution Priority** based on the urgency of the launched workflow.
- Use **Master ID** to group related work orders for easier tracking and management.
- When launching multiple workflows in parallel, consider using asynchronous mode (unchecked Wait for Completion) for better performance.
- Monitor the **work_order_id** output to track launched workflows via UI or API.
- Use **Fail if Launched Work Order Fails** when the parent workflow should fail if the child workflow fails.
- Note that **Wait for Completion** must be used with **Fail if Launched Work Order Fails** to ensure that the parent workflow fails/succeeds based on the launched workflow's status.
- Consider using **Dedicated Execution Resource** for critical workflows that require guaranteed execution resources.

## Transcoding

### Akamai transcoding

![Akamai transcoding icon](icons/AkamaiTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin starts and monitors a Transcoding job using the Akamai Transcoding platform.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.1.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.8 | 2015-04-12 ADM - Fixed outputs format for first execution |
| 0.0.7 | 2015-04-11 ADM - Changed output format |
| 0.0.6 | 2015-04-11 ADM - Changed token validity period |
| 0.0.5 | 2015-04-10 ADM - Removed token validity |
| 0.0.4 | 2015-04-03 ADM - Added additional inputs |
| 0.0.3 | 2015-04-03 ADM - Added protection in case of RestClient errors when checking for status |
| 0.0.2 | 2015-04-03 ADM - Fixed transcoding submission specification |
| 0.0.1 | 2015-02-16 ADM - Initial release |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **User name**: The akami user name to use for transcoding.

- **Active password**: The active password generated by Akamai for API cased access.

- **Workspace ID**: The ID of the akamai workspace to use.

- **Origin ID**: The ID of the origin where the source object is stored.

- **Library ID**: The ID of the library where the transcoding collection is.

- **Collection ID**: The transcoding profile to use.

- **Source object**: The path, relative to the Origin, of the source file to transcode.

- **Target object name**: Optional. A base name for the transcoded files. If not specified the source object naked name will be used.

- **Target Path**: The target path of the transcoding operation.

- **Media Library Id**: The Id of the media library where the transcoded files will be stored.

- **Media Library Origin Id**: Optional. The Origin Id where to store the transcoded files.

- **Remove source object on success**: If selected the source object will be deleted once the transcoding completes with success.

- **Status Polling frequency**: The frequency for polling status changes.

All inputs can be specified both at design time via the configuration panel, or at run time via the Map Inputs panel.

##### Outputs description

- **Output object**: The transcoded file.
- **Status message**: The final status message.
- **Result code**: The result code. Check the Akamai Transcoding documentation for more informations

##### Operating Instructions

This plugin allows to trigger and monitor a transcoding job using the Akamai Content Preparation service.\
This plugin supports transcoding of source object already available in an Akamai Library.\
\
**How to activate API access**\
By default API access to Akamai Content Preparation platform is disabled. In order to enable it you need to log in with you accound to Akamai Luna Control Center and go to Configure-\>Content Preparation. Now click on API Access on the top right part of the page.\
In the Content Preparation API Access page make sure that **API Access** is set to ON.\
From this page you need to retrieve the **Workspace**,**User name** and **Active Password** that are needed in the plugin configuration.\
\
**How to retrieve Library ID and Collection ID** Using the Akamai Luna Control Center, go to the Collection you want to use.\
Click on the setting icons and select Edit.\
The browser will point to a url similar to this:\
https://control.akamai.com/media-workflow/call/collection/edit?collectionId=\_COLLECTION_UUID\_&libraryId=\_LIBRARY_UUID\_&workspaceId=.....\
Both Collection ID and Library ID can be retrieved from this URL. They are both UUID of the form:\
0.....2b-4..9-4..6-b..d-c..........b (where . replaces standard characters for security reasons)\

### Alchemist Snell OD operations

![Alchemist Snell OD operations icon](icons/AlchemistSnellOdOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to control Alchemist Snell OD systems.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.0 | added configurable polling frequency |
| 0.0.3 | removing polling frequency reference from the edit screen |
| 0.0.2 | set default polling frequency. Polling frequency not defined by the model |
| 0.0.1 | first tracked version |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

  Document all available template parameters using add_parameter_help

- **Url**: Enter the entire URL along with the Port and the endpoint to reach the snell service.

- **Operation**: Choose between the three options available. (Submit Job, Query Job Status by Id, Query Job Status by Filter)

- **Payload**: We have pre-populated each operation with a respective payload. This can be used or modified with a custom payload

- **Polling Frequency**: For asynchronous operation the frequency at which the server needs to be polled

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the AlchemistSnellOdOperation action template.

--\>

- **Payload**: We have populated a corresponding payload associated with every operation. This payload can be used or replaced with a custom payload

##### Outputs description

- Document all available outputs using add_output_help
- **ResourceId**: In case of submit job we show the job id of the job
- **Output Hash**: The entire response received is shown in the form of a hash
- **Error Message**: In case of an error this field is populated with the error message.
- **Responce_XML_For_Job_Submit**: For a new job, this variable holds its associated response

##### Operating Instructions

For the submit job, asynchronous operation is performed to get the status of the job.\

### Amazon Elastic Transcoder

![Amazon Elastic Transcoder icon](icons/AmazonElasticTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to use Amazon Elastic Transcoder

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.4.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.0   | Add assume role                                     |
| 0.2.1   | Fix for secret masking                              |
| 0.2.0   | Add http proxy support                              |
| 0.1.2   | Avoid autofill of key and secret                    |
| 0.1.1   | Masking of AWS Secret                               |
| 0.1.0   | Allows for SSL to not be used                       |
| 0.0.1   | Initial release.                                    |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved AmazonElasticTranscoding configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved AmazonElasticTranscoding configured instance.

- **Amazon access Key**: The access key credentials provided by amazon

- **Amazon secret key**: The secret key credentials provided by amazon

- **Region**: The region under which transcoder is hosted

- **Verify SSL?**: Check this box to use SSL.

- **External id**: External id when assuming a role (optional).

- **Policy**: Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy in JSON format when assuming a role (optional).

- **Role arn**: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role. Required to assume a role.

- **Token duration**: Duration of the generated token. When assuming a role. In seconds. Default is 3600s (1 hour).

- **Inputs**: Inputs required for the transcoder

- **Outputs**: Outputs required for the transcoding job

- **pipeline_id**: The pipeline that has to be used to launch the job

- **output_key_prefix**: The value to prepend to every outputs

- **user_metadata**: the metadata that you can associate with a job

- **playlists**: If a preset Id is selected, playlist should contain the information about the master

- **Proxy URL**: The URL of the proxy server through which AWS will be accessed. Leave this field blank if you do not wish to use a proxy server.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the AmazonElasticTranscoding action template.

Changes in this list of inputs will override any values set in the action template.\
\

- **Aws_key**: The Amazon access key id.
- **Aws_secret**: The Amazon secret access key.
- **Aws_session_token**: The Amazon session token (optional).
- **Aws_region**: The Amazon region.
- **External_id**: External id when assuming a role (optional).
- **Policy**: IAM policy in JSON format when assuming a role (optional).
- **Role_arn**: Role arn to assume.
- **Token_duration**: Generated token duration in seconds, when assuming a role.
- **Proxy_url**: The HTTPS Proxy URL.

\

##### Outputs description

- **Response**: Response received from Amazon
- **Error_Message**: Error message if any received from Amazon
- **Temporary_access_key**: Temporary access key generated if assume role is called
- **Temporary_secret**: Temporary secret generated if assume role is called
- **Temporary_token**: Temporary token generated if assume role is called

##### Operating Instructions

This action plugin is used to perform queuing action to Amazon Elastic Transcoder.

**Assume role:** for an IAM user to assume a role.\
Pre-requisites:\
The assume role operation is called only if there is a specified role arn.\
The IAM user must be allowed as a principal in the role trust relationship (sts:AssumeRole action).\
Instructions:\
The IAM user must first be identified with its access key, secret and region.\
Specify the external id if any defined in the role trust relationship.\
Specify the IAM policy in JSON format. Don't put backslashes. For example, use something like { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": \[{ "Sid": "Stmt1", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": "elastictranscoder:\*", "Resource": "\*" }\] }. This policy will be effective for all operations realized with the generated temporary credentials.\
Specify the role arn. For example arn:aws:iam::921583127875:role/elt_access\
Temporary credentials (access key, secret, token) are generated, used and returned if a role is specified.\
Specify the token duration in seconds. The value must be more than 900 seconds (15 minutes) and be less than the role Maximum session duration (check in the AWS Console). The Maximum session duration can be set to 12 hours maximum.\

### Amberfin transcoding

![Amberfin transcoding icon](icons/AmberfinTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-in provides the ability to submit file transcoding job to a Amberfin server.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.3.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.2.2 | Remove dependency on custom Rails initializers |
| 0.2.1 | Fixes javascript error when selecting port |
| 0.2.0 | Making plugin asynchronous |
| 0.1.7 | removing soap4r dependency |
| 0.1.6 | Supporting new API port 21012 for Amberfin connection |
| 0.1.5 | 2015-02-24 ML - New icon, change display name |
| 0.1.4 | makes server address and node runtime inputs |
| 0.1.3 | adds dynamic inputs from XML payload to inputs |
| 0.1.2 | deleted reference to modal_dialog in edit.html and also corrected the plugin version |
| 0.1.1 | fields will use default_get instead of field value |
| 0.1.0 | added support for repurpose as well as transcode |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Amberfin Transcoding template

- **Amberfin Server Node**: The remote node name of the server which hosts the Amberfin Transcoding server

- **Amberfin Server IP address (or hostname)**: The remote host name or remote IP of the server which hosts the Amberfin Transcoding server

- **Amberfin API port (optional)**: The port number of remote server which hosts the Amberfin Transcoding server

- **Operation**: Select from dropdown if the job is a repurpose or transcode

- **Amberfin Templates**: A dropdown of Amberfin templates available on Amberfin server to repurpose and transcode files

- **Template Id (hidden)**: Id of the Amberfin template selected

- **Source file**: The absolute path to the file on the server's file system

- **Target directory**: The absolute path to the directory on the server's file system.

- **Transcode XML**: The fully constructed XML with job details. This will stop Orchestrator from constructing its own XML

- **Status Polling frequency**: How often progress will be reported. Default is 5 seconds

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the AmberfinTranscoding action template.

- All required parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory for both repurpose and transcode operations:**\

- **Amberfin Server Node or Amberfin IP Address**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.
- **Template Id**: A template ID is mandatory while submitting a job, hence a template must be selected from dropdown.
- **Source file**: Absolute path to the source file on remote server must be provided
  \
  **Mandatory Fields if transcode operation is selected**
- **Target directory**: Absolute path to the directory on remote server must be provided for transcode operation

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.
- **Operation**: Selecting an operation from dropdown tells the plugin what to do. Defaults to repurpose when not chosen.
- **Amberfin API Port**: The port is optional as a default value is used if not specified.
- **Transcode XML**: The fully constructed XML with job details. It will overwrite the default transcode specification that the plugin builds.
- **Status Polling frequency**: Specify if progress needs to be reported within a time other than default.

##### Outputs description

- **Input_file**: The input file path involved in the transcode operation
- **Output_file**: The output fil obtained as a result of the transcode operation
- **Job_Id**: The ID of the job triggered by the transcode operation
- **Errors**: Errors reported by transcode server during transcode operation

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Amberfin server to do a transcode job.\
\
The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\
An example of a transcode job is given below. Currently only a single source file can be specified. Target if specified is used by the template setup on the Amberfin server to place the output file\
\

##### An example of a transcode

\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Decide what operation is needed - Repurpose or Transcode\
c) Click the button Get Amberfin Templates. This provides a dropdown with templates from the provided Amberfin server\
d) Select a template from the dropdown\
e) The template ID is submitted along with source file path to Amberfin server\
f) Provide source file name (Ex: "/data/input/expendables.avi") and a target directory "/data/output/"\
g) Amberfin server takes these inputs and performs the job\
\

### Ateme transcoding

![Ateme transcoding icon](icons/AtemeTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plugin provides the ability to transcode video files via Ateme Titan File. It implements the Ateme Titan 3.7 REST API 1.7.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
