| 0.0.2   | Adding RemoteNode Dependency.                       |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

### How It Works

- Integrates with Ateme transcoding servers to perform video and audio transcoding operations through web service APIs.
- Supports preset-based transcoding using predefined configurations available on the Ateme server for streamlined workflow setup.
- Provides flexible transcoding options through either preset selection or custom XML payload configuration for advanced use cases.
- Handles Windows-based file paths on the Ateme server with support for both forward slash and backslash path formats.
- Includes automatic preset retrieval functionality to dynamically populate available transcoding profiles from the connected server.
- Monitors transcoding job status and provides detailed output including XML results, status codes, and error messaging.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`Ateme Transcoding`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the server connection:`
    - **`Ateme Node`**`: Select a remote node pointing to your Ateme server.`
    - **`Ateme server IP address`**`: Enter the IP address or domain name of the Ateme server.`
3.  `Set up transcoding parameters:`
    - **`Get Presets`**`: Click this button to retrieve available presets from the server.`
    - **`Ateme Presets`**`: Select a preset from the populated dropdown list.`
    - **`Source`**`: Specify the source file path on the Ateme server.`
    - **`Destination`**`: Specify the destination file path on the Ateme server.`
4.  `Optional advanced configuration:`
    - **`Transcoding payload`**`: Provide custom XML payload for advanced transcoding capabilities.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                   | Example Value                           |
|-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| **Ateme Node**              | ateme-server-01                         |
| **Ateme server IP address** | 192.168.1.50                            |
| **Ateme Presets**           | HD_H264_Standard                        |
| **Source**                  | d:\media\input\HCN00080.mxf             |
| **Destination**             | d:\media\output\HCN00080_transcoded.mp4 |
| **Transcoding payload**     | (optional custom XML)                   |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Ateme Node** | The Ateme node name from Orchestrator defined Remote Nodes. |
| **Ateme server IP address** | Ateme node IP address or domain name for direct connection to the transcoding server. |
| **Ateme Presets** | Ateme list of defined presets retrieved from the server. Select a preset after clicking 'Get Presets'. |
| **Source** | The file path on the Ateme server for the source file. Must be a complete file path, not a directory. |
| **Destination** | The file path on the Ateme server for the destination file. Must be a complete file path, not a directory. |
| **Transcoding payload** | The XML payload for the create transcode job. Optional custom configuration for advanced transcoding requirements. |

### Inputs Description

Changes in this list of inputs will override any values set in the action template.

- **server address**: Remote Node name or remote server IP address/domain name.
- **preset_name**: The Ateme preset name (optional) without the kpreset extension.
- **source**: The file path on the Ateme server for the source file.
- **destination**: The file path on the Ateme server for the destination file.
- **transcoding_payload**: The XML payload for the create transcode job (optional).

### Outputs Description

- **XML_result**: The Ateme transcode job XML output (in parsed form like name:value).
- **Ateme_status_code**: The Ateme status code indicating the result of the transcoding operation.
- **Ateme_error_msg**: The Ateme error message if an error condition is met during processing.

### Operating Instructions

- **Server Configuration:** Provide the Remote Node or remote IP address/domain name of the Ateme server. Ensure network connectivity is established before proceeding.
- **Preset Retrieval:** Get the presets from the Ateme system by clicking on 'Get Presets'. A list of presets will appear in the 'Ateme presets' dropdown. Select a preset from this list for your transcoding job.
- **Preset Persistence:** When the plugin configuration is re-opened, the selected preset is preserved. It can be changed by selecting a new preset from the presets list.
- **Runtime Input Considerations:** Note that 'Get Presets' cannot be clicked if the Remote Node or server address is not filled in the configuration. If connection details come as runtime input, define the preset name as runtime input as well.
- **File Path Configuration:** Specify the source and destination file paths. Both source and destination are paths on the Ateme server using Windows path format. They can be expressed like `d:\media\HCN00080.mxf` or `d:/media/HCN00080.mxf`. Note that destination must be a file path, not a folder path.
- **Custom XML Payload Alternative:** Alternatively to preset+source+destination, a custom XML payload can be specified. This may allow more transcoding capabilities than the preset-based combination. When using custom XML payload, source and destination must also be specified.
- **Runtime Variables in XML:** When using custom XML payload, you can reference source and destination as runtime inputs with this notation: `<%=source%>` and `<%=destination%>`. In this case, leave the Source and Destination fields empty and map them through inputs instead.

### Dependencies

- Network connectivity to the Ateme transcoding server on the specified IP address or domain name.
- Valid Ateme SDK license required to use the web services functionality.
- Proper file system access permissions on the Ateme server for both source and destination paths.
- Available transcoding presets configured on the Ateme server.
- Sufficient storage space on the Ateme server for both input and output files.

### Troubleshooting

- If the Ateme server is unreachable, an 'Error Occurred' message will appear in 'Ateme presets' after a timeout of 1 minute. Verify network connectivity and server availability.
- If you receive 'RestClient::Conflict : 409 Conflict' error, check that the Ateme SDK license is properly installed and valid (required to use the web services).
- If you get 'RestClient::Conflict : 409 Conflict' during job execution, this may indicate the Ateme server Engine was restarted while transcode jobs are running. Wait for the engine to fully restart before retrying.
- If a preset passed as runtime input is not found, you'll receive 'RestClient::Conflict : 409 Conflict'. Verify the preset name exists on the server and is spelled correctly (without the .kpreset extension).
- If you encounter 'RestClient::ResourceNotFound : 404 Resource Not Found', this indicates a transcode job was removed (e.g., manually through the Ateme supervision GUI) while in progress. Check the Ateme server console for job status.
- For file path issues, ensure you're using the correct Windows path format and that both source and destination are complete file paths, not directory paths.
- If custom XML payload is not working, verify the XML syntax and ensure that runtime variables like \<%=source%\> and \<%=destination%\> are properly referenced.

### Bitmovin encoding

![Bitmovin encoding icon](icons/BitmovinEncoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in provides the ability to start and monitor a Bitmovin encoding.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | enhance help file                                   |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved BitmovinEncoding configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved BitmovinEncoding configured instance.

- **Bitmovin domain name**: The Bitmovin domain name, by default api.bitmovin.com.

- **Bitmovin port** : The Bitmovin port, by default 443.

- **Bitmovin api key**: Your Bitmovin API key to authenticate with.

- **Inputs**: List of available Bitmovin inputs.

- **Outputs**: List of available Bitmovin outputs.

- **Cloud region**: The Bitmovin cloud region, by default automatically adjusted.

- **Infrastructure id**: Your infrastructure id if you have servers in premises. Optional.

- **Source filepath**: The source file path.

- **Output path root**: The output folder root path.

- **Overwrite output?**: To allow overwriting existing files.

- **Output format**: The output format within MPEG-DASH, HLS, MP4.

- **Streams**: The streams to create, one per codec.

- **Segment length**: The muxing segment length, default is 4.

- **Polling frequency**: Interval in seconds to check for the job submission status.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the BitmovinEncoding action template.

Changes in this list of inputs will override any value set in the action configuration/template.\
\

- **Bitmovin API key**: Your Bitmovin API key to authenticate with.
- **Cloud region**: The Bitmovin cloud region.
- **Infrastructure id**: Your infrastructure id.
- **Source file path**: The source file path
- **Output path root**: The output folder root path
- **Segment length**: The muxing segment length

\

##### Outputs description

- **result**: The job submission output in text format
- **result_json**: The job submission output in JSON format
- **manifest_names**: The names of the manifests being generated for DASH or HLS

##### Operating Instructions

The Bitmovin domain name is by default api.bitmovin.com\
The Bitmovin addessable port is by default 443 (HTTPS protocol).\
\
Specify your Bitmovin API key for authentication. Note that the Get Inputs, Get Outputs and Add stream actions will return a data list out of Bitmovin only if a valid API key is specified.\
\
Click on 'Get Inputs' to obtain a list of available inputs from Bitmovin. Select one of them. After you save and re-open the plugin, the selected input is visible under the Inputs field name\
Click on 'Get Outputs' to obtain a list of available outputs from Bitmovin. Select one of them. After you save and re-open the plugin, the selected input is visible under the Inputs field name\
\
When changing the API key, close and re-open the plugin to refresh the inputs, outputs and codecs lists.\
\
The cloud region is by default automatically computed. If necessary, you can specify it (e.g. GOOGLE_EUROPE_WEST_1).\
Add an infrastructure id if necessary.\
\
Specify the source file path. We support only one source file path (e.g. /pub/graphics/blender/demo/movies/Sintel.2010.1080p.mkv)\
\
Specify the output folder root path (e.g. output/bitmovin-ruby). This is used to build the muxing output path as explained below.\
\
Click on Overwrite output? if you want to avoid generating variable output path names and manifest names. As explained below an hex number is added automatically to the generated names. This can be disabled with this check box, allowing files to go into the same target folder or manifest name over multiple invocations of the plugin.\
\
Specify the output format(s) within the MPEG-DASH, HLS, MP4 list. Multiple of them can be selected. Note that you can type on the label (e.g. MPEG-DASH) to select or unselect a format.\
\
Add a stream per codec. Specify the stream name, select a codec from the list of available codecs and select audio or video based on your type of codec.\
A muxing is created per stream and per output format. A fmp4 muxing is created for the DASH output format. A ts muxing is created for the HLS output format. A mp4 muxing is created for the MP4 output format. The generated muxing output path is the combination of the output root path, the stream name plus a variable 4 bytes hexa number and the muxing type (fmp4, ts, mp4).\
If no stream is configured, an error is returned by Bitmovin. Check the output result for the error cause.\
\
Specify the muxing segment length, default is 4.\
\
A DASH manifest is created if the output format is MPEG-DASH. The .mpd file is created under the output root path. The .mpd file name is based on the source file name plus a variable 4 bytes hexa number. A fmp4 representation is created per muxing.\
\
An HLS manifest is created if the output format is HLS. The .m3ua and rendition.m3ua files are created under the output root path. The .m3ua file names are based on the source file name plus a variable 4 bytes hexa number. A manifest video or audio media is created per muxing.\
\
In outputs, you'll get the content returned from the encoding submission in json and text format.\
The filenames of the manifests being generated are also in outputs. Extension .m3ua for HLS and extension .mpd for DASH.\
If the manifest startup fails (always executed after the encoding startup), the outputs are the one from the manifest operation, otherwise it's the outputs from the encoding operation.\

### Carbon/WFS transcoding

![Carbon/WFS transcoding icon](icons/CarbonCoderTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This is Action plug-ins provides the ability to submit file transcoding job to a Harmonic Carbon Coder/WFS server.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.4.1 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name for the Carbon Coder Transcoding template

- **Comments**: The comments of the Carbon Coder Transcoding template

- **Carbon/WFS Server Node**: The remote node name of the server which hosts the Carbon Coder Transcoding server

- **Carbon/WFS Server IP address (or hostname)**: The remote host name or remote IP of the server which hosts the Carbon Coder Transcoding server

- **Carbon/WFS API port (optional)**: The port number of remote server which hosts the Carbon Coder Transcoding server

- **API version**: Used to identify which version or Carbon coder is deployed on the target server.

      - Carbon API Version 1.2:  This version must be selected when the remote server hosts an Harmonic Carbon Coder server (pre WFS). When users select this API Version, they can connect to a Carbon Coder server on port 1120 and send transcoding jobs and receive updates on the submitted job.
      - Carbon API Version 1.4.6 legacy: This version must be selected when the remote server hosts on a Rhozet WFS server but the jobs are being submitted using the legacy format. Selecting this option would allow to maintain continuity after an upgrade to WFS by still using plugin configuration that were set-up for the Carbon API 1.2.  What the plugin does is submit the job to Carbon Coder using the legacy API (as on 1.2), as the legacy submit is still supported by WFS, however, polling for status is done using the WFS manager (on port 8731). That complexity however is abstracted from the end-user by Orchestrator and to them the  behavior will be exactly the same as the one offered by the previous version of the plugin for the Carbon API 1.2.
      - Carbon API Version 1.4.6: This version must be selected when the remote server hosts a Rhozet WFS server and the job sent  use new features only supported by WFS. When users select this API Version, both submission and status polling are done using the new SOAP based Carbon API (on port 8731).

- **Status Polling frequency**: How often progress will be reported. Default is 5 seconds
  Fields for WFS API Version 1.4.6

- **WFS Workflows**: A dropdown of remote WFS workflows

- **WFS Workflows GUID**: GUID of the WFS workflow selected from above dropdown of remote WFS workflows

- **Source**: The absolute path to the file on the server's file system

- **Target**: The absolute path to the file or directory on the server's file system
  Fields for WFS API Version 1.2 and 1.4.6 legacy

- **Preset Payload File**: A dropdown of templates available on Orchestrator server to transcode files

- **File Name**: The absolute path to the file on the server's file system

- **Carbon Job Name**: An optional input when provided identifies

- **Module GUID**: The absolute path to the file on the server's file system

- **Target dir**: The absolute path to the directory on the server's file system

- **Transcoding Payload**: An XML which represents the template selected above in dropdown

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the CarbonCoderTranscoding action template.

- All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

**These inputs are mandatory:**\

- **Carbon/WFS Server Node or Carbon/WFS IP Address and Carbon/WFS API Port**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address and its port must be provided.
  Fields for WFS API Version 1.4.6
- **WFS Workflows GUID**: A workflow ID must be provided
- **Source**: Absolute path to the source file on remote server must be provided
  Fields for WFS API Version 1.2 and 1.4.6 legacy
- **Preset Payload File**: A preset payload must be provided
- **File Name**: Absolute path to the source file on remote server must be provided
- **Module GUID**: A module GUID must be provided

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.
- **Target**: Target directory on remote server is identified automatically if not provided during operation
- **Status Polling frequency**: Specify if progress needs to be reported within a time other than default.
- **Carbon Job Name**: If specified, the Carbon Job Name can be used to identify the transcode job.

##### Outputs description

- **input_file**: The input file path involved in the transcode operation
- **output_file(s)**: The output file obtained as a result of the transcode operation
  *(not available in API v1.2)*
- **Job_GUID**: The GUID of the job triggered by the transcode operation
- **Warnings**: Warnings reported by transcode server during transcode operation
- **Errors**: Errors reported by transcode server during transcode operation

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Carbon/WFS server to do a transcode job.\
\
The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\
The plug-in supports Carbon API version 1.2 and WFS API version 1.4.6 and also an intermediate mode in which a job can be submitted which uses Carbon API 1.2 to submit a job and WFS API 1.4.6 to poll status of the job (termed 1.4.6 legacy)\
\
When using WFS API 1.4.6 the workflow template must be\
\

##### An example of a transcode

\
**API Version 1.2 and API Version 1.4.6 Legacy**\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Provide transcode payload (either custom or select from payload drop down)\
c) Provide source file name (Ex: "C:/Data/Production/Transcode/Input/test.avi")\
If "DEFAULT" preset payload was selected, select Module from drop down\
d) The Module's GUID is submitted along with source file path to Carbon server\
e) Carbon server takes these inputs and assigns a priority to the job and performs the transcode\
**API Version 1.4.6**\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Select workflow id from the dropdown\
c) Provide source file name (Ex: "C:/Data/Production/Transcode/Input/test.avi")\
d) The workflow's GUID is submitted along with source file path to Carbon server\
e) WFS server takes these inputs and assigns a priority to the job and performs the transcode\
**API Version 1.4.6 but using Transcoding Payload**\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Provide a Transcoding payload that needs to be submitted to Rhozet. The Transcoding payload are usually the PCP files obtained from Rhozet/Carbon. c) WFS server takes these inputs and assigns a priority to the job and performs the transcode\
\

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the imagine streamz Transcoding template

- **Stream Server Node**: The remote node name of the server which hosts the imagine streamz transcoding server

- **Server ip address (or hostname)**: The remote host name or remote IP of the server which hosts the streamz Transcoding server

- **Stream api port (optional)**: The port number of remote server which hosts the streamz Transcoding server

- **Stream wsdl uri (optional)**: Default is :/Stream?wsdl

- **Status Polling frequency**: How often progress will be reported. Default is 5 seconds

- **Encoder wait timeout (sec)**: The wait timeout value for the encoder

- **Encoder stall timeout (sec)**: The stall timeout value for the encoder

- **Project Name**: The name of the project to be transcoded. Can be selected from a list of projects on the stream server.

- **Transcoding Payload**: An XML which represents the template selected above in dropdown

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the imagine StreamZ transcoding action template.

- All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

**These inputs are mandatory:**\

- **Stream Server Node or server IP Address and server API Port**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address and its port must be provided.
- **Project**: A project must be selected from the list of projects in the stream server.
- **Transcoding payload**: A preset payload must be provided if not present in the project

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.
- **Status Polling frequency**: Specify if progress needs to be reported within a time other than default.

##### Outputs description

- **Output_files**: The output file obtained as a result of the transcode operation
- **Orchestrator_project**: The output project obtained as a result of the transcode operation

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Streamz server to do a transcode job.\
\
The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\

##### An example of a transcode

\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Provide transcode payload (either custom or select from project drop down)\
e) streamz server takes these inputs and assigns a priority to the job and performs the transcode\
\

This is Action plug-in provides the ability to submit a file transcoding job to a DigitalRapids Transcode Manager server.

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Digital Rapids TM template

- **Transcode manager server node**: The remote node name of the server which hosts the Digital Rapids Transcoding server

- **Server ip address (or hostname)**: The remote host name or remote IP of the server which hosts the Digital Rapids Transcoding server

- **Transcode manager api port (optional)**: The port number for the transcoding service on the remote server which hosts the Digital Rapids Transcoding server

- **Role**: Role of the transcoding server. Either ROLE_TRANSCODE or ROLE_KAYAK_TRANSCODE

- **Source file**: The absolute path to the source file to transcode on the Digital Rapids server file system

- **Separate Audio File**: If checked, the value provided in Audio File Path will be submitted to DRTM

- **Audio File Path**: The absolute path to the audio file to transcode on the Digital Rapids server file system

- **Destination directory**: The absolute folder path for the output files on the Digital Rapids server file system

- **Project list**: The transcode project path selected within the list of available projects

- **Group list**: The transcode group name selected within the list of available groups

- **Priority**: The transcode job priority. Within LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH

- **Tag values**: Metadata name/value pairs

- **Status Polling frequency**: How often progress will be reported. Default is 5 seconds

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the DigitalrapidsTmTranscoding action template.

- All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory:**\

- **Transcode_manager_server**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address
- **Source_file**: Absolute path to the source file on the remote server
- **Project_path**: The full project path
- **Project_id**: The project id obtained from the Transcode Manager
- **Group_name**: The resource group name
- **Tm_group_id**: The resource group id obtained from the Transcode Manager
- **Tag_values**: Metadata name/value pairs

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Destination_dir**: The absolute folder path for the output files on the Digital Rapids server file system
- **Audio File Path**: The absolute file path for audio file to transcode on the Digital Rapids server file system

##### Outputs description

- **Output_files**: The output file(s) obtained as a result of the transcode operation
- **Job_id**: The ID of the job triggered by the transcode operation
- **Errors_during_transcode**: Errors reported by transcode server during transcode operation

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide the inputs required by an Digital Rapids TM (Transcode Manager) server to perform a transcode job.\
The plugin has been tested with the Digital Rapids TM SOAP API 2.0 up to 2.5\
Each transcode task uses either a Digital Rapids Stream project (choose role ROLE_TRANSCODE) or a Digital Rapids Kayak blueprint (choose role ROLE_KAYAK_TRANSCODE) for processing. Stream transcoding projects are created with Digital Rapids Stream software, while the Digital Rapids Kayak Workflow Designer or Kayak SDK can be used to create Kayak Transcode blueprints.\
Currently only a single source file can be specified.\
If you want to pass project and group as inputs, you need to pass Project_path + Project_id and Group_name + Tm_group_id. Passing Project_path and Group_name is not sufficient.\

##### An example of a transcode

\
a) Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Click the button "Get Projects and Groups". This provides 2 dropdowns with projects and groups from the Digital Rapids server\
c) Select a project and a group from the dropdowns\
d) Provide the source file name (Ex: "\\drcuk_colo1\\AsperaShare\\JS_Scary2.mxf")\
e) Provide the audio source file path if different from the video file (Ex: "\\drcuk_colo1\\AsperaShare\\JS_Scary2.wav")\
f) The Digital Rapids server takes these inputs and performs the transcode\
\

### Dolby VM600/DP600

![Dolby VM600/DP600 icon](icons/DolbyOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plugin provides the ability to invoke Dolby VM600/DP600 API operations for audio processing and workflow management.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.6   | New icon                                            |
| 0.0.5   | Improving test compatibility                        |
| 0.0.4   | Deal with incorrect parameters                      |
| 0.0.3   | Make plugin compatible with Orchestrator 2.5.1      |
| 0.0.2   | More error handling and force payload encoding      |
| 0.0.1   | Initial Release                                     |

### How It Works

- Integrates with Dolby VM600/DP600 services to perform audio processing operations through REST API calls.
- Supports two primary operations: listing available workflow profiles and submitting workorders for audio processing tasks.
- Handles various workorder types including SWO, MULTI_FILE_SWO, PINNACLE_SWO, OMNEON_SWO, HFWO, and others for different audio processing scenarios.
- Provides flexible URI support for source and destination files across multiple protocols (SMB, FTP, SFTP, local file shares).
- Includes workorder monitoring capabilities with configurable polling intervals and automatic status tracking until completion.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`Dolby Operation`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the basic connection settings:`
    - **`URL`**`: Enter the complete URL with port and endpoint for the Dolby service.`
    - **`API Operation`**`: Choose between 'Listing Workflow Profiles' or 'Submitting a Work Order'.`
3.  `For workorder submission, configure additional parameters:`
    - **`Work Order Name`**`: Specify a name for the work order.`
    - **`Work Order Type`**`: Select the appropriate type (SWO, MULTI_FILE_SWO, etc.).`
    - **`Source/Destination Filenames`**`: Provide URIs for input and output files.`
    - **`Workflow Profile`**`: Choose the processing profile to use.`
    - **`Polling Frequency`**`: Set the interval for status checking.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                | Example Value                                   |
|--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| **URL**                  | http://dolby-server.domain.com:8080/api/v1      |
| **API Operation**        | Submitting a Work Order                         |
| **Work Order Name**      | AudioProcessing_001                             |
| **Work Order Type**      | SWO                                             |
| **Source Filename**      | smb://user:pass@fileserver.com/audio/input.wav  |
| **Destination Filename** | smb://user:pass@fileserver.com/audio/output.wav |
| **Workflow Profile**     | Standard_Audio_Processing                       |
| **Work Order Priority**  | 5                                               |
| **Delete on Cancel**     | checked                                         |
| **Polling Frequency**    | 30                                              |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **URL** | Enter the entire URL along with the port and the endpoint to reach the Dolby VM600/DP600 service. |
| **API Operation** | Operation to be performed. Currently supports: Listing Workflow Profiles and Submitting a Work Order. |
| **Work Order Name** | The name of the workorder to be submitted. Required for workorder submission operations. |
| **Work Order Type** | The type of the workorder to be submitted. Accepted types are: SWO, MULTI_FILE_SWO, PINNACLE_SWO, OMNEON_SWO, HFWO, PINNACLE_HFWO, GVHFWO, OMNEON_HFWO, MONO_HFWO, and MONO_WO. |
| **Source Filename** | The URI of the file to be used as the source. Supports SMB, FTP, SFTP, and local file protocols with optional authentication credentials. |
| **Destination Filename** | The URI of the file to be used as the destination. Supports SMB, FTP, SFTP, and local file protocols with optional authentication credentials. |
| **Workflow Profile** | The profile of the workflow to be used for processing the audio content. |
| **Work Order Priority** | A unique number identifying the priority of the workorder with 1 being the highest priority and 10 being the lowest. |
| **Delete on Cancel** | If checked, the Dolby workorder will be deleted if the plugin step is canceled. If unchecked, the workorder will remain as is. |
| **Polling Frequency** | The time interval (in seconds) for how often the server needs to be polled for status updates. |

### Outputs Description

The outputs differ between the API operations:

#### Listing Workflow Profiles:

- **Workflows List**: Hash of workflows retrieved with keys being the names and values being the descriptions of the workflows.
- **Workflows List Count**: Number of profiles that were retrieved from the Dolby service.

#### Submitting a Work Order:

- **Refresh Time**: Minimum number of minutes until next checkResponse if status was not 'FINISHED'.
- **Correlation Id**: Correlation ID of the workorder that was submitted for tracking purposes.
- **Status**: Current status of the workorder that was submitted to the Dolby service.

### Supported URI Formats

- **SMB:** smb://\[username\[:password\]@\]hostname\[:port\]\[absolute-path\] or smb://\[\[domain;\]username\[:password\]@\]hostname\[:port\]\[absolute-path\]
- **FTP:** ftp://\[username\[:password\]@\]hostname\[:port\]\[absolute-path\] or ftp://\[\[domain;\]username\[:password\]@\]hostname\[:port\]\[absolute-path\]
- **SFTP:** sftp://\[username\[:password\]@\]hostname\[:port\]\[absolute-path\] or sftp://\[\[domain;\]username\[:password\]@\]hostname\[:port\]\[absolute-path\]
- **Local File:** file://username:password@/mount/someshare/somedir (for local shares)

**Note:** Username and password can be optional if remote shares are configured for anonymous access.

### Supported Work Order Types

- SWO (Standard Work Order)
- MULTI_FILE_SWO (Multi-file Standard Work Order)
- PINNACLE_SWO (Pinnacle Standard Work Order)
- OMNEON_SWO (Omneon Standard Work Order)
- HFWO (High Frequency Work Order)
- PINNACLE_HFWO (Pinnacle High Frequency Work Order)
- GVHFWO (GV High Frequency Work Order)
- OMNEON_HFWO (Omneon High Frequency Work Order)
- MONO_HFWO (Mono High Frequency Work Order)
- MONO_WO (Mono Work Order)

### Operating Instructions

- **Operation Selection:** Choose between 'Listing Workflow Profiles' to retrieve available processing profiles, or 'Submitting a Work Order' to process audio files through the Dolby service.
- **Profile Listing:** When listing workflow profiles, only the URL and API Operation parameters are required. The operation will return all available profiles with their descriptions.
- **Work Order Submission:** For workorder submission, ensure all required parameters are configured including source/destination URIs, workorder type, and workflow profile.
- **File URI Configuration:** Use the appropriate URI format for your file storage system. Include authentication credentials in the URI or configure shares for anonymous access.
- **Priority Management:** Set workorder priority between 1 (highest) and 10 (lowest) to manage processing queue order on the Dolby service.
- **Status Monitoring:** The plugin will automatically poll the Dolby service at the specified interval until the workorder status reaches 'FINISHED'.
- **Cancellation Behavior:** Configure the 'Delete on Cancel' option to determine whether workorders should be removed from the Dolby service if the plugin step is canceled.

### Dependencies

- Network connectivity to the Dolby VM600/DP600 service on the specified URL and port.
- Valid authentication credentials for accessing remote file shares (SMB, FTP, SFTP) if required.
- Proper file system permissions for local file share access when using file:// protocol.
- Available workflow profiles configured on the Dolby service for workorder processing.

### Troubleshooting

- If connection to the Dolby service fails, verify the URL format includes the correct protocol, hostname, port, and endpoint path.
- For file access issues, check that the URI format is correct and authentication credentials are valid for the specified file shares.
- If workorder submission fails, ensure the selected workorder type is supported and the workflow profile exists on the Dolby service.
- For polling issues, verify the polling frequency is appropriate for your workorder processing time and service load.
- Check that source files exist and are accessible from the Dolby service before submitting workorders.
- Ensure destination paths are writable and have sufficient space for the processed output files.

### Elemental Media Convert

![Elemental Media Convert icon](icons/ElementalMediaConvert.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plugin provides the ability to submit a file transcoding job to the AWS Elemental MediaConvert service.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.0 | Add operations start job and check job status |
| 0.2.1 | Add endpoint as a configurable parameter |
| 0.2.0 | Add more output group types and the destinations structure |
| 0.1.0 | Add reload job templates option |
| 0.0.3 | Remove wrong mention if help about max job templates |
| 0.0.2 | Make job template id optional when a custom json payload is filled in |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

### How It Works

- Integrates with AWS Elemental MediaConvert to perform cloud-based video and audio transcoding operations using predefined job templates.
- Supports three distinct operation modes: convert (full workflow), start job (initiate only), and check job status (monitor existing jobs).
- Utilizes AWS IAM authentication with access keys, secret keys, and role-based permissions for secure service access.
- Dynamically retrieves and manages job templates from MediaConvert, allowing selection from available transcoding configurations.
- Handles multiple output group types including FILE_GROUP_SETTINGS, MS_SMOOTH_GROUP_SETTINGS, DASH_ISO_GROUP_SETTINGS, and HLS_GROUP_SETTINGS.
- Provides flexible configuration options through custom JSON payloads for advanced transcoding scenarios beyond standard templates.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`Elemental Media Convert`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure AWS authentication and region:`
    - **`Access key`**`: Enter your AWS access key for authentication.`
    - **`Secret key`**`: Provide your AWS secret key for secure access.`
    - **`Region`**`: Select the AWS region where MediaConvert will process jobs.`
3.  `Set up MediaConvert job configuration:`
    - **`Operation`**`: Choose operation type (convert, start job, or check job status).`
    - **`Get Job Templates`**`: Click to retrieve available templates from MediaConvert.`
    - **`Job templates`**`: Select a template from the populated dropdown list.`
    - **`Role`**`: Specify the IAM role ARN for MediaConvert execution.`
4.  `Configure source and destination paths:`
    - **`Input file path`**`: Provide S3 path to the source media file.`
    - **`Output path`**`: Specify S3 destination folder for transcoded output.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter | Example Value |
|----|----|
| **Operation** | convert |
| **Access key** | AKIA... |
| **Secret key** | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* |
| **Region** | EU (Ireland) |
| **Job templates** | Standard_HD_Transcode |
| **Endpoint** | po21umhx.mediaconvert.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com |
| **Role** | arn:aws:iam::123456789931:role/MediaConvert3 |
| **Input file path** | s3://aspmediaconvert/test.mxf |
| **Output path** | s3://aspmediaconvert/outputs/ |
| **Status Polling frequency** | 5 |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | The comments of the Elemental Media Convert template for documentation and identification purposes. |
| **Operation** | The operation to perform. Choose from: convert (full transcode with status monitoring), start job (initiate transcode only), or check job status (monitor existing job). |
| **Access key** | The AWS access key used for authentication to access MediaConvert services. |
| **Secret key** | The AWS secret key used for authentication to access MediaConvert services. |
| **Region** | The AWS region where MediaConvert jobs will be processed (e.g., EU Ireland, US East). |
| **Job templates** | The list of defined MediaConvert job templates retrieved from the AWS account. |
| **Endpoint** | The AWS account-specific endpoint where API requests are sent (e.g., po21umhx.mediaconvert.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com). |
| **Role** | The IAM role ARN that MediaConvert will assume to access S3 resources and perform transcoding operations. |
| **Input file path** | The S3 path to the source file that will be transcoded. |
| **Output path** | The S3 path to the destination folder where transcoded files will be stored. |
| **Custom json payload** | Custom JSON data to send in the transcode request body for advanced configurations beyond standard templates. |
| **Reload job templates?** | If checked, reload the job templates data structure from Elemental MediaConvert before each transcoding job execution. |
| **Status Polling frequency** | How often the job status is checked during transcoding. Default is 5 seconds. |

### Inputs Description

All parameters can be available at runtime if not specified in the plugin configuration.

#### Mandatory Inputs:

- **Job_id**: The job ID, only required for the check job status operation.
- **Access_key_value**: The AWS access key for authentication.
- **Secret_key_value**: The AWS secret key for authentication.
- **Region**: The AWS region for MediaConvert processing.
- **Job template id**: The job template ARN. Visible only if a job template is not selected in the plugin and a JSON payload is not entered.
- **Endpoint**: The AWS account-specific endpoint for API requests.
- **Role**: The AWS IAM role for MediaConvert execution.
- **Input_file_path**: The S3 path to the source file for transcoding.
- **Output_folder_path**: The S3 path to the destination folder for output files.

#### Optional Inputs:

- **Custom JSON payload**: Custom JSON data to send in the transcode request body for advanced configurations.
- **Destinations**: The destinations JSON structure for cases where multiple output paths must be passed. See operating instructions for format examples.

### Outputs Description

- **result**: The result from the transcode operation expressed as a Ruby hash structure containing job details and status information.
- **result_json**: The result from the transcode operation expressed as a JSON structure for easy integration with other systems.
- **result_job_template_json**: The chosen job template JSON data structure containing the complete template configuration used for the transcode operation.

### Supported Output Group Types

- FILE_GROUP_SETTINGS - File-based outputs
- MS_SMOOTH_GROUP_SETTINGS - Microsoft Smooth Streaming
- DASH_ISO_GROUP_SETTINGS - DASH streaming format
- HLS_GROUP_SETTINGS - HTTP Live Streaming

### Operating Instructions

- **Prerequisites:** The plugin works with Orchestrator 3.1 release and upward. Earlier versions may experience SSL connectivity issues. Ensure the AWS domain mediaconvert.\<region\>.amazonaws.com (e.g., mediaconvert.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com for EU Ireland) is reachable on port 443 from your Orchestrator system.
- **Basic Transcode Setup:** Specify the operation to perform: 'convert' to start a job and monitor status, 'start job' to initiate without monitoring, or 'check job status' to monitor existing jobs. The convert operation is equivalent to start job + check job status. In some cases, splitting operations helps avoid restarting jobs when status checks return failures.
- **Authentication Configuration:** Provide AWS access key and secret key for authentication, select a region from the available list (e.g., EU Ireland), and specify the IAM role ARN (e.g., arn:aws:iam::123456789931:role/MediaConvert3).
- **Job Template Management:** Click 'Get Job Templates' to retrieve available templates from MediaConvert. Select a template from the dropdown, which will be preserved when you save and re-open the plugin. The AWS account endpoint is automatically fetched and displayed when job templates are retrieved.
- **File Path Configuration:** Enter S3 paths for input file (e.g., s3://aspmediaconvert/test.mxf) and output folder (s3://aspmediaconvert/outputs/). Currently, only single source files and single output paths are supported through the standard interface.
- **Runtime Job Template Specification:** When no custom JSON payload is used, job templates are fetched from a shared_states database entry. The job template ID (ARN) passed as runtime input must match stored templates. Use 'Get Job Templates' button or enable 'Reload job templates?' option to refresh template data.
- **Multiple Destinations:** Pass multiple destinations as runtime input using JSON format: { "destinations": \[ { "type": "FILE_GROUP_SETTINGS", "destination": "s3://xxx/" }, { "type": "DASH_ISO_GROUP_SETTINGS", "destination": "s3://yyy/" } \] }
- **Custom JSON Payload:** For advanced configurations, provide custom JSON payload matching the MediaConvert API request body specification at https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mediaconvert/latest/apireference/jobs.html. Use \<%=custom_payload%\> syntax for runtime input variables.

### Dependencies

- Orchestrator version 3.1 or higher for proper SSL connectivity with AWS services.
- Network connectivity to AWS MediaConvert endpoints on port 443 for the specified region.
- Valid AWS account with MediaConvert service access and appropriate IAM permissions.
- Configured IAM role with permissions to access S3 buckets and execute MediaConvert operations.
- S3 buckets and appropriate permissions for both source file access and output file storage.
- MediaConvert job templates configured in the AWS account for template-based operations.

### Troubleshooting

- If SSL connectivity issues occur, verify you're using Orchestrator 3.1 or higher and that the MediaConvert endpoint is accessible on port 443 from your system.
- For authentication failures, confirm AWS access keys are correct, have appropriate permissions, and that the specified IAM role exists and has MediaConvert and S3 access.
- If job templates don't load, verify AWS credentials, check network connectivity to MediaConvert, and ensure the region is correctly specified. Try clicking 'Get Job Templates' again after verifying connectivity.
- For job submission failures, verify S3 paths are correct, accessible, and that the IAM role has permissions to read source files and write to destination locations.
- If the endpoint value appears incorrect or empty, clear the endpoint field and re-open the plugin after clicking 'Get Job Templates' to fetch the current endpoint automatically.
- When using runtime job template IDs, ensure the template exists in the shared_states database by clicking 'Get Job Templates' at least once, or enable 'Reload job templates?' option for automatic refresh.
- For custom JSON payload errors, validate the JSON syntax and ensure it matches the MediaConvert API specification. Test the payload structure with AWS CLI or SDK first.

### Elemental transcoding

![Elemental transcoding icon](icons/ElementalTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plugin provides the ability to submit file transcoding jobs to an Elemental server.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.7.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.6.3 | Show completion percentage in status details |
| 0.6.2 | Fixes profiles list |
| 0.6.1 | List all available profiles (not just the first 20) |
| 0.6.0 | Added option to return ERROR instead of STATUS_UNDEFINED for backward compatibility |
| 0.5.3 | Return an error instead of STATUS_UNDEFINED when an exception is raised |
| 0.5.2 | Modifying reporting to reduce chances of timeout |
| 0.5.1 | Adding certificate_file nil for s3 authentication support |
| 0.5.0 | Add S3 Authentication params support |
| 0.4.0 | Make the API port accept other than 443 and 80 |
| 0.2.8 | Bug fix - fetch more profiles than the default 20 |
| 0.2.7 | Adding percent complete for progress bar support |
| 0.2.6 | adding remote node dependency |
| 0.2.5 | adding missing statements |
| 0.2.4 | 2016-01-07 JG - Adding server_address as a runtime parameter to allow mapping |
| 0.2.3 | 2015-02-24 ML - New icon, new name |
| 0.2.2 | add custom payload example |
| 0.2.1 | add proxy data |
| 0.2.0 | moves to asynchronous execution mode |
| 0.1.7 | exception handling of RemoteNode.find_by_name() |
| 0.1.6 | Improved exception handling when response is non 200 |
| 0.1.5 | Removes explicit dependency on xmlsimple |
| 0.1.4 | Fix for edges cases where a) percent complete is not returned when job is submitted b) output folder is not returned during status check |
| 0.1.3 | Considers the pre and post processing polling statuses |
| 0.1.2 | Fixes issue with username and port fields when the info is provided within a remote node |
| 0.1.1 | Fixes issues with Elemental server version 2.1.2 where request header has to be explicitly set and returns hash with error when job profile listing fails |
| 0.1.0 | Adds support for custom XML payloads |
| 0.0.4 | deleted reference to modal_dialog in edit.html |
| 0.0.3 | support for non-SSL requests (users have to chose port as 80 or 443) |
| 0.0.2 | fields will use default_get instead of field value |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

### How It Works

- Integrates with Elemental Transcoding servers to perform video and audio transcoding operations using predefined profiles.
- Supports flexible authentication options including username/API key authentication and S3 credential management for secure access.
- Provides dynamic profile retrieval from Elemental servers, allowing selection from available transcoding configurations.
- Handles SSL/HTTPS connections with configurable port settings and optional proxy server support for network flexibility.
- Supports custom XML payload configuration for advanced transcoding scenarios beyond standard profile-based operations.
- Monitors job progress with configurable polling intervals and provides comprehensive error reporting and status tracking.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`Elemental Transcoding`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure server connection:`
    - **`Elemental Server Node`**`: Select a remote node pointing to your Elemental server.`
    - **`Elemental Server IP address`**`: Enter the IP address or hostname of the Elemental server.`
    - **`Elemental API port`**`: Specify port number (default 443 for HTTPS).`
    - **`Use ssl?`**`: Check to enable HTTPS (automatically enabled for port 443).`
3.  `Set up authentication (if required):`
    - **`Enable Authentication`**`: Check this box if the server requires authentication.`
    - **`User name`**`: Provide the Elemental server username.`
    - **`API Key`**`: Enter the API key from your Elemental user profile.`
4.  `Configure transcoding parameters:`
    - **`Get Elemental Profiles`**`: Click to retrieve available profiles from the server.`
    - **`Elemental Profiles`**`: Select a profile from the populated dropdown.`
    - **`Source`**`: Specify the absolute path to the source file on the server.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                       | Example Value               |
|---------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| **Elemental Server Node**       | elemental-server-01         |
| **Elemental Server IP address** | 10.0.103.85                 |
| **Elemental API port**          | 443                         |
| **Use ssl?**                    | checked                     |
| **Enable Authentication**       | checked                     |
| **User name**                   | elemental_user              |
| **API Key**                     | \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*        |
| **Elemental Profiles**          | HD_H264_Profile             |
| **Profile Id**                  | 12345                       |
| **Source**                      | /data/input/expendables.avi |
| **Status Polling frequency**    | 5                           |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | The comments of the Elemental Transcoding template for documentation and identification purposes. |
| **Elemental Server Node** | The remote node name of the server which hosts the Elemental Transcoding server. |
| **Elemental Server IP address (or hostname)** | The remote host name or remote IP of the server which hosts the Elemental Transcoding server. |
| **Elemental API port (optional)** | The port number of remote server which hosts the Elemental Transcoding server (by default 443). |
| **Use ssl?** | To specify if HTTPS must be used instead of HTTP. If API port 443 is specified, no need to check this option as HTTPS will be used automatically. |
| **Proxy ip address or hostname (optional)** | The proxy host name or IP address used to reach the Elemental Transcoding server. |
| **Proxy port (optional)** | The port number of the proxy server for network connectivity. |
| **Enable Authentication** | If the Elemental server requires authentication, check this box to enable authentication parameters. |
| **User name** | The user name to be used to login/authenticate with the Elemental server. |
| **API Key** | API Key of the user used to authenticate with the Elemental server (found under Elemental User profile). |
| **Elemental Profiles** | A dropdown of Elemental profiles available on Elemental server to transcode files. |
| **Profile Id** | ID of the Elemental profile selected from the dropdown list. |
| **Source** | The absolute path to the file on the Elemental server file system. |
| **Use S3 Authentication** | Check this box if you want to enter S3 authentication credentials for accessing S3 resources. |
| **S3 authentication type** | Enter the authentication type for S3 access. Defaulted to 'basic'. |
| **S3 Shared Key** | Shared key for S3 that will be used as user name for S3 authentication. |
| **S3 Secret Key** | Secret Key for S3 that will be used as password for S3 authentication. |
| **Return error?** | Return an ERROR status rather than an UNDEFINED status (default) upon exception in the check status method. |
| **Custom xml payload** | Custom XML data to send to Elemental server for advanced transcoding configurations. See operating instructions for format examples. |
| **Status Polling frequency** | How often progress will be reported during transcoding. Default is 5 seconds. |

### Inputs Description

All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template. This plugin does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.

#### Mandatory Inputs:

- **Elemental Server Node or Elemental IP Address**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.
- **Profile Id**: A profile ID is mandatory while submitting a job, hence a profile must be selected from dropdown.
- **Source**: Absolute path to the source file on the remote server must be provided.

#### Mandatory Fields if Authentication is Needed:

- **User name**: Username must be provided when authentication is enabled.
- **API Key**: API key must be provided when authentication is enabled.

#### Optional Inputs:

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.
- **Elemental API Port**: The port is optional as a default value is used if not specified.
- **Proxy_server_address**: Proxy server IP address or host name.
- **Proxy_port**: Proxy port number for network connectivity.
- **Enable Authentication**: If the check box is not checked, the requests to the server do not include authentication parameters.
- **Status Polling frequency**: Specify if progress needs to be reported within a time other than default.

### Outputs Description

- **Input_file**: The input file path involved in the transcode operation.
- **Output_file(s)**: The output file(s) obtained as a result of the transcode operation.
- **Job_Id**: The ID of the job triggered by the transcode operation.
- **Errors**: Errors reported by transcode server during transcode operation.

### Operating Instructions

- **Plugin Purpose:** This plugin allows users to provide the inputs required by an Elemental server to perform a transcode job. The plugin supports authentication as requested by the Elemental Server in the latest API release. Currently only a single source file can be specified.
- **Basic Transcode Setup:** Provide remote node or remote server IP and port (e.g., 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com). Decide if authentication should be enabled - if yes, check the Enable Authentication box, otherwise leave it unchecked.
- **Profile Configuration:** Click 'Get Elemental Profiles' button to retrieve available profiles from the Elemental server. Select a profile from the dropdown - the profile ID will be submitted along with the source file path to the Elemental server.
- **File Processing:** Provide the source file name (e.g., '/data/input/expendables.avi'). The Elemental server takes these inputs, assigns a priority (default is 50) to the job, and performs the transcode operation.
- **Custom XML Payload:** A custom XML payload can be sent to Elemental for advanced configurations. When using custom payloads, source, username, password, and profile ID are typically defined as runtime inputs. However, if source and profile ID are also defined in the configuration, they take precedence.
- **Variable Precedence:** If names other than 'Source' and 'Profile_Id' (or 'Profile_ID' or 'Profile ID') are used in the custom payload, only runtime inputs are considered. Existing configuration values for these fields are not used in such cases.

### Custom XML Payload Example

A custom XML payload can be sent to Elemental, including for example this information:

    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <job>
      <input>
        <file_input>
          <uri><%=Source%></uri>
          <username>aspera</username>
          <password>toto</password>
        </file_input>
      </input>
      <profile><%=Profile_Id%></profile>
    </job>

With such a payload, source, username, password, and profile ID are typically defined as runtime inputs. But if source and profile ID are also defined in the configuration, they take precedence. Actually, no runtime inputs are available for Source and Profile_Id in this case.

### Dependencies

- Network connectivity to the Elemental Transcoding server on the specified IP address and port.
- Valid Elemental server user account with appropriate permissions for transcoding operations (if authentication is enabled).
- Proper file system access permissions on the Elemental server for source file locations.
- Available transcoding profiles configured on the Elemental server.
- Sufficient storage space on the Elemental server for both input and output files.
- For S3 integration: Valid AWS S3 credentials and appropriate bucket permissions.

### Troubleshooting

- If connection to Elemental server fails, verify the IP address and port are correct, and check network connectivity. Ensure SSL settings match server configuration.
- For authentication failures, confirm the username and API key are correct and that the user has appropriate permissions for transcoding operations.
- If profiles don't appear in the dropdown, verify server connectivity and authentication credentials. Try clicking 'Get Elemental Profiles' again after confirming access.
- For source file errors, ensure the file path is correct and accessible from the Elemental server, and that the file format is supported by the selected profile.
- If proxy configuration is causing issues, verify proxy server details are correct and test connectivity through the proxy to the Elemental server.
- For S3 authentication issues, confirm the S3 shared key and secret key are valid and have appropriate permissions for the specified S3 resources.
- If custom XML payload fails, validate the XML syntax and ensure runtime variables like \<%=Source%\> and \<%=Profile_Id%\> are properly referenced.

### Emotion operation

![Emotion operation icon](icons/EmotionOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to invoke Emotion API operation.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.1.8 | Encapsulating the job and workflow id field withing curly braces {} if not already done. |
| 1.1.7 | Making the Job id field dynamic for Initiate a Job operation. |
| 1.1.6 | Fixes source and destination runtime value conversion |
| 1.1.5 | Fixed documentation |
| 1.1.4 | Adding percent_complete variable. |
| 1.1.3 | Supporting Windows File Path |
| 1.1.2 | Making Source Field Mandatory |
| 1.1.1 | Added percentage complete to inprogress message |
| 1.1.0 | 2015/03/12 - JG -\> JG Added Asynchronous nature to trigger a job |
| 1.0.0 | 2015/20/08 - JG -\> Bug Fix and Feature Enhancement- Added get job status, list workflows, payload execution |
| 0.0.1 | 2015/20/08 - JG -\> Initial Commit |

### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved EmotionOperation configured instance.
- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved EmotionOperation configured instance.
- **Service URI**: The URL of the server where the Emotion service is hosted.(Example: http://localhost:44544/jobs)
- **API Operation**: Operation to be performed. Currently supports Triggering a Job (Asynch), Submitting a Job through Payload (Asynch), Listing Workflows(Synch), Polling Job Status (Asynch).
- **Source File**: The file(only 1) on which the operation is to be performed. It uses a hash format. Example : { "sourceUUID" =\> "/tmp/source/first.wav" }. Note that the source UUID must already be present in the Emotion system.
- **Destination File**: The file into which the output has to be stored. This uses a hash format.Example : { "destinationUUID" =\> "/tmp/source/first.wav" }. Note that the destinationUUID UUID must already be present in the Emotion system.
- **Workflow ID** : The UUID of the workflow that describes the operation on the source file must be entered here. We manually add the curly brackets for workflow id when they are not there for both the parameters and inputs.
- **Job UUID** : Optional field. If not assigned, the server would assign a unique id to every job. We manually add the curly brackets for job uuid when they are not there for both the parameters and inputs.
- **Inputs** : Some workflows require extra inputs for its execution. That could be entered here. It again follows a hash format(Example given below)
- **Outputs** : Some workflows require extra outputs after its execution. That could be entered here. It again follows a hash format(Example given below)
- **Polling Frequency**: The frequency of polling for the asynchronous operations.

### Inputs Description

The Mandatory inputs are service uri, source, workflow id. Some workflows doesnt require destination. So set it up according to the workflow that you are triggering. For all the Asynchronous job submissions, after the job is submitted the process keeps polling for a status till it gets a valid stable status ("Success", "Error"). The polling continues for the "Queued" and "InProgress" return status.

### Outputs Description

- The output fields obtained are the Job UUID, Job Raw Response, State Description and the message received. In case of an exception, we have a error msg field which displays the error associated with it.

### Operating Instructions

Note: - The path for triggering job is http://host:port/jobs. We have added code to handle /jobs. So enter just the IP(or host) and the port.\
- Source, Destination, Inputs, Outputs are all entered as a hash. Follow the same.\
- \*IMPORTANT\* - The file path entered should follow different naming to not fail on Windows Machines.(Follow one of the below methods)\
  1. Change the Windows path to Unix Path.\
     "C:\\Users\Administrator\Documents\wav-files\audio-file.wav" Should be changed to "C://Users/Administrator/Documents/wav-files/audio-file.wav"\
  2. Add extra "\\ for every "\\ present to escape it right.      "\\127.0.0.1\tfs\effengine\inputs\abc.mov" should be changed to "\\\\10.20.104.40\\tfs\\effengine\\inputs\\abc.mov"

Example:\
\
  service URI : *http://localhost:44544/*\
  workflow Id : *{ be8ce50d-62f9-4e9a-8a8f-9966d63804d1 }*\
  Source : *{ '7621eb1b-bc75-45c5-9bf4-a90aabae1afb' =\> 'C:/Users/Administrator/Documents/wav-files/audio-sample.wav' }*\
  Destination: *{ 'a09751ae-3ec1-4cef-80d7-0a06f89c84a4' =\> 'C:/Users/Administrator/Desktop/orchestraddtor.wav' }*\
  Inputs : *{ 'a09751ae-3ec1-4cef-80d7-0a06f89c84a4' =\> 'C:/Users/Administrator/Desktop/i1.wav', 'a09751ae-3ec1-4cef-80d7-0a06f89c84a4' =\> 'C:/Users/Administrator/Desktop/i2.wav' }*   

### Encoding.com transcoding

![Encoding.com transcoding icon](icons/EncodingdotcomTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-in provides the ability to submit file encoding job to a Encoding.com server.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.3.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.2.2 | remove dependency on custom Rails initializers |
| 0.2.1 | Adding percent complete for progress bar support |
| 0.2.0 | 2016-03-10 RT - Fixed custom payload, added JSON payload support |
| 0.1.3 | 2015-02-24 ML - New icon, fixes name for consistency |
| 0.1.2 | Removes explicit dependency on xmlsimple |
| 0.1.1 | prevents autocomplete of user field |
| 0.1.0 | added support for custom payload to be submitted to Encoding.com server |
| 0.0.3 | deleted reference to modal_dialog in edit.html |
| 0.0.2 | added a default_get method for all plugin fields |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Encoding.com Transcoding template

- **User ID**: The user id to be used to login/authenticate with the Encoding.com server (found under Encoding.com User profile)

- **API Key**: API Key of the user used to authenticate with the Encoding.com server (found under Encoding.com User profile)

- **Use SSL**: If SSL is desired, check this box

- **Operation**: Select one of the four operations. See below for description.

- **Source Files**: The absolute path to the files. Use comma separated for multiple files. See below for instructions.

- **Media IDs**: A media id is an unique identifier for Media available in the queue of Encoding.com server.

- **Output format**: An output format specifies to which format the source file must be encoded to.

- **Delete file after encoding**: If files are to be deleted from queue after encoding, check this box.

- **Notify URL**: Encoding.com server sends a notification to this URL (can be an HTTP server or a simple mailto:)

- **Status Polling frequency**: How often progress will be reported. Default is 5 seconds

- **Use json transcoding payload**: Check if the custom payload is in JSON format

- **Transcoding Payload**: If the operation selected is Submit Custom Payload, the plugin submits the xml/json payload given here to encoding.com

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the EncodingdotcomTranscoding action template.

- All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory:**\

- **User ID**: A User ID must be provided.
- **API Key**: An API Key must be provided.
- **Operation**: A task must be specified for the plugin to act on.
  \
  **Additional Mandatory Fields if Adding to Queue or if Adding and Encoding**
- **Source Files**: The absolute path to the files. Use comma separated for multiple files. See below for instructions.
- **Output format**: An output format specifies to which format the source file must be encoded to.
  \
  **Additional Mandatory Fields if Selecting Media to Encode**
- **Media IDs**: A Media must be selected from the dropdown or an array of multiple ids can be specified from Map Inputs.

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.
- **Use SSL**: If SSL is desired, check this box.
- **Delete file after encoding**: If files are to be deleted from queue after encoding, check this box.
- **Notify URL**:
- **Status Polling frequency**: Specify if progress needs to be reported within a time other than default.

##### Outputs description

- **Input_and_Output_media**: Contains both the input file(s) path involved in the encoding operation and the output file obtained as a result of the encoding
- **Errors**: Errors reported by Encoding.com server during the encoding operation for that specific media

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Encoding.com server to do an encoding job.\
\
Different operations require different set of input parameters. User ID, API Key are needed for all operations. The below table provides information about each operation and what inputs are needed.\
\

| Operation | Description | Input(s) and Output |
|----|----|----|
| Add to Queue or Add and Encode | The "Add to Queue" operation adds a source file to the queue of Encoding.com server. The "Add and Encode" operation adds the file to the queue and immediately encodes it. | Source file(s) and Output format are needed. For each media, the input file url, media_id and output file url are provided as output |
| Selective Encoding | Once Media ID(s) is/are provided, those media are encoded to requested format. | Media Id must be selected or provided via Map Inputs. For each media, the input file url, media_id and output file url are provided as output |
| Encode all | All media in Encoding.com server's queue are encoded to requested format. | No more inputs required. For each media, the input file url, media_id and output file url are provided as output |

\
**Source file URL formats:** A source file URL can be of any of the following protocols ASPERA(FASP), FTP, HTTP, AMAZON S3, RACKSPACE, CLOUDFILES, MICROSOFT AZURE or DROPBOX\
The plug-in supports authentication as requested by Encoding.com Server in the latest API release. An example of a encode job is given below. Both single source file and multiple source files (comma separated) can be specified. Encoding.com server uploads the result file to one of its Amazon servers and is available for download.\
\
**Important Note1:** An output format can be assigned only during Add to Queue or Add and Encode operations.\

##### An example of a transcode

\
a) Provide User ID and API Key\
b) Decide if SSL is necessary. If yes, check the box else leave it un-checked\
c) Select an operation that needs to be performed.\
d) If the operation is add to queue or add and encode provide source file(s) and output format. Save.\
e) When run, the source file(s) and output format is submitted to Encoding.com server and a Media ID (unique identifier) is generated\
f) If the operation is selective encoding. Click the button Get Encoding.com Media. This provides a drop-down with media from the Encoding.com server's queue\
g) Select a media from the dropdown. Save.\
h) When run, the Media ID is submitted to Encoding.com server for encoding\
i) If the operation is encode all just Save.\
j) When run, all the media in Encoding.com server's queue is encoded media by media\
\

### Envivio transcoding

![Envivio transcoding icon](icons/EnvivioVodEncoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This is Action plug-ins provides the ability to submit file encoding jobs to an Envivio VOD Encoder thru a 4Balancer manager.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.1.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.0.6 | Adding more debug information on failures |
| 1.0.5 | Fixing URL to fetch 500 presets and increasing timeout to 10 seconds |
| 1.0.4 | Adding Remote Node Dependency |
| 1.0.3 | 2015-02-24 ML - New icon, change display name |
| 1.0.2 | exception handling of RemoteNode.find_by_name() |
| 1.0.1 | Fixes job submission |
| 1.0.0 | Making asynchronous |
| 0.1.0 | Adds support for additional parameters |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved EnvivioVodEncoding configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved EnvivioVodEncoding configured instance.

- **4balancer server node**: The node where the 4Balancer controlling the 4Caster VOD is located.

- **4balancer server ip address (or hostname)**: Can be used to provide a path that overwrite the address of the node.

- **Input file name** : The source file for the encoding operation. This value can be entered using dynamic attributes

- **Output file name** : The output file for the encoded media. This value can be entered using dynamic attributes

- **Preset ID**: Select from the list of encoding presets configured on that 4Balancer.

- **Priority**: Optional priority for the job - the default is 5.

- **Polling frequency**: The progress status polling frequency in seconds (the default is 5 seconds).

- **Job name** : Optional - if not provided, one is generated by Orchestrator. Dynamic attributes are supported.

- **Content ID**: Optional - used to submit an encoding request with encryption turned on. Dynamic attributes are supported.

- **Input start-time**: Optional - by default 0 is used. If a value is provided, the Encoding will start at the specified timecode. Dynamic attributes are supported.

- **Input end-time**: Optional - if a value is provided the encoding will stop at the specified timecode. Dynamic attributes are supported.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the EnvivioVodEncoding action template.

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the AuroraFileVerification action template.\
\

- **4Balancer_server**: Points to the 4Balancer Server or service address. Can be either a node ID (i.e. 'Envivio_node') or a node address (i.e. '192.168.56.101'). This input is only requested when neither the node nor the server address is provided in the action template configuration.
- **Input_filename**: All the variables listed in 'Input file name' field are mandatory and must be of type string. If the 'Input file name' is left blank, then 'Input_filename' must be provided at run-time.
- **Output_filename**: Same as above for the path to the output file name.
- **Priority**: A number between 1 and 10. Default is 5.
- **Preset_ID**: Provides the ability to select the Preset to use for the encoding at run-time. The named in this variable needs to be configured on the 4Balancer server where the execution is routed.
- **Content_ID**: Provides the ability to set a value for 'Content ID' at run-time, if it is not set in the configuration template used.
- **Job_name**: Provides the ability to set a value for 'Job name' at run-time, if it is not set in the configuration template used.
- **Input_start_time** : Provides the ability to set a value for 'Input start-time' at run-time, if it is not set in the configuration template used.
- **Input_end_time** : Provides the ability to set a value for 'Input end-time' at run-time, if it is not set in the configuration template used.

\

##### Outputs description

- **Output_file_name**: The file path for the output of the encoding operation.
- **Job_name**: The specified job name for the encoding operation
- **Job_ID**: The job id assigned by 4Balancer for the encoding operation

##### Operating Instructions

This action executes the file encoding operation configured in the specified Preset on the 4Caster VOD instance managed by the specified 4Balancer server. The connection to the remote server is done using the 4Balancer web-service.

Note: The file path used by Envivio 4Balancer uses the format file://host/path in which host is a server where the network file share is located and path is the mounting path. if a path is provided without a 'file://' prefix, the prefix will be automatically added.

Important Note: this action plugins does not do any file movement. Hence, for it to run a file encoding operation, the specified file has to be accessible on the 4Balancer server to which the job is sent. Either the file is transferred to that server or it must be accessible from that server either on some type of network share.

### EolementheWorkflowManager

![EolementheWorkflowManager icon](icons/EolementheWorkflowManager.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in provides the ability to run an Eolementhe workflow.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.0 | Support of the new API with apikey |
| 0.0.2 | Correcting the name of metadata.yml for the Help tab to load. It should be same as class name. |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved EolementheWorkflowManager configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved EolementheWorkflowManager configured instance.

- **Remote Node**: The Eolementhe server node name from the list of Remote Nodes.

- **Remote server ip address**: The Eolementhe server node IP address or domain name.

- **Remote service port** : The Eolementhe service port (443 by default).

- **API key**: The API key to authenticate against the Eolementhe server.

- **Eolementhe workflows**: List of available workflows.

- **Workflow id**: The id of the workflow to execute. This is populated automatically upon choosing a workflow name in the workflow list.

- **Source filename**: A source file name.

- **Polling frequency**: Interval in seconds to check for the job submission status.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the EolementheWorkflowManager action template.

Changes in this list of inputs will override any value set in the action configuration/template.\
\

- **remote node or server address**: Eolementhe remote node name or node IP address or domain name
- **API key**: API key to authenticate against the Eolementhe server
- **source**: source filename
- **workflow id**: ID of the workflow to execute. Careful: it has to be the workflow id, not the workflow name.

\

##### Outputs description

- **result**: The job submission JSON output

##### Operating Instructions

Specify a remote node or an IP address/domain name and a port for the Eolementhe server.\
The IP address/ domain name takes precedence over the remote node if both are defined (which is typically not a good practise).\
Specify an API key for authentication.\
Click on 'Get Eolementhe Workflows' to obtain a list of available workflows from the Eolementhe server. Select a workflow from the drop down list. The workdlow id is automatically displayed.\
Add the source filename (not the full path). Multiple file names can be specified one after the other with a ; separator\
In output, you'll get the JSON content returned from the job submission.\
Each time you open the plugin, if a workflow has already been selected, a new request is made to the Eolementhe server\
to refresh the list of available workflows.\
The port can be passed optionaly in input. This input value is not taken into account when clicking on\
'Get Eolementhe Workflows', so it may return an error. In this case, specify the workflow id in input and\
don't click on 'Get Eolementhe Workflows'.\

### Episode transcoding

![Episode transcoding icon](icons/EpisodeTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}
