This is Action plug-ins provides the ability to submit file transformation job to a Telestream Episode engine.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.1.7 | Corrected the variable name and removed colon from :episode_transcoding_epitask_payload for rich text editor helper in edit.html.erb. |
| 1.1.6 | Returning percent_complete from check_status method to be consumed by the activities screen. |
| 1.1.5 | Adding remote node dependency |
| 1.1.4 | Added a parameter in check_staus method to get % complete for all the in-progress tasks. |
| 1.1.3 | 2015-02-24 ML - New icon |
| 0.1.2 | Sometimes output file path is not an array, accounting for this while assigning the output variable |
| 0.1.1 | Bug fix in server get method |
| 0.1.0 | Added field to allow share-io to be on/off |
| 0.0.3 | Submits destination dir, priority, naming with API call. Fetches output file paths correctly. Provides workflow ids as output. Added help file. Tested at Turner 3/12/2014 |
| 0.0.2 | Exposes epitask filepath |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Episode Transcoding template

- **Episode Engine Node**: The remote node name of the server which hosts the Episode Transcoding engine

- **Episode RPC port(optional)**: The XML RPC port of remote server which hosts the Episode Transcoding engine

- **Input filepath**: The absolute filepath of the input file which needs to be transcoded

- **Output filepath**: The directory in which the output file needs to be written into

- **Naming**: The name which needs to be assigned to the output file

- **Epitask Filepath**: The absolute path to the file on the server's file system

- **Jobname (optional)**: The absolute filepath to the Epitask file.

- **Priority (optional)**: The priority that needs to be assigned to the Episode task

- **Submit in demo mode**: If checked, the job will be submitted in demo mode.

- **Epitask Payload**: The fully constructed XML with job details. This will stop Orchestrator from constructing its own XML

- **Polling frequency**: Determines how often progress will be reported. Default is 5 seconds

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the EpisodeTranscoding action template.

- All required parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory for transcode operations:**\

- **Episode Server Node or Episode IP Address**: Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.
- **Epitask Filepath**: Absolute path to the Epitask file accessible to the Episode engine must be provided
- **Input filepath**: Absolute path to the source file accessible to the Episode engine must be provided

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.
- **Episode XML RPC Port**: The port is optional as a default value is used if not specified.
- **Output filepath**: The output directory will be chosen by Episode using the Epitask file, if not provided.
- **Naming**: The output filename will be chosen by Episode using the Epitask file, if not provided.
- **Jobname**: The jobname will be chosen by Episode using the Epitask file, if not provided.
- **Priority**: The priority will be chosen by Episode using the Epitask file, if not provided.
- **Submit in demo mode**: Default value is false. Check the box to submit in demo mode.
- **Epitask Payload**: Users can chose to not specify a Epitask file but submit a Epitask payload.
- **Polling frequency**: Specify if progress needs to be reported within a time other than default.

##### Outputs description

- **Output_file_paths**: The output file path as a result in the transcode operation
- **Workflow_IDs**: The task ID of the tasks triggered by job
- **Job_ID**: The ID of the job triggered by the transcode operation

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Episode server to do a transcode job.\
\
The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\
An example of a transcode job is given below. Currently only a single source file can be specified. Output filepath and Naming if specified is used by the template setup on the Episode server to place the output file\
\

##### An example of a transcode

\
a) Provide remote node port (example: 10.0.103.85 or transcode.lala.com)\
b) Provide input file name (Ex: "/data/input/expendables.avi") and a output file path "/data/output/"\
c) Provide Epitask file path (Ex: "/data/input/epitask.tsk")\
d) Provide optional inputs such as Naming, Priority and Job Name\
e) Orchestrator submits these parameters to the Episode server takes these inputs and performs the transcoding job.\
\

### FFMPEG transcoding

![FFMPEG transcoding icon](icons/FfmpgTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides the ability to execute file transcoding operations using the FFMPEG toolset on remote nodes.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.6.0 | Removing Windows support code, Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.5.0 | Add execution on Windows flag |
| 0.4.3 | Fixes possible timeout when transcoding audio only files |
| 0.4.2 | Fixes hide/show in configuration screen |
| 0.4.1 | Parse variables in binary_path |
| 0.4.0 | Manual Command |
| 0.3.0 | Extending option column to text |
| 0.2.7 | Adding Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.2.6 | New icon, fixes name for consistency |
| 0.2.5 | -\> Fixed constants in help file (Matched it to their purpose) |
| 0.2.4 | -\> Fixed constants in help file |
| 0.2.3 | -\> Corrects description of options in the UI "Command options (after target filename)" |
| 0.2.2 | -\> Exposes overwrite option as an optional runtime input |
| 0.2.1 | -\> fixes typo in post_path_parameters_get |
| 0.2.0 | -\> Adds possibility to specify "after file path" parameters. Added help file. |
| 0.1.1 | -\> Fixed execution node passed as run time parameter |
| 0.1.0 | -\> Adds overwrite mode to UI |
| 0.0.5 | -\> Ensure non-interactive exection in Unix |
| 0.0.4 | -\> suppress error in ffmpeg return |
| 0.0.3 | -\> Error out if no status is provided |
| 0.0.2 | -\> Fix bug in default output var name and correct the command line option position |
| 0.0.1 | -\> Initial release. Does not support add hoc node definition |

### How It Works

- Executes FFMPEG transcoding operations on remote nodes via SSH connections for distributed media processing.
- Constructs FFMPEG command lines with configurable input/output paths, binary locations, and custom options.
- Supports overwrite protection to prevent accidental file replacement and Windows-specific execution handling.
- Allows flexible command option placement before and after target filenames for advanced FFMPEG parameter control.
- Provides detailed operation reports and output file path information for workflow integration.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add an `**`FFmpeg Transcoding`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the essential parameters:`
    - **`Execution node`**`: Select the remote node where FFMPEG is installed.`
    - **`FFMPEG Binary path`**`: Full path to the FFMPEG executable on the remote node.`
    - **`Input file path`**`: Path to the source media file as seen in the SSH session.`
    - **`Output file path`**`: Path where the transcoded file will be created.`
3.  `Optionally configure advanced settings:`
    - **`Command options`**`: Add FFMPEG parameters before or after the target filename.`
    - **`Overwrite output if present?`**`: Enable to allow overwriting existing files.`
    - **`Execution on windows?`**`: Check if the remote node is a Windows system.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter                           | Example Value                |
|-------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| **Execution node**                  | media-processing-server      |
| **FFMPEG Binary path**              | /usr/bin/ffmpeg              |
| **Input file path**                 | /media/input/source.mp4      |
| **Output file path**                | /media/output/transcoded.mp4 |
| **Overwrite output if present?**    | checked                      |
| **Execution on windows?**           | unchecked                    |
| **Command options (before target)** | -c:v libx264 -preset medium  |
| **Command options (after target)**  | -c:a aac -b:a 128k           |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Comments** | Comments associated to this template or configuration for documentation purposes. |
| **Execution node** | The remote node to execute the FFMPEG operation from. List of already configured Remote Nodes. |
| **FFMPEG Binary path** | The path (as seen in the SSH session) of the ffmpeg executable on the remote node. |
| **Input file path** | The path (as seen in the SSH session) of the input file to FFMPEG for transcoding. |
| **Output file path** | The path (as seen in the SSH session) of the output file of FFMPEG transcoding operation. |
| **Overwrite output if present?** | Allows to overwrite an existing output file. If not selected, FFMPEG will fail if the output file already exists. |
| **Execution on windows?** | To avoid the \>/dev/null at the end of the command line in case the execution node is Windows. |
| **Command options (before target filename)** | Any FFMPEG option or parameter. This string will be added to the FFMPEG command BEFORE the input file path. |
| **Command options (after target filename)** | Any FFMPEG option or parameter. This string will be added to the FFMPEG command AFTER the input file path. |

### Inputs Description

- **Runtime variables**: The runtime inputs of this action depend on how the configuration is done. Variables exposed in the command line will be exposed as mandatory inputs.

### Outputs Description

- **Result_file_path**: The path of the output file created by the FFMPEG transcoding operation.
- **transformation_report**: A detailed report of the FFMPEG operation including processing information and any error messages.

### Command Construction

- The FFMPEG command line is constructed in the following order:
- \<FFMPEG Binary path\> -i \<Input file path\> \[-y (if overwrite flag is set)\] \<Command options (before target filename)\> \<Output file path\> \<Command options (after target filename)\>
- Example: `/usr/bin/ffmpeg -i /input/video.mp4 -y -c:v libx264 -preset medium /output/transcoded.mp4 -c:a aac -b:a 128k`

### Operating Instructions

- This action plugin provides the ability to submit and control a file transcoding operation using the FFMPEG toolset.
- FFMPEG is executed on a remote node via an SSH connection, allowing for distributed media processing across multiple servers.
- The plugin constructs the complete FFMPEG command line based on your configuration parameters and executes it remotely.
- **NOTE: FFMPEG needs to be installed on the remote node.** Ensure the binary path is correct and the FFMPEG executable is accessible.
- Variables in command options are exposed as runtime inputs, allowing dynamic parameter configuration during workflow execution.
- The overwrite flag adds the -y parameter to FFMPEG, forcing overwrite of existing output files without prompting.

### Dependencies

- FFMPEG must be installed and accessible on the target execution node.
- SSH access must be enabled on the remote node for command execution.
- The remote user account must have read permissions for input files and write permissions for output directories.
- Network connectivity between Orchestrator and the execution node is required for SSH operations.

### Troubleshooting

- FFMPEG not found errors: Verify the FFMPEG Binary path is correct and the executable exists at that location on the remote node.
- File already exists errors: Enable 'Overwrite output if present?' option or ensure output paths are unique for each execution.
- SSH connection failures: Check network connectivity and SSH credentials for the remote execution node.

### Flics transcoding

![Flics transcoding icon](icons/FlicsTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to submit and monitor a file transcoding job to a Flics server.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | Returns message in case of failure                  |
| 0.0.1   | Initial implementation                              |

#### How It Works

- This action plug-in submits a transcoding task to a Flics server.
- It supports both standard and advanced task creation modes.
- The plug-in monitors the transcoding task until completion using polling.
- Generated destination files are returned as outputs once the task completes.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **FlicsTranscoding** plug-in.
2.  Complete the following configuration fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved FlicsTranscoding configuration.
    - : Optional comments explaining the purpose of this transcoding step.
    - : The IP address or hostname of the Flics server.
    - : If selected, SSL is used to connect to the Flics server.
    - : The port number of the Flics server.
    - : The username used to authenticate with the Flics server.
    - : The password used to authenticate with the Flics server.
    - : If selected, a custom transcoding task definition is used.
    - : JSON body used to create a custom transcoding task when advanced mode is enabled.
    - : Path to the source file inside a Flics source storage.
    - : Dropdown list of available Flics source storages.
    - : ID of the selected Flics source storage.
    - : Dropdown list of available Flics templates.
    - : ID of the selected Flics template.
    - : Title assigned to the transcoding task.
    - : Tags associated with the transcoding task.
    - : Interval in seconds used to check the transcoding task status.

#### Example Configuration

Below is a minimal configuration example for the FlicsTranscoding plug-in.

| Parameter         | Value               |
|-------------------|---------------------|
| Address           | flics.example.com   |
| Use ssl?          | Selected            |
| Username          | flics_user          |
| Source file name  | /incoming/video.mov |
| Template name     | HD_Transcode        |
| Polling frequency | 10                  |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved FlicsTranscoding configuration. |
| **Comments** | Optional comments explaining the purpose of this transcoding step. |
| **Address** | The IP address or hostname of the Flics server. |
| **Use ssl?** | If selected, SSL is used to connect to the Flics server. |
| **Port** | The port number of the Flics server. |
| **Username** | The username used to authenticate with the Flics server. |
| **Password** | The password used to authenticate with the Flics server. |
| **Use advanced mode** | If selected, a custom transcoding task definition is used. |
| **Custom task** | JSON body used to create a custom transcoding task when advanced mode is enabled. |
| **Source file name** | Path to the source file inside a Flics source storage. |
| **Source storage name** | Dropdown list of available Flics source storages. |
| **Source storage id** | ID of the selected Flics source storage. |
| **Template name** | Dropdown list of available Flics templates. |
| **Template id** | ID of the selected Flics template. |
| **Title** | Title assigned to the transcoding task. |
| **Tags** | Tags associated with the transcoding task. |
| **Polling frequency** | Interval in seconds used to check the transcoding task status. |

#### Inputs Description

Mandatory and optional inputs depend on the selected configuration mode.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *address (mandatory)* | IP address or hostname of the Flics server. |
| *username (mandatory)* | Username to log in to the Flics server. |
| *password (mandatory)* | Password to log in to the Flics server. |
| *custom_task (mandatory)* | JSON body defining a custom transcoding task when advanced mode is enabled. |
| *source_file_name (mandatory)* | Path to the source file inside a Flics source storage. |
| *source_storage_id_or_name (mandatory)* | ID or name of the Flics source storage (must be an input storage). |
| *template_id_or_name (mandatory)* | ID or name of the Flics template. |
| port | Port number of the Flics server. Defaults to 80 (non-SSL) or 443 (SSL). |
| title | Title of the transcoding task. |
| tags | Tags associated with the transcoding task. Default is 'local'. Comma-separated values are supported. |
| polling_frequency | Polling interval in seconds. Default is '10' seconds. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter         | Description                              |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------|
| destination_files | Files generated by the transcoding task. |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

Network connectivity to the Flics server and valid Flics credentials.

#### Operating Instructions

1.  The plug-in connects to the configured Flics server using the provided credentials.
2.  A transcoding task is created using either a template-based or custom task definition.
3.  If advanced mode is enabled, the provided JSON body is used directly.
4.  The task status is polled periodically until completion or failure.
5.  Once completed, generated destination files are returned as outputs.

### FlipFactory transcoding

![FlipFactory transcoding icon](icons/FlipFactoryTransformation.png){.plugin-icon}

This is Action plug-ins provides the ability to submit file transformation job to a Telestream FlipFactory server.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 1.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 1.1.2 | Added remote Node dependency |
| 1.1.1 | Added help file |
| 1.1.0 | 2015-02-24 ML - New icon, change display name |
| 1.0.9 | Exposing server node as a runtime input |
| 1.0.8 | exception handling of RemoteNode.find_by_name() |
| 1.0.7 | Bug fix in default payload which was submitting user password without substituting provided value. |
| 1.0.6 | Expose password as optional input |
| 1.0.5 | detect network errors when communicating with Flip server and handle failures |
| 1.0.4 | exposes failures as outputs |
| 1.0.3 | fix possible uuid without curly |
| 1.0.2 | fix accessing the URL variable |
| 1.0.1 | fix typo in polling frequency |
| 1.0.0 | Move to asynchronous |
| 0.2.0 | Move FLIP_DIR creation out of clean-up function |
| 0.1.5 | Bug fix for over-written message |
| 0.1.4 | Automated clean up of state files |
| 0.1.3 | adds rich text editing for XML message |
| 0.1.2 | fix bug in output of files |
| 0.1.1 | adds password and mediatype to default message |
| 0.1.0 | adds support for node selection |
| 0.0.3 | remove dependency on ROXML |
| 0.0.2 | expose flip factory server as parameter |
| 0.0.1 | initial development |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved FlipFactoryTransformation configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved FlipFactoryTransformation configured instance.

- **Name**: Name used to identify the Plugin Step

- **Comments**: The description field where you can enter any comments to be stored for future reference

- **Flipfactory Server Node**: The remote node on which the TlipFactory Transcoding server is installed

- **Server url (or address/hostname)**: If the node is not provided, you can enter the server url here

- **Polling Frequency**: The frequency at which the status has to be polled

- **Message**: This field is to store the external payload. All the Ruby inputs given here will come on the Preset Parameters on refresh

- **user name**: Default field for empty message. This is to collect the user name of the transcoding user

- **user_password**: Default field for empty message.This field is used to collect the password of the trancoding user

- **factory name**: Default field for empty message. This field is used to collect the factory name

- **Media type**: Default field for empty message.This field is used to store the media type of the field being transcoded

- **file_paath**: Default field for empty message.This field stores the path to the file to be transcoded

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the FlipFactoryTransformation action template.

\

- **Remote Node/Service URL,** : Is the only mandatory field needed
- **user_name, user_password, media_type, factory_name, file_path**: These fields are mandatory if the default payload is chosen.

\

##### Outputs description

- **output_directory**: The directory in which the output file is stored
- **output_files_array**: This stores the list of files that has been transcoded
- **total_processed**: Total number of files processed
- **first_error_msg**: Stores the first error message if any
- **first_error_type**: Stores the type of the first error message if any
- **error_messages**: This stores all the error messages received from the transcoder
- **error_types**: This stores the type of all the error messages

##### Operating Instructions

This plugin helps to perform the flip factory transcoding operation on a given file.\
NOTE: From the service provider -\> FlipFactory and all products in the FlipFactory family (including Ad Manager, TrafficManager and Graphics Factory) are no longer available for purchase.\

### HandBrake transcoding

![HandBrake transcoding icon](icons/HandbrakeTranscoding.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to submit and control a file transcoding operation using the HandBrake toolset.

| Version | Comment                                                         |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8             |
| 0.0.5   | Removing Windows support code                                   |
| 0.0.4   | Adding Remote Node Dependency                                   |
| 0.0.3   | 2015-02-24 ML - New icon, fixes display name for consistency    |
| 0.0.2   | \- 2015/02/09 - JG -\> Parse Code Commented since its not used. |
| 0.0.1   | -\> Initial release.                                            |

- This action allows to execute an HandBrake task on a remote node.\

  ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: Comments associated to this template or configuration.

- **Execution node**: The remote node to execute the HandBrake operation from. List of already configured Remote Nodes.

- **HandBrake Binary path**: The path (as seen in the SSH session) of the HandBrakeCLI executable.

- **Input file path**: The path (as seen in the SSH session) of the input file to HandBrake.

- **Output file path**: The path (as seen in the SSH session) of the output file of HandBrake.

- **Command options (before source filename)**: Any HandBrake option or parameter. This string will be added to the HandBrake command BEFORE the input file path

<!-- -->

- Input file path, Output file path and the Execution node, Handbrake binary path are mandatory parameters for the execution of this plugin.\
  All of them support both design time and runtime specification. They also support dynamic entry (Example shown below)\
  The run time inputs of this action depend on how the configuration is done. Variables exposed in the command line will be exposed as mandatory inputs.

##### Outputs description

- This action executes an HandBrake transformation action on a remote node, via SSH.\
- **Result_file_path**: The path of the output file
- **transformation_report**: A report of the HandBrake operation

##### Operating Instructions

This action plug-in provides the ability to submit and control a file transcoding operation using the HandBrake toolset.\
HandBrake will be executed on a remote node via an SSH connection.\
**NOTE: HandBrakeCLI needs to be installed in the remote node.**\
The transformation result file will be stored on the specified path in the remote node.\
Below are some examples showing different ways the plugin can be used.\
Note: Execution node is not included below since the examples are all run on a SSH client.\
**Example 1 (Inputs at Design time):**\
In this example all the inputs required are provided at design phase.\
*DESIGN TIME CONFIGURATION:*\
  Input file path : "/usr/bin/input/test.flv"\
  Output file path : "/usr/bin/output/test.mp4"\
  Handbrake binary path : "/usr/bin/./HandBrakeCLI"\
*OUTPUTS:*\
  Shows the progress report along with the status of the transcoding\
\
\
**Example 2 (Dynamic entry: Inputs at Design and Run time):**\
In this scenario, we are providing partial inputs at design name and the filename value is collected at runtime.\
*DESIGN TIME CONFIGURATION:*\
  Input file path : "/usr/bin/input/\<%=filename %\>.flv"\
  Output file path : "/usr/bin/output/\<%=filename %\>.mp4"\
  Handbrake binary path : "/usr/bin/./HandBrakeCLI"\
*RUNTIME CONFIGURATION:*\
  filename : "test"\
*OUTPUTS:*\
  Shows the progress report along with the status of the transcoding\
\
\
**Example 3 (Dynamic entry : Inputs at Design and Run time using inbuilt functions):**\
In this example, we collect the input_file_path at runtime and use inbuilt function to create the output_file_path from it.\
*DESIGN TIME CONFIGURATION:*\
  Output file path : "/usr/bin/output/\<%=File.nakedname(input_file_path) %\>.mp4"\
  Handbrake binary path : "/usr/bin/./HandBrakeCLI"\
*RUNTIME CONFIGURATION:*\
  input_file_path : "usr/bin/input/test.flv"\
*OUTPUTS:*\
  Shows the progress report along with the status of the transcoding\
\
\

### Hybrik Transcoder

![Hybrik Transcoder icon](icons/HybrikTranscoder.png){.plugin-icon}

This Action plug-in provides the ability to submit a transcoding job to the Hybrik transcoder

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | output error message in case of error               |
| 0.0.1   | initial development                                 |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Hybrik Transcoder template

- **Endpoint**: The URL endpoint for the API requests to Hybrik (e.g. https://api-demo.hybrik.com/v1)

- **Oapi key**: The API key used for authentication

- **Oapi secret**: The API secret used for authentication

- **Username**: The Hybrik account username

- **Password**: The Hybrik account password

- **Json payload**: The JSON payload defining the transcode job

- **Status Polling frequency**: How often the job status is checked. Default is 5 seconds

<!-- -->

- All parameters can be available at runtime if not specified in the plugin configuration.\
- **Endpoint**: The URL endpoint for the API requests to Hybrik.
- **Oapi_key**: The API key used for authentication.
- **Oapi_secret**: The API secret used for authentication.
- **Username**: The Hybrik account username
- **Password**: The Hybrik account password
- **Json_payload**: The JSON payload defining the transcode job

##### Outputs description

- **result**: The result from the transcode operation expressed as a Ruby structure
- **result_json**: The result from the transcode operation expressed as a Json structure
- **job_id**: The transcode job id
- **error_message**: The transcode error message if there is a failure

##### Operating Instructions

Pre-requisites:\
The endpoint must be reachable on port 443 from your Orchestrator system.\
\
To configure a transcode operation:\
a) Specify the API URL endpoint (e.g. https://api-demo.hybrik.com/v1)\
b) Specify the API key and secret key used for authentication. They can be found in the Hybrik WEB GUI under Account + Info + API details.\
c) Specify your Hybrik account username + password\
d) Enter your JSON transcode payload\
It must match the transcode request body as defined here:\
https://docs.hybrik.com/api/v1/HybrikAPI.html?#transcode-task\

### Marquis Mews Operation

![Marquis Mews Operation icon](icons/MarquisMewsOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in provides the ability to use the API's listed under the Medway Web Service solution of Marquis.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.2   | fixing a commit bug.Variable missing                |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Comments**: The comments of the Vantage Transcoding template

- **Service URL**: The remote url along with its port to access the Medway server

- **Operation**: This Marquis plugin supports Simple MEWS Transfer, EDL Transfer to Interplay and Get Job Status Operations currently.

- **Payload**: The parameter shows the payload based on the operation chosen.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the MarquisMewsOperation action template.

- All parameters are available as inputs at runtime if not specified by the template.\
  This plugin loads the payload based on the Operation chosen. So the inputs vary according to the payload. Users, can create addition fields by appending \<%= FieldName %\> to the payload.\
  This plug-in does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\

\
**These inputs are mandatory if you are submitting with the Simple Transfer Operation**\

- **Service URL**: The url with the port to access the Medway Service
- **Payload**: Payload for the operation chosen. This payload is submitted as is after filling in the required fields. Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.
- **SourceFile**: The path to the source file
- **DestinationFile**: The path to the destination file
- **ResourceId**: The unique resource id that is need for each job

\
**These inputs are mandatory if you are submitting with Get the Job Status Operation:**\

- **Service URL**: The url with the port to access the Medway Service
- **Payload**: Payload for the operation chosen. This payload is submitted as is after filling in the required fields. Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.
- **ResourceId**: The resource id of the job that you want the status for

\
**These inputs are mandatory if you are submitting with the EDL Transfer to Interplay Operation**\

- **Service URL**: The url with the port to access the Medway Service
- **Payload**: Payload for the operation chosen. This payload is submitted as is after filling in the required fields. Either a remote node must be selected or a remote IP address must be provided.
- **AvidInterplayLocationURL** : The path to the AVID location URL
- **DestinationFile**: The path to the destination file

\
**These inputs are optional:**\

- **Name**: It is advisable that a name is entered to identify the template.
- **Comments**: Information about the template which will help identify the template.

##### Outputs description

- **Response Received**: The XML received as is from the Medway Server
- **Error Message**: In case of error this holds the cause of the exeption

##### Operating Instructions

This plug-in allows users to provide necessary inputs required for a Medway server to do a transcode job. The mandatory inputs are listed above.\
\
Any input can be added to the payload by using "\<%= FieldName %\>" format.\
\

### Minnetonka AudioTools Server

![Minnetonka AudioTools Server icon](icons/MinnetonkaAudiotoolserver.png){.plugin-icon}

This plug-in provides the ability to process, convert, encode, and decode audio files with the Minnetonka AudioTools Server. It implements the AudioTools SOAP based WEB service API.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.3.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.2.2 | Updating to standard coding style (snake case variables) |
| 0.2.1 | Removing Windows support code |
| 0.2.0 | Adding a way to disable escaping HTML |
| 0.1.1 | Parsing ATS response XML to provide more info on execution from ATS log |
| 0.1.0 | Adding new dropdown to support new WSDL file |
| 0.0.5 | Fix to load the right WSDL path for Windows |
| 0.0.4 | percent complete for progress bar support |
| 0.0.3 | Added Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.0.2 | Added metadata.yml file |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved MinnetonkaAudiotoolserver configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved MinnetonkaAudiotoolserver configured instance.

- **Remote Node**: The Audiotools server node name from the list of Remote Nodes.

- **Remote server ip address**: The Audiotools server node IP address or domain name.

- **Remote web service port** : To change the Audiotools SOAP service port (9090 by default).

- **Workflow XML**: A file path or a memory buffer to the workflow XML to execute.

- **Init from type**: The method used to load the workflow XML: from a file path or from a memory buffer.

- **Priority**: The submitted job priority: 0 for low, 1 for medium, 2 for high.

- **Disable escaping?**: To disable escaping the workflow XML content. By default this content is sent to Minnetonka with escaping.

- **Polling frequency**: Interval in seconds to check for the job submission status.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the MinnetonkaAudiotoolserver action template.

Changes in this list of inputs will override any value set in the action configuration/template.\
\

- **remote node or server address**: Audiotools remote node name or node IP address or domain name
- **workflow xml**: A file path or a memory buffer to the workflow XML to execute
- **priority**: The submitted job priority

\

##### Outputs description

- **XML_result**: The workflow XML output
- **ATS_status_message**: The job status code

##### Operating Instructions

Specify a remote node or an IP address/domain name for the Audiotools server.\
Specify the workflow XML content in clear (non escaped).\
The referenced workflow XML content can be in a file. In this case, specify 'file' in 'Init from type' and the full filepath to the file in 'Workflow XML'. The filepath must be visible from Orchestrator.\
Or it can be in a memory buffer. In this case, specify 'buffer' in 'Init from type', don't put anything in 'Workflow XML' and map the XML content as 'Workflow XML' input. For example, the XML content can be built with a File Generator plugin in an earlier step.\
Specify a priority for the job to submit.\
In output, you'll get the Audiotools status code and the workflow XML output.\
The completed workflow jobs are removed from the Audiotools server. The failed workflow jobs are left on the server.

### MOG MXF Speedrail

![MOG MXF Speedrail icon](icons/MogMxfspeedrail.png){.plugin-icon}

his plug-in provides the ability to run a MOG workflow. It implements MOG mxfSPEEDRAIL v2.22 SOAP API.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.2 | Fix progress report display issue |
| 0.1.1 | Adding new flow name mog.speedrail.flows.MetadataCheckin |
| 0.1.0 | Adding new column, job name to help identify the jobs submitted uniquely |
| 0.0.3 | Adding percentage complete variable |
| 0.0.2 | Adding Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved MogMxfspeedrail configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved MogMxfspeedrail configured instance.

- **Remote Node**: The MOG server node name from the list of Remote Nodes.

- **Remote server ip address**: The MOG server node IP address or domain name.

- **Remote soap service port** : The MOG SOAP API service port (8731 by default).

- **Username**: The login name to authenticate against the MOG server.

- **Password**: The password for the login to the MOG server.

- **Flow name**: The type of workflow to execute.

- **XML payload**: The XML payload for the job submission request.

- **Polling frequency**: Interval in seconds to check for the job submission status.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the MogMxfspeedrail action template.

Changes in this list of inputs will override any value set in the action configuration/template.\
\

- **remote node or server address**: MOG remote node name or node IP address or domain name
- **user name**: login name to the MOG server
- **password**: password for the login to the MOG server
- **flow name**: type of workflow to execute
- **xml payload**: XML payload for the job submission request

\

##### Outputs description

- **XML_result**: The job submission XML output
- **MOG_error_message**: MOG error message in case of error

##### Operating Instructions

Specify a remote node or an IP address/domain name for the MOG server.\
Specify a username and password for authentication.\
Select the type of workflow to execute in the flow name field.\
Specify the XML payload for your specific type of workflow. This is the section within the CDATA delimiters, not including the delimiters. For example, if we consider a mog.speedrail.flows.transferplainmxf flow name, the XML starts with \<pf:TransferPlainMXF\> and ends with \</pf:TransferPlainMXF\>.\
In output, you'll get the XML content returned from the job submission and the MOG error message in case of error.\

### Unified packager

![Unified packager icon](icons/UnifiedPackager.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to create a server manifest file with mp4split tool.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.1   | Initial implementation                              |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved UnifiedPackager configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved UnifiedPackager configured instance.

- **Execution node**: The remote node where mp4split is installed.

- **Output file path**: The path of the output file.

- **Binary path**: The path of the mp4split executable.

- **License path**: The path of the license file.

- **Option (before output file path)**: The options added BEFORE the output file path.

- **Option (after output file path)**: The options added AFTER the output file path.

- **Input file path**: The path of an input file.

- **Track options**: The related track options to a specific input file.

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the UnifiedPackager action template.

- \
  **These inputs are mandatory:**
- **execution_node**: The remote node where mp4split is installed.
- **output_file_path**: The path of the output file.
  \
  **These inputs are optional:**
- **binary_path**: The path of the mp4split executable (default is 'mp4split').
- **license_path**: The path of the license file.
- **options_before_output_file_path**: The options added BEFORE the output file path.
- **options_after_output_file_path**: The options added AFTER the output file path.
- **input_files_data**: The list of input file paths and related track options expressed as a string with content such as \[\["/tmp/test1.mp4","--track_id=1"\],\["/tmp/test2.mp4","--track_id=2"\]\]

##### Outputs description

- **report**: The execution report.
- **status_code**: The mp4split status code.

##### Operating Instructions

mp4split will be executed on a remote node via an SSH connection.\
File path can be relative or absolute.\
License file can be set in a mp4split alias on remote node.\
mp4split command line used is constructed as follow : \<binary_path\> --license-key=\<license_path\> \<options_before_output_file_path\> -o \<output_file_path\> \<options_after_output_file_path\> \<input_file_path\> \<track_options\> \<input_file_path\> \<track_options\>
