### Slack notification

![Slack notification icon](icons/SlackNotification.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to interact with Slack

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.1 | Update to be compatible with latest Slack API (tested with Slack v4.47) |
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.0 | added attachments |
| 0.0.3 | regategorized |
| 0.0.2 | changed token type to password |
| 0.0.1 | Initial release. |

### How It Works

- Sends messages to Slack channels using the Slack API.
- Supports both user tokens and bot tokens for authentication.
- Allows posting messages as the authenticated user or as a bot.
- Supports rich message formatting with attachments, colors, titles, and text.
- Returns message metadata including timestamp and team information for tracking.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`SlackNotification`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the basic parameters:`
    - **`Name`**`: Enter a descriptive name for the Slack notification.`
    - **`Comments`**`: Add notes or description for this step.`
3.  `Set up Slack API connection:`
    - **`token`**`: Token used to authenticate Slack API (user token or bot token).`
    - **`slack url`**`: Provide the Slack URL.`
    - **`operation`**`: Currently only 'send message to a channel' operation is supported.`
4.  `Configure message parameters:`
    - **`channel`**`: Enter the channel name here (e.g., #general, #notifications).`
    - **`text_message`**`: Text message to be posted in the channel.`
    - **`as_user`**`: Pass true to post the message as the authenticated user, instead of as a bot. Defaults to false.`
5.  `Configure message attachments (optional):`
    - **`color`**`: The border color along the left side of a message attachment.`
    - **`title`**`: The title displayed as larger, bold text near the top of a message attachment.`
    - **`text_attachment`**`: The main text in a message attachment.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter | Example Value |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Workflow Completion Notification |
| **Comments** | Sends notification when workflow completes |
| **token** | xoxp-1234567891234-1234567891234-1234567891234-1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv |
| **slack url** | https://slack.com/ |
| **operation** | Send a Message |
| **channel** | \#my-channel |
| **text_message** | Workflow completed successfully |
| **as_user** | true |
| **color** | \#3AA3E3 |
| **title** | Workflow Status |
| **text_attachment** | All processing steps completed without errors |

### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | Display name for this Slack notification instance. |
| **Comments** | Notes or description for this step. |
| **token** | Token used to authenticate Slack API. Can be a user token or bot token. |
| **slack url** | The slack URL (e.g., https://slack.com/). |
| **operation** | The operation to perform. Currently only 'send message to a channel' is supported. |
| **channel** | The target channel name where the message will be posted (e.g., \#general, \#notifications). |
| **text_message** | The main text message to be posted in the channel. |
| **as_user** | Pass true to post the message as the authenticated user, instead of as a bot. Defaults to false. |
| **color** | The border color along the left side of a message attachment. Can use preset colors (good, warning, danger) or hex codes. |
| **title** | The title displayed as larger, bold text near the top of a message attachment. |
| **text_attachment** | The main text content in a message attachment. Supports markdown formatting. |

### Inputs Description

Changes in this list of inputs will override any values set in the action template.

**Mandatory Inputs:**

- **token**: Token used to authenticate Slack API (user token or bot token).
- **slack_url**: Provide the Slack workspace URL.
- **channel**: Enter the channel name here (e.g., \#general, \#notifications).
- **text_message**: Text message to be posted in the channel.

**Optional Inputs:**

- **attachments**: List of message attachments expressed as JSON-based array. Example: \[{ "color":"warning","title":"Message1","text":"Details1" }, { "color":"#FF0000","title":"Message2","text":"Details2","footer":"Signature" }\]

### Outputs Description

- **Team_Name**: Team name associated with the authentication token.
- **Team_Id**: Team ID for the authentication token.
- **Company_Url**: URL of the company/workspace the token belongs to.
- **Error_Message**: In case of error, this field stores the error cause.
- **Auth_Response_Received**: Raw response from authentication API call.
- **TS_Value**: Timestamp value of the sent message (used for message threading and updates).
- **Send_Text_Response**: Raw response from the send message API call.

### Supported Actions

None

### Dependencies

- Valid Slack workspace with appropriate permissions.
- Slack API token (User Token or Bot Token) with chat:write scope.
- Network connectivity to Slack API endpoints.
- Channel must exist and the bot/user must be a member of the target channel.

### Operating Instructions

#### Overview

The **SlackNotification** plugin enables workflows to send messages to Slack channels using the Slack API. Messages can be posted as the authenticated user or as a bot, and support rich formatting with attachments, colors, and titles.

#### Authentication Setup

- **Token Types**: The plugin supports both User Tokens (xoxp-) and Bot Tokens (xoxb-).
- **User Token**: Messages posted with user tokens appear as if sent by that user. Requires user-level permissions.
- **Bot Token**: Messages posted with bot tokens appear as sent by the bot. More suitable for automated notifications.
- **Required Scopes**: The token must have the `chat:write` scope at minimum. Additional scopes may be needed for advanced features.
- **Obtaining Tokens**: Create tokens in your Slack workspace settings under 'Apps' \> 'OAuth & Permissions'.

#### Channel Configuration

- **Channel Format**: Prefix public channels with \# (e.g., \#general, \#notifications).
- **Private Channels**: The bot or user must be invited to private channels before posting messages.
- **Direct Messages**: Use the user ID format (e.g., @U1234567890) to send direct messages.
- **Channel Validation**: Ensure the channel exists and the bot/user is a member to avoid errors.

#### Message Formatting

##### Basic Text Messages

- **text_message**: The primary message content. Supports Slack's mrkdwn formatting.
- **Markdown Support**: Use \*bold\*, \_italic\_, ~strikethrough~, and \`code\` formatting.
- **Links**: Format as \<https://example.com\|Link Text\> for hyperlinks.
- **Mentions**: Use \<@U1234567890\> to mention users or \<!channel\> to notify all channel members.

##### Message Attachments

Attachments provide rich formatting with color-coded borders, titles, and structured content.

- **JSON Format**: Attachments must be provided as a JSON array.
- **Color Options**: Use preset values (good, warning, danger) or hex codes (#FF0000).
- **Structure**: Each attachment can include color, title, text, and optional footer.
- **Multiple Attachments**: Include multiple attachment objects in the array for complex messages.

**Attachment Example:**

    [
      {
        "color": "good",
        "title": "Success",
        "text": "Workflow completed successfully"
      },
      {
        "color": "#FF0000",
        "title": "Error Details",
        "text": "No errors encountered",
        "footer": "Orchestrator Bot"
      }
    ]

#### Posting As User vs Bot

- **as_user = true**: Messages appear as sent by the authenticated user. (Required if using a user token)
- **as_user = false (default)**: Messages appear as sent by a bot.
- **Bot Customization**: When posting as bot, you can modify the display name in Slack app settings (App Home \> Display Information). The username parameter (available in \< v0.2.0 of this plugin) is deprecated in modern Slack API.
- **Use Case**: Use bot mode for system notifications, user mode for messages that should appear as from a specific person.

#### Sample Request

Here's a complete example configuration:

    channel: #plugin_test
    text_message: Workflow execution completed successfully
    username: Orchestrator Bot
    attachments: [
      {
        "color": "good",
        "title": "Execution Summary",
        "text": "All steps completed without errors"
      }
    ]

#### Output Processing

- **Error_Message**: Check this output first to determine if the message was sent successfully. Empty value indicates success.
- **TS_Value**: The timestamp of the sent message. Save this if you need to update or thread messages later.
- **Team_Name and Team_Id**: Useful for verifying which workspace the message was sent to.
- **Send_Text_Response**: Contains the full API response for debugging purposes.
- **Auth_Response_Received**: Contains authentication details, useful for troubleshooting token issues.

#### Common Color Values

- **good**: Green border (use for success messages).
- **warning**: Orange/yellow border (use for warnings).
- **danger**: Red border (use for errors or critical issues).
- **Hex codes**: Use any hex color code for custom colors (e.g., \#0000FF for blue).

#### Error Handling

- **Invalid Token**: Check Error_Message output for 'invalid_auth' or 'token_revoked' errors.
- **Channel Not Found**: Ensure the channel name is correct and includes the \# prefix for public channels.
- **Not in Channel**: The bot/user must be invited to the channel before posting. Check for 'not_in_channel' error.
- **Missing Scopes**: Verify the token has the required chat:write scope in Slack app settings.
- **Rate Limits**: Slack enforces rate limits. Space out notifications if sending many messages rapidly.

#### Best Practices

- Use bot tokens for automated notifications to keep user tokens secure.
- Always check the Error_Message output to verify successful message delivery.
- Save the TS_Value output if you need to reference or thread messages later.
- Use attachments for structured, visually distinct messages rather than plain text.
- Choose appropriate colors for attachments to convey message severity (good, warning, danger).
- Test messages in a development channel before implementing in production workflows.
- Configure bot display names in Slack app settings (App Home \> Display Information). The username parameter (available in \< v0.2.0 of this plugin) is deprecated in modern Slack API.
- Avoid sending too many messages in rapid succession to prevent rate limiting.
- Ensure the bot is invited to all target channels before deploying workflows.
- Store Slack tokens securely and rotate them periodically for security.

### User input

![User input icon](icons/UserInput.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin provides an interactive way to present information and collect data from users through web forms within the Orchestrator control application.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.8.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.7.0 | Added option to output all information about the user completing the task |
| 0.6.4 | Fixed wrong name when detecting exception in listing user assignments |
| 0.6.3 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.6.2 | Fixes backward compatibility with pre-0.6.0 version |
| 0.6.1 | Bumping up the version since I fixed the UI code |
| 0.6.0 | Add The user names into the db for export import purpose. |
| 0.5.1 | Added help file |
| 0.5.0 | Adds support for update push (instead of polling) |
| 0.4.8 | Fix email notifications when no users are selected |
| 0.4.7 | New icon |
| 0.4.6 | Fix for additional email fields (to, cc, bcc) to be considered |
| 0.4.4 | Fix UI glitch (removing one input, removed all) |
| 0.4.3 | Making event description optional runtime input - doing it right |
| 0.4.2 | Making event description optional runtime input |
| 0.4.1 | Sets polling frequency to a default, switching UI to localtime |
| 0.4.0 | Adds support for required user input fields |
| 0.3.3 | Uses default mailer if field is left blank (requires 1.9.8 00010) |
| 0.3.2 | Isolates notification failures |
| 0.3.1 | Fixed problem in exposing the event description and correctly resolve the task description |
| 0.3.1 | Prevents failure of all notifications if one address is corrupted |
| 0.3.0 | Adds email notification |
| 0.2.9 | Fixes glitch in 0.2.8 preventing the outputs from being set |
| 0.2.8 | Only prepare actual inputs (ignore passed-thru variables) |
| 0.2.7 | Deghost active assignment before generating new ones |
| 0.2.6 | Clean {} null assignment thru migration |
| 0.2.5 | supports for legacy {} null assignment |
| 0.2.4 | improves backward compatibility |
| 0.2.3 | adds user and role selectors |
| 0.2.2 | adds support for file attachments |
| 0.2.1 | moved the templates directory out of install path |
| 0.2.0 | provides option to use as trigger |
| 0.1.2 | added input provider output |
| 0.1.1 | added event_description field (payload) |
| 0.1.0 | stable version used in IBC demo |

### How It Works

- Creates interactive HTML-based forms that appear in the Orchestrator Tasks tab for human users to complete and provide input.
- Supports configurable mandatory and optional inputs with various data types, allowing flexible data collection from users.
- Enables workflow pause functionality where execution waits for user completion of requested data before proceeding to the next step.
- Provides comprehensive email notification system with customizable recipients, subjects, and message content for task assignments.
- Offers user and group assignment capabilities with complete user information output options including names and email addresses.

### Configuration Instructions

1.  `Add a `**`User Input`**` step to your workflow.`
2.  `Configure the basic task settings:`
    - **`Event description`**`: Provide a clear description for the user task (mandatory).`
    - **`View template`**`: Select custom form template from available options.`
    - **`On-going trigger`**`: Check to keep plugin execution ongoing if needed.`
3.  `Set up data collection requirements:`
    - **`Mandatory requested data`**`: Define required outputs users must provide.`
    - **`Optional requested data`**`: Define optional outputs for additional information.`
    - **`Mandatory inputs`**`: Configure required runtime inputs with data types.`
    - **`Optional inputs`**`: Configure optional runtime inputs with data types.`
4.  `Configure user assignment and notifications:`
    - **`Users`**`: Assign specific users to the task.`
    - **`Groups`**`: Assign user groups to the task.`
    - **`Send notification email`**`: Enable email notifications with server and content settings.`

### Example Configuration

| Parameter | Example Value |
|----|----|
| **Event description** | Review and approve transcoded media file |
| **View template** | \_media_review.html.erb |
| **On-going trigger** | unchecked |
| **Provide all user informations in outputs?** | checked |
| **Send notification email** | checked |
| **Mail server** | smtp.company.com |
| **Subject** | Media Review Required - Workflow Task |
| **Email body** | Review the transcoded media file and provide approval. |
| **Users** | john.doe, jane.smith |
| **Groups** | media-reviewers, supervisors |

### Saved Parameters Description

#### Basic Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Event description** | The description of the user task that gets created on the tasks tab. |
| **On-going trigger** | The On-going trigger checkbox is to be checked if the plugin execution is to be kept ongoing. |
| **View template** | The template of the form that should be displayed when user clicks on the task. Template files should be located in '/opt/aspera/orchestrator/config/orchestrator/actions_config/user_inputs' and need to have their name start with '\_'. |
| **Provide all user informations in outputs?** | If checked the action will return complete information about the user completing the tasks, including name, email address etc. |
| **Mandatory inputs** | Multiple runtime mandatory inputs can be created by adding Mandatory inputs. Input name and the data type should be selected for each input that is added. |
| **Optional inputs** | Multiple runtime optional inputs can be created by adding Optional inputs. Input name and the data type should be selected for each input that is added. |
| **Mandatory requested data** | Multiple mandatory inputs/data can be created by adding Mandatory requested data. Input name and the data type should be selected for each input that is added. These inputs act as plugin outputs and execution will not move to next step until these fields are provided with data. |
| **Optional requested data** | Multiple optional inputs/data can be created by adding Optional requested data. Requested data name and the data type should be selected for each input that is added. These inputs act as plugin outputs. |

#### Email Notification Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Send notification email** | This checkbox is to be checked if email notification is to be sent upon execution of this plugin. |
| **Mail server** | Mail server that is to be used to send email notification. |
| **Subject** | Subject line of the email that will be sent. |
| **Email body** | Email body of the email that will be sent. |
| **Additional to destinations** | Additional 'to' email address apart from the users/groups (discussed below) that are selected. |
| **Additional cc destinations** | Additional 'cc' email address apart from the users/groups (discussed below) that are selected. |
| **Additional bcc destinations** | Additional 'bcc' email address apart from the users/groups (discussed below) that are selected. |

#### User Assignment Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Users** | All the users that are supposed to be assigned with the user task that will be created by the execution of this plugin. |
| **Groups** | All the groups that are supposed to be assigned with the user task that will be created by the execution of this plugin. |

### Inputs Description

Event description is a mandatory field. The inputs created by the Mandatory inputs field are mandatory. If email notification is selected, Email subject and Email body are mandatory fields and should be populated.

- **Event description**: Required description of the user task (mandatory).
- **Mandatory inputs**: Runtime inputs marked as required with specified data types.
- **Email subject**: Subject line for notification emails (mandatory if email notifications enabled).
- **Email body**: Content for notification emails (mandatory if email notifications enabled).

### Outputs Description

- **Mandatory requested data**: Mandatory requested data will appear as outputs and will serve as outputs to next step.
- **Optional requested data**: Optional requested data will appear as outputs and will serve as outputs to next step.

### Operating Instructions

- The User Input plugin is designed to display and request information from human users through HTML-based forms that appear in the Orchestrator GUI's "Tasks" tab.
- Custom forms must be located in the folder `/opt/aspera/orchestrator/config/orchestrator/actions_config/user_inputs/` with filenames that begin with "\_" and end with ".html.erb" (example: `_myform.html.erb`).
- These forms will appear in the dropdown for 'View template' selection during plugin configuration.
- Example use case: A workflow that takes an input file and performs transcoding can include a UserInput plugin step to enable users to view the transcoded media and validate it. Upon validation, the workflow continues (with validation parameters as part of Mandatory requested data).
- Form customization is unlimited - server-side processing can be implemented with embedded Ruby (e.g., database access, file parsing), client-side processing with JavaScript (e.g., REST requests, HTML form generation), and GUI customization using standard web technologies like HTML5 and CSS3.

### Dependencies

- Custom form templates must be properly placed in the designated directory with correct naming conventions.
- Email notification requires a configured and accessible mail server for sending task notifications.
- Assigned users and groups must exist in the Orchestrator system for proper task assignment.
- The aspweb user must have read/write permissions for the user_inputs configuration directory.

## Virus Scan

### ClamAV check

![ClamAV check icon](icons/ClamavCheck.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to check a file, folder or set of files for viruses using the clamAV utility.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.3.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.2.1 | Fix bug preventing to run concurrent clamav scans |
| 0.2.0 | Bug fix - typo times method |
| 0.1.9 | Fixing the process_output to match clamdscan output |
| 0.1.8 | Attemps file moves 3 times and errors out in case of failure to move |
| 0.1.7 | Changed the Category to Virus Scan |
| 0.1.6 | Fixes file-base option for move to clean and quarantine directories |
| 0.1.5 | Provides system path for service command and only performs validation on error |
| 0.1.3 | Adapted status check for calmv-daemon service |
| 0.1.2 | Adapted status check for non US OS |
| 0.1.1 | 2015-05-23 PM - Correcting status check for CentOS 7 |
| 0.1.0 | 2015-03-06 PM - Better distinguish the failures that occur on a ClamAV scan |
| 0.0.9 | 2015-03-06 PM - Check for CLAMAV status before scan |
| 0.0.8 | 2015-02-17 ML - Fix display name for consistency |
| 0.0.7 | 2015-02-17 ML - New icon |
| 0.0.6 | -\> Fixed the command_line_get method so that MAX File Size to be entered into the command line call |
| 0.0.5 | -\> Fixed the missing method for truncate_get |
| 0.0.4 | -\> Corrected the clean file move, quarantine move and truncate functionality to use remote calls instead of assuming local |
| 0.0.3 | -\> Adds help file |
| 0.0.2 | -\> Fixes support for dynamic node |
| 0.0.1 | -\> Initial release. Does not support add hoc node definition |

#### How It Works

- Performs **virus and malware scanning** using ClamAV antivirus software on remote nodes.
- Executes ClamAV command-line tools (**clamscan** or **clamdscan**) via SSH connection.
- Scans individual files or entire directories for viruses, malware, and threats.
- Automatically **moves clean files** to staging folder if configured.
- Automatically **quarantines infected files** to isolation folder if configured.
- Supports **file size limits** to skip scanning of very large files.
- Can **truncate infected files** to zero-byte placeholders to obliterate infections.
- Maintains **directory structure** when moving files using configurable file base path.
- Returns detailed reports including **clean files, infected files, and infection descriptions**.

#### Configuration Instructions

1.  Create a new action step using the **ClamavCheck** plugin.
2.  Fill out the following fields:
    - : The name used to identify a saved ClamAV Check configured instance.
    - : Comments about this saved ClamAV Check configured instance.
    - : The node where the ClamAV command line operation must be executed (i.e., the node where the ClamAV application is installed).
    - : The path to locate ClamAV command line executable, typically clamscan or clamdscan. Not needed if the binary folder is in the system path for the account used to remote connect on the specified server.
    - : The path to the file or directory to be scanned. Can use dynamic attributes (e.g., '/ingest/\<%=file_basename%\>').
    - : If provided, files that pass the AV scan will be moved to this folder. The file base is used to maintain tree structure if provided.
    - : If provided, infected files will be moved to this folder. The file base is used to maintain tree structure if provided.
    - : If provided, it is applied to clean file staging and quarantine folders when files are moved. Represents the part of the full path that is not kept (e.g., file base '/ingest' removes this prefix when moving files).
    - : Indicates whether the step should exit in failure or complete if an infection is found.
    - : Specifies whether infected files should be reduced to zero-byte files, obliterating the infection but keeping a file placeholder.
    - : Specifies a maximum size over which files won't be submitted to the scanner (e.g., '100M', '1G').
    - : Selects whether to block or pass files that are skipped because they are above the maximum size set.
    - : Any optional parameters supported by the virus scanner command line binary. These options will be passed on the command line as first arguments, after the command line executable.

#### Example Configuration

This is an example configuration for the ClamavCheck plugin to scan incoming media files with automatic quarantine.

| Parameter | Value |
|----|----|
| Name | Scan Incoming Media Files |
| Comments | Virus scan all incoming media files before processing |
| Execution Node | SecurityServer01 |
| Clamav binary path | /usr/bin/clamdscan |
| To be scanned file or directory path | /ingest/\<%=filename%\> |
| Clean file staging folder | /clean |
| Quarantine folder | /quarantine |
| File base | /ingest |
| Fail on infection? | Selected |
| Truncate infected files? | Not selected |
| Scanned maximum file size | 500M |
| Action if above max size | Pass |
| Additional Command line arguments | --verbose |

#### Saved Parameters Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| **Name** | The name used to identify a saved ClamAV Check configured instance. |
| **Comments** | Comments about this saved ClamAV Check configured instance. |
| **Execution Node** | The node where the ClamAV command line operation must be executed (i.e., the node where the ClamAV application is installed). |
| **Clamav binary path** | The path to locate ClamAV command line executable, typically clamscan or clamdscan. Not needed if the binary folder is in the system path for the account used to remote connect on the specified server. |
| **To be scanned file or directory path** | The path to the file or directory to be scanned. Can use dynamic attributes (e.g., '/ingest/\<%=file_basename%\>'). |
| **Clean file staging folder** | If provided, files that pass the AV scan will be moved to this folder. The file base is used to maintain tree structure if provided. |
| **Quarantine folder** | If provided, infected files will be moved to this folder. The file base is used to maintain tree structure if provided. |
| **File base** | If provided, it is applied to clean file staging and quarantine folders when files are moved. Represents the part of the full path that is not kept (e.g., file base '/ingest' removes this prefix when moving files). |
| **Fail on infection?** | Indicates whether the step should exit in failure or complete if an infection is found. |
| **Truncate infected files?** | Specifies whether infected files should be reduced to zero-byte files, obliterating the infection but keeping a file placeholder. |
| **Scanned maximum file size** | Specifies a maximum size over which files won't be submitted to the scanner (e.g., '100M', '1G'). |
| **Action if above max size** | Selects whether to block or pass files that are skipped because they are above the maximum size set. |
| **Additional Command line arguments** | Any optional parameters supported by the virus scanner command line binary. These options will be passed on the command line as first arguments, after the command line executable. |

#### Inputs Description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the ClamAV Check action template as the fields for the paths and options all support dynamic inputs.

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| *Input_file_path* | By default, a file name must be provided as an input. However, if the 'To be scanned file or directory path' field is populated, then instead all the dynamic variables listed in it are mandatory and must be of type string. |
| *Options* | If 'Additional Command line arguments' is left blank, an optional 'Options' input is provided. However, if it is populated, all the dynamic variables listed in it will be mandatory and must be of type string. |

#### Outputs Description

| Parameter | Description |
|----|----|
| Clean_files | An array containing the path to the files that passed scanning |
| Infected_files | An array containing the path to the files that failed scanning |
| Infection_report | The text description of the infections found reported by the scanner, if any |
| Output_file_path | The path to the file submitted for scanning where it lands after scanning (can be moved if clean staging or quarantine directories are specified) |

#### Supported Actions

None

#### Dependencies

ClamAV must be installed on the execution node. The connection to the remote server is done over SSH, so ensure the target server has SSH enabled.

#### Operating Instructions

This action executes the specified file ClamAV scanning operation on a remote node where a ClamAV command line is available. The connection to the remote server is done over SSH.

**clamscan vs. clamdscan\**
There are two different command line executables available:

- **clamscan (Standalone Scanner):**
  - Stateless executable - fully loaded every time it runs
  - Results in a few seconds delay for each file scan
  - No daemon required - simpler setup
  - Suitable for occasional scanning or batch operations
  - Command: `/usr/bin/clamscan`
- **clamdscan (Daemon Client):**
  - Client that communicates with clamd daemon running in background
  - No load time delay - much faster for frequent scans
  - Requires clamd daemon to be running
  - Suitable for high-volume, real-time scanning
  - Command: `/usr/bin/clamdscan`

**File Movement and Quarantine Logic\**

- **File Base Path Concept:**
- The file base represents the part of the full path that is not kept when moving files.
- **Example:**
  - Full file path: `/ingest/sub1/sub2/file.ext`
  - File base: `/ingest`
  - Quarantine folder: `/quarantine`
  - Infected file moved to: `/quarantine/sub1/sub2/file.ext`
  - The tree structure `sub1/sub2` is preserved
- **Clean File Movement:**
  - If 'Clean file staging folder' is specified, files that pass scanning are moved there
  - Original directory structure is maintained using file base
  - Example: Clean to `/clean/sub1/sub2/file.ext`
- **Infected File Quarantine:**
  - If 'Quarantine folder' is specified, infected files are isolated there
  - Original directory structure is maintained using file base
  - Prevents accidental access to infected files
  - Example: Quarantine to `/quarantine/sub1/sub2/file.ext`

**Common ClamAV Command Line Options\**

- **Scanning Options:**
  - `--recursive` or `-r` - Scan directories recursively
  - `--infected` or `-i` - Only print infected files
  - `--remove` - Remove infected files
  - `--move=DIRECTORY` - Move infected files to specified directory
  - `--exclude=REGEX` - Exclude files matching regular expression
  - `--exclude-dir=REGEX` - Exclude directories matching regular expression
- **Output Options:**
  - `--verbose` or `-v` - Be verbose
  - `--quiet` - Only output error messages
  - `--no-summary` - Disable summary at end of scanning
- **Performance Options:**
  - `--max-filesize=SIZE` - Files larger than SIZE won't be scanned
  - `--max-scansize=SIZE` - Max amount of data to scan for each file
  - `--max-recursion=NUMBER` - Max directory recursion level

**Common Use Cases\**

- **Incoming file quarantine:** Scan files as they arrive and quarantine any infections before processing.
- **Media file validation:** Ensure video/audio files are virus-free before transcoding or distribution.
- **Archive scanning:** Scan archived content before retrieval or restoration.
- **Upload validation:** Scan user-uploaded files before storing in production systems.
- **Periodic scanning:** Regular scans of file repositories to detect new threats.
- **Pre-delivery scanning:** Scan files before delivery to external parties or clients.
- **Compliance scanning:** Meet security compliance requirements with automated AV scanning.

**Best Practices\**

- Use **clamdscan with daemon** for high-volume scanning to avoid load-time delays.
- Keep virus definitions **up to date** with regular freshclam updates (automate with cron/scheduled task).
- Configure **clean file staging and quarantine folders** for automatic file segregation.
- Set appropriate **maximum file size** limits to avoid scanning extremely large files that tie up resources.
- Use **file base paths** to maintain directory structure when moving files.
- Enable **'Fail on infection?'** for critical workflows where infected files should halt processing.
- Implement **quarantine monitoring** to review and analyze infected files.
- Ensure files are **accessible from the scanning node** (local or network share).
- Test ClamAV commands **manually via SSH** before configuring in Orchestrator.
- Monitor **clamd daemon status** if using clamdscan to ensure continuous availability.

**Important Notes\**

- **File Accessibility:** This action plugin does not perform any file movement before the scan. The specified file must be accessible from the execution node (local path or network share).
- **Virus Database Updates:** Keep ClamAV virus definitions current using the `freshclam` command (typically automated via a cron job).
- **Daemon Requirement:** If using `clamdscan`, ensure the `clamd` daemon is running.
- **Path Formats:** Provide explicit paths to ClamAV binaries to avoid configuration and PATH-related issues.
- **Windows Paths:** Use Unix-style paths when executing on Windows nodes (forward slashes, optional Cygwin POSIX format).
- **SSH Shell:** On Windows, configure the SSH user with a `/bin/sh` shell, not `/bin/switch`.
- **File Base Requirement:** File base is applied only when clean staging or quarantine folders are configured.
- **Truncation Behavior:** Truncating infected files removes the infection while retaining a placeholder, which is useful for audit and tracking.

**Troubleshooting\**

- **ClamAV not found:** Verify ClamAV is installed and provide an explicit binary path. Use `which clamscan` on Linux.
- **clamd connection refused:** Ensure the `clamd` daemon is running when using `clamdscan`: `sudo systemctl start clamav-daemon`.
- **File not accessible:** Verify the file exists and is accessible from the execution node. Check file permissions.
- **Outdated virus definitions:** Update the virus database using `sudo freshclam`.
- **Permission denied on quarantine:** Ensure the SSH user has write permissions for the quarantine and clean staging folders.
- **SSH connection failure:** Verify the Remote Node configuration and confirm the SSH service is running.
- **Windows path issues:** Use Unix-style paths with forward slashes, for example `C:/path/file.ext`.
- **Large file timeouts:** Configure an appropriate maximum file size or increase SSH timeouts for very large files.
- **False positives:** Review infection reports and configure ClamAV exclusions if required.

**Understanding ClamAV Output\**

ClamAV provides detailed output about scan results:\
\

    /path/to/file.txt: OK
    /path/to/infected.exe: Trojan.Generic FOUND
    /path/to/suspicious.doc: Heuristics.Phishing.Email FOUND

    ----------- SCAN SUMMARY -----------
    Infected files: 2
    Time: 5.234 sec (0 m 5 s)

**Output indicators:**

- **OK** – File is clean
- **FOUND** – Virus or malware detected
- **Infection names** – Indicate the type of threat (Trojan, Heuristic, Worm, etc.)
- **Scan summary** – Overall scan statistics

\
The plugin parses this output to populate the `Clean_files` and `Infected_files` arrays and the `Infection_report` text.

### ICAP virus scan

![ICAP virus scan icon](icons/IcapVirusScan.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in communicates with an ICAP enabled virus scanner engine to quarantine infected files.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.1.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.9 | Making plugin compatible with 3.0.x |
| 0.0.8 | Added styling to parameter help text. |
| 0.0.7 | Changed category to Virus Scan |
| 0.0.6 | Adding help file |
| 0.0.5 | 0.0.5 Adds display name |
| 0.0.4 | 0.0.4 adds generation of test virus file |
| 0.0.3 | 0.0.3 modifies process loop to avoid lock-up |
| 0.0.2 | 0.0.2 genericized to work with symantec icap client ssecls and ssecls.jar |
| 0.0.1 | 0.0.1 initial creation works with LINUX icap-client command |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: The name used to identify a saved IcapVirusScan configured instance.

- **Comments**: Some comments about this saved IcapVirusScan configured instance.

- **ICAP server address**: The address of the server hosting the virus scan

- **Client Command**: The command to be executed can be entered here

- **Result Parsing Command**: The result is parsed usually with the return code 0/1. If a custom parsing needs to be done, thats entered here

- **List infection command**: Infection found is identified by matching the regex "X-Infection-Found". If custom identification code is needed that can be entered here

- **Keep trigger on going**: This field is to keep looking for multiple files as soon as 1 is done

- **Scan Directory**: The directory from where the files has to be scanned

- **To-be-scanned filename pattern**: The pattern of the files to look out for in the given directory

- **Filename base (leading part to remove from full name)**: The base name of the file to be scanned

- **Use Regex Pattern matching?**: By default, GLOB matching is used. By checking this REGEX can be used instead for more complex patterns (i.e. 'movie\_.\*.(avi\|mpg)')

- **Max file size (in bytes)**: The maximum size of the file that should be considered

- **Action for files above max size**: Choose beterrn Pass/Fail/Leave/Error defaultly without called the virus_scan at all

- **Scan frequency (seconds)**: The frequency for looking at the incoming files

- **Triggers on partial file?** : If checked the scan will also be triggered on partial file

- **Clean directory**: The directory to which the clean files have to be moved after the scan

- **Quarantine directory** : The directory to which the infected files have to be moved after the scan

- **Scan suffix**: The suffix to be appended to the file while scanning(example .scanning)

- **Truncate infected file to 0 bytes?**: Truncates the infected files to 0 bytes if checked

- **Infection manifest directory**: The directory into which the infection manifest file is written to

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the IcapVirusScan action template.

\

\

##### Outputs description

- **Clean file list**: List of files that are found clean
- **Quarantined_file_list**: List of files that are found infected
- **Infection_manifest**: This gives the location to the manifest file if asked for

##### Operating Instructions

This Plugin helps to stream data to a server using the ICAP protocol for virus scanning. (Verify if the anti-virus software being used supports ICAP before using this plugin)\
Basic Example:\
Server Address: 10.0.142.20\
File_list : \*.txt\
Scan Directory: /tmp\
Keep trigger on-going? : checked\
this will scan all the files under the /tmp folder that has the "txt" extension.\

### McAfee virus check

![McAfee virus check icon](icons/McAfeeVirusCheck.png){.plugin-icon}

This action plug-in provides the ability to check a file, folder or set of files for viruses using the McAfee anti-virus application.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.2.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.1.9 | Fixes UTF-8 encoding in response |
| 0.1.8 | Changed Category |
| 0.1.7 | Added styling to help text. |
| 0.1.6 | Add Remote Node Dependency |
| 0.1.5 | For windows, ensure that file is recognized as infected even when exit code is 0, loosen matching of file pattern |
| 0.1.4 | Adds display name |
| 0.1.3 | New icon |
| 0.1.2 | -\> Formatted lines in help file |
| 0.1.1 | -\> Adding double quotes for all filenames in the command line calls to escape whitespace. Corrects commands for Windows and Unix |
| 0.1.0 | -\> Extended support for file size limit, check for node type, help file, move to clean folder, move to quarantine folder |
| 0.0.1 | -\> Initial release. Only single file support for McAfee in single file mode |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Name**: Name of the template

- **Comments**: (optional) Provide comments to define the template

- **Execution Node**: Select node from the dropdown which has the McAfee antivirus installed on. Visit Remote Nodes page for node creation.

- **Node Type**: Check the box if the target server is Unix. Else it will get considered or Windows.

- **Binary Path**: Path to the McAfee scan utility

- **Input File Path**: Full path to the file which has to be scanned

- **Quarantine Folder**: If provided, McAfee will move the provided file to this folder, if it fails virus check.

- **Delete Infected File**: If checked, McAfee will delete the provided file, if it fails virus check.

- **Clean Folder**: If provided, Orchestrator will move the provided file to this folder, if it passes virus check.

- **File base**: If provided, the part of the file name will be removed and the rest of it will be created under target folder

- **Fail on infection**: Fail the step if the virus check fails. By default, step will compelte

- **Truncate Infected File**:

- **Options**: Refer to the list of options below and provide extra options.

- **Max Scan Size**: Ignore files above the specified size (in MB)

##### Input description

The list of inputs depends on the configuration of the McAfeeAntiVirusScan action template.

- **Execution Node**: If a 'Execution Node' is not provided in the configuration, it can be provided as an runtime input.
- **Input file path**: If a 'Input file path' is not provided in the configuration, it can also be provided as an runtime input.
  Also, if dynamic attributes are used in the 'Watch directory' or 'Folder pattern', they will be required inputs typed as strings.\

##### Outputs description

- **Clean_files**: Returns and array with full path of clean files
- **Infected_files**: Returns and array with full path of infected files
- **Scan_report**: Returns a report of the McAfee virus scan execution on provided file
- **Scan_Summary**: Returns a summary of the McAfee virus scan execution on provided file
- **Output_file_path**: Returns and array with full path of clean files

##### Operating Instructions

This plugin does not support value overwrites: if a value is specified in the template it will not be available as input of the step at runtime.\
\
\
**McAfee VirusScan Windows Command Line options:**\
\
/? : Display this help screen.\
\
/AD : Scan all drives (not removable media).\
\
/ADL : Scan all local drives (not removable media).\
\
/ADN : Scan all network drives.\
\
/AFC=\<ache size\> : Set the Size(in MB) of the Internal Cache Used When Decompressing Archive Files.\
\
/ALL : Scan all files regardless of filename extension.\
\
/ALLOLE : Treat all files as compound/OLE regardless of extension.\
\
/ANALYZE : Turn on heuristic analysis for programs and macros.\
\
/APPEND : Append to report file rather than overwriting.\
\
/APPENDBAD : Append to bad file rather than overwriting.\
\
/ASCII : Display filenames as ASCII text.\
\
/BADLIST=\<filename\> : Filename and path for bad list log file.\
\
/BOOT : Scan boot sector and master boot record Only.\
\
/CHECKLIST=\<filename\> : Scan list of files contained in \<filename\>.\
\
/CLEAN : Attempt to clean infected files.\
\
/CONTACTFILE=\<filename\> : Display contents of \<filename\> when a virus is found.\
\
/DAM : Remove all macros from infected MS Office files.\
\
/DEL : Delete infected files except archive files.\
\
/DOHSM : Scan migrated files(hierarchical storage management).\
\
/DRIVER=\<dir\> : Directory specifying location of DAT files.\
\
/EXCLUDE=\<filename\> : Do not scan files/directories listed in \<filename\>.\
\
/EXTENSIONS : Scan defaults & user extension list.\
\
/EXTLIST : List file-extensions scanned by default.\
\
/EXTRA=\<filename\> : Specify the full path and file name of any extra.dat file.\
\
/FAM : Find all macros - not just infected macros. Used with /DAM will remove all macros.\
\
/FDC : Force digital signature check.\
\
/FREQUENCY=\<hours\> : Do not scan \<hours\> after the previous scan.\
\
/HELP : Displays this help.\
\
/HTML=\<filename\> : Create and specify a HTML report file.\
\
/LOAD=\<filename\> : Load options from \<filename\>.\
\
/LOUD : Include all scanned files in the /REPORT file.\
\
/MAILBOX : Scan inside plain text mailboxes.\
\
/MANALYZE : Turn on macro heuristics.\
\
/MANY : Scan many floppy diskettes.\
\
/MAXFILESIZE=\<size\> : Examine Only those files smaller than the specified size(in MB).\
\
/MEMSIZE=\<size\> : File size(in KB) to load into memory for scanning limited by a maximum file size defaulting to 1MB.\
\
/MIME : Scan inside MIME, UUE, XXE and BinHex files.\
\
/MOVE=\<dir\> : Move infected file into directory \<dir\>, preserving path.\
\
/NOBKSEM : Prevent scanning of files that are normally protected.\
\
/NOBOOT : Do not scan boot sectors.\
\
/NOBREAK : Disable Ctrl-C / Ctrl-Break during scanning.\
\
/NOCOMP : Do not scan self extracting executables by default.\
\
/NOD : Don't switch into /ALL mode when repairing.\
\
/NODDA : Do not scan boot sectors.\
\
/NODECRYPT : Don't scan password-protected MS Office documents.\
\
/NODOC : Do not scan MS Office files.\
\
/NOEXPIRE : Disable data files expiration date notice.\
\
/NOJOKES : Do not alert on joke files.\
\
/NOMEM : Do not scan memory for viruses.\
\
/NORECALL : Do not move files from remote storage into local storage after scanning.\
\
/NORENAME : Do not rename infected files that cannot be cleaned.\
\
/NOSCRIPT : Do not scan files that contain HTML, JavaScript, Visual Basic, or Script Component Type Libraries.\
\
/PANALYZE : Turn on program heuristics.\
\
/PAUSE : Pause at end of each screen page.\
\
/PLAD : Preserve the last-accessed time and date for files that are scanned.\
\
/PROGRAM : Scan for potentially unwanted applications.\
\
/RECURSIVE : Examine any subdirectories in addition to the specified target directory.\
\
/REPORT=\<filename\> : Report names of viruses found into \<filename\>.\
\
/RPTALL : Include all scanned files in the /REPORT file.\
\
/RPTCOR : Include corrupted files in /REPORT file.\
\
/RPTERR : Include errors in /REPORT file.\
\
/RPTOBJECTS : Reports number of objects at all levels scanned in summary.\
\
/SECURE : Equivalent to Analyse, doall, unzip.\
\
/SHOWCOMP : Report any files that are packaged.\
\
/SILENT : Disable all screen output.\
\
/STREAMS : Scan inside NTFS streams (NT & DATAPOL Only).\
\
/SUB : Examine any subdirectories in addition to the specified target directory.\
\
/THREADS=\<nn\> : Set scan thread count.\
\
/TIMEOUT=\<seconds\> : Set the maximum time to spend scanning any one file.\
\
/UNZIP : Scan inside archive files, such as those saved in ZIP, LHA, PKarc, ARJ, TAR, CHM, and RAR.\
\
/VERSION : Display the scanner's version number.\
\
/VIRLIST : Display virus list.\
\
/WINMEM\[=\<pid\>\] : If pid given scans the Windows Process with Process ID \<pid\> otherwise scans all Windows Processes.\
\
/XMLPATH=\<filename\> : Filename and path for XML log file.\
\
\
\

\
--? : Display this help screen.\

\

\

\
--EXTENSIONS : Scan defaults & user extension list.\
\
--EXTLIST : List file-extensions scanned by default.\
\
--EXTRA=\<filename\> : Specify the full path and file name of any extra.dat file.\
\
--FAM : Find all macros - not just infected macros. Used with /DAM will remove all macros.\
\
--FILE=\<filename\> : Scan list of files contained in \<filename\>.\
\

--FLOPPYB : Scan the boot sector of the disk in drive B.\
\
--FREQUENCY=\<hours\> : Do not scan \<hours\> after the previous scan.\

--HTML=\<filename\> : Create and specify a HTML report file.\
\
--IGNORE-COMPRESSED : Do not scan self extracting executables by default.\
\
--IGNORE-LINKS : Do not resolve any symbolic links and do not scan the link targets.\
\
--LOAD=\<filename\> : Load options from \<filename\>.\
\
--LOUD : Include all scanned files in the /REPORT file.\
\
--MACRO-HEURISTICS : Turn on macro heuristics.\
\
--MAILBOX : Scan inside plain text mailboxes.\
\
--MANALYZE : Turn on macro heuristics.\
\
--MAXFILESIZE=\<size\> : Examine Only those files smaller than the specified size(in MB).\
\
--MEMSIZE=\<size\> : File size(in KB) to load into memory for scanning limited by a maximum file size defaulting to 1MB.\
\
--MIME : Scan inside MIME, UUE, XXE and BinHex files.\
\
--MOVE=\<dir\> : Move infected file into directory \<dir\>, preserving path.\
\
--NOBOOT : Do not scan boot sectors.\
\
--NOCOMP : Do not scan self extracting executables by default.\
\
--NOD : Don't switch into /ALL mode when repairing.\
\
--NODECRYPT : Don't scan password-protected MS Office documents.\
\
--NODOC : Do not scan MS Office files.\
\
--NOEXPIRE : Disable data files expiration date notice.\
\
--NOJOKES : Do not alert on joke files.\
\
--NORENAME : Do not rename infected files that cannot be cleaned.\
\
--NOSCRIPT : Do not scan files that contain HTML, JavaScript, Visual Basic, or Script Component Type Libraries.\
\
--ONE-FILE-SYSTEM : Scan an entire directory tree without scanning mounted file systems.\
\
--PANALYZE : Turn on program heuristics.\
\
--PAUSE : Pause at end of each screen page.\
\
--PLAD : Preserve the last-accessed time and date for files that are scanned.\
\
--PROGRAM : Scan for potentially unwanted applications.\
\
--RECURSIVE : Examine any subdirectories in addition to the specified target directory.\
\
--REPORT=\<filename\> : Report names of viruses found into \<filename\>.\
\
--RPTALL : Include all scanned files in the /REPORT file.\
\
--RPTCOR : Include corrupted files in /REPORT file.\
\
--RPTERR : Include errors in /REPORT file.\
\
--RPTOBJECTS : Reports number of objects at all levels scanned in summary.\
\
--SECURE : Equivalent to Analyse, doall, unzip.\
\
--SELECTED : Look for infections in any file that has execute permissions.\
\
--SHOWCOMP : Report any files that are packaged.\
\
--SILENT : Disable all screen output.\
\
--SUB : Examine any subdirectories in addition to the specified target directory.\
\
--SUMMARY : Produce a summary of the scan.\
\
--THREADS=\<nn\> : Set scan thread count.\
\
--TIMEOUT=\<seconds\> : Set the maximum time to spend scanning any one file.\
\
--UNZIP : Scan inside archive files, such as those saved in ZIP, LHA, PKarc, ARJ, TAR, CHM, and RAR.\
\
--VERBOSE : Display a progress summary during the scan.\
\
--VERSION : Display the scanner's version number.\
\
--VIRLIST : Display virus list.\
\
--VIRUS-LIST : Display virus list.\
\
--XMLPATH=\<filename\> : Filename and path for XML log file.\
\

### Opswat metadefender

![Opswat metadefender icon](icons/OpswatMetadefender.png){.plugin-icon}

Integration with Opswat Metadefender

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.1   | Initial implementation                              |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **OPSWat Server Address**: OPSWAT Metadefender server address

- **Port**: OPSWAT Metadefender server port, default is 8008

- **apikey**: Optional, APIKEY to use with scan requests. Default is empty API ey

- **username**: Optional, username to login to Metadefender. The resulting session will be used for scan requests. Default is empty username

- **password**: Optiona, password to login to Metadefender. Default is empty password

- **Fail if infected file found**: Causes the step to fail if an infected file was flaged by Metadefender

- **files**: List of files, separated by the separator below, to scan

- **separator**: Optional, by default is is a comma

- **user_agent**: Optional, user_agent value will be used in all requests headers to Metadefender. Default is Aspera Orchestrator

- **rule**: Optional, Metadefender rule to request. By default it is File scan

- **archivepwd**: Optional, if you are scanning a password protected archive, provide the password. Default is empty password

- **Poll status every**: Optional. How often to check for scan job status. By default it is 5 seconds

<!-- -->

- **Files**: MandatoryList of files to scan.
- **opswat_address**: Mandatory. OPSWAT Metadefender server address

##### Outputs description

- **Scan result**: Hash. Provides the scan result of each scanned file
- **Clean Files**: Array. List of clean files
- **Infected Files**: Array. List of infected files
- **Sanitised Data IDs**: Array. List of data_id to files that have been sanitisation and need to be downloaded

##### Operating Instructions

Provide a list of files separated by the specified separator. The result is 2 arrays, one containing list of clean files and the other one contains list of infected files. IF you selected "Fail if infected file found", then the step will fail, otherwise, it will succeed. The hash outpput is structured as following:

- *Key:* file path. *Value:* A hash:
  - *Key:* data_id. *Value:* data_id, as it comes back from Metadefeneder
  - *Key:* result. *Value:* Result returned by Metadefender REST call for scanning the file

To download sanitised files, use CURL plugin with a custom command like this:\

    cd DOWNLOAD_PATH ; curl -X GET http://OPSWAT_ADDRESS:OPSWAT_PORT/file/converted/DATA_ID?apikey=APIKEY -O -J

\
replace the upper case names to variables and map them as input\
You need to get an API_KEY. You can either assigned an APIKEY to the user inside Metadefender dashboard (accounts) or login and use the session_id as apikey\

### Symantec Icap DLP Operations

![Symantec Icap DLP Operations icon](icons/SymantecIcapDlpOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin is used to make a ICAP call to Symantec DLP Engine.

| Version | Comment                                             |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| 0.1.0   | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.0.1   | initial release                                     |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Service Ip**: Provide the ip of the Symantec server.

- **Port**: Provide the port of the CMM server.

- **Service Name**: Provide the of the anti-virus scan utility.

- **File Name**: Name of the file to be scanned.

- **Sender Email**: Email id of the sender

- **Receiver Email**: Email id of the receiver.

- **Timeout Value**: Timeout value for the streaming operation

<!-- -->

- Document all available inputs using add_input_help
- **All Inputs**: These inputs are made mandatory for the ICAP call to be executed.

##### Outputs description

- **Raw_Execution_Output**: Shows the raw execution output of the call
- **Log_File_Path**: Shows the path to the log file where the outputs are saved.

##### Operating Instructions

This plugin is used to make a call to the Symantec DLP utility.\

### Symantec Icap Virus Scan Operations

![Symantec Icap Virus Scan Operations icon](icons/SymantecIcapVirusScanOperation.png){.plugin-icon}

This plugin is used to make a ICAP call to Symantec Antivirus Engine.

| Version | Comment |
|----|----|
| 0.4.0 | Updating to be compatible with Ruby 3.4 and Rails 8 |
| 0.3.0 | Add database migration |
| 0.2.2 | Fixing bug in the command line option code |
| 0.2.1 | fixing readline bug |
| 0.2.0 | adding command line execution |
| 0.1.4 | Fixed code for remote execute after testing on SCB. |
| 0.1.3 | Fixed process_output to return hash instead of string |
| 0.1.2 | Fixed bug in the service_ip get method |
| 0.1.1 | Fixed variable name, removed the encrypted part of the request and added an extra parameter to see raw log file content |
| 0.1.0 | Adding Remote Node execution option |
| 0.0.1 | initial release |

- ### Description

  #### Saved Parameters Description

- **Operation Type**: We support two types. Command line Symantec Utility execution or to execute our jar file

- **is Remote Execution**: Check this if the execution is on a remote node

- **Remote Node**: Choose the remote node for the execution

- **Remote Java jar path**: Provide the path along with the file name for the Symantec virus scan Java jar file

- **Service IP**: Provide the IP of the Symantec server.

- **Port**: Provide the port of the CMM server.

- **Service Name**: Provide the of the anti-virus scan utility.

- **File Name**: Name of the file to be scanned.

- **File Size**: Size of the file to be scanned

- **Send Size**: The size of the date to be streamed. Default(8192)

- **Local Jar Path**: For command line utility enter the jar path along with the file name

- **action**: Choose from the list of actions the command line utility supports

- **clobber**: select this if you want to always overwrite the scanned file with server response

- **verbose**: select this option if you want to print the scan result on standard output

- **api_version**: Select the appropriate API version

<!-- -->

- Document all available inputs using add_input_help
- **Service IP, Port, Service Name and File Name**: These inputs are made mandatory for the ICAP call to be executed.

##### Outputs description

- **Raw_Execution_Output**: Shows the raw execution output of the call
- **Log_File_Path**: Shows the path to the log file where the outputs are saved.

##### Operating Instructions

This plugin is used to make a call to the Symantec Anti-Virus utility.\
